## Trier and Mainz, merely another chapter of the Feud at Katzenelnbogen

When Leopold von Habsburg [hired the Winsen Blue Army](https://discord.com/c
events.fullText:
## Trier and Mainz, merely another chapter of the Feud at Katzenelnbogen

When Leopold von Habsburg [hired the Winsen Blue Army](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510745345426980926) to do the dirty business of [feuding](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1506573253462851665) with Mainz, he did not expect the Winsen Blues to fight and then win a decisive battle. He expected them to maraud the lands of Mainz, burning the fields, chasing the peasants out of their homes and forcing the peasants to seek refuge within the fortifications of the Archbishopric of Mainz. Leopold expected to humiliate the Wittelsbach Bishop of Mainz though economic means. He expected the Wittelsbach Bishop of Mainz to try the same. To hire an army and maraud though the lands of the Moselle valley, maybe besiege [Coblenz](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1506900952173641738). 

But then the Wittelsbach Bishop of Mainz died and the situation changed. The Cathedral Chapter of Mainz took a very different approach, they raised their levies and aimed to humiliate Trier once and for all in a pitch battle, a truly dangerous all or nothing proposition.A Gamble with the lives of men and the very prestige of two imperial Archbishoprics. And at Katzenelnbogen for Mainz that gamble truly failed.

For a local affair the 1410 levied army of the forces of Mainz was a mighty host, and it had truly depleted the reserves of the Mainz. Men had been be pulled from the autumn harvest for nothing but the pride of a Bishopric. Facing them was the hired 1000 man Winsen Blue army, made up of a row of heavy footmen and a row of light archers. This was for the day a professional army, but professional in the sense that they knew how not to die in a battle. The Winsen Blue knew how to save its own skin if things went bad.

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# Chaos in Famagusta

The Crown of Jerusalem, it was perhaps the most solemn of the crowns the Lusignans claimed. The significance of it still brought
events.fullText:
# Chaos in Famagusta

The Crown of Jerusalem, it was perhaps the most solemn of the crowns the Lusignans claimed. The significance of it still brought pride even to the most pessimistic of the Frankish nobles and knights in Cyprus. Every aspect of the crowning ceremony was intricately planned out to ensure it went off without a hitch and gave proper honor to all the pillars of the Kingdom. To the Roman Church, to the nobility and knights...and to the merchants who facilitated the wealth of the old Kingdom of Jerusalem. The coronation was steeped in tradition, one forged in the past nearly three centuries, last changed when the Lusignan Kings had to withdraw fully from the Holy Land in 1291 and thus could no longer be crowned in Tyre. Shortly after, fierce negotiations began in the royal court, and Famagusta was eventually chosen as the new location for the coronation in the early 14th century. Any further alteration, especially to the ceremony itself, would doubtlessly offend someone. As was about to made clear, it was a delicate balance, and one change might throw the entire coronation into chaos.

All seemed to be normal at first, it was going no different from the other coronations that Peter I had partaken in the last few days. The young King received the crown from the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem to jubilant celebration, and prepared to parade briefly throughout the city of Famagusta atop his steed. However when the Genoese and Venetian leaders of their respective communities prepared to share the reins of the King's horse and lead it along, as was the traditional act, the King's uncle, also named Peter, suddenly and without warning snatched the reins from the Venetian leader, and proclaimed that the Genoese were to be allowed the sole honor of holding *both* reins.

Almost in an instant there was an uproar from the Venetians, who began to shout at the Genoese and at the King's uncle.

The Genoese, of course, gladly and *loudly* accepted this great honor, and began to jeer at the Venetians as another prominent member of the local Genoese community grabbed hold of the previously Venetian reins. From atop his steed the King was growing more anxious as the shouting match below him continued in intensity. In an effort to defuse this suddenly hostile situation, the young Peter hesitantly asked his uncle about when this change was made, and the experienced Prince quickly and confidently told the King that he had made this radical change in ceremony with his authority as vice-King, a position that the elder Peter held when his brother, the King Guy II, was staying in Rome in 1356. The Venetians again began to shout with vigor and they vehemently protested this blatant favoritism, pleading with the young King to not recognize the change, and to even throw his uncle in prison for conspiring with the Genoese.

King Peter I was only 16, inexperienced and uncertain about his own abilities. In this moment of sudden and completely unexpected chaos during a time that was supposed to be joyful, he hesitated before eventually acquiescing to his uncle. The Genoese were to have both reins of the royal horse. The Venetians were utterly livid, but the ceremony continued onwards. There could be no illusions about how humiliated the Venetians were however, and they weren't going to tolerate this great slight anytime soon, as the rest of the coronation would make clear. The celebration dinner saw many incidents between the Venetians and Genoese. The limited number of royal guards in Famagusta could only try to separate the opposing parties when this happened, but they simply didn't have the numbers to properly enforce the peace. Afterwards, when the ceremony was all over and the King retired to his quarters in the city, the fighting between the Italians continued into the streets.

Here was where the conflict truly escalated.

There was no longer merely shouting matches, but physical fights soon broke out, and using their connections in the city both the Genoese and Venetians acquired deadly weapons to use against each other. Fires began to spread throughout the city as the situation only deteriorated more. There were many deaths that night and they were not limited to the Italians either, as many innocents would fall victim to the fires which started in the city and also to the blind fury of the rioters. As there was nothing he could do to quell the fighting, the King and his entourage left Famagusta the following morning, with scenes of mounting chaos behind them. In this contest between the Merchant Republics it was the **Genoese** who ultimately triumphed, receiving reinforcements from their nearby colonies and using their influence on Cyprus, succeeding in expelling the Venetians from the city, but even more radically placing the whole of Famagusta under their control. It was clear that the Genoese wouldn't give up Famagusta without a fight...or perhaps a negotiation.

When King Peter I returned to his capital of Nicosie, his nobles were furious with the Genoese occupation, and Peter agreed to raise an army with the sole objective to oust them from Famagusta. Chaos had reigned in Famagusta for many days, but now the young King was to restore order, even if the task was to be far more difficult than he expected. For the Genoese held a strong defensive position, and an army there to rival whatever Peter could muster on short-notice. 

The outcome of the conflict was yet to be decided, but it would be soon, and hopefully it would not end in disaster for the young Lusignan King...

-# Map of Famagusta

events.snippet:
# The Padova Concession

The Most Serene Republic is pleased to announce the successful conclusion of negotiations with the House of Este regarding th
events.fullText:
# The Padova Concession

The Most Serene Republic is pleased to announce the successful conclusion of negotiations with the House of Este regarding the status of Padova. Following constructive discussions between Venice and Ferrara, an agreement has been reached whereby the rights and claims held by the House of Este over the city of Padova are formally transferred to the Republic. The transition has been achieved through mutual consent and compensation, ensuring the preservation of peace and stability throughout the Veneto.

The previous noble ruler of the city of Padua, previously appointed by the Este, has been prematurely relieved of their duties to the city and has been offered an estate in the Paduan counteyside, as the Republic intends to directly control the interests of the city.
Padova's institutions, traditions, and privileges shall be respected during its integration into the Venetian sphere. The Republic's objective is not disruption, but stability. Merchants, craftsmen, landowners, and citizens alike may continue their affairs under the protection of Venetian law and administration. The acquisition of Padova also strengthens the security of the Veneto as a whole. The city's position along the routes connecting the mainland to the Adriatic makes it an important center of commerce and communication. Its incorporation into the Republic will help ensure the continued prosperity of the region and safeguard the interests of all who depend upon its markets and roads.

The Republic extends its gratitude to the House of Este for conducting these negotiations in good faith and looks forward to continued friendship between Venice and Ferrara in the years ahead. May Saint Mark watch over Padova, Venice, and all those who prosper beneath the peace they have chosen together.

# The Treviso Resolution
The question of Treviso has, at last, been settled.

Following negotiations between the Most Serene Republic and the Prince-B
events.fullText:
# The Treviso Resolution
The question of Treviso has, at last, been settled.

Following negotiations between the Most Serene Republic and the Prince-Bishopric of Aquileia, an agreement has been reached regarding the future administration of Treviso and its surrounding territories. In exchange for a contribution of 300 ducats to the Church for the relief of communities still suffering from the effects of the Black Death, Aquileia has agreed to transfer its claims and authority over the region to Venice.

As part of this agreement, the Trevisan Republic in exile at Belon shall be restored and incorporated into the Venetian state. Its citizens, officials, and leading families will be welcomed into the Republic, bringing with them their experience, traditions, and long-standing ties to the region. The return of Trevisan administration to Trevisan lands under the protection of Saint Mark ensures continuity while avoiding the disruptions often associated with territorial change.

Treviso occupies an important position within the Veneto. Its roads, markets, and agricultural lands contribute greatly to the prosperity of the region, while its location strengthens the security of the mainland routes connecting the Adriatic to the interior. Under Venetian administration, these advantages will be preserved and expanded for the benefit of all. With Padova and Treviso now joined to the Republic through peaceful agreement, Venice's presence upon the mainland enters a new chapter. The Republic remains, first and foremost, a maritime power. Yet the prosperity of the sea and the prosperity of the mainland are inseparable, and the security of one increasingly depends upon the stability of the other.

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
................ 
 
**[The declaration of Rouen](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505768744759070873/1511244996454649931)** 

Under the cover of the eve of the Christmas feasting the grand meeting of French nobles at Normandy will end with the declaration of a series of legal demands upon the crown in the name of the rightful king John the Second. Called the Griffin leauge due to the documents ending with the proclamation that the.  

> ***“Dragon shall be undone by the Griffin” ***

Looking to Unseat the king of France and replace him with his older uncle (who had been barred from inheriting) their aims are focused upon re balancing legal power of the crown against their noble fiefs, enforcing the Griffen laws which would halt the king from taxation of the fiefs and stop their ability to unanimously revoke titles.  

The coalition of nobles swiftly working to secure legitimacy both inside and out.  

First and foremost re-asserting the absolute <@1106651545296195655>  illegitimacy that the King ‘Black’ Edward Plantagenet and primacy of salic law, along with asserting the importance of the papacy being secured and protected in <@150411715757080576> Lyon away from the corruption of the Italians, drawing parallels to the behavior of the Orsini and wider moral corruption of the church caused since their move back to Rome (if somewhat biasedly so). Many knights looking to redirect their resources and military into supporting a faltering knight orders of the East, in the name of which the chivalric order of the star, led by the famed knight Geoffroi de Charny author of Livre de chevalerie.

Despite holding to the position of a French Papacy they do look to avert an <@222439858927435776> <@261290667106369536> <@763652399784919080>  anti French Holy Roman Empire coalition, angling more on the position of this coalition is not just in the name of French nobles but the universal law that defined by God creates a holy hierarchy in all systems, making use of and counterbalancing Aquinas thought against rising Italian humanism. In practice offering that French nobles free from the power of the crown and the crown itself would act to defend the rights of all nobility if so, threatened by kings... or even emperors. 

Outside of the nobility the coalition founded by many pious knights opposing the avariciousness of the crown would oppose the spread of the <@288410456526422016>  Hanse in the north or the <@170593650584059904> <@550686589043343360> <@234699731715096576>  Italian Merchant republics in the south. While for their oldest allies in the north many view the relationship with <@1417260821674852363> Scotland to be contradictory in nature and an issue of oddity for foreign policy, rather than courting the lowland Laird’s the knights of the alliance would rather the clans of the north of Scotland fight alongside them directly rather than waste away fighting fruitlessly on the English border, while hoping to expand Scottish naval capabilities if victorious where in Alba may frustrate England.  

As 1372 approaches and France shall see Three Kings all vie for power Europe stands silently, for here and now the right man may make a dynasty to rival the Carolingians and change the direction of Europe, the paths of Absolutism, English Dualism or French Nobility. **God Give us all strength!**

events.snippet:
**The Yaroslav Ploy**

Yaroslav now remains as the only eastern russian principality not under formal control or vassalage of Muscovy. It finds itself
events.fullText:
**The Yaroslav Ploy**

Yaroslav now remains as the only eastern russian principality not under formal control or vassalage of Muscovy. It finds itself in a curious situation, as its vassal principality, Kostroma, is de jure part of the titles of Grand Prince Ivan II, making the Principality both subordinate and sovereign over him.

It also finds itself ruled by a Princess. Premyslava Rurikovna, 2nd daughter of the late Prince Vasiliy Rurikovich, she is married to the "Basileus" Andrónikos IV Komnenos, he who has descent of the Imperial Komnenos dynasty, yet without Constantinople and a resurgent Palaiologos house, finds himself without much legitimacy within the city-fortress of Trebizond, proclaiming power through the Black Sea until Taman.

Now this is a conundrum. Premyslava finds herself much far from her husband, and it would be impossible for any Trapezuntine troops or support to arrive. But Grand Prince Ivan II is a righteous man, he does not seek to force Premyslava, his first cousin no less, to kneel directly through force.

As such, he wishes to make a ||forceful|| agreement with Premyslava and her husband. Her firstborn daughter will be married to his firstborn son, who shall be heir to Russia, while Vasiliy Komnenos, her son, shall rule in Trebizond, and Muscovy shall make a guarantee of support to them against foreign threats.

Premyslava urges her husband to accept the deal, as a kind hearted and ||craven|| woman, she does not wish for blood to be spilled. [Andrónikos heeds the call of his wife](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1449171134317723791/1511472552122515467), knowing that with the fall of the Horde, passage to the north may soon be open. 

Grand Prince Ivan II welcomes his cousin warmly within Moskva, and makes the trip to Trebizond to visit the soon to be father-in-law of his son.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**The Arrival of Muscovites**

Heavy snowfall marked the arrival of the Muscovites. It was near the confluence of the Kama and Chusovaya rivers. A hun
events.fullText:
**The Arrival of Muscovites**

Heavy snowfall marked the arrival of the Muscovites. It was near the confluence of the Kama and Chusovaya rivers. A hundred, no, a half-a-thousand Komi stood gathered upon and around the *Hill of Muköd*, but in the snowstorm it was impossible to make out their faces from a distance.

The Muscovites came slowly up the hill upon three enormous sleds, pulled by six horses each. Upon the first sat a man in a crimson red coat lined with sable fur, a black veil upon his head and a cross of silver and gold hanging around his neck. The sled’s cargo was well-covered, but glass, silver and other fine things glinted forth. Upon the second sat two men likewise covered in furs of all sorts, yet beneath which glinted gilded steel armour of a very fine make. The wares they carried were also covered, only an anvil and a few chests clear to see. Upon the third sat two priests, marked by their high kamilavkas. Their sled was almost empty, with only a few small chests and empty tarps.

The Muscovite procession stopped atop the hill, where the bare branches of a great rowan tree loomed over the assembled crowd.

The leader of the Muscovites climbed atop his sled, and from beneath his ice-crusted beard came a speech in the Komi language. "*I am Father Stepan, son of Simeon, of Ustyug by Nikolsk, he began, I come by the blessing of Prince Ivan II of Moskva and your Prince Opoch Uni of Nytva.*" The anticipation in the crowd was clear, movement was abound as people attempted to hear the priest’s words through the heavy snow. "*I have come to teach you the way of Christ!*" As his words disappeared into the white winds, the two armoured men approached him bearing a large axe. Father Stepan, son of Simeon, took the axe in his hand and declared:

"*In the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit, let this idol be destroyed!*"

And he began to swing his axe.

<@307587752860581888> <@160455024994942976>

"To Sir Philip, 
the Scots and their Gaelic allies are opposing his Majesty Edward IV's noble and v
events.fullText:
## The Lord-Lieutenant of Ireland, and His Orders

"To Sir Philip, 
the Scots and their Gaelic allies are opposing his Majesty Edward IV's noble and valiant efforts in France. Your orders are to assemble an army of your own and attack the Scot's allies in Ireland, to render them unable to lend aid to the fighting in Northumbria and Scotland. Should you need any resources to accomplish this, make haste to inform me. 

You are hereby made Lieutenant of the King's Lordship of Ireland and granted all the powers to act in the King's stead. 

Failure will result in your replacement.

- Simon Sudbury, acting treasurer of His Majesty Edward IV Plantagenet of England and France."

Edward IV "the Black" is, by all accounts, a fearsome warrior. By those same accounts, he takes much after his father in being a w
events.fullText:
## Difficult Times

Edward IV "the Black" is, by all accounts, a fearsome warrior. By those same accounts, he takes much after his father in being a wise general. It certainly would not take a keen military mind to note that his father struggled against France, without much success. 

Edward IV had tried for much of his reign to broker a peace with the Valois. However, it was obvious with the King's treatment of Brittany that any Plantagenet holdings remaining would soon be forfeit. 

When Anna von Wittelsbach, an envoy from Germany, arrived to encourage English involvement in the coming conflict, Edward IV assumed that this would be England capitalizing on an Imperial victory, or at least England fighting a much weakened France. 

Unfortunately, as Edward landed in Brittany he received news of a **tremendous Imperial defeat in the east...**

Could the English army, diminished from supply issues through the channel, stand alone?

**Edward suspected not. **

 Sir Alfonso de Borgonha ( <@298192682554228738> ) is sent to meet with Duc Jehan II of Normandie and Anjou ( <@255439449448644608> ) to discuss a "united front" against the tyrannical Phillipe. As Phillipe's army is quickly approaching, the isle of Jersey is suggested for the two lords to discuss.

events.author: Guy
events.snippet:
# SEE YOURSELF: WHAT'S ALL INSIDE THAT DEAD SHELL?

# 'TILL THE ROTTEN DYIN' BITS ALL WITHIN'D BLOOM AWAY

*As the Karamanids have grown exponentially
events.fullText:
# SEE YOURSELF: WHAT'S ALL INSIDE THAT DEAD SHELL?

# 'TILL THE ROTTEN DYIN' BITS ALL WITHIN'D BLOOM AWAY

*As the Karamanids have grown exponentially in power within Anatolia, so too has the government been forced to adapt to not just the increased load, the diverse peoples that have slowly come under their array.*

*The Karamanid government, before it's rapid expansion that is, was borne from the shattred and corupt remains of Rum. These bits and pieces then were forged by the Mongols to where, even if not excelling governance, the tithe could still come every year. That same system, which has remained the cornerstone of almost all modern Anatolian governence besides a key exceptions, could not serve in this age of rebirth. It's institutions were told rotted, pervading with the sickly sweet smell of decay and the distinct tinge of blood upon one's throat.*

*That was certainly no fault of Mongols, at least for their aims, but the system relies far too much on the greater Mongol governance to not only collect their tithe, but to keep the general peace amongst the other Beyliks as well. And in all frankness, one could've seen with the last 40 odd years how such a system struggles to do such a thing when their no longer exist the supreme authority over the squabbling tribes and princes. Moreover, intentionally designed, it gives the Estates far too much power in comparison to the crown. Due to the Mongols seeking to have very little rebellion amongst their tributaries, privileges of the Estates are enshrined in law to a point wherein they would be near impossible even for a sufficiently strong enough ruler to remove them, requiring a level of tact and skill that very men ever hold*

*So Fahreen was left, upon his ascension to throne, with a corrupt, overbearing, over bloated governance that could not, or more accurately would not, enforce his will without accommodation given in return.*

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**Gods light goes dim** 
<@460814348487753730> <@259426601865838592> <@564196329132261376> <@150411715757080576> 

Already in northern France, in the dense forests of Brittany and the free ports of Flanders was faith never so frayed as the days of the Albigensian Crusade’s. For in a single man's life they had fought in the fields of Aquitaine against England, smashed by the Black Ankou at Rennes, watched their family succumb to the black death, watch their priests killed, bicker and steal rather than bring comfort, see their lords hung by a dreadful king and their coin taken. To be a man in such times is to walk empty streets, find no comfort in community or faith, to be a man in such times is to be without hope. Under such pitiless sorrow many in France take to all manner of religious offshoots, German millenarism spreading through Hanse Kontors and traders, mixing in turn with flagellant cultists of Normandy, Beguines and Beghards in Flanders and independent mendicant and mystic cults of Brittany. 

Not just the lay people but knights as well, many of whom have sworn to the order of the star making the chivalric order in truth a semi heretical fanatic hotbed. The war of the Griffin league seen not only as a political war by lords but for these knights and common people as one of the great signifiers for the end of days, the king likened to one head of Satan, that of Mammon.  

If John is victorious, such religious mania may spread even further afield, so that one day the cross of the Cathars flies in the south, and Lollards once more come to make the world clean.  

```ansi
[1;2m[1;33mIf the Griffin League wins reform desire and heresy may spread, forcing John to turn against former followers or allow the spread.... [0m[0m
```

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Battle for Cyprus
**The Aftermath of Famagusta and the Genoese dominance of the Lusignan Kingdom.**

With Famagusta coming under Genoese occupat
events.fullText:
# The Battle for Cyprus
**The Aftermath of Famagusta and the Genoese dominance of the Lusignan Kingdom.**

With Famagusta coming under Genoese occupation during the [Chaos of Famagusta](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1511434144570277969), King Peter I of Cyprus hastily raised an army with the support of many of his Frankish nobles to oust the Italians from the vital trading city. The King, young and inexperienced in ruling, much less commanding an army, nevertheless decided to set out towards Famagusta at the head of his ragtag army. His nobles, unfamiliar with just how strong the Genoese garrison was, assured the King that the army he commanded, ill equipped and undermanned as it was, would be more than enough to rout the small Genoese force. Peter I was therefore confident that this wasn't a situation where he needed an experienced commander and he could win the battle himself. Little did he know that the Doge of Genoa had been secretly supplying the Genoese in Famagusta and elsewhere on Cyprus for months with quality weapons. This would be no meager force the King's army faced, but one that had been preparing for this conflict for much time.

When King Peter I and his army reached Famagusta, they found the garrison guarding the walls much more numerous and stout than they had expected. Early probes against the defenses were easily repulsed, before the army eventually set up camp for the night. The next morning, on the dawn of battle, Peter I became increasing reluctant to fight. The Genoese were evidently more prepared than everyone in the Lusignan camp expected, and so Peter sent an envoy to the Genoese under the cover of a truce. There the envoy presented the King's demands to return Famagusta to the Kingdom in exchange for a "compensation" for what the Genoese endured in the coronation.

The nobles in Peter's army were not exactly pleased by this olive branch, and if the Genoese refused the demand there could be no option to withdraw. A glorious battle would be the only remaining course of action. Unfortunately for Peter, the commander of the Genoese garrison refused the demand, and instead gave his own demands. Upon hearing what the Genoese sought, it became more clear to the King that this uprising was more prepared than anyone had initially suspected, for the terms of the Genoese felt too specific to be conjured up by a mere Catepano. The King, according to the Genoese, was to expel all Venetian traders, gift Genoa with exclusive trading rights in Famagusta, and allow the Genoese garrison to have sole occupation of the citadel in the city. There could be no world where these terms were accepted, and yet there was also no option to withdraw and build a proper army to face the well equipped and led garrison. So, with the upcoming battle a certainty, Peter I began to assemble his men for the assault.

---
It was a disaster. The Lusignan force was utterly routed after less than a few hours of combat, its ill prepared soldiers fleeing from the scene without any semblance of order. The Genoese in Famagusta were evidently reinforced not just with arms, but also with soldiers from the other Genoese colonies on Cyprus. The assaults against the walls of Famagusta were completely ineffective, and only led to the deaths of many dozens of the attackers, and when the Genoese sallied out of the city they tore through the haphazardly led mob of men in the young King's army. With the battle evidently lost, Peter tried to withdraw with what forces were still intact. This however, was pointless. Caught in personal combat with several Genoese, Peter was unable to retreat safely, and thus chose to surrender himself to the Genoese. Peter's uncles, who had accompanied him to the battle, all scattered to different places on the island to prepare its defense.

The Genoese, competently led and with a clear strategy in mind, did not let their momentum cease whilst they negotiated a treaty with the captured Lusignan King. The responsibility of organizing the defense of the capital, Nicosie, fell to the King's uncle, also named Peter. Perhaps purposefully, Nicosie found itself completely unprepared to receive an attack by the Genoese and thus the force met little resistance when it entered the city. Of course, the elder Peter would blame his lackluster defense on the fact that most of the King's routed army came from Nicosie, and so there was too few men left to raise to defend it. Elsewhere, however, the Genoese finally met with defeat. In the north of Cyprus, Peter I's uncles, James and John, successfully organized the defense of northern Cyprus. The Genoese were repulsed from the town of Kyrenia and the St. Hilarion Castle despite multiple efforts to take both, and attacks against Limassol and Paphos ultimately failed to succeed before peace was reached.

However, these minor victories were truly minor in the face of the overwhelming Genoese victories in both routing King Peter's army and capturing the King himself. With this...the peace was sure to be overwhelmingly in Genoa's favor. After some weeks in captivity, Peter I signed the utterly humiliating treaty declaring that what remained of Famagusta after the fires and battle would fall under a joint Genoese-Lusignan sovereignty, that he would pay huge compensations to the Genoese, and the Genoese Republic would receive exclusive trading privileges over the whole of the island. In exchange, he was released from captivity and the Genoese force would withdraw from Nicosie. There could be no hiding the great defeat that the King had suffered at the hands of the Genoese.

If the Lusignan Kingdoms were suffering after the loss of Cilicia, now they are truly in a state of despair. The Chaos of Famagusta has taken its toll on the island, which is now weaker than ever before.

King Peter I de Lusignan, still just 16 years old, is stung badly by the defeat, and will without doubt attempt in the decades ahead to slowly detach the Kingdom from the treaty he was all but forced to sign with the Genoese. In the meantime however, there is little he can do but try to restore some semblance of authority and stability on Cyprus, even whilst his Kingdom's wealth increasing fills Genoese pockets instead of his own...

-# The Genoese dominance of Cyprus.

<@170593650584059904>

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Ramifications of the Treaty of Famagusta (1371)

With the signing of the Genoese-favored treaty in Cyprus after weeks of warfare on the island,
events.fullText:
# The Ramifications of the Treaty of Famagusta (1371)

With the signing of the Genoese-favored treaty in Cyprus after weeks of warfare on the island, it is declared that all other states are barred from trading on the island, for the Genoese will now have the *sole* responsibility (and profit) of facilitating the movement of goods to and from Cyprus. The Venetians, who had their own small number of colonies on the island, are now expelled from the Kingdom and are barred from returning.

This is sure to be insulting to the Venetians, but probably not unexpected. The Venetian colonies on the island had been outperformed by the Genoese for decades, and they had less and less influence or profit in trade on the island anyway. It is doubtful that this will hurt the Venetian economy in any meaningful way, but it is still a humiliation which gives more trading power to their foremost rival. However, with the Genoese dominance of the island it is unlikely that the King of Cyprus, fresh off a major defeat, will be able to do anything which opposes the terms he was made to sign in the treaty with Genoa.

For now at least, the island of Cyprus belongs in all but name to the Genoese, and that means whoever Genoa's enemies are, Cyprus has to make them their enemy as well...

...for better or worse.

-# The Treaty of Famagusta (1371)

<@550686589043343360>

**(End)**

events.fullText:
**The End of Times** 🔔 <:unrest:1162434338525290569> 

A millenarist cult has been spreading throughout northern Germany. Despite being in a peaceful situation compared to the rest of Christendom, some priests and common people have become alarmed by the constant war after the Black Death: “Have you not seen the Pale Horse ride through Christendom? Have you not seen the Four in our lands, in our homes?” The movement has been spreading like wildfire, no doubt helped by the web of trade in the Baltic and below. 

The Shepherds of the Church are two, both waging war at each other and causing untold deaths in Italy. The greedy King in France, some say the Beast incarnated to bring doom to Earth, has beaten the armies of the Holy Roman Empire and is due to beat the ones of England. Only the rumour of a rebellion brings hope, but few think good can triumph against evil, for the rebel is but the uncle of the Beast. Meanwhile, the fake Empire has allied to muslims and stopped a Crusade for the Holy Land, perhaps the only hope for Salvation. Will Christ even find us when he comes? Is there hope for us?

The End of Times is near. Repent for your sins.

events.author: The Kryft
events.snippet:
# The House of Wittelsbach (1371 AD)

In the year of our Lord __1371__, the House of Wittelsbach remains ever-influential and prestigious amongst it's
events.fullText:
# The House of Wittelsbach (1371 AD)

In the year of our Lord __1371__, the House of Wittelsbach remains ever-influential and prestigious amongst it's compatriots in the HRE. Ruling vast territories, from the left bank of the Rhine to the southern reaches of the Alpine mountain range to the black forests of Brandenburg, the progeny of **Otto the Redhead** has ruled and reigned well indeed.

Alas, tragedy would claim many in quick succession within these past few years. Year after year, a Wittelsbach would be sought by the reaper whom sought to take his due from the prospering house.

**Ruprecht the Red** would [die first in **__1366__**](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510745185162887309). Having lived a well life full of mutiny and struggle, his passing would be celebrated dearly by his brother.

The youngest of Ludwig's sons, **Maximillian Josef of Bavaria-Pfalz** would be claimed by the Lord a year later in **__1367 __**at 27. The young lord would fortunately leave an heir for the duchy of Bavaria-Pfalz.

Following were **[Count-Palatine Rudolf II](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510750869673476337).** and Margrave **Ludwig 'the False'**, both passing in **__1368__**. 

Then there was the blasted year of [**__1369__**](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1510748165362417735), the empire fighting a struggle against the French crown and their Avignon anti-popes, Stephan leading at it's head as the Kaiser. It was as brutal as it was bloody, the imperial cohorts slaughtered by the French hounds.

In the year of our Lord [**__1370__**, death itself marched upon the Rhine](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1511044439244669159), bearing the banners of France and insisted that it must pass through Heidelberg. **Countess-Palatine Anna** would be among their victims, executed and buried without dignity by the French king and his Savoyard queen.

[**Kaiser Stephan**'s himself would fall in battle in **__1371__**](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510759035887030333), defending the town of Dordrecht against a French army. Such marked six Wittelsbach dead in six years and with the reaper hopefully content with it's harvest.

And now, the survivors rule over the house and it's many titles. In Heidelberg, Anna's husband **Albrecht** has accrued the titles of *Palatine of the Rhine; Duke of Bavaria-Chiemgau and Count of Tyrol*, forming the **Rudolf-Ludwigian** line. They hold the prestigious title of elector as well as seniority, giving them much influence within the empire and respect from the other lines.

In Munich, **Ludwig V.** the Roman rules Upper Bavaria and holds seniority over the many lesser titles of his younger brothers, heading the **Ludwigian Line**. They rule most of Upper Bavaria as well as **Brandenburg**, being the most influential among the other lines.

And lastly, duke **Johann von Wittelsbach** rules still Lower Bavaria, having no heir and being the sole descendant of duke Heinrich XIII. Whilst formally independent and having more than enough power to assert such, her wife— Anna's influence and her loyalty to Ludwig V. has seemingly secured Lower Bavaria for Munich all in but name.

And thus, these three lines constitute the House of Wittelsbach. May they live long and prosper well.

-# (d0ne.!)

events.snippet: The current Emperor of the Great Eastern Empire has followed the steps of many that had preceded him. As many Emperors before him, he would often negl
events.fullText:
The current Emperor of the Great Eastern Empire has followed the steps of many that had preceded him. As many Emperors before him, he would often neglect the affairs of the Asia Minor in favor of European affairs. John V was not a bright man, and most of his great decisions can be attributed to other, Greater man, who were hiding in his shadow. 

One of those men was Nikiphoros Apokaukos. He was a senator and responsible for managing most of the garrisons in Anatolia. He used his large inheritance to push for certain reforms in the Senate, and with luck - those reforms have passed. He even persuaded the Emperor to designate a large amount of Imperial funds to build a network of forts that would protect the borders of Anatolia from those who would threaten it.

He knew that if the Empire had repeated the mistakes of the past - it could very well find itself back in a ruinous state from which it had recently started recovering. And so, Nikiphoros has started preparing a delegation to the eastern neighbors of the Empire. He would search for peace and stability, at least until the Emperor would see the importance of the matters of the Anatolia, and would stop using all the time and resources of the Empire on the European affairs. The noble could not change the mind of the Emperor, but he could give the Empire time.

And so the delegation with the noble himself has moved from Constantinople with intention of reaching the City of Konya. Standing under the gates of the city, they would call for an audience with the Bey. 

-# Would such an audience be granted?

# The Fulfilment of the Concord of Suceava #

During the earliest days of Wallachian and Hungarian cooperation as brotherly Catholic countries under t
events.fullText:
# The Fulfilment of the Concord of Suceava #

During the earliest days of Wallachian and Hungarian cooperation as brotherly Catholic countries under the Roman church, the lands of Moldavia were a contentious issue where both realms held major interests and goals. Then in the small town of Suceava, capital of the Moldavian County of Baia, a concord was reached between the Wallachian and the Hungarian as to the fate of this land. The Concord of Suceava would lay the groundwork for the creation of a consolidated Moldavian realm protected and freed from the infamous Tatar Yoke. 

As stipulated in this deal, the many counts and lords of the subject lands of Moldavia would be consolidated into a singular catholic Duchy of Moldavia and become a formal march of the Hungarian kingdom. The ruler of Moldavia would be the venerable catholic Basarab lords of Wallachia, holding the territory in a personal union.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## The Lusatian League

The decline of stable authority in the Margraviate of Brandenburg was not an entirely localized force, and its occurrence was
events.fullText:
## The Lusatian League

The decline of stable authority in the Margraviate of Brandenburg was not an entirely localized force, and its occurrence was only further bolstered in its outpouring with the chaos following the end of the Přemsylid dynasty on the Bohemian throne some years prior to Waldemar’s death. The anarchy of both these events straddled each other due to their geographic proximity, and between those two realms was Upper Lusatia, a land which historically fell under neighboring states for varying periods and most recently with the extinction of the Brandenburg Haus Askanier, was granted to Jan *der Blinnen* of Bohemia by Kaiser Ludwig IV, and was thus incorporated once more into the Bohemian Crown. The nature of ruling Lusatia was, for much of its history, often left to the local systems. Burgraves and judges exerted considerable power, even though the structure of allodial titles was late-coming, and the Landvogt operated effectively as the region’s chief authority on behalf of whomever owned it at the time. 
-# Upper Lusatia as part of the Bohemian Crown.

The decline in the states of Brandenburg and Bohemia resulted in an inability to exert central rule over Upper Lusatia, which as lent itself into the aforementioned realms, also resulted in the rise of robber barons and marauding knight-errants in Lusatia. The state of affairs which this presented would eventually be answered in 1349, the year after Karel IV’s death, in the formation of the **Lusatian League** between the prominent cities of the area, in pursuit of protecting one another from the lawless plunder of wandering warriors, or extrajudicial taxes from petty lords, and to secure the safety of the trade routes which they depended upon in the absence of a strong royal authority. Karel himself during his reign as King of Bohemia had endeavored in ending at last the many robber barons across Bohemia, long ignored by his father, but had so quickly found himself entangled in imperial ambitions that his goals were left unfinished. 

This move swiftly ascended their recognition and viability in the region, even beyond Bohemia, as the Lusatian League hired soldiers and stormed the lowly castles of the robber barons and their hideouts, and took it upon themselves to seize those marauding knights, hailing from impoverished noble families, and dispense upon them the justice they deemed fit. His Majesty Otto *der Fröhliche**, Karel’s successor as King of Bohemia, recognized the opportunity before him in providing royal assent to the League’s formation and even going so far as to grant them additional rights as enforcers and deputies of the royal will in Lusatia, to the expense of the landed gentry therein. [ <@190640828954181632> Otto the Merry mention. ]
-# The chief cities of the Lusatian League, Bautzen and Görlitz.

With the Lusatian Mountains serving as natural barriers between Bohemia itself and the Lusatian League, the king’s representative therein was often an appointed notable from the League itself, and thus they retained a good deal of autonomy from the crown which in turn benefited the king, with the revenue raised from their lucrative trade and the easement of burdens in paying for Lusatia’s defenses as they were handed over to the League.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## *The Second Coming*

-# His Majesty Jan, by the Grace of God, King of the Romans, of Bohemia. [ Made through AI editing in-game portrait.]
The pass
events.fullText:
## *The Second Coming*

-# His Majesty Jan, by the Grace of God, King of the Romans, of Bohemia. [ Made through AI editing in-game portrait.]
The passes, for now, slumber, the way they always do when commerce and movement through them cease. They lie there, blanketed in that pristine snow, awaiting the change of seasons. In towns such as Brenner, or San Candido, the faithful clergy address the foolish wanderer and merchant in the warmth of hospitality, their misjudging of the seasons haranguing them into the arms of strangers. 

The Etschtal sleeps soundly beneath its coatings, hibernating. The valley, which has seen many hosts of would-be Emperors, of ambitious conquerors, awaits its next figure of renown to traverse its course. It has but a few months left. 

Forty years ago, Jan *der Blinnen*, King of Bohemia, traveled through the Etschtal in his opening invasion of Lombardy, rife and stigmatic with chaos. Through the Etschtal he retained his supply lines to Tyrol, and through it to Bohemia, as he sustained his ambitions of creating a Luxembourg Signoria in Northern Italy. Though he would eventually take for himself Cremona, Brescia, Bergamo, and other such cities, his defeat ultimately came and forced his withdrawal from the peninsula. No such German princely host has made an imprint on the region since, and the regional powers have only strengthened at the expense of the Della Scalla, leaving the north more impenetrable than before. 

Forty years later, his grandson prepares to make the same journey. Jan II of Bohemia has since returned to Praha after his election and coronation as **King of the Romans**, occurring in the aftermath of [Stephan’s death at Dodrecht](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510759035887030333). The assembled Roman invasion of Flanders afterwards was thus soundly defeated, and Jan negotiated the peace which saw the Kingdom of Arles rendered asunder in all but name. From Praha he has endeavored to create a [Unified Court](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512182768522629292) for his imperial reign, [settle the expansionism of Haus Welf](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512590575407333516), maintain [Franconian peace and stability](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512595846301089803)  between the Count Palatine of the Rhine and the Archbishop of Trier, and mediate His Holiness Félix V’s [desires to seek refuge in Germany](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512235519692767262) against fears of French dominion. In all of these he hopes to settle on behalf of imperial authority and in due regard to righteousness and his obligations as a pious Christian ruler, but none shall be deceived as to what comes next.

The mountains sit now, still and silent, their snow-capped peaks having blanketed the world beneath them. Come April, when the Sun at last feasts itself upon the face of their frozen heights, and the meltwater begins its descent through the gorges, the road will open. And when it opens, **he** will come. His Majesty, the King of the Romans, will travel the Etschtal to Trent, whereupon his ***Italienzug*** shall begin in earnest. Those frozen passes which now leave Germany cleaved from the French war in Italy, shall soon bleed open and bring forth the retinues, banners, and the **Emperor-in-Waiting**. Jan will come for the **Iron Crown**. He will come for the **Kingdom of Italy**. He will walk among his Italian subjects and set their affairs in order. He will mediate their disputes, he will handle their pretensions, and he will observe who opens their gates to him, or who sits behind them in quiet defiance. 

The mountains cannot hold forever. While the Pope looks north in flight, the King looks south for submission.

Germans: <@190640828954181632> <@792904515795550218> <@271318019052011532> <@288410456526422016> <@491096374507601922> <@215639063502258176> <@222439858927435776> <@763652399784919080> <@137101075411894272> <@112960565344804864> <@420614608399761419> <@883471267506843668> <@385875387608989714>

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## A Domineering action for a continuous realm, the illegal Seizure of Cochem.

Leopold von Habsburg is a malevolent and domineering man, to Leopold t
events.fullText:
## A Domineering action for a continuous realm, the illegal Seizure of Cochem.

Leopold von Habsburg is a malevolent and domineering man, to Leopold those that beneath are to be ordered and used. And Leopolds relations with Count Reinhard of Daun a direct vassal of Trier, are nothing but a perfect illustration of that.

The land of Cochem while a wine producing territory is not a rich land. The land is poorly cultivated, with what flat land there is, dominated by thick woods before it sharply ascends into the foothills of the Eifel Mountains. Cochem ravaged by the black death as it was and only being home to 4,600 souls in the year 1356, is worth nothing for its economic value.
But Cochem is a strategic land, nestled on the western bank of the Moselle river. Cochem lies equal distance between the Toll Castle at [Coblenz](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1506900952173641738) and the town of Trier.

And most importantly of all to Leopold, Cochem lies within the lands of the Counts of Daun. And Cochem for all its lack of material value **divides the Bishopric of Trier in two.**

The von Daun have ruled Cochem since 1280. That all changed in the year of our lord 1361, 1 year into his tenure as Archbishop of Trier. Leopold unilaterally marched the mercenary men of the Winsen Blue [(famed victors of the Battle of Katzenenlnbogen)](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1511250819452764230) into what was the County of Daun and seized the territory of Cochem. 
Leopold did not ask, nor did he negotiate with the von Daun. He merely marched in and told his vassal to get out. And if Count Reinhard von Daun were to protest? The Milestone of an Excommunication and an Interdict placed on his lands, would be placed around his neck before he would be thrown into the river Moselle.

Why did Leopold march into his vassals territory and seize Cochem, with no pretext or legality? Because Cochem gave Leopold a continuous realm from where the Moselle meets the Rhine in Coblenz, down the length of the west bank of the Moselle to the tow of Trier finally ending in the Hunsruck foothills of Meerzisch.
**And a continuous realm is worth breaking Imperial Law for.** 
-# The Count Palatinate certainly believes this to be true. [Extorting protection from the French to connect their Western lands](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1511412383535599646)

From Coblenz to Meerzisch, Leopold can march whatever men he has at his disposal, with no complaint, no pretext and without regard for ancient rights.

From Coblenz to Meerzisch, Leopold now has it within his grasp to be Judge, Jury and Executioner of the law. A continuous realm where there shall be no Robber Barons nor Bandits to disrupts Leopolds Tolls, nor stifle his decrees.

From Coblenz to Meerzisch, Leopold shall centralise, he shall tax and he shall control. 

And Leopold as his nature, **shall dominate**.

*figure 1, A Map of the lands of Trier and its vassals, highlighted with the Orange border and the vassal lands with the Yellow hatching. Cochem, Trier and Coblenz are named and pointed too.*
*figure 2, Cochem in the year of our lord 1371, in the 10 years since its seizure it has prospered as part of the continuous realm of Trier.*

events.author: Cayde
events.snippet:
# Remembering Allies

Rupert "the Red" von Wittelsbach was once a staunch ally of Albert "the Wise" von Habsburg. Despite the grave defeat at Mosbach,
events.fullText:
# Remembering Allies

Rupert "the Red" von Wittelsbach was once a staunch ally of Albert "the Wise" von Habsburg. Despite the grave defeat at Mosbach, these ties were not to fade, even after the death of Albert. Otto "the Merry" von Habsburg, King of Bohemia, Archduke of Austria and Count of Holland, was tied up in the demands and responsibilities of his rule, and as such was not able to directly involve himself in the affairs of the Palatinate. However, his son Friedrich II, was particularly interested in restoring the ties of his uncle Albert, and as such would invite Ruprecht to Wiener Neustadt to stay in the Habsburg castle there, while also dispatching an emissary to the King of Hungary, Wallachia and Poland to seek restoration of old ties. He would reiterate the commercial ties built by his father, to ensure trade between Praha & Vienna would continue to mutually benefit both cities.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**East of the Urals**

The Mansi and Komi peoples have long known of and interacted with each other, yet the Ural mountains separating them have meant
events.fullText:
**East of the Urals**

The Mansi and Komi peoples have long known of and interacted with each other, yet the Ural mountains separating them have meant they have developed in different directions. Where the Komi chieftains are united in a large realm of their own making, the Mansi are divided into half a hundred petty princedoms; and where the Komi would rather trade for iron and gold than dig it up themselves, the Mansi are experienced miners, smelters and smiths.

It is thus no surprise that when the Rus’ look east, it is not vast and to their eyes empty lands of Perm which catch their eyes but instead the rich Principalities of Yugra where statues of gold and copper gods are said to be made and worshiped. Doubtless, were it not for Great Perm, the men of Novgorod and Muscovy and Kyiv would centuries ago have charged heels over heads into the vast Yugran lands in their endless lust for wealth.

It is just because of these reasons that Opochi of Perm has decided (with the blessing of the Elder Council) to send good women and men to the east to discuss settling among the western Mansi and in return for western wares and a defensive alliance the Mansi prince of Uktus has allowed the Komi to settle by Tagil and promised that they shall be taught in the ways of good Mansi miners and smiths. 
Doubtless he hopes the Komi, ancient partners in both trading and raiding, are better friends than Rus or Tatars.
The Mansi are also promised a place in Cherdyn, should they desire it.

*The prince of Uktus accepts the wares of Perm*

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**The Wild Steppes**

The collapse of the Golden Horde, mainly in part due to instability after the death od Ozbek and the final blow when Ivan II "Br
events.fullText:
**The Wild Steppes**

The collapse of the Golden Horde, mainly in part due to instability after the death od Ozbek and the final blow when Ivan II "Breaker of Chains" captured Sarai and forced the Gengiskhids to renounce the Tartar Yoke.

Many different khans sought to secure their own rule, and the central rule in Sarai was broken, with only feudal authority within Astrakhan remaining as the only city in the steppe.

Conflicts became more open as raids, border conflicts and attacks by the various khans against each other. This also started attempts to attack and raid Muscovite settlements near the steppe, who many khans seek to destroy to prove their authority.

Muscovy in return has started the process of building fortified wooden settlements, known as Gorod-Kreposts to secure the border. Though this freedom has also started to entice some of the peasants of Muscovy...

**The Cossacks**

While small in number at start, some peasants and laborers, tired of feudal persecution and looking for a freerer lifestyle, ventured south, near the settlement of Yelets, taking similar traditions to the horde riding sheperds of the Steppes. They became known as Cossacks.

While small in number for now, they have been serving as a nomadic guard and settlers, protecting small settlements in the area in exchange for food and furs.

These nomadic warriors are fierce, almost always on horseback, counteracting tartar raids and taking their loot.

Grand Prince Ivan II has recognized their prowess, and while some of the nobility is angry at the escaped peasants, Ivan II has granted them a degree of autonomy, around the village of Yeletsk, where he appointed his cousin Vasiliy Rurikovich to serve as an administrator and central authority.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# Hungarian in the Swamps #

The sky was filled with stars and the air was heavy. Within the swampy lands of Argenta, a vast array of tents and campfi
events.fullText:
# Hungarian in the Swamps #

The sky was filled with stars and the air was heavy. Within the swampy lands of Argenta, a vast array of tents and campfires could be seen spreading across the landscape, causing a warm glow on the horizon. Within one the largest tents resided King Louis I of Hungary. He sat there in his royal tent reading over letters and reports from the previous couple weeks, his hand firmly holding the side of his head in an agitated state. What the Balkans and the East had failed to do, Italy had succeeded in overwhelming the King of Hungary and compromised his decisive nature. They initially came to Italy to protect the Pope, but now the Hungarian army found itself mired in a swamp, lost and disorganized. All to protect a Pope that wanted to fight regardless of the prospect of peace.

As Louis looked over the reports, the information varied wildly. In some letters, the Ferrarisi and Milanese had voiced interest in peace. In some, the Ferrarisi had voiced interest in a comprehensive peace. In others, the Ferarrisi had claimed to know of the hostage Princess Elizabeth, daughter of Louis, and were threatening to kill her if the Hungarians did not leave. On the contrary, the Milanese had sent few letters, yet had claimed the Princess was safe and that they had accepted peace. Papal letters remained just as confusing. One letter claimed to have accepted the peace deals with Milan and Ferrara, whilst another from Pope Phelix demanded that the Hungarians arrest all Cardinals opposing the war, and order his holiness had no power to give and was beyond the obligations of Louis.

-# (Disclaimer: The image used was rendered in Midjourney using in game portrait images)

To add to this confusion that straddles the edge of absolute farce, reports of other scheming Italian states, reports of French mercenaries on the march, rampant hunger, and growing sickness within the Hungarian army had finally overwhelmed the Hungarians King.

After stressfully going over the papers, Louis suddenly succumbed to his anger, groaning and yelling curses as he flipped the table with all the documents that had caused him much discomfort. After collecting his thoughts, he called for his army commanders and ordered them to make the men ready to break camp. Simultaneously, he ordered his scribe to write a final, public letter of proclamation to the Pope in Rome.

events.snippet:
# The Final Letter #

 A public letter arrives from the Hungarian Camp at Argenta to the audience of the Holy Father, Phélix V
Copies of the letter ha
events.fullText:
# The Final Letter #

 A public letter arrives from the Hungarian Camp at Argenta to the audience of the Holy Father, Phélix V
Copies of the letter have also been sent to the Ferrarisi, Milanese, and any French contingent outside Ferrara
To Phélix, the Pope in Rome,

I will make this clear as the empty blue sky. I have warned you about disregarding the peace proposals made and the consequences thereof. This is not the only sin you have committed against the Hungarians. In addition, you have demonstrated a complete and utter lack of appreciation for the valued aid the Hungarians have provided to you, the disregard of my daughter and a Princess of Hungary’s safety, and  the unacceptable demands of using MY army and MY men to arrest holy men of YOUR church. 

You lack the senses of reason, humbleness, and piety that is demanded of a Pope and your actions not only have led you to your current predicament, but have drag the Hungarians with you like a helpless man being tied to an anchor and being dropped into the sea to be dragged down into its darkest depth. No more.

I warned you of the consequence of your action and now you shall reap the harvest you have sown. As of this day, December 12, the Hungarian army will remove itself from this conflict in Northern Italy, extricate itself from all hostile conflicts, and return to Hungary in earnest. Any force that possesses hostile intentions, may it be Ferarrisi, French, Milanese, Knights, or Papal, shall be driven off with equal measure. I warned you, Phelix, and these are the consequences. Had you possessed virtues of your forbearer, His holiness John XXIII, the situation would have resulted differently, but why regret the past.

I pray that Holy Justice be delivered swiftly in this conflict as swiftly as this letter is delivered to you.

Signed,

Louis the First, King of Hungary,
King of Croatia and Dalmatia,
of the Capetian House of Anjou.

-# (Disclaimer: Image made using Midjourney)

<@150411715757080576> <@315868897012219904> <@200833745606017024> <@423689694258397195> <@545657694489542666> <@460814348487753730>

events.author: Bear
events.snippet:
# The Foundations of History

Within months of the continued campaigns and bloodshed split across the French Rhine, the people of the south grow weary
events.fullText:
# The Foundations of History

Within months of the continued campaigns and bloodshed split across the French Rhine, the people of the south grow weary, tired, and more enraged at the greedy King's commands.

First, the King who came for their lands, splitting them among French nobles from the north who had no rights or claims to the lands beneath the Pyrenes shadow. Year after year, the nobles tilled and ruined farmland kept pristine through centuries of family-management. Yields grew poorer. What little food did survive was sold in the markets at a premium via merchants working for the nobles. Atop of that, the taxes on each premium bundle of wheat kept robbing families blind.

Second, the insistent tithes and taxes that must be paid to Paris. Each time the people of Gascony think they can start gaining footholds after each campaign. Each making more ducets than the previous year, the taxes and tithes to the Church go up at the behest of the French Crown. The people feel trapped in a never ending cycle servicing the rich blood in Paris with their joints... their youth... their blood.

Third, and certainly not last, the loss of their sons. Each new generation has seen a new campaign or new promise to return glory to France. First against the English bastards who still occupy Aquitaine, much to the Gascon people's dismay. The Iberians and Basques to the south seem thirsty for conquest and the King has ignored all warnings to condemn the Castilian Empire's rapid expansion towards the Pyrenes. The Holy Roman Empire has taken this generation's young men and feed them to the river Rhine, their blood split and growing flowers in a land the Gascon's will never see.

As such, the people's council barge into the court of Armagnac and demand Centule IV denounce the King and has bastard lineage. The people seem eager and awaiting change.

events.snippet:
**## Pope Phelix V placed under House Arrest <:key:1308639217353490432> **

**The Roman Curia has taken control over the Papal States** and declared t
events.fullText:
**## Pope Phelix V placed under House Arrest <:key:1308639217353490432> **

**The Roman Curia has taken control over the Papal States** and declared that the Pope has gone mad, seeking to wage war and spill the blood of Christian men. Even going so far as asking to duel... this is evidence of the popes madness.

To avoid this conflict and the dangerous situation they find themselves in, they have secretly met with the French Cardinals and reached a conclusion: a new Pope shall be elected to govern Christendom. However, no candidate has yet been chosen, and deep disagreements remain over who should assume the papacy.

In the meantime, the Curia demands that the knights <@200833745606017024>  stand down, for without Hungarian support, a war with France would be pointless. Furthermore, the Curia calls for a white peace, so that the French and Italian Cardinals may come to a peaceful agreement without further bloodshed.

<@423689694258397195> <@315868897012219904> <@545657694489542666> <@460814348487753730>

Pope Phelix is beyond angry and demands that the cowardly House of d'Este send forth a champion, so that he may face them in an honourable duel. Should they refuse, let them admit to their cowardly ways before all of Christendom.

events.fullText:
# The Iron Crown of Italy is offered to Holy Roman Emperor Jan II

The army in Ferrara will withdraw to Milan, where it will secure the Alpine passes and prepare for the arrival of the Holy Roman Emperor.

To bind this new alliance, Azzo Visconti has betrothed the eldest daughter of his heir and the eldest son of Jan II. In return, Azzo is formally recognized as Imperial Vicar.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
**The Arelat Drift**

With the defeats of the splintered German hosts across the western marches, His Majesty Jan acquiesced to the demands of the Kin
events.fullText:
**The Arelat Drift**

With the defeats of the splintered German hosts across the western marches, His Majesty Jan acquiesced to the demands of the King of France, Philippe VII *le Dragon*, and with it the western frontier of the Holy Roman Empire suffered egregiously.

Formally, it did, but in truth the losses were still minimal materially, it was simply the forced hand of the formality that stung the prestige of the empire the most, and the character of the French King which made it sting ever bitterly. Namur, known more recently in the expressions which brought France and the Empire to war as “Imperial Flanders,” that portion of Flanders opposite that which obeyed the French Kings and instead were subject to the King of the Romans, despite being ruled by the same House of Dampierre, was formally withdrawn from imperial immediacy. This was a direct blow to the ambitions which had surprisingly united a broader imperial front at the time, which was to seize “Royal Flanders” and incorporate it into the Empire, that is, to resolve the disputes between the Two Flanders in their favor. This itself was a movement occurring at the outburst of a unique and rare consolidation of identity and union among the Empire’s princes hailing from the aftershocks in the post-**Golden Bull** political landscape and the stability which Stephan of Bavaria had offered after the fraught reign of Ludwig IV, and the civil wars and rapid turnover of elected Kings following his demise. 

The County of Valentinois, under the Poitier Dynasty, had similarly endured overlapping subjecthood in obligations to the King of France, and the King of the Romans. This was not a new dilemma for those straddling the two monarchies, as even Heinrich VII, the first King of the Romans from Haus Luxemburg, had acceded to being a subject to the King of France for a time while equally a subject in the Empire. But, in the failed campaign for Flanders, this too was resolved in favor of France.

Viennois, in pursuit of brevity, was a purchased fief by the Valois Crown in promise of remaining an independent territory and not integrated into the royal demesne. It too was an imperial fief, like Valentinois, in the Imperial Kingdom of Burgundy. It too, like Valentinois, was settled in favor of the French Crown.

The **Kingdom of Arles** now has gone two centuries without seeing an elected King of the Romans come to be crowned King of Burgundy, and the imperial neglect of the region now faces immediate and dire territorial losses to the Valois. The rest of its remaining lands now, effectively, departed as well. 

The County of Provence, held in union by the French House of Anjou, ruled by the Queen of Naples, was now geographically isolated from the imperial core in Germany save for the **Italian route**, itself fraught with many difficulties, seasonal obstacles, and internecine rivalries and conflicts which consistently undermined even German-Italian ties, never mind the fading Burgundian realm.

And now, Amède VI, Count of Savoy, has taken up with his neighbors and even goes so far as to subject themselves to not merely the political and legal authority of the French over the Empire, but even in what is seen as many as blatantly becoming a subject to the Crown of France.

It is not a situation Jan himself desires to see, but it is also not a situation he is readily available to challenge. His army was throughly battered at Thallictenburg, he recently just signed his truce with the King of France’s representatives, and he still needed to cross the Alps into Italy to resolve the crises therein and receive their homage before he could even dream to work directly on the Arelat Neglect.

<@460814348487753730>

As Andreas makes way to Praha to join the court of Emperor Jan, some measures were made to ensure the function of the state, two br
events.fullText:
# The new council

As Andreas makes way to Praha to join the court of Emperor Jan, some measures were made to ensure the function of the state, two branches are prominent, the Domkapitel and the municipal council. Granted extended powers to both.

The Domkapitel ensures the religious proceedings are continued and justice is divinely decided rather than by those who are self interested. Additonaly, diplomatic affairs are deligated to the Domkapitel, with the religous connections of the diocese is best rested with those of cloth.

The municipal council gained significant control of the treasury and direction of development in the country in the absence of the Prince-Bishop.

These two factions sit at odds against eachother, the disgruntled council of the pious and the wealthy work barely in tandem and a new opportunity for either to consilidate power has opened.

events.author: Bear
events.snippet:
## A Message in Measured Caution


In accordance with the mob of commoners stuck in his court, Centule IV calls his scribe over and tells him to start
events.fullText:
## A Message in Measured Caution


In accordance with the mob of commoners stuck in his court, Centule IV calls his scribe over and tells him to start dictating.

> To the his Royal Excellency Philippe the 7th, ||your increased tithes and taxes upon my land have drained my people of their trust in the crown. Your needless greed has caused clashes with the Church and the Holy Roman Empire. You have caused the collapse of a generation in the south of France. For what, our blood to be meaningless? Our lives offered to be remembered in the few extra ducets a month you rub between your fingers?
> 
> I hope, for your sake, that you make peace with God. For he is the only one among us who can make you see righteousness. I pray, for my people, that I do not have to turn against the crown. But don't excuse my wariness for cowardice.
> 
> I hear rumblings from the north that the Griffin wishes to cause an upset among your lands. Don't make the Gascon people choose against our wills||

He dispatches a messenger at post-haste to the royal seat in Paris to deliver the message. However, Centule directs the scribe to begin dictating again...

> Dear Jehan the 2nd, Griffin of the North
> 
> ||The people of the Gascony are weary and tired of the greedy King's campaigns and bloodshed for a life and benefit our people will never dream of seeing. An entire generation of young, fighting age men have become fertilizer for the flowers along the Rhine. I fear uncertainty in the lands beneath the Pyrenees, as well as the Castilian expansion to the South. 
> 
> Our neighbor to the south, Portugal, has been brutally beaten and bastardized by the Castilian crown. Our great lands, through court marriage, have obtain lineage rights to the seat of Portugal. 
> 
> If you intended to speak what you mean in your correspondence, in spreading the French crown's hoarded wealth among for reconstruction, then the Duchy of Gascony may be interested.
> 
> You see what I have to offer in ways of men, trade, and claims. The Wolves of the Pyrenees await your correspondence||

Again, Centule dispatches another messenger at post-haste speed to the holdings of the Griffin in Normandy.

<@255439449448644608> <@460814348487753730>

As the German funds begin to flow into Novgorod's coffers. The Veche has done its best to keep its part of the Agreement
events.fullText:
***The Settlement of Neva***

As the German funds begin to flow into Novgorod's coffers. The Veche has done its best to keep its part of the Agreement to begin laying the foundations of a town upon the Coast. Agreed on the coastal location. Efforts and funds have been invested in building a settlement and attracting as many people as possible to this location.

While currently the small rural village in place shall need more time to further develop and grow to the size required for the infrastructure and other efforts requried to be able to found a town within this location, but whenever it is possible to found a proper city and staple port in this location. it shall be done!

events.author: Murlock
events.snippet:
**Standardised Latin Bible <:bible:1456033549613535405> **

There has long been confusion and dispute surrounding the direct translation of the Holy B
events.fullText:
**Standardised Latin Bible <:bible:1456033549613535405> **

There has long been confusion and dispute surrounding the direct translation of the Holy Bible <:HolyBible:1082040764340633640> , as generations of scribes and priests, through error or misunderstanding, allowed certain meanings to become obscured. Seeking truth and clarity in the Word of God <:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> , the Scriptors have been hard at work looking into the matter.

In this great undertaking, local Jewish rabbis were consulted for their version ancient Hebrew scripture of the old testament, while sacred texts and manuscripts dating back more than 223 years were carefully examined and compared from the old Roman Archives. Through this diligent work, numerous discrepancies in wording, interpretation, and transcription were uncovered, revealing errors that had persisted unnoticed for generations.

The main Scriptor around this work is**Rorikos Filusu Jacobus** <@1218063544940232784> <@1106651545296195655> , an Irish monk renowned for his devotion to scripture. For many years, he has laboured tirelessly in founding of new Scriptoriums in rome where the new standardized version of the Holy Scripture is being compiled, with the intention of preserving the true Word of God.

As such this is being called the **Jacobus Bible**, and it is being requested to be enforced as the source of truth.** We ask all Bishops to accept this bible!**

The Kingdom of france is requested to start using the Jacobus Bible <@460814348487753730> 

The Hungarian Realm is requested to start using the Jacobus Bible <@423689694258397195> 

The Empire is requested to start using the Jacobus Bible <@306458253674545153> <@261290667106369536> 

The Kingdom of England is requested to start using the Jacobus Bible <@1106651545296195655>

By the grace of **God ** <:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> and the authority vested in the Holy See, His Holiness the Pope hereby crowns** Jan II as Emperor of the Romans, King of Italy, King of Germany, King of Burgundy, Defender of Christendom, Protector of the Holy Roman Empire, and Sovereign Lord of the Imperial Realms <:hre:587042877641588736> .**

May his reign be just, his sword righteous, and his devotion to the Holy Mother Church <:MotheroftheRosary:1336753726467342378>  unwavering, so that the lands of Christendom may prosper beneath his rule.

*He uses the new Jacobus Bible during the coronation, and uses a new translation of Tobit 12::*

> *"For alms delivereth from death and purgeth away sin; and blessed is he who giveth unto the House of God in humility, for his penance shall be pleasing before the Lord."*

**The Kalmar Union**
Following extensive negotiations, it was agreed that the heir to Sweden-Norway, Princess Catherine, would be wed to the heir of Denmark, Prince Eric. 

The best way to put past grievances behind has always been marriage and this marriage goes a step further to firmly bind the thrones of Scandinavia together. Wars in the north shall become a distant memory and new age of peace will reign. Or, perhaps, this is merely is short interlude in Scandinavian rivalry. Only time will tell.

***The Synod of 1376***

A proper and full religious convention has taken place in Novgorod, A gathering of all the great and pious clergy within the realm. Banding together to set forth their interests and the continued prosperity of the state of Novgorod. Given the continued settlement and growth of small rural towns, the clergy have been, and remain an important part of society.

The spread of the efforts of the faith to these further reaches filled with folk religions, It has been of much importance to spread the faith through prosperity as well as generosity. To ensure that these communities shall be able to gain the stability required to properly convert to the faith.

events.author: Hoang
events.snippet:
# The question of Emilia

South of the Po River, the cities of Emilia hold sway over their own affairs. Yet as Milan grows, so too will its authority.
events.fullText:
# The question of Emilia

South of the Po River, the cities of Emilia hold sway over their own affairs. Yet as Milan grows, so too will its authority.

With the ascension of Barnada Visconti, a ruler more diplomatic than his father, Milan will seek to bind the Emilian cities to the Visconti state. The Emilian elites shall be integrated into the Milanese order, granted offices, honors, and a place within the growing administration.

In return, the cities and towns of Emilia shall receive the rights of Free Towns, preserving their local privileges and assemblies under Milanese protection.
