events.snippet:
The Siege of Lisbon

Enrique de Trastamara, the young Castilian monarch, decided to take decisive action to bring the Kingdom of Portugal back into th
events.fullText:
The Siege of Lisbon

Enrique de Trastamara, the young Castilian monarch, decided to take decisive action to bring the Kingdom of Portugal back into the fold. He claims to be the King of Portugal, through direct succession from his father. As such, the Castilian army swiftly marched across the border and straight to Lisbon, meeting minimal resistance along the way.

A small Swedish expeditionary force, claiming the throne through right of marriage and supported by the Danes, was decisively turned away from the City of Lisbon, and the Swedish King, who personally lead the force and his retinue were allowed safe passage to return to Scandinavia. And with this, along with the acquisition of Gibraltar, the entirety of Western Iberia has come under Castilian control.

## The War of the Griffin and the Dragon has concluded with the death of Duke Jehan II the Griffin of Normandy
With his brother dead, and no standing army to siege Normandy, a compromise was made. Not one the  King wanted, but one he has to make. 
- The Law of the Griffin is partially employed over France
- Normandy, and all its supporters, will return to being beneath King Philippe VII as vassals with all given a pardon.

**A NEW SPANISH DYNASTY**

In a whirlwind of events, Enrique, the bastard son of the late King Alfonso the Avenger, has ascended the throne of Castile through popular support, replacing his nephew Juan de Borgona. The nobles have supported Enrique near unilaterally, and only a few pockets of resistance to his rule exist. Glory to the Trastamara dynasty, and glory to Castile!

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**End of the War of the Griffin**
<@460814348487753730><@1123821809704374313><@259426601865838592>

Routed from the failure to grasp the Dragon King P
events.fullText:
**End of the War of the Griffin**
<@460814348487753730><@1123821809704374313><@259426601865838592>

Routed from the failure to grasp the Dragon King Phillipe the Griffin army, now made up ironically mostly of just Mercenaries and those lucky few levies who had somehow made it through fell back to Paris where John had attempted to rebuild swiftly and place his seat in the city in the name of legitimacy only to then suffer the consequences as the victorious royalist armies in chase would meet the Griffin forces in the ruined city. 

the fighting would be a brutal affair, the last elite core of knights of Griffin would battle with the royal officers of france, the battle seeing the Maidens of Brittany slain, the Butcher, Jehan le Meingre, Phillipe of Artois and Sir Jean de Carrouges all dead. After bloody days of desperate fighting, command mostly fallen the french royalists would be forced to retreat, the Griffin leauge morale restored yet it would be a false hope for in the chaos the claimant John II Valois had fallen defending the french parliament to a charge led by Louis de Sancerre newly minted marshal of France after the death of the Black Eagle. 

With his death the cause for the war would falter, no figurehead or claiment to which they could legally claim the throne would mean king Phillipe would win over his uncle, the surviving nobles either surrendering to the Crown or fleeing to England <@1106651545296195655> . Leaving france devestated but in the talons of the Dragon, though one so wounded and forced in turn to make concessions seeing has now his southern vassals who had remained neutral could demand anything they so wished alongside the fall of most major fortress and cities in the north to rebels. Seeing the Laws of the Griffin be enacted as the first new duty of the Paris restored parliment, much to the anger and annoyance of the wounded pride of the king.

# Venice resists the Genoan aggression! - Miracle in the Otranto!
Despite suffering a terrible defeat in the Adriatic as the Venetian navy was taken o
events.fullText:
# Venice resists the Genoan aggression! - Miracle in the Otranto!
Despite suffering a terrible defeat in the Adriatic as the Venetian navy was taken off guard by this vile aggression against the Republic in such tumultous times in the Mediterrenan, as the Guild War against Byzantium intensifies, the Republic contracted a mercenary fleet to hunt down and destroy the Genoan fleet in the strait of Otranto, forcing a Genoan withdrawal.

On paper, this conflict has ended under an ambiguous white peace, despite the rather brutal conflict partaken in the Adriatic and Aegean, as the Republic of Venice, already busy with the war against Byzantium, has been unable to mount a proper response against the Republic of Genoa. Inner circles of the Republic cite the so called *agreement* cited specifically to collaborate against any significant threat on the East, explicitly broken by the proposing Genoese. This is likely to cross off any chances at reconciliations between the two Merchant Republics for the foreseeable future.
-# <@170593650584059904> <@423689694258397195> <@315868897012219904> <@545657694489542666>

-# Venice was unfortunately involved in two important conflicts. This is purely accidental.

Denmark seems to have developed into an early adopter and manufacturer of firearms. Despite having recovered from the century of
events.fullText:
**Guns and Cannons**
Denmark seems to have developed into an early adopter and manufacturer of firearms. Despite having recovered from the century of mismanagement, the state finds itself still considerably behind the other major nations of Europe. Seeking to ammeliorate this, King Valdemar has taken a gamble on weapons. Seeking to become the creator of the finest artisan guns, he developed industry surrounding their manufacturing and sale. 

Today, Denmark is one of the largest exporters of Guns in Europe, occupying markets from England, to France, to Barcelona.

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**Foundation of the Town of Kamianets**

In the year of our Lord 1381, the Halych-Volhynian King has granted the rights of independence from noble rul
events.fullText:
**Foundation of the Town of Kamianets**

In the year of our Lord 1381, the Halych-Volhynian King has granted the rights of independence from noble rule to the Town of Kamianets. The town, for centuries, has set in the strategically advantageous position, occupying the hills and cliffs, surrounded by a fairly wide canyon with a river running through it. Despite this, the local fort and small palisade have proven to be as much investment, as one was willing to make. Yet, with the growth of the Kingdom of Halych-Volhynia, the town has started gaining some relevance. Mongol period has proven devastating and utterly destructive, but also crucial in finally realising the potential of the location.

Nevertheless, it has still taken over a century and a half to finally bring meaningful change - the Town of Kamianets has finally become, by the decree of the true Architect-King Trojden. The plan for the town is expalsive - occupying the rest of the plato it is situated on, it also has a plan to expand the town significantly into a local centre of trade and a walled city. Yet, the true masterpiece is the castle - occupying the nearby hill, it runs as one of the few gates leading into the town, having a controlling view of most of the approaches and absolutely dominating the valley formed by the river.

Yet, that is all far into the future. The town, for now, will simply grow and expand into the regional capital, as the East of the Kingdom has much lacked in local artisanry. Trade security will also increase, as the routes from East and South will now find a well-defended town with all the necessary services to keep any caravaneer happy.

May the town prosper and grow and may Trojden's vision hold true.

events.author: Murlock
events.snippet:
**Monetary Papal Salary Reform<:prestige:587042877356376103> <:rosary:898776109246726154> **

In years past, members of the Curia were often granted t
events.fullText:
**Monetary Papal Salary Reform<:prestige:587042877356376103> <:rosary:898776109246726154> **

In years past, members of the Curia were often granted the same salary regardless of rank, or subject to inconsistent increases and reductions based upon age, influence, or repeated requests for funds to expand estates and privileges. Such practices have created confusion and unfairness within the Holy Mother Church <:MotherOfGod:1082040844237946930> .

**These matters must be resolved <:eu4_gold:839128116436664370> **

Thus, in the Year of Our Lord 1381, His Holiness Pope Urbanus has enacted a number of monetary reforms, seeking to end the unfair and often confusing salaries granted to bishops and members of the Church. Through these reforms, compensation shall be made more orderly, just, and fitting to one’s office and responsibilities within Christendom.

The salaries shall henceforth be as follows:

**His Holiness the Pope**
250 Florins per month

**Cardinals of the Curia**
100 Florins per month

**Archbishops**
70 Florins per month

**Bishops**
45 Florins per month

**Abbots and Abbesses**
30 Florins per month

**Priests of Great Cathedrals**
18 Florins per month

**Parish Priests and Vicars**
10 Florins per month

**Monks and Friars**
4 Florins per month

**Scribes, Church Scholars, and Administrators**
8 Florins per month

These are to be paid from the Rome, if you need your payment for being a Cardinal, please @ me and i will give you your salary in Florins.

**A New King**
At the age of 62 King Valdemar was succeeded by his son, King Erik VII. Erik, much like his father, comes from a military background, long serving as general of the Holy Roman Armies. Unlike his father, he lacks a penchant for administration. His warlike nature and zeal for battle will no doubt lead him to seek conquests abroad. May he lead Denmark to Glory!

As Friedrich had once discussed with the Emperor himself in a correspondence years ago, the foreseen and feared threat has arisen. Ro
events.fullText:
# Robber Barons

As Friedrich had once discussed with the Emperor himself in a correspondence years ago, the foreseen and feared threat has arisen. Robber Barons have entrenched themselves in the mountains of the Alps, levying heavy taxes on the valuable silver flowing through these routes, and growing fat with the riches of their plunder. Friedrich, for the first time in nearly a decade, is preparing himself to ride out for war, to crush these Robber barons and remind all those who question the authority of the Archduke, most especially the nobility who paint themselves as anything more than their birthrights.

Klaas Stortebeker with his heavy purse and devilish smile approached the Court of Denmark, he sought passage and safe port...maybe an acquisition of land, but what he was offered he could simply not refuse. events.snippet:
# The regrowth of Koln
After the failures in Flanders and occupied nature of the coffers aimed towards war, the city of Koln face an era of stagnancy.
events.fullText:
# The regrowth of Koln
After the failures in Flanders and occupied nature of the coffers aimed towards war, the city of Koln face an era of stagnancy.

As the municipal council has taken over the treasuries usage, the city has experienced far greater growth of it's industry while clerical investments have started to dwindle, for those looking to trade a plethora of trade goods are on the market whilst the state of minor churches has started to disappoint pilgrims visiting places outside the cathedral. This resurgance has benefited the city greatly for it's people and 

More so, laws have shifted in the favour of the municipal council rather than the Domkapitel, the reduction clerical administration is noticable both within the city and outside the city. Tradition falls in the shadow of innovation, the dominance of the Clergy is starting to fade.

Such shifts in power with the absence of the Prince-Bishop could leave the state of Cologne ever so closer moving further away from the theocratic nature of law.

<@306458253674545153> <@137101075411894272> <@150411715757080576> <@792904515795550218> <@420614608399761419>

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Peace of Abbeville 

Young King William III had scarcely made the journey from his holdings in Wales to the palace in London, being quickly sworn i
events.fullText:
## Peace of Abbeville 

Young King William III had scarcely made the journey from his holdings in Wales to the palace in London, being quickly sworn in by the Archbishop of Canterbury following news of Edward's death in Brittany, when he was told the English Army had barely made it home. 

The country wept. The nobility rejoiced, in a bittersweet manner.

Edward was dead. 

In parliament, chaos ensued. Nobles shouted, shoved, and pushed eachother over what this could mean.

At the advice of his mother, Edward III signed the Peace of Abbeville with the King of France, Phillipe VII, conceding the last holdings on the continent and forever renouncing the Plantagenet claim to the throne of France. 

Peace at last, the Lords say. 
Peace at last. 

<@460814348487753730> <@1417260821674852363> <@1123821809704374313>

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**The Great Vellum Brandenburger Guilds**
<@763652399784919080><@222439858927435776><@385875387608989714><@288410456526422016> 

Brandenburg is in terms of mercantilism an akward one, rural but not one of vast farmlands and having the need to still support the growing urban populations and infrastructure in the lands around, while having no grand cities of commerce nor industry. Leaving the land poor and politically only relevant in terms of its electorship.  

In turn seeing a certain level of creativty on display from the Hanseatic and local born merchants, creating a market of various ancillary and supporting goods that are otherwise too monopolised elswhere or too expensive to bother with. With the political crisis of the dynastic feud of the Luxembourgs and Wittlesbachs deepening along with the growing obsessiveness of the Margrave Ludwig he has commissioned and worked with his local Landtag to create a noble back guild, not one centered on a single city but as part of the wider territory on the creation of paper of all things. Outwardly grasping upon a resource that can be readily produced in the region but inwardly also attempting to give himself and the wider Wittlesbach Imperial faction control and ability to produce works, records and other documents far more easily, logistically a boon to the wider political feud... though such a tool could as well be used by others as well.

**The Imperial Law Must Stand!**

In a fiery speech (in VC) Ludwig of Brandenburg calls upon the entire weight of the imperial legal system and political apparatus to resist such a blatant attempt to qaush the electors rights who so clearly oppose the emperors interests, attempting to push away from this being a dynastic issue to instead a political one, drawing parreles to the recent civil war in France and the evil Dragon King Phillipe.

> *"THE LAW MUST STAND NONE MAY SAY A MANS NAME OR TITLE IS WITHOUT MEANING BUT OF THEIR BAD HUMOURS ALONE! IF THIS DIET DOES DECLARE ME WITHOUT BRANDENBURG I SHALL ACCEPT AND NOT A MOMENT BEFORE!"*

Joining with both his relatives and other noble lords Ludwig propositions if the emperor will not stand to negotiations as has been offered then a leauge of noble imperial interests must be met before a second France takes place

events.fullText:
# Venetian Campaign of Euboea
With the war against Byzantium over and the rights of Venetian control over the County of Negroponte restored, the Republic has seen fit to mobilize and seize the island of Euboea back into the Republican fold after the withdrawal of the Byzantines from the region. The goal of the republic is the **abolishment of the County of Negroponte in favor of the establishment of the Republic of Euboea**, unifying the County's territories and the newly acquired land of Karystos.

events.fullText:
**The Votian Crusade!**

Years ago, the disaster at Lake Peipus occured. The forces of Prince Nevsky defeated the Teutonic force of Dorpat. With this defeat, any attempt at further crusading efforts east of the Livonian lands were halted. This marked a heavy stain upon the tapestry of the Livonian Order's efforts.

Now, with Papal approval, Teutonic forces shall once more march east! The Grandmaster himself shall lead the army of Prussia; and with the support of Europe's Christian kings, shall see that the Rus see the true light of Christianity!

(Dec Novgorod, in 4 months. November 1384)

**Deus Vult!**

events.snippet: After the expansion of the Hansa within the profitable Channel and the Bay of Biscay, as well as inland in Germany, some of the aldermen have started
events.fullText:
After the expansion of the Hansa within the profitable Channel and the Bay of Biscay, as well as inland in Germany, some of the aldermen have started complaining, especially those in German communities in Visby and Riga, renowned Hanseatic towns. <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 

Türing only follows profit, and in a time where Lübeck is nearing bankruptcy, it is clear the Baltic is no longer what it once was, not if one wants to maximise profit. Nevertheless, political pressure is a powerful tool, and the cities in the Baltic have voiced their concerns in the Hansetag.

To appease the Baltic, Türing has extended a proposal to the Velche and Grand Prince Ivan: to build a town with Hanseatic funds (500.000 hohlpfennig <:Price_expensive:1345840629258780703> / 500 gold) in Neva, a most suitable place to access Novgorod... with the only string attached of, due to the generous contribution, being able to build new kontor communities and offices to trade in the new town. After all, most of the stone, timber and buildings will come from german money. This will allow the Velche better access to its sea neighbours, no doubt allowing them to pursue their own opportunities. <@160455024994942976> <@307587752860581888> 

This will hush the Baltic cities, as it means a new community of traders and more furs to be brought to trade. The closest towns have complained (more competition, even if they will be the seniors for some time), but alas, all for the good of the Hansa.

Thanks to the trade in the Adriatic, where the Hansa is now 2nd only to Venice in profit, going to Constantinople is easier, if still difficult. For now, some outposts must be built.

Exempt from sound tolls, able to build in Byzantium to promote trade, wealth and prosperity for the greeks, sooner than later the italians will be long gone (well, except for all the land they already have...). This weird partnership between the ERE and the traders of the HRE is as strange as is advantageous for both <@302128861209165824>

events.author: GMB
events.snippet:
# THE PALATINATE IS FINALLY CONNECTED! # 

<@306458253674545153> <@150411715757080576> 

And with it the lands were finally reconnected. What was a co
events.fullText:
# THE PALATINATE IS FINALLY CONNECTED! # 

<@306458253674545153> <@150411715757080576> 

And with it the lands were finally reconnected. What was a combined dream of Rudolf II "the Restored" and Ruprecht "the Red" has finally been achieved. And so with it the Treaty of Bamberg also upheld. But with it also came a sudden change. Something unexpected even. 

At the gates of Bamberg, when Bavarian and Palatinate Troops gathered to storm the Towns walls, a defeated and penant Lady Barbara von Schönberg called for a ceasefire. Asking for a surrender. 

She offered herself to God, she renouced her Noble roots and would become a nun, continue to rule Bamberg as its ruler. All in return for an end of hostilities. 

In an act of Justice, Rudolf II "the Restored" obliged. He was technically not breaking the Treaty of Bamberg of 1363, since he only took the lands promised to him. Bambergs holy rule was restored and no longer a Noble ruled over a Bishopric, now only Nun Barbara.

Some say though this decision had a sinister side. Yes it was Justful but also an act of arroganz and vengence, for the clarity was there: Rudolfs brother, the monster and kin he hated:** Had taken over rulership in Mainz.**

*In a public adress to his brother he would state: *`"Order has been restored to Bamberg brother, You now my friend: You have to uphold your side of the deal if you wish to take these lands of a loyal nun of the church for yourself. The decision is yours: I've upheld my part of the deal."`

events.snippet: Following the expansion of the League in Bordeaux, which now has a kontor of German traders, Türing has set its sights towards the Cantabrian Coast. T
events.fullText:
Following the expansion of the League in Bordeaux, which now has a kontor of German traders, Türing has set its sights towards the Cantabrian Coast. The plan laid out by his predecessor in the previous Hansetag is to be fulfilled: building kontors and trade offices in Castille. <@422498641425596418> <:Burghers:1139045024890368001> 

Presently, the few (if any) traders that move merino wool from Castille to Brugges are genoese <@170593650584059904>. But they lack both the means (as in, ships) and the vision to make of this a proper enterprise. Who are the merchants who monopolize Köln and Brugges, biggest cloth producers? The Hansa. Who are the merchants with outposts in Bordeaux & Nantes? The Hansa. And who, aside from England, is the biggest wool producer? Castille. 

And thus, it is only natural that a partnership would rise between the Hansa and Castille; the German traders will take the wool from Castille and bring great cloths <:fine_cloth:1512788718140919991>  for the nobles and burghers alike, all while the Crown gets the money from tariffs. And the links with the Hansa will also allow Castille to benefit from other products (timber, furs...), and cheap credit should it be needed.

This offer is extended to the King and his Council, and Türing awaits for an answer.

[Date:1380-12-01][Country:Hansa][Location:Edinburgh][Tag:Trade]

Given the already existing trade relationships in the island, the Hansa is open to it, and Türing asks for some germans from the dutch kontors to start looking to establish themselves in scottish lands and foster trade opportunities. The Hansa will act as intermediaries between the english and scots in this way. Maybe in the future the common trade will lead to a better relationship between both crowns...

events.snippet:
## HOUSE OF CARDS 

Although William III was forced to agree to [re-instate the Great Charter due to the crippling war debt he inherited](https://disc
events.fullText:
## HOUSE OF CARDS 

Although William III was forced to agree to [re-instate the Great Charter due to the crippling war debt he inherited](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513295235025998069), there are many in parliament who mistrust the King, especially as William has rather amateurishly been attempting to assemble a network of spies across the country. Yet another faction decries William's lack of effort in stamping out the "Lollards", [a growing mass of peasants and independent preachers](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513295787138875534) who have broken from Rome and Lyons both. 

On one hand, William is derided for not doing enough. On the other, he is given no leeway with which to do it! It is undoubtedly true that this is a difficult situation, but whispers are growing - that someone greater could have navigated this mess. 

William's greatest detractors are a group of Northern nobles and commercial interests lead by Sir Thomas Percy - labelled the "Perciards" by their peers.

Unbeknownst to William, the Perciards aim to **land Lady Eleanor Plantagenet on the throne of England**, whether whether via open rule or not.

This is only a possibility because up until a few years ago, Eleanor was de-facto running the country. While Edward IV was off in France, and later as young William was first ruling, Lady Eleanor handled much of the business of state. 

The Perciards, and Eleanor, **secretly aim to garner support for their prospective coup.**

**Sir Thomas Percy himself travels to Ireland, where he will try to garner support from the English settler-lords and loyalist clans.**
<@1218063544940232784>

**Lady Eleanor tries to court the support of the Borgonha exiles, who are themselves very popular and hold a lot of sway over the nobles of the upper house.** ( <@298192682554228738> you can rp as them for this)

**A clergymen heavily involved in the wool trade, Laurence Dixon, travels to Lubeck to discuss, in very vague terms, how the Hansa feels about the current king.** ( <@288410456526422016> )

**An elder from the Archbishopric in Canterbury travels to meet with the Pope, to express his concern with the... lack of zealotry among parliament as a whole.** 

**Ferdinand expresses his belief that William III is a deeply commited Catholic, but that his reign is too uncertain for him o take the decisive action he needs against these "Lollards".** ( <@150411715757080576> )

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet:
[Treaty]
To my brother, Tinti d'Alsace,

It brings me great joy to hear of your election as Bishop of Trier. Our father, Raoul d'Alsace, always valued
events.fullText:
[Treaty]
To my brother, Tinti d'Alsace,

It brings me great joy to hear of your election as Bishop of Trier. Our father, Raoul d'Alsace, always valued the friendship between Lorraine and Trier, and now a part of that responsibility rests in your hands. I have no doubt that you will continue to strengthen both the Moselle region and the bonds between our lands.

As you know, our father always held great respect for the House of Wittelsbach and for the Emperor of Bavaria. His loyalty was such that he willingly marched to war against France and ultimately gave his life with honor upon the battlefield.
Now the House of Wittelsbach once again finds itself in need of friends and allies. Recent events within the Empire have raised many concerns regarding the conduct of the current Emperor, whose threats and demands have caused unease among many princes.
For this reason, I ask you to consider standing alongside Lorraine and our allies, just as our father once stood by those he trusted. Together we may preserve the stability of our region and uphold the honor of our family.
Whatever your decision, know that you shall always have my respect as both Bishop of Trier and my brother.

Your brother,
Tombo d'Alsace
Duke of Lorraine
<@137101075411894272>

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet:
[History]
# Raoul's Legacy

As a great general, but an even greater Duke, Raoul d'Alsace shall forever be remembered in the history of Lorraine. His s
events.fullText:
[History]
# Raoul's Legacy

As a great general, but an even greater Duke, Raoul d'Alsace shall forever be remembered in the history of Lorraine. His successful campaign for Metz not only restored the city to Lorraine, but also laid the foundations for a period of prosperity that benefited both the city and the duchy alike.

When Raoul stood beside his Emperor of Bavaria in loyal service during the war against France, the conflict left a deep wound upon Lorraine. Yet it also claimed the life of Raoul himself, who fell honorably as a commander in service to his allies and to the Empire.

Following the devastation of war and the flight of countless people from their homes, great administrative skill was required to rebuild what had been lost. Raoul's heir and successor, Duke Tombo d'Alsace, not only restored the economy of Lorraine, but expanded it further, transforming the duchy into one of the most prosperous realms within the Empire.

[History]

In recognition of the long-standing friendship between the Houses of d'Alsace and Wittelsbach, both dynasties strengthened their bonds through marriage. Today, the House of d'Alsace stands closely connected to the House of Wittelsbach not only through friendship, but through family itself.
Recent events within the Empire have raised serious concerns. The King of Bohemia and Emperor has chosen to question the loyalty and legitimacy of those who oppose him, even going so far as to accuse his rivals of serving French interests. Such accusations are an insult to the memory of those who fought and died against France during the Flanders War, including many sons of Lorraine and Duke Raoul himself.
Furthermore, the Emperor has chosen threats and intimidation against recognised Imperial Princes, seeking obedience through fear rather than loyalty earned through respect.
Lorraine desires peace, stability, and prosperity. We have spent years rebuilding our lands and healing the wounds left by war. We do not seek another conflict, nor do we glorify bloodshed.
However, neither shall we abandon our allies, nor allow the memory of those who sacrificed everything for Lorraine and the Empire to be dishonoured. Should conflict come, Lorraine will stand beside its friends and family, just as Raoul d'Alsace once did before us.

Tombo d'Alsace
Duke of Lorraine, 1386

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
# The Dragon is victorious, but forever wounded
Just barely had the Dragon beaten its brother the Griffin, the one man to challenge him at his best an
events.fullText:
# The Dragon is victorious, but forever wounded
Just barely had the Dragon beaten its brother the Griffin, the one man to challenge him at his best and nearly defeat him. Only barely clawing a “victory” against the Griffin on the misfortune battlefields of Paris before the Dragon could be defeated. 

Then the Lion of the Isle sought his blood, and the Dragon had to rest in Paris without engaging the Lion to gather what strength he had left. Raising many ships and men over a years time until the Bull south of him charged forth at the Lion with fury against the Lion’s crimson words.

With the Bull’s aid, the Dragon yet again got by and defeated the Lion. However, the Eagle of the East too wanted to exploit the bloodied Dragon, but all it could do was soar and circle in watch of the Dragon’s armies before departing to care for its nest instead.

Now the Dragon finally sits peacefully upon a throne with a significantly smaller hoard, dearly wounded, terribly ill, and exhausted. 

Several times the Dragon’s heart betrayed him throughout all these wars. Forcing him into nearly permanent fatigue, muscle damage, shortness of breath, and pain across his chest and neck. Yet his pride and greed is far too consuming, and he stands as if all are beneath him despite his current state. Now he rests to recover what is left of himself, but is still vigilant of the enemies and serpents around him in case yet another bloody conflict will be forced out of him.

## Consequences of the Dragon-Griffin War
Victory was not cheap, and barely achieved. It took the sacrifice of tens of thousands noble knights from both France and Normandy, and far more from the peasant levies of both sides, to even achieve an end. The best knights and generals of both died across France, and with them so too does chivalry. Even worse, when all things finally settled with the Dragon’s victory, a plague of smallpox had assaulted France as some kind  of divine punishment upon the Dragon who still stood. 

At the very least, the Dragon had lost something to the Griffin in the end. That being with his dignity and his hoard’s size due to the Griffin Law limiting the crown’s taxations upon its vassals.

### Holy Plague & Thousands Dead
Over 368k men and women died from the Dragon-Griffin War, yet death had not reaped enough souls just yet. What some could view as divine punishment upon the Dragon of France is the smallpox plague that descended upon France right after the conclusion of the Dragon-Griffin War. Taking 778k lives within the French crown’s lands alone, and much more across all of its subjects.

War and plague has taken many souls within France, and it will take decades, maybe more, to recover the Kingdom. Such a high toll in life has made the Dragon of France's decision making significantly less harsh and aggressive, but his priorities are still well within the coin and his pride. One of the most major shifts in his interests is that he no longer desires to wage a war of revenge against the Holy Roman Empire for its aggressiveness less than two decades ago. Even when considering the Holy Roman Emperor just recently broke the peace treaty, but did little to no fighting. He would rather enjoy peace, and amass coin as he once did in his youth. At least, while he has time to live.

### The dying breath of chivalry
Of the thousands dead, roughly twenty thousand noble knights died in the Dragon-Griffin War alone. France’s best generals are dead, France’s best and most chivalric men are dead, France’s icons of chivalry are dead and some forgotten. Esteemed knights from both sides like the Black Eagle of Brittany, Jeanne de Clisson, Jeanne de Montfort, and many more are all dead. Even the intervening German knights such as the legendary Austrian knight that led the Norman armies into battle in Tours has died and been forgotten in obscurity. In France, chivalry is an honor that is fading away with the new generations of noble men and tactics in warfare. An honor left to the dead.

In its place, King Philippe VII had desperately and expensively [invested into a new army](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513259395998417020) solely for himself. One not reliant on the nobility and levy, nor as costly and unreliable as the mercenaries he once employed. Soldiers that once numbered less than 500 men in the several last stands against the Griffin’s army that are now numbering to seven thousand with the only worthwhile general left in all of France leading them, himself.

### The Griffin Law
The legacy of Duke Jehan II de Valois is the Griffin Law. Where he failed to usurp the Dragon, he forced the Dragon to write a new French law limiting his taxation upon its vassals. The Dragon groans at such a development, but nothing can be done about it unless he wants to incite another major civil war within his Kingdom. The vassals of France can celebrate and praise Duke Jehan II just efforts, since it is him who knocked the Dragon from the sky.
-# <@259426601865838592> <@1123821809704374313> <@564196329132261376> [You  will now lose less money from subject taxes](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1513339107823058944/1513339107823058944)
https://youtu.be/CogOs2jMnGI?si=SAQSSXV9SpLDyZRF

## Important Topics relating to the Dragon
While tired and humiliated, the Dragon had defeated all of its foes. Giving him a firmer grip to assert his authority within his own Kingdom, empower himself, and continue to utilize the Pope of Lyon for his gain. Denying any attempt at ending the schism, and ensuring that indulgences and the tithe are directed to Lyon.

### Centralization grows under King Philippe VII
With things once again stable King Philippe VII can spend his remaining days amassing coin, maintaining stability within his Kingdom, and developing the crown's power. While King Phillippe VII has ruled France, two different annexations have already occurred to improve the crown's power. The most known being the illegal seizure of Brittany, and the less known be the bartered and pressured Blois annexation a few decades ago. One started by his father through bribery, and continued by King Philippe VII on the same grounds but with implicit threats to the Count of Blois at the time. 

Now King Philippe VII will be continuing to use his authority for centralization by silently attempting to centralize the smallest counts of France into the crown. Giving up gold, roles in court, marriage promises into the de Valois family, and continuing to recognize the nobles rights to their lands once brought closer to the crown. 

The most notable internal diplomatic attempt in regards to centralization is him setting up incestuous marriages with the de Valois of Normandy. Duke Francois, son of the Griffin, and Princess Marie, daughter of the Dragon, have already been married to one another to one day bring Normandy into the hands of the French Crown. More pureblooded marriages may follow, especially as the embezzling son of the Griffin sees more eye to eye with the Dragon.

### The de Valois marriage with the Trastamara 
The first daughter of the Dragon was married to the first son of the Bull 14 years ago upon a much older arranged marriage between the two families. This kind of diplomatic tie helped King Philippe VII find an ally when he needed it most, but in truth it was the Bull’s short temperament towards the minor English insult that drove the Bull head first to fight the Lion. While now victorious, the Bull suggests receiving a reward for its efforts; however, the Dragon is heavily unwilling. Especially since it was the Bull who insisted and intervened into the conflict, and not the Dragon outwardly seeking the aid.

How this matter will be resolved has yet to be determined, but there is a growing divide between two would be allies because of it existing in the first place.

### King Phillippe VII vows support to Pope Gregory XII
Upon Pope Gregory's XI death in 1384, the Cardinals, who are mostly loyal to Lyon, elected Pope Gregory XII as their newest Pope. Following this holy election King Philippe VII gave his vocal support and praises for the new Pope. A new Lyon Pope that has allowed him to embezzle greater sums of the tithe.

Where some Cardinals of France less than two decades ago agreed upon making peace and agreed upon electing a third new Pope to act as a peace maker and solution to the schism, the current cardinals of France think differently. Some have come to enjoy the temporal riches of the church gathered via tithe, donations, and indulgences. Others have simply come to fear opposing the Dragon's desired Pope.

Thus, the western schism continues.
-# <@150411715757080576>

The situation remains delicate. Despite the Portuguese nobility's ostensible support for Enrique of Castile a
events.fullText:
**The Governor-Constable of Portucale**

The situation remains delicate. Despite the Portuguese nobility's ostensible support for Enrique of Castile as King of Portugal, as has been shown many times over the past decades, the nobles of Iberia are of a fickle and opportunistic disposition. As easily as they allowed Enrique in they would cast him out. As such, there has not been and will not be (at least for the foreseeable future) any attempt to integrate Portugal into a greater Castile.

The Portuguese Kingdom will return its seat of power to Lisbon, and will retain its courts, legislative bodies, laws, institutions, and assemblies. It shall remain a completely distinct state from Castile with the exception of deference to the King of Castile in matters of foreign diplomacy. King Enrique shall rule from Cordoba as the King of Castile, Portugal, and the Algarve, and shall make regular trips to Lisbon to attend to council and assembly.

As he cannot be in Lisbon for more than 2-3 months every calendar year, he has appointed the capable Nuno Álvares Pereira as Governor and Constable of Portugal, who in the absence of the King, will oversee the governance of the state and the organization of its military.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
**Gotland and the Victual Brothers**

Privateers had long been familiar figures in the North, a common instrument of kings and merchants, sailors who
events.fullText:
**Gotland and the Victual Brothers**

Privateers had long been familiar figures in the North, a common instrument of kings and merchants, sailors who could be bought when fleets were lacking, smugglers who knew every harbour, and mercenaries whose loyalty lasted only as long as silver did. In recent years, they had escorted the invasion fleets of Sweden and Denmark in their bid to claim the Portuguese throne. Before that, they had served in trade wars, feuds between merchant leagues, and even open conflict between Sweden and Denmark.

They were rich sailors, hired blades, and sea-wolves, men who slept in foreign ports and sailed under whatever banner promised coin, plunder, or a pardon. They were useful, convenient and always hunted by someone. But as their fleets grew, so too did their ambition.

Among them, **Klaas Störtebeker**, one of the richest and most infamous captains, began to see more than the shimmer of gold and silver. He saw a future. He saw a haven. In the middle of the sea lay Gotland, surrounded on all sides, yet untouchable so long as his fleet ruled the waves. Its walled town of Visby was wealthy, proud, and full of men who understood that gold often mattered more than oaths.

There, Störtebeker struck his bargains. Merchants were bribed, officials persuaded, warehouses opened, and harbour chains lowered. In Visby, the Victual Brothers could become more than raiders. They could become landlords, patrons, and kingmakers. They could build a **stronghold**.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
**The Career of Sven Vävare**

What made Gotland vulnerable was not only the strength of the brotherhood, but the weakness of the island´s loyalties.
events.fullText:
**The Career of Sven Vävare**

What made Gotland vulnerable was not only the strength of the brotherhood, but the weakness of the island´s loyalties. The Gutnish ruling class had long resented distant Stockholm and its demands. The islanders had their own laws, their own pride, and little love for kings who remembered Gotland only when taxes were due.

At the centre of this discontent stood Governor Sven Vävare, the island´s local ruler. Born among the old Gutnish nobility, Vävare had risen through service to the crown, but his ambitions had long since outgrown royal obedience. He dreamed not of serving Stockholm, but of ruling Gotland as his own. A free island between kingdoms. The Victual Brothers gave him the means. In return for safe harbour, they brought ships, weapons, silver and most importantly the bite of cannons upon the open sea. The merchants of Visby gained protection and profit. The island´s nobles gained autonomy. The privateers gained a homeland.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
**The Dual State of Gotland**

To organize such a state required the reconciliation of two very different powers, the captains and sailors of the Vict
events.fullText:
**The Dual State of Gotland**

To organize such a state required the reconciliation of two very different powers, the captains and sailors of the Victual Brothers, and the burghers, merchants, and landholders of Gotland. The Brotherhood was heavily outnumbered by the islanders, yet it possessed the fleet, the hired blades, and the naval strength needed to defend the island. The captains could enforce their will on the seas, but they could not truly rule Gotland. Their hearts remained at sea, and their eyes were fixed always on ventures, prizes, and distant expeditions.

Thus the old **Landsting of Gotland**, once composed of the island´s twenty district judges, was expanded by the **Pact of Visby** to include a new **Captains´ Bench** with eight formal votes. On parchment, the captains could be easily outvoted, in practice, their influence was far greater than their number of seats suggested. They controlled the fleets, the mercenary companies, the harbour chains, and much of the islands ready silver. Above all, they commanded the violence upon which Gotland´s independence depended. No great decision could be made without the judges and burghers, but no great decision could be enforced without the Brotherhood.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## DE NEVILLE MOVES IN TURN

The "Perciards" and [their plotting](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513459503390265
events.fullText:
## DE NEVILLE MOVES IN TURN

The "Perciards" and [their plotting](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513459503390265406) may not be public, but it was definitely noticed. House Percy's plotting may have little to do directly with their role in Northumberland, but the Duke of Northumberland's rivals in the north have **noticed that something is afoot.**

**IN THE COURTYARD OF MIDDLEHAM CASTLE, A MEETING**

"Lord Neville, I must say - I've never had boar so delicious! You must tell me, where did you get this?"

The jovial and absentminded remark was from none other than the Countess of Lancaster, Joan Lancaster. While ostensibly the head of one of the greater English houses, she was by no means impressive herself. Notorious for her carelessness, dim-wit, and expensive tastes. 

"Ehm, a good host must never reveal his secrets! How else can I ensure your return?"

The reply came from a man of greater note and faculty than the Countess Lancaster; the Marquess of Egremont, Richard de Neville. By all accounts, a swordsmen second to none. He truly was the spitting image of his father and namesake, who was granted peerage for heroic leadership in the earlier siege of Berwick.

Richard let the response hang in the air as they both pondered the true purpose of this meeting. 

Finally, the Countess Lancaster broke the silence. 

"I assume this is... the actions of the Percys which you admonished on, our last meeting?

Richard was tense, yet calm in his reply. 

"Does it not seem a matter for concern, the Percys and their friends in the House gallivanting around, as though they are knights of Edward, on some foolish quest?"

Joan leaned back, as if considering this thought deeply, but the blank stare behind her eyes betrayed the distinct lack of consideration occurring.

"So... what was it you wanted to ask for? Money? An introduction?"

Richard sighed.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
## Spiritual sovereignty

Can we truly call ourselves **sovereign**? Our fleets project might across the Baltic, and our banners are feared from Lübec
events.fullText:
## Spiritual sovereignty

Can we truly call ourselves **sovereign**? Our fleets project might across the Baltic, and our banners are feared from Lübeck to Stockholm, yet in spirit we remain dependent. Gotland has no diocese of its own, no bishop seated among us. The clergy of Gotland speak with one voice. Visby, devout in faith and rich in merchants, craftsmen, and artists, has begun the raising of a great cathedral, a house of God meant to rival the **finest** churches of Scandinavia in beauty and dignity. But stone alone does not make a cathedral. A bishop must be granted. A diocese must be created, and only the pope may give such authority.

The Roman pope, consumed by crusades, wars, quarrels of distant princes, has shown little care for the spiritual needs of the northern sea. He neglects us, and so we must seek justice elsewhere. Therefore, the Landsting shall send clergymen, canon lawyers, merchants, and lawful representatives of Gotland to the court of the French Antipope at Lyon. There they shall petition for the creation of a Diocese of Gotland.

**Petition of Visby**

> *We, the clergy, judges, burghers, and lawful representatives of Gotland, humbly place our petition before the Apostolic See.
> 
> Our island stands in the midst of the Baltic Sea, rich in churches, trade, and faithful people, yet we remain without a bishop of our own. Distant prelates cannot properly tend our souls, defend our churches, or govern the clergy of this island in these troubled times.
> 
> Therefore we beseech Your Holiness to raise Visby to the dignity of a cathedral city, and to create a **Diocese of Gotland**, with its bishop seated in the great church now being raised there.
> 
> Should Your Holiness, in your benevolence, also see fit to raise the **Bishop of Visby** to the dignity of **cardinal**, Gotland shall remember such grace with steadfast loyalty.*

## Victual Brothers presence in the Mediterranean

All good things must end, so will our presence around the eastern Mediterranean, for a fe
events.fullText:
[Treaty]

## Victual Brothers presence in the Mediterranean

All good things must end, so will our presence around the eastern Mediterranean, for a few years we have secured ports to resupply in from Genoa, but as time passes so does their favor. It seems the Doge of Genoa found his backbone and decided to repeal our previous agreements. But our sailors and captains have for years now enjoyed the rich seas and they petition us, after all, if the Genoese wont allow us into port, their enemies might?

There are a few promising business partners we could approach, among them the Signore of Ferrara, a man known for his talent of procuring *personal finances* to then partake in the *spirits* of the Good Lord. He surely wouldn't turn away such a profitable business opportunity...

events.author: Guy
events.snippet:
# NOW COME TO LIFE:

# BEFORE THE LANDS UNRAVELED IN THE NIGHTMARE BEGINNING,

*In the ashes of victory, a new reality daws on the Karamanids: the slo
events.fullText:
# NOW COME TO LIFE:

# BEFORE THE LANDS UNRAVELED IN THE NIGHTMARE BEGINNING,

*In the ashes of victory, a new reality daws on the Karamanids: the slow ticking of the clock.*

*The war, despite near possibility of defeat being grasped from the jaws of failure, was by all means a success. The Byzantine state had been forced to stop its endless march onward, and would be forced, for the time being to keep its eyes west. Yet that small boon may well yet be the doom of the Karamanids, for whilst true they had secured a truce with the Roman for 40 years, it was doubtful that it would hold sway for more than a generation or two, and soon the Roman armies would march east once more. It shouldn'thave been much a concern, since that was an inevitability anyway, but the extra ire drawn was certsintly not welcome.*

*The Ottoman Pasha, regent of his Father-in-Laws Kingdom, contemplated this matter, and within it he only saw one choice. For while the Hungarians may show pity and garuntee the treaty with their force of arms, that too may only last for so long, thusly, one party remained that may yet offer a path through from his view. The Mamlukes had long stood as the nuetral observer before the squabbles of Anatolia, and his advisors not only encouraging, but inviting, the Egyptian Empire for aid was only to backfire. He was told time and time again that they need not go to such drastic action yet, and that binding the Karamanids to the Mamlukes would be ill advised over the long term. With them likely to demand more, and more, and even more for limited terms of protection, and certaintly extremes for more than just defensive protection. Despite that however, the Pasha would order an emmissary to postrate themselves before the Mamluke court.*

-# EVERY MOMENT'S WHERE DEMISE IS

events.author: Guy
events.snippet:
# HATRED BLOOMS ON A DIME.

# SHATTRED, SHRIVELING LIES.

# ALL THIS IS HOW THE FIRE RISES.

*With victory gained, so too is respect for the hardened
events.fullText:
# HATRED BLOOMS ON A DIME.

# SHATTRED, SHRIVELING LIES.

# ALL THIS IS HOW THE FIRE RISES.

*With victory gained, so too is respect for the hardened warrior and hatred for the coward.*

*So many promises had been extracted from the vassals by the late Fahreen, that their armies would follow his, and they would march unified unto the Byzantine mountains
 No such thing ever came to pass, as upon his death, the many roving bands of Turks marched for their own glory, and were promptly decimated by the Romans. Even the regents own peoples, the Ottoman, saw their army exterminated in a vain sack on one of the mountain forts lain by the Byzantine.*


*The Georgian mountains had always lain far beyond what any Bey had considered relevant to their affairs, however the Roman war had changed that perspective, for as many a bloody battle were fought between marauding bands of Turks and the Roman army, one of the few true battles of the war raged within the rocky slopes of Anatolia. The Karamanid army, now reduced 10000 strong, had encountered the Georgian army, 9000 strong, once thought so distance, attempting to march through the passes.*

 *It was a slaughter, disorganized and exhausted from the long march, thousands of prisoners were taken and enforced unto slavery for the Pasha. However, that was not the most important part of the battle, for what the war and overall the greater state of affairs eithin the Karamanids proved is that the Turkic warlords are at best unreliable. A lesson that, perhaps jot be taken by regent, will certaintly be drilled unto the head of all future beys of the Karamanids, else an actual event of disastrous proportions may be faced by the nascent Beylik.*

*Perhaps most startling for all within the Turkic court, was the settling of reality that not only were they isolated both geographically and diplomatically from aid, but that they were conpletely surrounded as well.*

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Resilience of the Lusignan Kingdoms.
**The Early Reign of King Peter I: 1371 - 1386.**

The young King of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia did not
events.fullText:
# The Resilience of the Lusignan Kingdoms.
**The Early Reign of King Peter I: 1371 - 1386.**

The young King of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia did not have the best start to a reign back in 1371. Just sixteen years old, he heeded bad advice, allowed youthful uncertainty to cloud his judgement, and was then [defeated and captured in battle](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1512156327269372224). This disaster led to the current Genoese dominance of Cyprus, who for the early reign of Peter I held unprecedented power over the economy and the King, for the threat of another Genoese expedition was a frightening one indeed. All this was to be expected after the great Genoese victory, but what wasn't expected was the determination and skill of Peter I, who very quickly proved himself a wise ruler who had learned from his major defeat at Famagusta.

Over the next fifteen years, Peter would work to carefully untangle his Kingdom from the Treaty of Famagusta. His uncle, also named Peter, was both influential in court and the most fervent supporter of the Genoese dominance of Cyprus, so the King had move cautiously, so that his uncle suspected nothing was out of the ordinary. Thankfully, the King had been able to consistently outmaneuver his uncle relatively easily in the royal court. With his skilled handling of politics in the royal court, there were many amongst the nobility and clergy who supported Peter I's efforts to reassert Lusignan control over the Kingdom. The results of these careful internal political maneuvers were clear: The Genoese undoubtedly still have great influence in the Kingdom, but by 1386 it has much lessened from where it was nearly two decades ago. It will take the death of the King's uncle to truly wrest back any more meaningful control, but the aging Prince is already in his late 50's, so it is doubtful that King Peter I will have to wait much longer to throw off the last chains of his imprisonment to the Genoese.

# --- Cilicia ---
But King Peter wasn't done with merely working to throw off the Genoese influence over his Kingdom. Abroad there was to be success as well. Peter's father, King Guy II, had been the conqueror of Cilicia before the Mamluk <@874750350538727434>  invasion put an end to the short-lived mainland adventure. Peter was still crowned King of Cilicia, and he had little desire to see the Kingdom remain a purely titular title. Acquiring land in Cilicia proper seemed extremely unlikely, but the Kingdom of Cilicia had other dejure lands which could be pursued. So, after negotiations with the Karamanids <@1216097200401154181> in the mid 1370's, Peter acquired the small town of Silifke and the lands surrounding it on the southern Anatolian coast, in exchange for a large tribute and the renunciation of the rest of the Cilician claims on the Karamanid-owned lands. This wasn't a grand prize, to be sure, but it was a legitimizing victory for Peter I, which secured at least a tiny part of another of the three Kingdoms he claimed. Peter visited Silifke shortly after acquiring it, and set about rebuilding its defenses and implementing Frankish law there before he promptly returned to Cyprus. More than anything else, securing Silifke would give Peter an opportunity years later that wouldn't have been there otherwise.

This success was to be followed up on in the early 1380's. For during Peter's early reign, the northern half of the Mamluk Sultanate was in chaos. Multiple ethnic revolts and civil wars in the 1370's and 1380's paralyzed both Syria and the Dulkadirids. This gave Cyprus the opportunity to increase the frequency of its raids in the region. This though was always a limited affair, as it had been for decades. The real opportunity arose when the Armenians in Cilicia revolted against the Dulkadirids. King Peter I amassed a small army which was amassed in Silifke. There, he acted with wise caution as he entered into negotiations with the Cilician rebels.

Peter I hoped to carefully probe the reaction to his potential reconquest of western Cilicia. However, this was an Armenian revolt born of ethnic violence against their hated overlords, and to these peasant rebels the Frankish Catholics were hardly any better than the Turks. Peter I had no desire to reconquer the whole of Cilicia for fear of a Mamluk retaliation, but the peasants there refused all of his attempts to see western Cilicia returned to his domain. After the negotiations fell through, things very quickly went downhill for the revolt, as it quickly met with a great defeat when Mamluk reinforcements had arrived to the region. Not letting this golden opportunity slip away without any benefit at all, Peter reacted quickly to the news of the Cilician defeat and marched east, seizing the castle of Korikos after its garrison quickly opened its gates to him. The Cilician revolt shortly afterwards completely collapsed, but Peter held on to his minor conquest. With any further military action in Cilicia now practically impossible, Peter contented himself with Korikos and thus largely disbanded his force, using what remained to garrison the castle as he reintegrated its surrounding lands into the Lusignan domain.

King Peter I was careful in his Cilician campaign. He initially wanted to find a peaceful way to detach western Cilicia from the Mamluk sphere, but was met with naught but stubbornness from the revolters. His reasons for not amassing his whole army and forcibly reconquering the whole of Cilicia are clear, however. To take over all of Cilicia, especially violently, would inevitably mean another Mamluk invasion. The King obviously wanted to avoid that outcome as much as was possible, whilst still gaining something from the opportunity presented to him. Peter I is far more confident that his tiny acquisition of Korikos will slip under the gaze of the Sultan in Cairo, especially since the civil wars that were crippling Syria only just ended at that point.

This line of thinking has of yet been vindicated for the past half-decade, as Korikos still remains under Lusignan rule as of 1386.
# ---Austria--- 
Another point of pride for Peter I has to be the connections he has established in Europe. In 1372 an envoy arrived at Cyprus from a very unusual, unexpected place. The Archduchy of Austria <@190640828954181632>  was his origin, and his task was simple: to see if King Peter I's younger brother, Louis de Lusignan, would wed the Archduke's daughter, Maria Theresia von Habsburg. The dowry was to be some minor title in Austria and more importantly: a position of influence at the Archduke's court. Naturally, such an offer was readily accepted both by Peter and Louis. Peter for the close dynastic connection to a powerful Catholic state, and Louis for his own personal desire to live in Vienna, a city far larger and wealthier than anything in Cyprus could offer.

Later that year, Louis left the island to wed his noble bride in the Archduchy, and Peter has of yet never seen him again, though there are every few years a letter or two shared between them. Finding marriages for Peter's other younger brothers proves a more difficult task, but with Louis newfound connections, perhaps a new door has been opened in Europe, one that Peter I is sure to pounce on...
# ---Rome--- 
When trade was monopolized under the Genoese, many would've expected the economy of Cyprus to suffer greatly. Yet, that has not been the case. Much wealth does flow into Genoese pockets, but the prosperity of the island after the Genoese invasion has been remarkable. Due to the skilled administrative mind of Peter I, there has been an economic recovery on the island, and with the Crusade taxes flowing from Rome <@150411715757080576> , the Kingdom was eventually doing just as well as it was fiscally two decades ago when it had conquered the whole of Cilicia.

But a large chunk of that income was indeed the Crusade taxes which the Papacy collected from its tithe and then redistributed to the Lusignan Kingdom. Unfortunately for Peter I, events in Europe were to cut these payments from his Kingdom. A schism in the Roman Church had been ongoing for many years by the 1380's. With conflicts in Italy, France, Germany, and a war brewing again for Sicily, the Pope residing in the Eternal City decided to end the funds to Cyprus.King Peter I did not allow himself to fall into a rage and denounce the Pope in Rome for this action, but instead rather brilliantly used the sympathy gained by both his own heroics in regaining a small part of Cilicia and his Kingdom's soon to be unstable economic situation to gain a concession from Pope Urban V in exchange for the cancellation of the payments. 

The exiled Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem had temporarily been residing on the island of Cyprus for nearly a century. The Patriarch naturally never had a real permanent residence on the island due to the belief that any year now surely the Holy Land would be retaken. By 1386, the prospect of even successfully *organizing* a crusade, much less *winning* one, was growing more and more dim. Thus, in light of the cancellation of the crusade taxes to the Lusignan Kingdom, Pope Urban V granted the current Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem, Fernandus, permission to have constructed for him a permanent place of residence on the island of Cyprus. For Peter I, this move is brilliant because it would prevent any loss of prestige and legitimacy in the future if the Patriarch potentially decided to find a different temporary location to stay, such as in Italy. Thus it effectively ties the Latin Patriarchate of Jerusalem, with all the honor and prestige that comes with having such a presence in one's country, to the Lusignan Kingdoms.

Furthermore, the Patriarch of Jerusalem and his representatives are to be allowed to wield some influence in the Curia, and they are sure to be the proud voice of the Catholic east in Ecclesiastical affairs. Naturally, in return for these concessions, the Patriarch Fernandus is sure to be a close ally of Roman Pope Urban V. 

As of 1386, the residence of the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem, in the capital city of Nicosie, is still under construction. When it is completed, it is sure to mark the beginning of a new age of Lusignan influence in the Roman Church, something that has never really been seen before in such a manner. It is nearly assured that Peter I will continue to grant the clergy in Cyprus more privileges and rights to maintain the positive relations between his Kingdom and Rome.
# ---A Tour of Europe---

Peter I has, by all accounts, had an exciting and successful early reign. He has not allowed the defeat at the time of his ascension to the throne crush his spirit. It has led him to be a more careful, patient King than his father, Guy II was, but perhaps that's exactly what Cyprus needed in these uncertain times. He had also put much study into the disastrous campaign of Famagusta, and has come off far more knowledgeable in the art of medieval warfare. Now, with his Kingdom's influence abroad more prominent than ever, he has been planning for a **grand tour of Europe.** A grand tour that could be used to hopefully remind Christendom of the perilous Catholic east, and win support for, if not a crusade, than for Cyprus' continued independence and strength. Peter I must be careful, however, for Europe is split not just politically, but also religiously. There are currently two Popes, each claiming to be rightful, with powerful supporters on both sides.

Yet King Peter I de Lusignan has learned how to be careful, for he is not the same bungling boy who watched his army collapse outside the walls of Famagusta. With the aid of Christendom, he will attempt to see to it that such a defeat never comes again to the Lusignan Kingdoms...

-# (Left to Right) Map of the Lusignan Kingdoms, Fernandus: Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem.

**(End)**

events.snippet:
**Foundation of the Cathedral of Saint George (Yuriy)**

In the last days of summer, King Trojden, with the approval and funding from several noble an
events.fullText:
**Foundation of the Cathedral of Saint George (Yuriy)**

In the last days of summer, King Trojden, with the approval and funding from several noble and merchant families, has announced a reconstruction of an old, burnt-down church of Saint George into a cathedral - a cathedral commemorating not only his 40th year on the throne, but mark the heights the Kingdom has reached in these difficult decades.

The Cathedral, itself an Orthodox temple, has been fully designed by a Byzantine architect. While heavily strapped for space, the drawings completed by the architect look promising, making up for the lack of space with a somewhat irregular shape of the temple, making it slightly elongated compared to the more typical Byzantine Cathedrals.

Despite the size and wealth, however, the project promises to take several additional years - way longer than the fortieth year since the coronation of King Trojden. Yet, this has made little impact on the plans - glory of the city of the Lion may not be bound by a life of one man, however great or capable. Yet, his mark will remain - as much as it will carry the name of the Saint, the cathedral will also carry the name of the founder ages beyond his natural time.

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**Delegation to the German Merchants of the Hanseatic League (Part 1)**

In the year of our Lord 1386, after a controversial petition by the burgomeis
events.fullText:
**Delegation to the German Merchants of the Hanseatic League (Part 1)**

In the year of our Lord 1386, after a controversial petition by the burgomeisters of Sianok, Peremyshl and Lviv, King Trojden has sent out envoys to the Germanic lands far West. Travelling along the great road seemingly endlessly winding from East to West, the group of Halych nobles and prominent traders have visited several small towns and villages in Poland, before coming to Krakow - the stronghold and gem of the Polish Crown, now uncertain and somewhat tarnished, as local nobles squabble over every right ever granted without their direct permission. 

They spend little time dwelling on the happenings and soon take to the road once more - now moving North-West, along the road through the land they call Silesia. It was once the region pillaged by the armies of Halych - centuries back, under the rule of King Daniel and his son Leo, this was the westernmost point reached by them. King Trojden has not been capable of that and probably never will, but at least men of Halych can still make it this far and, perhaps, even further, without needing strength of arms.

They push on through ever-frequent towns and villages, stopping only for special trades, if merchants call for it, supper or sleep. Soon, the language they hear becomes completely different - while many still speak something resembling a Slavic language, now constant German loan-words make it difficult to understand anything of worth and the delegation resorts to relying on the few merchants well-versed in German due to their trade.

Not long after, they make it to the land the locals call 'Brandenburg' - a land utterly Germanized, yet with a heavy influence of the recent previous native population - other Slavs. The language is still an odd mix of German and Slavic, but becoming ever-more reliant on the former. They push on through the forests, swamps and occasional plains, now finding occasional mentions of their final destination - the wealthy city of Lubeck. In fact, in Berlin, they find a whole group of merchants who claim to be citizens of the city. They speak of the League of Merchants who come from hundreds of towns and cities across Europe, all working together. That is it!

The delegation take their time asking anything and all about this League, for their quest was to find them and offer them an invitation to the Kingdom of Halych-Volhynia. While the merchants prove informative and helpful in many regards, they simply refuse to discuss the matter, claiming that the company must move on to Lubeck - they are not in position to make such a deal, but a council in Lubeck would be the most influential and capable to do so.

And so, after another night in Berlin, the group once more hits the road North-West. Along the road they notice that people start noticing them more - with the word of the odd company travelling fast, the people begin the take interest in such an envoy. Yet, the road now is not long - in days after leaving Berlin, they finally reach their target - a walled, surrounded by river on all sides - merchant city of Lubeck. After weeks of travel, they hope that this Council and League will hear them out and their mission will prove a success. 

They approach the town, letting the merchants, who know German, approach first along with a single noble - young Prince Danylo of Ternopil, who had studied Latin in the University of Lviv. They greet the guards and explain why they seek entry - __they are an envoy of King Trojden of Halych-Volhynia, seeking to negotiate with the League__.

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
# The Bishopric of Trier in the last years of Prince Bishop Leopold von Habsburg, 1371 - 1385

## The Habsburg Family

As Prince-Bishop Leopold von Ha
events.fullText:
# The Bishopric of Trier in the last years of Prince Bishop Leopold von Habsburg, 1371 - 1385

## The Habsburg Family

As Prince-Bishop Leopold von Habsburg aged and mellowed he disappeared from the diplomatic limelight. He did so for two reasons. Once was to have more children, whose position within Trier he sought to secure. [Three more after the birth of the now 16 year old Franz Friedrich](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1511183754373369897) with his long term mistress Elenora von Saarburg. 
All four of Leopolds Habsburg children are ostensibly promised to the Church. One of the little "cultural" workaround many of the German Bishops have found to ensure the safety and prosperity of their families. With the children given education and connection within the German church that very few bishops of noble decent have. 
If Leopold were to have lived longer, he would of positioned his son Franz Freidrich as his successor in Trier rather than the Lorraine backed Tinti d'Alsce. But Leopold died with Franz far to young to be elected by the Trier Cathedral Chapter and now **Trier falls fully under the influence of the rich duchy of Lorraine.**

*figure 1-4: Leopold von Habsburgs children*

## Roads and Bridges, avenues for control.

While Leopold did not secure Trier for another generation of Habsburgs, he did in so many ways secure his architectural vision for Trier. And he stamped a Habsburg mark on that architecture. 
 Leopold viewed [his continuous section of the Moselle](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512710992981659729) and saw a river that he could control and a hinterland that he did not. So Leopold ordered the construction of maintained tracks stretching from points of control on the Moselle into the foothills of the Hunsrück to the south and the Eiffel foothills to the north.
These tracks while nothing but pounded dirt at present, provide not just control over the land from Trier. But also have the unintended benefit of encouraging trade and interconnection in the sparely populated area.
Leopolds next great work was the construction of an Armory in Trier to create a small standing force that could go out and use these tracks to enforce his will on the land. But this part of Leopolds dream has yet to come to fruition as Leopold passed in 1385

Leopolds great building projects during his later tenure where the  toll bridges at Meerzisch, which crossed the Saar (a tributary of the Moselle). And the toll bridge at [Cochem](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512710992981659729), which give Leopold a second corssing of the Moselle downstream from the [Römerbrücke at Trier](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1511157251438809291).

The latter bridge at Cochem contains heavy use of the Habsburgs iconography, with the Habsburgs lion taking pride of place next to the Archbishops seal on the towers of the Toll gates. 

*figure 1: Roads and Bridges in Trier 1385
figure 2: Roads and Bridges in Trier 1371*

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet:
[Meeting]
To my brother, Tinti d'Alsace,

It is always a pleasure to hear from you, little brother, especially now that it has been quite some time si
events.fullText:
[Meeting]
To my brother, Tinti d'Alsace,

It is always a pleasure to hear from you, little brother, especially now that it has been quite some time since you departed our court for Trier. Your frequent visits during our childhood, whether in Nancy or Trier, always brought life and joy to what could otherwise be rather monotonous days at court.
Now we both find ourselves in positions of rule. Though I have had the privilege of gathering experience for somewhat longer, I am very interested to hear your impressions of your new office. You have no doubt learned much from your predecessor, but do you feel that you have already established yourself among your people? And what do they think of the House of d'Alsace?
I am also curious to hear of your ambitions and planned projects, and how you believe we might further develop and strengthen the Moselle region together in the years to come.
In any case, I look forward to our cooperation and to the continued prosperity of the Moselle. May your tenure be as successful as it is prosperous, my brother.

Tombo d'Alsace
Brother and Duke of Lorraine

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
The Hansetag has concluded <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659>. Several points have been agreed to by the Hanseatic Cities.

**The Threat to the Empire**
events.fullText:
The Hansetag has concluded <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659>. Several points have been agreed to by the Hanseatic Cities.

**The Threat to the Empire**
The Hanseatic League has decided to side with the Emperor against the League of the Golden Bull to protect their *Dispensatio*. If the only legal League is the Hansa's... Some towns have refused, and those in the coalition itself have broken ties: Berlin (Brandenburg), Leipzig (Meissen), Heidelberg (Count Palatine), Wittenberg (Saxe-Wittenberg, union with Meissen). It's a serious blow, yet many consider it preferable to an internal war, and perhaps they may be brought back once all of this is over. <@222439858927435776> <@261290667106369536> 

Consul Türing will be in charge of coordinating and deciding the full extent of the imperial aid against the League of the Golden Bull. It's expected only members in the Empire will be asked for money and manpower if there is an all-out war.

**The Baltic Resurgence**
On other matters, the Baltic has become a hub of trade activity again, and is now clearly hegemonic in the League's reach. While London and Brugge remain profitable, the *firearms* industry in Denmark (Copenhagen) and Kiel have doubled the profits of the Lübeckian market. <@210779670663135232> 

The appearance of the pirates in Gotland has been seen with suspicion, but they are yet to attack Hanseatic ships, or remove the German kontors in Visby, and have apparently secured a title of count and a cardinal (weirdly enough). It would in fact be profitable if the Hansa could use them for their own benefit... <@326414282851876875>

# The Tour of Europe embarks!

With the preparations completed, King Peter I of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia embarked from Paphos, beginning his lon
events.fullText:
# The Tour of Europe embarks!

With the preparations completed, King Peter I of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia embarked from Paphos, beginning his long journey away from home. He is accompanied by his wife, the Queen Isabelle de Valois of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia. Peter I's younger brothers, William and Phillip, and some uncles, Peter and James, also joined the large Lusignan entourage, including some small number of personal guards and those to row the galleys in which they sailed upon. They also brought with them large chests full of various gifts, including fine cloth, silk, sugar, and spices from the east. In his stead, Peter I's uncle Prince John was to rule Cyprus until the King returned.

This was to be a journey that a King of Cyprus had never done before, unprecedented in its size and the length of Europe he was to go. For King Peter I did not plan on simply visiting some Christian cities on the Mediterranean, but instead he planned on touring nearly the whole of Europe, to hopefully win the support he needed for the decades ahead...

In late 1386 he embarked from Paphos, out into the great sea beyond...

-# King Peter I and his entourage embarking from Paphos

**(End)**
