Eleanor would then spend nearly a week after touring through Wales. If lords together would not lend her their support, perhaps s
events.fullText:
## Hearts and Minds

Eleanor would then spend nearly a week after touring through Wales. If lords together would not lend her their support, perhaps she could convince them separately.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Plot Revealed!

Richard de Neville had been, for a while, suspecting Percy involvement in a plot. When Countess Joan Lancaster revealed to him the
events.fullText:
## Plot Revealed!

Richard de Neville had been, for a while, suspecting Percy involvement in a plot. When Countess Joan Lancaster revealed to him the rumors of Eleanor's plotting in Wales, he at once knew that this must be connected! He had all the pieces of a grand conspiracy in his hands, one which would implicate House Percy and give the Neville incredible favor in the court of the King. He immediately sent for a small retinue, and rode south.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Desperate Times, Desperate Measures
Along the way, de Neville sent word to a group of Welsh sellswords, ones he knew from his business dealings wit
events.fullText:
## Desperate Times, Desperate Measures
Along the way, de Neville sent word to a group of Welsh sellswords, ones he knew from his business dealings with the Countess Lancaster. 

They were to meet him when he would cross the border into Wales. The Perciard conspiracy was obviously deep, if they had the Lady Plantagenet involved! He would have to apprehend her  to prove this to the King. Otherwise, his advisors could speak poison and make this slip-up go unnoticed!

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Ambush! 
Lady Eleanor so far had seen little success in wooing the Lords of Wales to her cause. This is likely why her intel was so poor - the Wels
events.fullText:
## Ambush! 
Lady Eleanor so far had seen little success in wooing the Lords of Wales to her cause. This is likely why her intel was so poor - the Welsh scouts she employed were of questionable loyalty and often would simply not do their job. 

Crossing through the village of Eglwys Fach just north of Aberyswyth, the townsfolk seemed awfully... quiet. 

Sir John FitzAlan, a knight in the retinue of Eleanor, rode his horse atop a hill just outside town, and spotted nearly half a hundred spearmen crouching in a field scarcely a few hundredpace away from the party! 

He rode back, shouting, 

**Ma dame, les Gallois! Fuyez, par deès!**
-# "My lady, the Welsh! Run, immediately!"

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Les Gallois, ils Attaquent!

The quick wit of Sir John likely saved Eleanor Plantagenet from certain death or capture. His warning gave the retaine
events.fullText:
## Les Gallois, ils Attaquent!

The quick wit of Sir John likely saved Eleanor Plantagenet from certain death or capture. His warning gave the retainers of the Lady Plantagenet enough time to assemble into a formidable rear-guard. Not one soul was lost, because the whole party was mounted. The rear guard, after fending off the Cymraic ruffians, fled to join the rest of the party and protect their Lady. 

 The path along the coast, to Carnarvon, had been blocked by the party of Richard de Neville, so the Lady Plantagenet was forced deeper into the Welsh hinterlands to escape their pursuers.

This did not go according to plan. Her pursuers were native sons of Cymru, and in fact were lead by a man from Machynlleth. Despite the Plantagenet camp being entirely mounted, they were still constantly hounded by the mercenaries, with constant skirmishes ensuing at every stop for water, or every turn-around. 

The party grew tired. It had been nearly evening when they were ambushed. Now, it was morning. None had slept. Worse still, there was a distinct feeling among the party that they were being herded - the only path clear seemed to resolve in the mountains of the Berwyn range. They would almost certainly be unable to cross, or at least slowed to the point of capture. 

By noon, the party arrived at the monastery of Strata Marcella, and asked to be let inside to rest. The monks agreed, as they could see no good reason to allow a Lady to be killed by ruffians. 

The leader of the bandits stood outside the front gates of the compound soon enough, and **demanded the surrender of Eleanor Plantagenet.** This was denied, of course, as the right of refuge is something no man can infringe upon, lest he be judged by Christ.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## The Assault on Strata Marcella

These mercenaries were undoubtedly Christian, but not particularly *good* ones. The payout that de Neville had prom
events.fullText:
## The Assault on Strata Marcella

These mercenaries were undoubtedly Christian, but not particularly *good* ones. The payout that de Neville had promised them was massive, at least compared to whatever fear of God laid in their hearts. 

By nightfall, they attacked. 

The monks declared that they themselves could do no fighting - but that the party of the Lady Plantagenet were free to defend the walls as they saw fit. 

Sir John FitzAlan rallied the defenders, and they - being well armored, well trained, and on the defensive, easily repelled what felt like thousands of Welshmen, who piled branches along the walls in an attempt to climb over into the monastery. 

Eleanor, not content to stand back, was helping to bring timber and nails to reinforce the gate-door. When she was turning to head back inside for more supplies, she, out of the corner of her eye, spied a dark shape with a shining shortsword running towards her at full speed. She ran, but the man was faster. She ducked through an archway into the courtyard, hoping to lose the man in the dark, but the rim of her dress was caught! She tripped, fell, and the man was nearly upon her!

If only she had a weapon. That thought burned through her as if it was branded upon her very soul. 

As if he could hear this thought, a voice from the shadows - not her assailant - answered out. 

**"A weapon, one fit for a King! Use it well, and you may live!"**

The hilt of a sword was then dropped into her hands, just as the attacker lunged.

Without consciously moving a muscle, **the sword in Eleanor's hands seemed to impale itself inside the brigand's heart. **

The blood splattered all over her travel cloak. Without time to consider the course of events that had just occurred, she leapt to her feet. 

*Alors!*, she cried out into the night.

Joining her, the Knights of the Lady Plantagenet routed the Welsh bandits, sending them scurrying away.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
"M-my lady," came the disbelieving cry of one of the monks, "where did you get that sword?"

She seemed to not understand the question, having little
events.fullText:
"M-my lady," came the disbelieving cry of one of the monks, "where did you get that sword?"

She seemed to not understand the question, having little recollection of the night before. 

"What ever do you mean? We were under attack, can I not defend myself?"

The monk was aghast, as if all the blood from his face had left for greener pastures.
"Surely, you must know which blade you now carry? How can you not?"

Eleanor was still confused. 
"It is a good sword, no doubt, but what of it? Speak, tell me  what of this sword is so special."

The monk lead Eleanor and a small throng of onlookers to a large stone, squarely in the middle of the courtyard. 

He gestured to the top of the stone, where there was an **opening, perfectly shaped for the sword in her hands.**

"My lady, I must know, did you take it from here?"

Eleanor's mind was racing. 

*Did she?*

She had no memory of this, but she had no memory of how she got the sword to begin with. 

Utimately, the truth would be whatever she said, right now. 

**"Yes, I did. I took the sword from the stone."**

"My Lady, you have just..."
The monk himself could scarcely believe it. 
"You have just taken the sword of King Arthur from the stone. That has not happened since... Arthur himself! **Since the times of Augustus, Ymerawdwr!"**

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Like Wildfire 

Across the land of Cymru, the story spread, almost faster than any messenger could manage.

First, the story simply said what had h
events.fullText:
## Like Wildfire 

Across the land of Cymru, the story spread, almost faster than any messenger could manage.

First, the story simply said what had happened - with healthy skepticism. 

Shortly after, the story told of Eleanor fighting off three assailants, men three times her size, expertly wielding the blade of Arthur. 

By the time Eleanor had left Wales, to journey to Lancaster, the story frequently was told as her single-handedly vanquishing a whole army (who, by now, were probably Scottish... or Norwegian) with the power of the Sword in the Stone. 

Nobles who previously knew nothing of Eleanor, or even who had outright rejected her in Swansea, were stumbling over themselves to be the first to send pledges of fealty to this *clearly* **divinely ordained Lady Plantagenet. **

Richard de Neville, riding back north, loudly uttered curses that no good Christian should utter. Few of his men judged him, however, as it is hard to imagine a worse setback than this. He needed an army. Fast.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**The Principalities of Russia**

Moskva has grown much ever since it has become the seat of the Grand Principality of Muscovy-Vladimir. What was once
events.fullText:
**The Principalities of Russia**

Moskva has grown much ever since it has become the seat of the Grand Principality of Muscovy-Vladimir. What was once a small fort and town has grown to surpass Novgorod itself, as the center of political and economic authority within Russia.

Russia itself is not a land filled with people and towns like western Europe, it is a harsh land, scarcely populated and barren in many resources such as metals and arable land. Its endless plains until the dense forests of the north serving for wheat and rye farming, as well as lumber working and fur trapping.

Russia stands as a country filled with pastures and farms and small semi-isolated towns, this is how it has been governed for most of its existance.

Yet, with the dissolution of the Golden Horde and the campaigns against the Tartars by Muscovy and the Cossacks, a sense of tranquility and growth have reached the large countryside.

Grand Prince Ivan II has left matters of trade and economy to his cabinet, not focusing too much on it himself. He saw the growth of Moskva, propelled by the sheep farms turned into cloth using fur, which has increased greatly the economy of the growing city.

In a sense, this growing security has allowed a great economic growth, while still very rural focused, a few urban centers like Moskva, Ryazan, Tver and Vladimir have started gaining proeminence, with the burghers, influenced by Novgorod, gaining more proeminence, though still vastly overshadowed by the nobility.

**Eastern Gold**

While matters of wars have taken the focus of Grand Prince Ivan II, even he is aware of the great value that gold has. Moskva has had to import Czech gold and silver for most of its existence to mint the Ruble, yet now, Perm, which has been a subject of Novgorod, has [claimed a vast gold mine](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1513178041260445877) in the Ural mountains, something that catched Ivan II's interest.

Not an economist, he knows how hard it is to travel through the Urals back to Russia itself, and the caravans have to contend with tribals and possible raids. Yet that will not stop them from harnessing this wealth compared to the barren lands of Russia.

The Ruble shall be made of pure Ural gold, and in order to secure its movement, they shall secure a proper path, securing the tribal land eastward until they reach that plentiful mine, and in the meantime, Russian miners shall be sent to help the settlement to harness the gold.

*Muscovy shall be focused on securing a direct road to the Urals' gold*

<@420161433523847169>

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Eleanor Rides South!

After the business in Wales, Lady Plantagenet had travelled in turn to Lancaster, then York. 

Countess Joan Lancaster had de
events.fullText:
## Eleanor Rides South!

After the business in Wales, Lady Plantagenet had travelled in turn to Lancaster, then York. 

Countess Joan Lancaster had denied any involvement, and seemed to, almost instantly, agree that Eleanor should rule England instead of William. 

In York, the reception was much the same. Perciard allies, and nobles who had heard of the Welsh business ahead of the arrival, all lined up to give Eleanor their support. 

With some nobles even joining the retinue, and Eleanor receiving word that everything was lining up, she rode South to reach London before William - who by now had certainly heard what happened in Wales - could assemble an adequate response. 

Near the village of Kettering, Richard de Neville made his final attempt to thwart this plot. He marched, his army nearly twelve-hundred strong, in an attempt to cut off the approach of Eleanor. 

Eleanor's party evaded east,  swinging past Cambridge, but ultimately would have been caught if not for one of de Neville's scouts being killed by  (allegedly) a wild boar, somewhere around Bedford.

In a few day's time from Kettering, Eleanor would arrive in London. 

**This was the endgame.**

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**Imperial Family Matters:**



The family of Palaiologos that was only recently in a major crisis, now finds itself in rebirth.

***Eirini Palailogos
events.fullText:
**Imperial Family Matters:**



The family of Palaiologos that was only recently in a major crisis, now finds itself in rebirth.

***Eirini Palailogos***

A girl of 16, a granddaughter to the present Emperor John V.

Born of the alliance of the long standing financial supporters of the Court, in the face of Kekela Bagrationi of Gergia and son of the Emperor Michail Palaiologos, future heir to the Empire, and a renowned General of its army. She is now in her ripe age, and the family is currently looking for a suitable match across the candidates who would wish to strengthen the ties with the imperial family.

Envoys of this news have been sent to the courts where Imperial interest lie, but any candidate may present themselves. <@297185126947880960> <@307587752860581888> <@698400527834415145> <@1432820281000333392> <@423689694258397195> <@234699731715096576> <@1106651545296195655> <@1417260821674852363> <@315868897012219904> <@1007414347347271730>

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
***Michail X Palaiologos***



A general, a Cataphract, a father of three, and an heir to the Empire.

Ever since his marriage, young Michail has enjo
events.fullText:
***Michail X Palaiologos***



A general, a Cataphract, a father of three, and an heir to the Empire.

Ever since his marriage, young Michail has enjoyed his position in the family. He was the second child of the Emperor, and had a privilege of living in a relatively prospering age. He was raised in the cities of Constantinople and taught military strategy from the young age. As he became adult, he chose his own path of contributing to the Empire. While his father and their advisors engaged reformed administration, and his older sister mostly organised envoys to foreign lands, Crown Prince was leading the military reform of the Empire. Under him, the first professional formation of κατάφρακτοι since the Great Imperial Kataphraktoi of the X century. He would join and lead that first formation himself.

Young Prince, in the company of his teacher, Dimitrios Kydones was reforming the military doctrine of the Empire to fit its modest means as well as current needs of the Empire. He would lay the groundwork for the re-establishment of the First Roman Legion. Right now the formation of such a Legion is still under way, but there is little doubt that it will be accomplished, sooner rather than later.

The Prince has also pushed his father to re-established many Themas which had either fallen into corrupt hands of the local nobles, or fallen into dysfunctionality. These Themas would show  a reliable way for the army to find new recruits in the time when most contemporary powers relied on peasants and mercenaries to wage their wars. 

The efforts of the young Prince have both saved the rule of his father in many ways, but also have laid a fundament for the support of his own future rule.

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**Deepening Ties through Marriage?**


Hearing from this envoy, Sultan Abu Bakr III Hafsid replied, offering the hand of his youngest and most capable
events.fullText:
**Deepening Ties through Marriage?**


Hearing from this envoy, Sultan Abu Bakr III Hafsid replied, offering the hand of his youngest and most capable son, in marriage to the Imperial Princess.  Cultural and religious differences do exist, however in keeping with the recent legal reforms in Tunis related to non-Muslims, the offer comes with two guarantees.  First, that under the policy of *Ahl al-Kitab*, the princess would **not** be required to convert to Islam, as Muslim men are allowed to marry outside of the faith.  Second, that the young Bahir would restrict himself to only a single wife--the Princess Eirini.  The faith of the children would be discussed between the parents, although according to strict interpretations of Shar'ia Law they would be expected to be raised as Muslims.  These laws can be bent, however, and there is precedent for this, most especially in the case of political marriages.

||A private letter is delivered to Emperor Ioannis V Palaiologos at the same time as the official response.  In this letter, the Sultan candidly admits to misgivings about the capabilities of his current heir, his namesake Abu Bakr Hafsid.  He acknowledges that his son simply does not have the same wit and abilities as Bahir on matters of administration and diplomacy, and in the letter he states that should Bahir and Eirini be wed, he will amend the succession laws and declare Bahir his heir.  The Princess Eirini would not marry the brother of a ruler--she would marry the heir to the Sultanate of Tunis, making her not only Sultana of Tunis, but Queen of Sicily as well.||

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**The Company, now merry, steps on the road back home**

After a successful completion of their quest, the Halych delegation to the Germanic Traders h
events.fullText:
**The Company, now merry, steps on the road back home**

After a successful completion of their quest, the Halych delegation to the Germanic Traders has spent the rest of the day conducting their business in the city of Hamburg - the last bits of goods from home were sold and purchased as many exotic items to sell back home, as possible: silks, a large variety of wines, the few spices they could manage to afford on such an ad-hoc expedition, and some novel items like 'fire-arms' and a few books to 'donate' to the library of the Lviv University.

Early in the morning, now colder than they had expected, they have met with the Hanseatic merchant who was to accompany them on the road to Krakow - they have exchange a few formalities and got around as far as teaching him a couple of words in Ruthenian - purely practical for the purposes of the journey. Soon enough, the delegation set out, now first moving South for a bit - at the advice of the man of the Hansa, they have chosen to avoid Brandenburg this time - though the delegation is well-armed and includes several dedicated household guards of the nobles, now that the company has succeeded in their quest, they may as well travel slower, but safer. The road to Hanover proved simple and uneventful, proving it to be simple now to find a place to stay, especially since the Hanseatic merchant has taken over the negotiations with the locals. The morning after reached Hanover, the snow has become quite deep, but the roads remained well-travelled and open enough, so they soldiered on, travelling Eastward from the town towards the several Hanseatic towns in the lands of Saxony.

Deep in the Germanic country, the Ruthenians took in the sights - buildings, castles, churches - everything was quite alien, foreign to the eye, yet the theme emerged - the architecture had certainly style to it, just like back home, merely different. The Churches were the single most unusual thing to them - unlike back home, they all stretched to the sky as high as, seemingly, the stone would allow. The village churches were not so impressive, but any sizable town certainly had a church that, by the looks of it, was about as expensive to build, as the entirety of the town put together. 

The man from Hansa has proven to be an invaluable guide in such a land, always staying on the track and leading the group through with little difficulty. When the opportunity arose, or, it seemed more likely, the merchant actually knew anything about the area they were in, with the help of the few Ruthenians who spoke German, he told them all about what they were seeing - tales or facts - mattered little.

The road has taken them through pleasant country - hills, forests, ocasional plains - much like home. But the timing was poor - the snows were getting quite deep and the road they have hoped wouldn't take even a week turned into a week and a half affair. Upon reaching Freiberg, they have decided to spend several nights to regain strength and to repair what damage the wagons have sustained. The horses definitely appreciated the respite in a warm stable of the town. 

Next morning, the company took to exploring the town a little bit, realising that they have stepped, perhaps, into the wealthiest German town imaginable - meticulous quality of the houses, incredible Churches - ones that could rival the cathedrals of Krakow or even Hamburg, the town, despite its' small size, was wealthy beyond imagination. And no wonder - the Hanseatic trader explained that they were staying in one of two largest Saxon silver-mining towns. This proved to be an incredible boon to the weary travellers.

A couple days later, well-rested and filled to the brim with additional food, the company has once again taken to the road. Still headed East, but now reaching familiar looking lands - Lusatia, a land of the few Slavs still left in the Germanic Kingdoms. The man of the Hansa continued to lead the way, as German remained the main language people here spoke, upon reaching villages and towns, the Ruthenians could once again understand some conversations of the locals without purely relying on their small group of interpreters. 

Not half a week later, the road turned smoothly South; and the views quite familiar - they stepped onto the road through Silesia to Krakow. This meant that much of the story of their travel later would not be as exiting as the previous weeks, but at least it was predictable. At their insistence, the company visited their previous hosts along the road and have given them some gifts from Hamburg. Nothing particularly expensive, as the quality of the goods they have purchased there varied, just like anywhere else, it was, nonetheless, a good image for both the company and their lands - tales of generosity travel far - much farther than they could.

The road was clearer than in Germany - Silesian roads were well-travelled, so in less than 4 days, the company finally reached Krakow - the last major city before heading home for good. Here also lay another quest for them - the company was never tasked with speaking with the Polish King and Nobility - a task made hundred-fold more complex by the current crisis - the struggle for power. But, emboldened by their success and not wishing to force the poor Friedrich either stay here for a month or travel to Halych for another delegation, they have decided to negotiate with the Poles as well...

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### A Hardly Mitigated Success

A succession dispute. An ambush. The blessing of God.

The first was expected, the second unfortunately common, and th
events.fullText:
### A Hardly Mitigated Success

A succession dispute. An ambush. The blessing of God.

The first was expected, the second unfortunately common, and the last a miracle. Each one alone was a story unto itself, but together? They were a saga that could lay down the foundation for a dynasty. In the unlikely land of Wales it has done just that. A Princess that came to this hardy land on the run has left it a Queen. Not just of England and its subjugated domains but also of Cymru and its fiercely resistant people.

*Eleanor Plantagant, now Queen of England after her brother’s ‘abdication’, leaves Wales with the unexpected support of most of its people and lords - English and Welsh. This may come to be a massive boon to her tenuous reign in London, or it may become a weight around its neck.*

The reaction of each powerbroker in Wales:
> - Baron Cardigan, the Justiciar of Wales, does not voice his support for Eleanor before or after her trip through Wales. He is rumored to find the exercise performative and superstitious - below the dignity of royalty, but has said nothing openly to suggest as much.
> - The Marcher Lords see ||the benefit in exploiting beliefs|| the justice in Eleanor’s actions. Some less astute individuals openly say what is whispered in Caernarfon but mostly tepidly support the new Queen.
> - The Welsh nobility and Burghers flock to Eleanor’s cause. They are swayed to support the new Queen like no English monarch before via a combination of self-interest and genuine belief. A minority remains stubbornly insistent that Caledfwlch is merely a cover for further English control, but these naysayers are drowned out by their enthused countrymen.

The people are their own beast. Most are illiterate and many unaware of the legends of their forefathers. The training and master tradition of Welsh Bards has nearly collapsed in the past 50 years due to the lack of Welsh speaking courts to patronize them, the Black Death, and ultimately English penal laws against them. Nevertheless some people in the limited urban areas are tepidly supportive, or at least curious, about this Queen that would deign to travel their realm for support.

Not that their opinion matters anyway. I'r pant y rhed y dŵr.

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**Another Pearl on the Shores of the Danube** 

The Danube and its tributaries are the lifelines of Wallachia, yet despite this, there were no major s
events.fullText:
**Another Pearl on the Shores of the Danube** 

The Danube and its tributaries are the lifelines of Wallachia, yet despite this, there were no major settlements on the Danube itself in Wallachia proper, only a handful of small villages alongside it, but no longer. Zimnicea, one of the villages, was chosen by the duke himself to be rebuilt and expanded into a town. It was the most populous village and had fertile land to support its people and its growth. The construction of the new town began under Duke Ladislau's patronage and with the spoils from the war against the Golden Horde. 

With the town's completion, it began as a way to strengthen Wallachia's presence on the Danube and as Ladislau’s personal project. Ladislau provided huge support for the town, promoted it and invested in it with gold from his personal coffers. New docks on the Danube and markets were built, new artisan guilds sprang up and people flocked into the town for opportunities. With all of this support, Zimnicea grew quickly and started to rival Targoviste and Bucharest in size and importance, and it is still growing at a rapid pace, while Ladislau is still alive… and will grow after his death.

...

After Duke's involvement in Zimnicea, life there has changed drastically in a few short years. Before the town everyone knew each other's faces, but now there are always many new faces moving in and out and passing through the town. Before, only a few Vlachs lived there, along with the occasional Bulgar, but now there are people from all over the Balkans. And the trade from the Danube brings more people from further away that had never before set foot in Zimnicea, with many merchants deciding to stop here. With Zimnicea’s rapid growth, it became one of Wallachia’s Catholic hubs alongside Targoviste, with the highest number of Catholics living in its streets and there were plans to even build Wallachia's first cathedral there too, but the Catholics are still the minority and many more are moving in, but the tensions rise within the Orthodox majority, over the question of building the cathedral and Catholic newcomers. Zimnicea has changed greatly in a short time, rising from the mud of a small riverside village into a bustling hub on the Danube. Whether this change is for the better or the worse, only time will tell. 

And with this growth of Zimnicea, Ladislau became more worried over his project, what if Bulgarians decide to avenge the Dobruja and Varna, to attack and sack his newly found town. Instead of sitting and waiting, Ladislau decided to strike first to deter future possibilities of revenge and take some land to buffer Zimnicea and Varna. The war drums sounded and the war began, placing Zimnicea on the frontlines of the conflict. People of Zimnicea can only hope that the town will come from this conflict unscathed.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Realm of the Hungarians #

At the point when the Hungarian army left the Italian deluge that was the Pope’s War on Ferrara and evacuated from th
events.fullText:
# The Realm of the Hungarians #

At the point when the Hungarian army left the Italian deluge that was the Pope’s War on Ferrara and evacuated from the Italian peninsula, the Hungarian Crown was the hegemon of eastern Europe, with holding that stretched from the lands of the Lithuanians and Rus to the Dalmatian Coast and along the Danube to the settlements of Varna and Nikopolis. Under the powerful Angevin monarchs, the Hungarian  and Polish Crowns were united under the Regna Andegavensis, standing as a steadfast bulwark against the eastern threats of the Rus and Tatars. However, despite the seemingly unstoppable nature of the Angevin Hungarian crown, the 15 years since the departure from Italy has seen Hungary’s situation change with new unforeseen circumstances that have laid forth a path to an unforeseen destination, be it endless prosperity or ultimate ruin.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Rule of King Louis of Hungary #

To those who heard of King Louis of Hungary, often one would be told of the endless exploits of the champion of
events.fullText:
# The Rule of King Louis of Hungary #

To those who heard of King Louis of Hungary, often one would be told of the endless exploits of the champion of Christendom in the east: Of his conversion of the heretical Vlachs of Wallachia, of his liberation and pacification of the Moldavian realms, of his Crusade against the heretical Bulgars, and his efforts against the devious, duplicitous, scheming, expansionist, and malevolent Byzantines of Constantinople. One would be emphatically regaled with these stories at the mere mention of King Louis or by the numerous town criers throughout Hungary and Europe. To all, King Louis was the epitome of a holy Crusader and virtuous man.

To those within King Louis’ inner circle, a far different truth was evident. A truth that stood in stark contrast to those great tales believed so easily by the peasantry. Behind the palatial doors of Buda, King Louis manipulated, schemed, and warred against those he perceived to be his enemies, using the Church’s agendas and holy mission as a facade to his ulterior motives. He forcibly converted Wallachia with the threat of war, attacked and slaughtered Bulgars, created Catholic counties to oversee the region's conversion, and constantly threatened the Byzantines, for whom he detested greatly. Even during Papal War after answering the Pope's call to arms, the scheming and malevolent King Louis sought to leave the conflict when the tides began to turn, abandoning is Papal ‘ally’ to the French and Northern Italian wolves.

After the evacuation of Italy, King Louis' attention returned to the detestable Byzantines, of whom he hated greatly and whose attitude of the ‘false romans’ had been shaped by years of dealing Byzantines threats, warnings, and paranoia. Conniving as ever, King Louis would meet with representatives of the Karamanid Beylik of Anatolia to discuss the Byzantine problem. The culmination of the meeting was the formation of the Royal Guild of the King's Merchants of Anatolia, a ‘trade’ alliance that would grow to include other member states that had nefarious designs upon the Byzantine state. Treaties were signed, plans were made, and King Louis would seek his goal for war against the hated Byzantines. 

In 1377, he and his accrued allies would begin hostilities against the Byzantines. Naples from the west, the Turks from the East, the Venetians from the South, and the Hungarians from the North. Although the war at the start mainly consisted of naval and army skirmishes, King Louis intended to expand the war by marching his way through Bulgaria and besieging Adrianople before laying captured to Constantinople. However, his nefarious machination came to an end in his tent on the cold night of January 19, 1379 outside Nicopolis when the great King of Hungary and Poland died of unknown causes. So came to an end the reign of King Louis and the driving force behind the bloody war that was taking place in the Balkans.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Ascension of Charles II de Anjou #

Upon the death of King Louis, his son Crown Prince Charles de Anjou was sought out and crowned that morning
events.fullText:
# The Ascension of Charles II de Anjou #

Upon the death of King Louis, his son Crown Prince Charles de Anjou was sought out and crowned that morning in the army camp surrounded by the army of Hungary in a battlefield coronation. Unlike his father, the tactical King Charles II was far more level headed than his father and had opposed the war with the Byzantines from its inception, much to the anger of his father. Although Charles II himself was considered a very offensive style planner in the army, he saw the folly of attempting to eradicate the Byzantines, a fellow Chrisitan state, with the aid of the Turks of Anatolia with little to no benefit for the Crown or Realm of Hungary. Furthermore, the war threatened his ascension and hold on the throne of Hungary. As such, the new King of Hungary began to make overtures to the Byzantines to achieve peace.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Angevin Divorce #

King Charles II fears were not unfounded. Between his coronation in Nicopolis and the conclusion of negotiations with the Byz
events.fullText:
# The Angevin Divorce #

King Charles II fears were not unfounded. Between his coronation in Nicopolis and the conclusion of negotiations with the Byzantines, news had reached him that his younger brother, Crown Prince Robert de Anjou, had been elected and crowned by the Szlachta as King of Poland and Grand Duke of Lithuania. Although the development irritated to no small degree, partially seeing it as a coup by the Szlachta to gain privileges, King Charles accepted the Szlachta decision and his brother’s ascension, for whom he loved and cared for deeply. However, this event, an Angevin Divorce of sorts between Poland and Hungary, stood as a reminder to the new king of what was at stake if his reign faltered.

## The Regna Andegavensis is Broken ##

||<@252560004069064705> ||

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Treaty of Thessaloniki #

A few months after the death of King Louis of Hungary, envoys of the Byzantines and the Hungarians, along with represe
events.fullText:
# The Treaty of Thessaloniki #

A few months after the death of King Louis of Hungary, envoys of the Byzantines and the Hungarians, along with representatives of other allied realms, concluded the Treaty of Thessaloniki within the walls of the great Byzantine city. Within the peace treaty, there were very few gains or concessions made by either side. Naples gained the location of Berat and Devol in Albania for the Angevin crown. Venice gained recognition of its ownership of Crete, the location of Karystos, and the Negroponte. The Papacy gained confirmation of the creation of a Bishopric in the village of Metria. The Turk, although wanting to deprive the Greeks of their Anatolian lands, was allowed a 40 year long truce. 

Finally, the Hungarians gained recognition from the Byzantines of the Catholic faith in the Balkans with a likewise guarantee given the Catholic Hungarians for the Orthodox inhabitants of the Balkans. ||Secretly however, the treaty also included provisions for the division of the Balkans, mainly the Bulgarian and Serbian realms into Hungarian and Byzantine spheres of influence. <@302128861209165824> ||

With the signing of the treaty, peace once more returned to the Balkans and the belligerents returned to their realms to move on from the matter.

<@1216097200401154181> <@550686589043343360> <@234699731715096576> <@150411715757080576>

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Seeds of Crisis Laid #

Though King Charles II had achieved his peace, he would not get to enjoy the peace for long, for tragedy would strike th
events.fullText:
# The Seeds of Crisis Laid #

Though King Charles II had achieved his peace, he would not get to enjoy the peace for long, for tragedy would strike the house of Anjou. In 1387 whilst on a hunting trip, Crown Prince Robert, only son and heir of King Charles II, would be mistakenly struck by an arrow in a hunting accident. Although the wound initially did not seem fatal, the crown prince’s condition took a turn for the worst and would die outside the village of Salgótarján from his septic wounds. 

This death, in addition to its tragic circumstances, also has laid the seeds for conflict regarding the future Hungarian succession following Charles II reign. Prince Robert was the only son of King Charles II, and as Hungary followed Salic Law, the succession would skip his daughters and pass to his brother, King Robert of Poland who himself had no male heirs. Although the funeral was conducted and people mourned, the seeds of conflict were laid upon the losing of that arrow in the forests of Salgótarján.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Building of a Pax Balcanorum #

Despite the tragedies and war that had defined the start of his reign, Charles II differed from his father in ma
events.fullText:
# The Building of a Pax Balcanorum #

Despite the tragedies and war that had defined the start of his reign, Charles II differed from his father in many ways and wished to rule the realm differently than his malevolent father. Although it is too early in his reign to determine what the new king’s goals are, what has been made evident is his more conciliatory attitude and willingness to seek honorable diplomatic solutions first before letting slip the dogs of war. To Charles II, his intentions align with the creation of a peaceful Balkan frontier that may allow for the Hungarian realm to focus on matters elsewhere that may be more pertinent and important. Regardless, only time will tell if Charles II is successful in building this ‘Pax Balcanorum’.

## Éljen Magyarország ~ Vivat Hungaria ##

events.snippet:
## *Pro Iustitia Imperii*

Ioannes de Luxemburgo, by the Grace of God, elected Roman Emperor, King of Bohemia, Duke of Wroclaw, Margrave of Moravia, e
events.fullText:
## *Pro Iustitia Imperii*

Ioannes de Luxemburgo, by the Grace of God, elected Roman Emperor, King of Bohemia, Duke of Wroclaw, Margrave of Moravia, etc., by Imperial Right, in full confidence of abilities and prerogative of imperial laws, hereby decrees from the Roman Throne,

The consortium of princes, arrayed in rebellion against the Emperor and his court, regardless of the descriptors they are veiled beneath, are declared in **disobedience** of the ***Golden Bull of 1350***, forbidding those leagues which, made among persons, cities, or persons and cities, establish between them conspiracy and guile for power, or otherwise to the detriment of imperial law, who are **not** established therefore in pursuit of peace and order in their lands, and so be it that the princes who make their alignment with the “Golden Bull League” known shall henceforth know **this**: the confederated array of such princes is **illegal**, and held in contempt for its pursuit against the sanctity of the imperial peace, by the dispense of rumors and creation of conspiracy against His Majesty, the Roman Emperor, shall be considered criminal and rebellious should they not publicly forswear the league and its conspiracy. 

With the sanctity bestowed upon Kings by the King of Kings Himself, the majesty inferred in the Frankfurt Election of 1371, and the August capabilities which have followed His Majesty in the past as they do now, it is declared from the foot of the Roman Throne that all members of this “Golden Bull League,” so much as they claim allegiance to it in **four months time**, shall be judged by and dispensed with the righteous authority of His Majesty Ioannes, Roman Emperor. 

[ Four Months after session start, the Emperor will declare a war against the members of the Golden Bull League for the primary aim of deposing the **Wittelsbach** Elector of Brandenburg, so that the Emperor may place a separate dynasty upon the throne and bestow them the electoral dignity. As GMB will be absent, no lands or vassals shall be taken from the Palatinate, nor any treaty option related to them taken. As disclosed in a prior post, Bohemian levies may **only** be raised for defensive actions on Bohemian soil, and will not be used outside of the Kingdom and its vassals. ]

events.author: Moro
events.snippet:
# The Dalmatian Settlement - The Treaty of Zara
The Dalmatian Settlement marked a significant turning point in relations between the Republic of Venic
events.fullText:
# The Dalmatian Settlement - The Treaty of Zara
The Dalmatian Settlement marked a significant turning point in relations between the Republic of Venice and the Crown of Hungary-Croatia during the years leading to the Guild War against the Byzantines. After decades of rivalry over influence along the eastern Adriatic coast, both the royal court of Buda and the Parliament of Venice increasingly came to view continued confrontation as less beneficial than a negotiated understanding, as both states stood in-between common rivals between the consolidating Byzantine Empire in the east and the Holy Roman Empire in the west, whom both sought to clash with the Republic and Kingdom in multiple theaters across Europe. 

For the Venetian Republic, Dalmatia had long held strategic importance. The coastal cities of the region such as Zara and Ragusa occupied key positions along the maritime routes linking the lagoon to the wider Mediterranean. For the Hungarian Crown, the region represented an important extension of royal authority toward the sea and a symbol of Angevin influence in the Adriatic over their Croatian subjects. Rather than allowing these competing interests to spill into open conflict, Venetian and Hungarian diplomats pursued a series of negotiations that became collectively known as the *Dalmatian Settlement* amidst the lower circles, while on paper being formally referred to as the **Treaty of Zara**. The agreement sought to normalize relations between the two powers and establish a more stable political framework for the Adriatic.

The settlement did not eliminate the underlying rivalry between Venice and Hungary. Neither side abandoned its long-term interests in the region. Instead, it established a mutual recognition that commerce and political stability required a degree of cooperation. The likelihood of immediate conflict had been reduced and paved the way for a strong friendship to be born.

events.fullText:
A Question of Marriage

With Duke Ladislau being occupied by his personal projects and ambitions, the matters of the future of House Basarab have slipped from the Duke’s aging mind. Balc Basarab, future heir to Wallachia and Moldavia, is yet to marry. To remedy the Duke’s negligence towards his house and family, the news of Balc’s candidacy for marriage has been spread through major European courts… and the news may have spread even further than expected.

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**The rise of the Szlachta **


The long rule of King Louis of Hungary and Poland had a curious effect on the Crown of Poland. Having consolidated the
events.fullText:
**The rise of the Szlachta **


The long rule of King Louis of Hungary and Poland had a curious effect on the Crown of Poland. Having consolidated their power over the Grand Duchy of Lithuania with a realm stretching from Krakow to Rzhev the Polish had enforced the Polish-Lithuanian Inquisition to end the pagan practices of the Lithuanian people and focused on the internal rationalisation of the realm.

King Louis's preoccupations in the Balkans and German campaigns in Italy gave Poland an unprecedented opportunity - a decade of peace. The szlachta, who had been taxed heavily for the campaigns into Lithuania, were loath to join more conflicts and so with the Hungarians treating Poland with a light hand the nobility were able to largely keep the king at peace.  

Who are the main families? The Ostrzeszówski, von Plauen, Orłowski and the Lithuanian Radziwiłł families stand most prominently alongside the Piast duchies whose throne now stands in the hands of the House of Anjou. Their petty agendas are many but they can be surmised mostly as pushing for low taxation, the right to dominate the state and to largely stay at peace. 

During the rule of King Louis they gained huge power - but this would change at the death of the king....

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**Lublin Konfederacja**


It was only in 1473 that the sleepy garrison village of Lublin was officially granted a royal charter to become the town of
events.fullText:
**Lublin Konfederacja**


It was only in 1473 that the sleepy garrison village of Lublin was officially granted a royal charter to become the town of Lublin, its castle now serving as a hub of activity east of the Vistula. Lublin's growing importance has become obvious - on the crossroads between Lwów to the east and Wilno to the north Lublin is a physical representation of the increasingly eastwards tilt of Poland as Silesia has slipped from its grasp and the vast lands of Lithuania has opened up. 

As Lublin has become a bustling centre it is here that several Polish nobles - mostly lesser szlachta but some being the grand magnates of the kingdom - have gathered to issue a royal petition. Worried about the possibility of King Robert of Poland taking the Hungarian throne and the succession going to his young daughter the nobles declare themselves as a *konfederacja* (confederation) - an association of nobles based on earlier municipal associations of the same name formed to defend noble liberties. 

The Lublin Confederation issue their own petition that not only reinforces the Angevin Articles as a concept of the *Corona Regni Poloniae* but goes further, demanding the sole right of the succession to be determined by the nobility. In practice this would confirm Poland as an elective monarchy, akin to Bohemia. 

The Lublin Confederation has received a cold response by the head of the Royal Chancellery Stephen and on his advice King Robert has stubbornly been disinterested...but [tensions are rising](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1513295529197568031) as the confederation's demands split the kingdom

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**Growth of Polish Jewry**


A curious fact of the development of Polish liberties and the concept of the *Corona Regni Poloniae* has been the growth
events.fullText:
**Growth of Polish Jewry**


A curious fact of the development of Polish liberties and the concept of the *Corona Regni Poloniae* has been the growth of the Jewish community in Poland. King Casimir III had been known for his relatively tolerant attitude with banning the desecration of Jewish cemeteries and the kidnapping of children for the purpose of enforced baptisms. Some of these legal privileges were enshrined in the Wiślica Statutes which were still undeveloped at Casimir's early death during the Great Plague. 

The Great Plague led to an upsurge of anti-semitism but this subsided as the clergy were directed to enforce the inquisition over Lithuania - many of the most zealous priests and knights thus emigrated east out of Poland directly. 

This was joined by the rapid growth of the capital of Krakow and the lesser growth of Poznan and Lublin. In time with the crushing weight of the inquisition in Lithuania rooting out pagan and Jew many would flee to Poland proper from the east 

The increasing commonality of these migrations has meant that local nobles have increasingly granted Jews charters of autonomy, or *kehillah*, that provide organisational benefits for the state. Much of this comes from views that the migration of Jewish communities - similar to that of Germans and to a lesser extent Hungarians and Greeks - will help with the development of local credit systems and widen the tax base. But similar to Germans and unlike in much of central Europe these communities have been granted freedom of trade and commerce.

Thus Poland has seen overall a growth to its Jewish community, although many look west to Regensburg and the Pious of Ashkenaz for religious teachings due to the underdeveloped rabbinical scene in the east.

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**An Imperial Betrothal**


After some negotiation an agreement has been reached with the Roman Emperor, betrothing the youngest son of the sultan, Ba
events.fullText:
**An Imperial Betrothal**


After some negotiation an agreement has been reached with the Roman Emperor, betrothing the youngest son of the sultan, Bahir Hafsid, to the Imperial Princess Eirini Palaiologos.  She would not be expected to convert to Islam, and the young prince would likewise foreswear the traditional formation of a harem.

This has triggered a week of celebration within the Sultanate, and during this week the Sultan has changed the succession laws of the nation, allowing him to choose his heir rather than his eldest son being his successor.  To the masses this is cause for celebration.  To the nobility, this is cause for tremendous upheaval and unrest, for as the Sultan knows from the ascendence of his father, a dispossessed son is a dangerous thing indeed…

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet: Two years past Stephen Simeonov had been anointed Bishop of Perm for his services in the east, and with it the Metropolitan in Moskva had made the Ort
events.fullText:
Two years past Stephen Simeonov had been anointed Bishop of Perm for his services in the east, and with it the Metropolitan in Moskva had made the Orthodox church’s suzerainty over the people of the east official. Yet for all the prestige he received and all the lands he was said to govern, Stephen *‘of Perm’* held little sway beyond the walls of his church.

Not only did he daily contend with the demands of Moskva, but in the north of the country Novgorodian merchants and officials still traded and collected tribute as if the Treaty of Cherdyn had never been signed and sealed. Likewise the heathen Komi cared little for their new western lords and Prince Opochi still ruled the country as if little had changed. What petty tribute in fur and copper ever actually made it all the way west to Moscow was far outweighed by the wealth and gifts the church brought (in large part sponsored by the Grand Prince) in the vain attempt to gain the favour and loyalty of the Permians.

Thus, in the winter at the beginning of the 6894th year since Holy God created the World, when the Grand Council of Perm gathered, Stephen did not hold high hopes. It had been a wonder that he had managed to convince them to move the meeting from Cherdyn to Perm in the first place and he suspected few would be willing to make any more compromises. After the gathering had been blessed and the proper rituals observed things went on as usual: old and new taxes were confirmed or upon discussion redrafted and changed to be more accommodating, old shamans complained about the church, Chiyaliks called for greater regulation on Mansi mining while the Mansi asked the same for the Chiyaliks, a handful of Novgorodians had come to reconfirm the suzerainty of the northern villages. 
It was all-in-all an uneventful thing, only made somewhat more interesting by the proud proclamation by one of the Grand Prince’s representatives that Ivan II had ordered that Ural gold would flow west. Proper roads were to be established and the flow of gold secured by all means necessary, he further issued.
The announcement was met first with silence, as only few knew the Russian tongue, and then with ambivalence after it was translated. Young Prince Mitruk responded to the man in Russian, though by how thick of an accent he spoke he might as well have just gone through the translator: “The men of Muscovy are welcome to travel on the Kama. If it is gold you seek, simply journey up the Belaya or Chusovaya rivers and you will find it at good prices but you know well as I that the Mongols rule the Volga and I cannot remedy that, but should the waterways open up I will be happy to establish posts along that route.”
With that the Muscovite is dismissed, and the assembly moves onto the next topic.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Cadomedd
-# (The Battle-Refuser)

Paramount among the noblemen of Wales is Sir Owain Aberffraw. He was knighted by King Edward IV himself and has
events.fullText:
### Cadomedd
-# (The Battle-Refuser)

Paramount among the noblemen of Wales is Sir Owain Aberffraw. He was knighted by King Edward IV himself and has served in London for decades now. Opinions on his actions are [mixed in Wales](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515552734890164245), but none dispute that he is the closest thing the Welsh people have to a universally respected figure due to his name, rank, and reputation.

The man is well-built and sharp even as most other men begin to whither. In another life he would have been a soldier of renown or fighter of [admirable nobility](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owain_Lawgoch). In this one he was the bold and fiscally astute advisor to three monarchs now.

*His manner in the days proceeding and following Eleanor’s arrival in London is **hostile**. He is not a man to throw away a life of service, but he is not afraid to make his voice heard…||particularly by a woman of doubtful legitimacy||.*

> - Eleanor Plantaganent, *the letter begins with courtesy but little respect.*
> 
> My people’s land has seen many Kings come and go. One of them was Cadafael ap Cynfeddw, or better known as Cadafael Cadomedd. His fame was earned when, instead of assisting a foolish plan by the King of Mercia, he simply turned his army around in the night and ensured his Kingdom’s survival for generations following.
> 
> Word of your exploits in Wales has reached all. We are granted privilege to know that the stories and myths of Bards are hardly accurate after so long. I implore you, as your family’s servant, to gently reveal this falsehood so that mania does not consume my homeland in your absence. I believe that God will forgive us for our impious impositions if we do so.
> 
> - Sir Owain Aberffraw of Anglesey

Though conciliatory, the presence of such a forward note is itself a breach of usual custom. Sir Owain also makes little effort at court to conceal his open dislike of an appeal to ancient legend in his homeland by an English monarch.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
## Landsting Wolves

The growing ambitions of the Kapteins have become a source of conflict within the Landsting. The district judges and landholding
events.fullText:
## Landsting Wolves

The growing ambitions of the Kapteins have become a source of conflict within the Landsting. The district judges and landholding elites of Gotland seek to exert greater control over them, yet this has proven difficult so long as the captains remain the chief guarantors of the island´s sovereignty and security.

In an effort to preserve Gotland´s reputation and maintain peace with its neighbors, the Landsting had previously compelled the Kapteins to limit their activities within the Baltic Sea. This restriction, however, posed little obstacle. So long as rich waters remained elsewhere, there would always be prizes to seize and fortunes to make. The only consequence was greater strain upon the ports and logistics of Gotland itself.

Many captains resented these limitations, seeing them as an unnecessary burden upon their profits. In time, a solution emerged. If Gotland´s harbors could no longer serve every need of the fleet, then the Brotherhood would simply acquire new ones. Their eyes turned toward the islands of the Baltic and the North Sea. By securing additional ports and anchorages, the Kapteins could extend the reach of their fleets, support longer expeditions, and increase their profits many times over.

This prospect alarmed many Gotlanders. Should they establish themselves upon other islands, they would become less dependent upon Visby and the harbors of Gotland. Yet the Landsting possessed little means to prevent such ventures. Rather than oppose them outright, the island´s elites chose a more pragmatic course. If the Kapteins were determined to conquer and profit, then Gotland would claim a share in the rewards.

Now the fleet readies, 3000 mercenaries gather in the port and they set out to subjugate the Islands of the North!

After being approached by Dixon, the hanseatic merchants in the kontor of London (the Steelyard) have decided to take sides and position themselves with the new claimant. The King, William, owes a lot of money, and they have not been paid for several years now! Furthermore, many englishmen they trade with are discontent with the King's attitude and incapable skills, unlike those of the previous regent (which, they suspect through northern connections, may be behind this). The wool trade should be expanded, and everyone join in the new riches, but for some time now progress has been stuck, all money going to war...

The Boston kontor, situated northward, is already supporting the Perciards, so they welcome this positioning by London. The hanseatic fleet used in patrolling the Channel trade, which is roughly 6 galleys, will be used to aid the Perciards if needed. <:Order_raid_convoys:1399308717391872000>

**Death of Opochi**

Opochi's death came suddenly after a short illness. After funeral and grieving ceremonies, the Grand Council would be assembled t
events.fullText:
**Death of Opochi**

Opochi's death came suddenly after a short illness. After funeral and grieving ceremonies, the Grand Council would be assembled to elect a new prince.
Unlike previous elections this was not a certain one however; the late Opochi had three children but her eldest children were Udmurts leaving only her youngest Mitruk. While many other noble candidates were put forward, it was eventually decided that Mitruk would despite his age be chosen as prince. In the meanwhile his father Örti would act as regent together with the Elder Council.

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**Foundations laid and ready themselves for work**

The first Germanic caravans arrive - almost two full years since Trojden's gambit has kicked off,
events.fullText:
**Foundations laid and ready themselves for work**

The first Germanic caravans arrive - almost two full years since Trojden's gambit has kicked off, yet much sooner than he expected. The Germans are few, but are stacked with goods and gold - money, promised to help build a Hanseatic Town. Many are recieved in the small town of Riashiv, directed to the new build site by a set of guards, directly paid for by King Trojden. After a days' worth of riding, the mechants arrive at a little settlement already set up in the woods.

The location seems odd, but the locals are sure that it is the best place imaginable - deeper in the forest, two streams meet, surrounding a future town from all sides, but the North. Additionally, the area around is full of good building material - clay, wood, stone... All in all, great material to build a good, fortified town that would keep it's residents safe and well stocked with goods. 

Weeks following the arrival of the Germans, many men from villages both near and far get to work - cutting down the wood around the settlement is hard work, but easy enough with good instructions and enough workforce. Tools are broken, injuries incurred, but much of what happens in this time is trees retreating - day after day, they are pushed back, revealing the little hill and the stream. Soon enough, an open view to the nearest plain is created - a village, not too far, can be seen, with farms and barns, peasants working away. The locals were right.

Much of the work is complete - now, master masons begin to arrive to lay the foundations, but metaphorically speaking, it is done - 'Hansyhorod', as the locals came to call it, has been founded.

events.author: Cayde
events.snippet:
# The Habsburg Lowlands
The Habsburgs have ruled as counts of Holland for forty years now <@420614608399761419> , assuming succession when the last co
events.fullText:
# The Habsburg Lowlands
The Habsburgs have ruled as counts of Holland for forty years now <@420614608399761419> , assuming succession when the last counts of Holland all but died out. Their rule has been tumultuous but prosperous, bringing an influx of trade and income to the marshy lowlands around Amsterdam and Den Haag. Friedrich II had deftly negotiated the territorial integrity of his domain after the disastrous Imperial campaign for Flanders. Through extensive amounts of monetary stimulus, and the legacy of Otto "the Merry", the nobility of Den Haag have all but ingratiated themselves to the court in Wien. 

With the news of the bastard Reniaartszoon breaking into the greater Imperial murmurs, Friedrich II had heard the news first, straight from his loyal subjects in Holland. With a debt owed to him by Emperor Jan II <@261290667106369536> and the war in Flanders a failure, Friedrich II used this golden opportunity to further the Habsburg domains in the lowlands by requesting a claim on the illegitimate throne of Brabant. The claim was granted, and Friedrich II put this claim in the back of his mind, at the time being too occupied with the Savoyard war to have any aspirations. But now that his hands are free, the Habsburgs shall look to bring the wealthy of Antwerpen under heel. ||<@99704182809853952> Prepare thine butthole for invasion, 6 months into session bucko||

events.fullText:
**The Eagle of Moravia Catches Robbers**
<@261290667106369536> 

Battle of Krosno Sees the Moravian Eagle Triumphant as the robber knights and cutthroats use the shadow of this war to effectively carry out a large scale sacking of Lusatia, such mismanagement would allow a large Moravian army led by the Margrave of Moravia Charles von Luxembourg to outflank the army, not caring for scouts or rearguard. 

The Battle would in fact be a series of skirmishes across the dark woods of the Spreewald, isolated parties dueling in bloody close quarters chaotic battles, more than some falling to friendly fire of crossbow or archers. Over the course of a week the Brandenburger army would be wiped out, practically to a man. Though the main Bohemian and imperial forces would be repelled in Bavaria this decsive action would not only save Upper Lusatia from pillaging but ensure the lands would be safe for some years, earning the loyalty of many native Sorbs while songs commerating such a conflict would paint the Germans ever more the villains, harking to legends of Dubroslav. the effects of such a battle having long term effects for years to come....

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**Carthago Iterum Resurgit**


On this day, an ancient legacy is reborn.

Across the Tunisian Sultanate old banners are taken down with care, replaced
events.fullText:
**Carthago Iterum Resurgit**


On this day, an ancient legacy is reborn.

Across the Tunisian Sultanate old banners are taken down with care, replaced with the stark blue and white emblem of Carthage.

Once more this great realm reigns at the crossroads of the Mediterranean.  Carthaginian merchants shall once again sail to every corner of the Mediterranean.  Carthaginian explorers shall once again push back the boundaries of the unknown, rewriting the maps of the world.  And, once again, Carthaginian fleets shall spread the power and influence of the realm across the seas.

Let this rebirth usher in a new age of prosperity in the Mediterranean.

Owain Aberffraw has been laid to rest after half a lifetime of service to the English crown. With him passes the last of the
events.fullText:
### The Passing of Kings

Owain Aberffraw has been laid to rest after half a lifetime of service to the English crown. With him passes the last of the Pre-Norman Welsh Royalty. All that is left is lesser princes and those with weak claims arising after the initial incursions from the continent.

*His hand had stabilized English Welsh relations for several decades now. His passing signals the end of an era.*

events.snippet:
## Welsh Revolt!

Sir Owain Mathrafal, the most senior remaining line of Welshman of Princely blood, has written to Queen Eleanor to inform her of inj
events.fullText:
## Welsh Revolt!

Sir Owain Mathrafal, the most senior remaining line of Welshman of Princely blood, has written to Queen Eleanor to inform her of injustices against ||welsh|| landowners in her lands <@1106651545296195655>. He knew that she understood the cause and may perhaps see reason to personally intervene in such an important matter even if the communication was improper.

***She didn't even open the letter***

This was a slight not just against the Mathrafal but against a large body of the Welsh smallholders. Queen Eleanor's more amicable ties to the region softened some views, but many still cited generations of discrimination and quickly took up arms against their abusive English lords - and by extension the crown...

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Glyndŵr Rebellion
||([Actual Revolt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glyndŵr_rebellion))||

Discontent at the Crown's lack of response and conti
events.fullText:
### The Glyndŵr Rebellion
||([Actual Revolt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glyndŵr_rebellion))||

Discontent at the Crown's lack of response and continued support for English colonial policies has brewed for decades. No matter how many stone mountains were raised a revolt would always come in time under these conditions.

Owain Aberffraw had kept these events...mostly, civil for the entirety of his tenure in London. Now Eleanor's support in Wales kept the worst out of the North that she personally traveled through to amicable reception. However the absently ruled south was a hotbed for bandits, resentment, and ultimately another great revolt against England's rule.

*The Marcher Lords were called to their ancestral service as the Welsh houses of Tudur and Mathrafals of Powys ||with some reservation|| moved against their kin. The revolt of Owain Glyndŵr Mathrafal, though idealistic and supported by a large number, simply had no organization or ability to contest the men at arms of professional soldiers.*

events.author: dani178i
events.snippet:
**An order.**

Mainz is not a big nation, but its tendrils and connections spread out all throughout germany, especially within its diocese. While the
events.fullText:
**An order.**

Mainz is not a big nation, but its tendrils and connections spread out all throughout germany, especially within its diocese. While the main focus has been on protecting and raising tithes from the smaller bishoprichs, the entire state of Bohemia is in fact still a part of the archdiocese of Mainz. 

A courier makes his way to the office of the Bishop of Prague and offers a light knock at the door, before leaving a sealed letter bearing the mark of Mainz:

||"To my brother in God and fellow servant of His will.

The empire is on the cusp of war, and the horsemen of the apocalypse are ready to begin their walk across our land. Too many men have forgotten their fear of God and openly trample on His will in hope of vain material gains. 

There are two forces involved in this affair. One side cries for peace, while the other readies their shields and sharpen their swords. 

You must convince kaiser Jan of the house of Luxemburg to yield to the golden bull league. It is they who offer peace and their terms are both generous and fair. You are the closest clerical figure to him and should he wish for a blessed existance in the afterlife, he ought to heed the word of God.

I write this letter to you as an order; do not confuse my friendlyness with lack of followthrough, your position is popular and there are many minor bishops and monks who would love to gain it under you. 

Your superior, Uilleag of Mainz."  <@261290667106369536> ||

events.snippet: Crown Prince Mikhail Rurikovich, head of the Cabinet as his father starts getting on age, despite his legendary status among the Russians, has noticed
events.fullText:
Crown Prince Mikhail Rurikovich, head of the Cabinet as his father starts getting on age, despite his legendary status among the Russians, has noticed a large issue within the tax revenue of the Grand Principality.

Russia has, for most of its existence, been without a great source of paper, and as such, records have been sparse, as well as lack literacy among those not of the clergy.

The process of keeping those records, as many of the nobility have not been paying the taxes they were due, has led to some controversy, but this census, the Razriadnyi Prikaz, will allow for proper accounting of taxes and military levies.

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**King Trojden is Dead - Long Live Queen Euphemia Piast!**

King Trojden, the King who saw Halych-Volhynia through a period of utter devastation and i
events.fullText:
**King Trojden is Dead - Long Live Queen Euphemia Piast!**

King Trojden, the King who saw Halych-Volhynia through a period of utter devastation and incredible rebirth, the wise King, who had reestablished ties with Constantinople and Krakow, a man who had brought people from across the world to Lviv to bring a learned word back to Ruthenia, has passed away in his sleep at the ripe old age of 53.

He has seen to foundation of two Universities across the Kingdom, construction of the Cathedral of St. George and he even managed to witness the beginning of his last project - Hanseatic Town in Ruthenia, one many now call Hansyhorod. He has seen the fall of the Tartars and several wars, carefully keeping the Kingdom out of them. He has brought much-needed stability in the face of the Black Plague and has guided through his subjects towards prosperity.

The burial has taken place in the Cathedral of St. George, with a sarcophagus, cut with his likeness on top, situated for the time-being in the middle of a chapel, separate from the Cathedral. While the crypt was being built, his remains were visited by many faithful subjects, including a large even some commoners.

His Daughter, now Queen Euphemia, has taken up her fathers' mantle - with the first step being her affirmation of all standing treaties. The people hope that a female monarch will not being about as much trouble as it often seems to, but many fear that Trojden was the stabilizing figure - a man so great, few will come close to matching him in image, and so many will compare them and find the heirs lacking. Only God knows what comes next...

events.author: Murlock
events.snippet:
**The Restoration of Rome <:romerome2:899964960506003477> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3wf6VW9gFFQ

For the first time in centuries, Rome has
events.fullText:
**The Restoration of Rome <:romerome2:899964960506003477> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3wf6VW9gFFQ

For the first time in centuries, Rome has once again grown to a population of 100,000. It has become a center of trade, activity, and innovation, serving as the beating heart of the Italian and Christian Renaissance. This renewed prestige is now being exported across the Mediterranean and throughout Europe.

The Colosseum, for generations, had been used for housing, its stones stripped away to rebuild the walls of broken homes and crumbling structures. Yet with the Renaissance came renewal. No longer merely a ruin scavenged by the desperate, the Colosseum began to reclaim its place as a symbol of Romes enduring glory <:prestige:587042877356376103> , standing once more as a monument to the greatness of the Eternal City <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> .

The city is witnessing vast economic, cultural, and intellectual growth, becoming a thriving center of commerce, art, and learning. New ideas flourish at the University of Rome and the Art College of Rome, where scholars, artists, and thinkers gather to shape the future of the Italian and Christian Renaissance. <:fine_art:1286339118841860237>

**An Army of God <:rosary:898776109246726154> **

*When the enemies of the Church gather, when the faith is mocked, when disorder spreads across the lands of Christendom, it is not enough for men to kneel in prayer alone. God grants peace to the righteous, but He also grants strength to those willing to defend His will.*

(He died shortly after)

<@460814348487753730> There are many in the Clergy in Rome who do not support the Roman Cardinals.

They call for <@460814348487753730> and <@423689694258397195> to march on Rome, and unite the church once more.

**Rome Becomes the Largest Market in Italy! <:trade:824666187718721577> **

"Are you foolish! Christendom burns! The Empire has taken over Northen Italy, and we are without a Pope in Rome! Now is your time to act!" <@423689694258397195> <@460814348487753730>

"I am reaching out to you in regards to Crown Princess Caterina del Carretto in regards to her prospects of Marriage.  As you may or may not be aware the Heir to Burgundy is soon to reach the age where discussions of marriage become a neccessary aspect in the future of the kingdom. I know you care for your child greatly as I do mine and as such I found it necessary to reach out to you in order to enter conversations for a potential match between our two children."

<@234699731715096576>  I apologize but it appears a match with the Hungarians was very quickly found after the rejection of your first daughter.

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Strangling of Brandenburg**
<@763652399784919080>
The Victory of the Golden bull for all its touting of legal rights would in truth have but one
events.fullText:
**The Strangling of Brandenburg**
<@763652399784919080>
The Victory of the Golden bull for all its touting of legal rights would in truth have but one effect for Brandenburg, the increased confidence of Ludwig to do exactly all that he wanted to do. Gaining greater ambitions to not only live a life of comfort and politcal power but greater than his father or relatives in the Palantine. To do so he shall take all he wants from his realm, allying with those robber knights who backed him in the war and in effect forming a core of corrupt tax collectors who with renewed confidence of royal backing rip the land apart. 

The land grows dim while Ludwigs palace in Bavaria grows much to the chagrin of his brothers and father who grow tired of their layabout scion.


**The Carthaginian Civil War**


As a direct result of the betrothal to Princess Eirini, the Carthaginian Nobility rose up in civil war against the cr
events.fullText:
**The Carthaginian Civil War**


As a direct result of the betrothal to Princess Eirini, the Carthaginian Nobility rose up in civil war against the crown.  Although they held support from many territories, not a single city sided with the rebels.  The reason for this was due to the complete and unwavering support of the Burghers for the Tunisian crown.

The war is already nearly at an end, with the rebels put to the sword, and the inheritance of the Crown Prince and the Byzantine Princess assured.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Flight of Owain Glyndŵr

The hastily seized castle at Pembroke stood in burning light as an army of Marchers stood outside waiting for his men
events.fullText:
### The Flight of Owain Glyndŵr

The hastily seized castle at Pembroke stood in burning light as an army of Marchers stood outside waiting for his men to die. The people of north had failed to rise up, the town had turned against him, and now his army was gone. Even calm Owain could not help but shed a small tear as his small vessel crossed the bay to Fishguard and an inglorious retreat from his homeland.

Owain Glyndŵr Mathrafal, his wife Lady Matilda Audley, and their three young children come ashore first in Cornwall and meet with some local sympathizers. They then land in Breton Brest and appeal for sanctuary with whomever was in charge.

*This infamous Welsh rebel seeks the Kings <@460814348487753730> support to liberate his homeland. Will he find safe refuge and allies in France?*

Villages burn and the crown of Poland is eventually informed that once more German knights come, not to bring God or take land but to bring death, bastards to a man.

**(Raid War into Poland July 1397)**

### A Second Rising

Welsh Peasants, apparently disinterested in the former revolt's timing, have chosen to rise up during the summer some years later
events.fullText:
### A Second Rising

Welsh Peasants, apparently disinterested in the former revolt's timing, have chosen to rise up during the summer some years later. They are equally as poorly organized and crushed in the field by Marcher men at arms.

Baron Cardigan, Justicar of Wales, died not long after the revolt was crushed. Though the position is still waiting to be filled by Queen Eleanor <@1106651545296195655>, his position administering Carnarvon Castle has been filled by Sir Arthur Tudur - a young Welshman whose family has shown repeated loyalty to the Crown.

Since the Griffin's death, there have been marriages between members of de Valois house between the Kingdom of F
events.fullText:
### Bringing de Valois lands together
Since the Griffin's death, there have been marriages between members of de Valois house between the Kingdom of France and Normandy. These incestious and strategic marriages, alongside two greedy monarchs seeing eye to eye on various administrative matters, have built the ground work for the formal annexation of  Normandy into the French crown.
