events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**First Catholic Church in Ruthenia**

As the town of Hansyhorod begins to take shape, a large spot, nearly at the very centre of the town, remains em
events.fullText:
**First Catholic Church in Ruthenia**

As the town of Hansyhorod begins to take shape, a large spot, nearly at the very centre of the town, remains empty - a large plot of land, dedicated to a stone church. The matter of a local church was addressed all the way back when the town was first being considered. But while the Orthodox Church near the town was quickly approved and constructed by the locals, the matter of a Catholic Church has been somewhat postponed - a small chapel, built mostly by the German settlers, has been quickly built in the beginning to serve the most basic functions, but it really served as nothing but a reminder of home. Now that years have passed, a question of creating a true Catholic Church in the region.

Considering the weight of the decision and how monumental such a building would be, considering that it is the first Catholic Church, that is to be build officially in Ruthenia, the locals have petitioned His Holiness the Pope Sixtos to send a special envoy to consecrate the ground and assign the bishop and go through all the formalities of establishing the Church. Queen Euphemia has approved the decision and has sent her personal messengers to Rome.

Legacy of King Trojden written all over it, this may, very well, determine how the Kingdom of Halych-Volhynia be seen in the world at large - as a useful ally at times, or a true Kingdom, respected and recognised by all as the only inheritor of the title of Ruthenia...

<@150411715757080576> <@288410456526422016>

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Eagles Die in the East**
<@252560004069064705> 

The Intensity of the Border raids would reach a fever pitch in the winter of 1396 when polish royal
events.fullText:
**Eagles Die in the East**
<@252560004069064705> 

The Intensity of the Border raids would reach a fever pitch in the winter of 1396 when polish royal troops bogged down by poor weather and a lack of roads would be unable to respond to the largest push by German robber knights yet, thousands of brigands and warriors going over the region of Pozan. Such was the intensity of forces arrayed than even when polish troops did arrive they would suffer heavy ambushes and attacks to the point of being forced to withdraw. 

After months of free reign led by Von Quitzow they would be forced into a bloody retreat by none other than elite Leitis of the Duchy of Lithuania whose light cavalry tactics made them well suited to out maneuvering these long ranging warbands, ensuring atleast for a time peace. However it is now clear that unless something changes either internally for poland in the form of new border fortifications or externally in Brandenburg such events are likely to only increase.

The Arch-Bishop declared a march to Utrecht, a march for the faith and re-establishing the Bishopric in guidance of man, with conviction and a taste for a fight of peers the men of Koln were met with the massacre upon Duren.

Even with the fighting men of coin, little was gained apart from the bloodshed of Kolns own men. Battered and broken they found little glory, little reason to keep fighting after such a disgraceful battle.

As such the lands of Zutphen fell out of Koln's influence and have regained the independent rule they once held.

events.snippet: Nuno Alvares Pereira has continually abused his position as Governor of Portugal, aiding his own corrupt ambitions. He was originally put in place to
events.fullText:
Nuno Alvares Pereira has continually abused his position as Governor of Portugal, aiding his own corrupt ambitions. He was originally put in place to help Portugal recover from the past decades of turmoil. In reality, he has only worsened the situation.

As such, he has been officially relieved of his station and imprisoned in Lisbon for a litany of crimes to ensure he will never see the light of day again.

This is an absolute embarrassment for Enrique's regime. As a form of apology, he has allowed the Portuguese nobles to select the next governor and sent a large sum of gold to the Portuguese coffers.

Once again, Gascony has grown whole and grows stronger with each passing year. Continued development in the region supports the mercantilist desire of the duchy, providing for neighbors in need

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**Mitruk the Leal**

Mitruk, Prince of Great Perm, comes from a long lineage of Komi and Udmurt nobility. Kind-hearted and tolerant of the Christian f
events.fullText:
**Mitruk the Leal**

Mitruk, Prince of Great Perm, comes from a long lineage of Komi and Udmurt nobility. Kind-hearted and tolerant of the Christian faith, during his first years as Prince he has allowed the flourishment of the young Permian church and even prayed for the Soul of Stephen of Perm, patronising him in return for his significant contributions to Permian society. In particular the Permian script has come to be embraced by much of the nation and allowed for a previously unseen era of unity in the nation.

His patronage of the Orthodox Church has however sparked controversy among the firmly pagan elite of the nation, many elders and shamans claiming he is little but the puppet of Moskva. Only time will tell if Mitruk will be able to reconcile Pagans and Christians, or if he must choose one path over the other.

events.author: Hoang
events.snippet:
# Milan builds its first University

With the Church divided between Lyon and Rome, Milan has revoked certain privileges granted to the clergy. Cultur
events.fullText:
# Milan builds its first University

With the Church divided between Lyon and Rome, Milan has revoked certain privileges granted to the clergy. Culture and learning will no longer be bound to Church authority. Milan embraces the Renaissance in full, sponsoring scholars, artists, and thinkers.

Restrictions on artistic expression have been lifted, allowing painters, sculptors, poets, and philosophers to pursue their craft without fear of censorship. From this new spirit of scientific inquiry and patronage, Milan establishes its first university, dedicated to the glory of the city and the advancement of knowledge.


<@150411715757080576>

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
**The Ideals of Wycliffe, and the Preaching of Hus**

With the marital and dynastic ties exhibited between the Crown of Bohemia and the Kingdom of Eng
events.fullText:
**The Ideals of Wycliffe, and the Preaching of Hus**

With the marital and dynastic ties exhibited between the Crown of Bohemia and the Kingdom of England, with that of the Luxembourg mother of William III, the religious exchange of their societies was buffeted certainly by their mutual relations to Rome, taken as means to oppose their French rivals, and have such allowed the exchange of ideas and theories.

Among those most principled in this exchange is the works and teachings of **John Wycliffe**,  which have found a receptive community those Czech who count among the reform movement for the Church, and outstanding from their various faces now is one—**Jan Hus**. As a professor of the **University of Praha**, installed in 1398, he came to the defense of some of Wycliffe’s propositions in 1399: specifically, notions of lordship founded in grace, thus, sinful and corrupt clergy forfeit any legitimate authority, and the replacement of the Universal Authority of the Church, which had been divided among differing papal claimants for decades, was instead rested in **scripture**. Naturally, of course, he supported as well the use of vernacular in preaching, for bridging the divide between faith and the faithful.

<@112960565344804864> <@137101075411894272> <@306458253674545153> <@150411715757080576> <@1106651545296195655>

-# Bohemia is also voting for Austria.

||His Majesty, the Kaiser, instructs upon the Prince-Elector and Margrave of Brandenburg, Karl Ludwig, to pursue a resolution to the currency empty chair for the Bishopric of Magdeburg regarding the chapter’s election.||
<@255439449448644608>

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Intellectual Expeditions**
<@763652399784919080> 

As the politcal struggle begins to deepen in Bavaria Karl Ludwig von Wittlesbach, Margrave of Bra
events.fullText:
**Intellectual Expeditions**
<@763652399784919080> 

As the politcal struggle begins to deepen in Bavaria Karl Ludwig von Wittlesbach, Margrave of Brandenburg looks to find any edge he can get, many despise him as an incompetent two faced liar, one who lacks for friends skill or prestige, so it is he looks for soft power over hard. 

With the creation of this new university that is securely driven in Italy the claimant to Bavaria seizes upon this to gain legitimacy. For years the lands of Lusatia, shrouded in dense forests and marshes have held truly ancient fortresses, for most a simple oddity or hiding place for a passing bandit lord yet in Munich as the Renaissance spreads notions of historical study and linkages arises, Kings and Lords draw parallels between themselves and the ancient Greeks or Romans, so to could Ludwig with the right evidence. 

So he formally write to the Duke of Milan offering support for his fine institution and officially speaking attempting to showcase himself as a patron of the arts, offering coin and art ~~(pillaged from Poland and Brandenburg)~~. However discretely he offers a dynastic alliance in exchange for the Milanese Scholars heading up an expedtion into the Spreewald,  the implication being to find historic ties that could be used to grant prestige to the Margrave. Ofcourse he leaves out ever so two facedly that his lands and those of the Lusatia especially are filled with all manner of robber knight and bandit lord, such an expedition will be not an easy feat....

**(Will Begin Midweek event chain to Carry out Expedition in Lusatia for ancient fortresses)**

In a show of complete ~~pridefulness~~ thanks the Margrave of Brandenburg sends his own heir Leopold to study in the Italian city, at length bragging as such to those in the court of Munich

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Second City of Wales

Carnarvron, and to a lesser extent nearby Anglesey, are effectively the **only** even semi-urbanized locations in Wales.
events.fullText:
### The Second City of Wales

Carnarvron, and to a lesser extent nearby Anglesey, are effectively the **only** even semi-urbanized locations in Wales. All these locations exist in the north. The south, though ironically ruled by the English for far longer than the rest of the land, is likely even more rural and parochial than the Welsh parts of the country.

Pembroke Castle and its surrounding Town is set to be the first exception to this. It had long been settled by English and Flemish colonists since the early 12th century and accordingly was referred to as "Little England beyond Wales". In the last revolts it was a bastion of English rule and held out for long months against the Glyndŵr Rebellion.

*The newly minted **city** has been granted right to self-government and fortifications. The first of such rights in Wales. Newly appointed Justiciar Iorwerth Mathrafal hopes to use this new settlement to appeal to Queen Eleanor's sent of justice despite the apparent contradictions with his background.*

events.snippet:
### City Tensions

Perceived interference by the sitting Justiciar of Wales - Iorwerth Mathrafal and his Welsh heritage, has led to the revolt of the
events.fullText:
### City Tensions

Perceived interference by the sitting Justiciar of Wales - Iorwerth Mathrafal and his Welsh heritage, has led to the revolt of the only recently elevated Pembroke. They appeal to loyalty to Queen Eleanor <@1106651545296195655> but cite the northern Welsh administration's meddling in their rights and privileges for his own advancement.

*The revolt is expected to be short lived and without blood, but it's setting and claimants are likely to reach the highest echelons of English law in London.*

His Majesty, the August Kaiser, Johann von Luxemburg, calls for a convention of the Imperial Princes to the **Free City of Nuremberg
events.fullText:
## *In the Wake*

His Majesty, the August Kaiser, Johann von Luxemburg, calls for a convention of the Imperial Princes to the **Free City of Nuremberg** to discuss matters of the future of imperial governance, after being humbled by the **League of the Golden Bull** years prior.

His hope for this endeavor, in light of the past, is to embark upon the path of **consensus** for imperial government.

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**Market Protectionism**


Since the formation of the Roma market, economic warfare has been waged by the Papacy against the Carthaginian markets, and
events.fullText:
**Market Protectionism**


Since the formation of the Roma market, economic warfare has been waged by the Papacy against the Carthaginian markets, and a red line was established--the silver mines of Igresia upon Sardinia.  So long as those mines supplies Carthaginian markets, the situation was tolerable.  The removal of those mines to the Roman market has crossed a red line.

The Carthaginian Sultanate is demanding immediate actions to correct this.  If preferential market access is not granted to Carthage, then the Carthaginians will be required to take action to secure the Sardinian resources--by force.

||I am demanding Preferred Market Access to be accepted.  If this does not return the island to the Carthaginian market, then an embargo of the Roman markets.  If this does not restore the flow of goods, then I will wage war for the island.||

events.fullText:
### The Queen's Ruling

Queen Eleanor received the news of revolt with sadness. However in short order she had her ruling; one that may spell the fortunes for some and ruin for others:

> The City was right. Justiciar Iorwerth Mathrafal is **deposed** and is replaced with Jean de Joinville, Baron Cardigan. The city of Pembroke shall retain its rights and privileges in perpetuity.

*Thus was the controversial appointment of a Welshman as the highest English representative in his own country **ended***

events.fullText:
## The Mad Duke

The Duke, during a routine skirmish with his noble knights, broke down with a case of insanity. He began speaking in Latin tongues and gained a mad look in his eye. He seems like a wild beast. Unreleated, the noble cook of the court died of dysentery recently. Some of the nobles call into question if the duke has been compromised by the poor peasent.

Carles already wordy and rambling messages become more incoherent, the meaning becoming more lost with each correspondance sent to his king and other country courtiers.

# Crown Princess Johanna de Anjou Proclaimed Heir of Hungary #

Following heated debates on the succession in the Kingdom of Hungary, the elderly King
events.fullText:
# Crown Princess Johanna de Anjou Proclaimed Heir of Hungary #

Following heated debates on the succession in the Kingdom of Hungary, the elderly King Charles II issued a proclamation stating the change of Hungarian succession laws would be undertaken to steer away from Salic laws of succession. Furthermore, he would declare that his heir would be second daughter, Crown Princess Johanna de Anjou. With the death of Crown Prince Robert and Crown Princess Hedvig de Anjou missing and presumed dead, King Charles II has seen that Johanna is the next in succession and has seen fit to proclaim her his heir infront of the court and take vows of allegiance and support.

events.fullText:
### Quelle Tragédie!

Hugh le Despenser, sole male heir of Lord William le Despenser of Glamorgan, has tragically **died** in an incident at the age of 14. The Despenser family may attempt to pass their long held southern Welsh lands to their daughters but by law they have little natural rights by customary practice after marriage. Justiciar Jean de Joinville, Baron Cardigan, is activly seeking to make sure these valuable lands continue to benefit the crown ||and his fickle family's fortunes||.

*Little save an intervention by the Queen herself <@1106651545296195655> is likely to cleanly secure the inheritances of the Despenser daughters.*

events.snippet:
# The Dubious Faith of Archduke Siegmund

Archduke Siegmund von Habsburg of Austria rules his country with a loose grip. He holds both the titles of B
events.fullText:
# The Dubious Faith of Archduke Siegmund

Archduke Siegmund von Habsburg of Austria rules his country with a loose grip. He holds both the titles of Brabant and Holland, and commands a great and vast realm, built upon the pragmatism of his father.  The clergy name him a sinner whose conduct has turned the religious establishment against him, yet none in his line is a more devoted theologian, a tireless student of the faith who has pursued the acrument of many religious artifacts, some heretical, some pious.

When his cabinet uncoveredd an underground workshop of forbidden artists and seize three works marked for destruction under the censorship statutes, Siegmund overrules his clergy and orders the pieces kept for the crown. In the same season a dispute breaks out over the veneration of the Virgin Mary, as radical theologians question the doctrine of her intercession. Siegmund declines to condemn them. He holds that she is a woman of singular grace and no more, a stance that many expect to bring the ire of the Papal See in Rome <@150411715757080576> . The same forbearance he holds for the prior mentions of heresy extends to the heresies taking root within his lands. In Steyr the Waldensians now number far greater than one in eight souls, and to the north the Hussite contagion spreads across the Bohemian marches and presses toward his border. The clergy demand fire and inquisition at once, in the same manner as his father's rule. But Siegmund offers neither fire, nor any iron crackdown. He lets the dissenters keep their conventicles and their plain preaching so long as they keep his peace and pay their taxes.

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**The Brandenburg Desolation**
<@763652399784919080> <@261290667106369536> <@222439858927435776> <@252560004069064705> 

Years of mismanagement, years of banditry, years of death, years of hate and all the world to see God so silent, Brandenburg is hollow. Though a certain stability was found in the post war of the golden bull as a collection of robber knights had become unofficially supported and allied with the Margraves own government such a system was fundamentally short term in focus, even the once loyalist robber knights now grow more desperate as coin and food dry up. 

Outside the major towns of Brandenburg and Berlin, forming a small beacon of stability and peace castles of robber knights are raided and besieged all the while the aging Ludwig grows focused on gaining control of Bavaria, leaving behind his abused realm. 

In the south the Lusatian league expands out forming for the first time a council in the style of a polish Sejm .  Voting to form a Sorbian unified army, the All Cities Companies (Všechny městské společnosti). Though making no direct announcement in practical terms Upper Lusatia will be in revolt, hunting down any robber knights with extreme prejudice, the Bohemian crown already more than busy to deal with such matters may see such things spread even more...

All the while Ludwig remains unbothered, having concerned himself with other matters in Bavaria, leaving Brandenburg in the whims of who shall have the ambition to take control.

**(Lusatia Revolts, Brandenburg is close to collapse of warlordism)**

events.author: Bas1leus
events.snippet:
**The Twin Battles of Bayonne**

Although not on the main front of the war, the Castilian Crown put Bordeaux in its sights, not as a target of conques
events.fullText:
**The Twin Battles of Bayonne**

Although not on the main front of the war, the Castilian Crown put Bordeaux in its sights, not as a target of conquest, but simply merely to force the French to pay for their continued insults and incessant plotting towards Enrique and his holdings in Iberia.

Castilian armies gathered in Navarre, after which they planned to march into south-eastern France, directly towards the regional capital of Bordeaux. However, they needed first to take the crucial stronghold in Bayonne, which could not be bypassed due to its strong fortifications.

However, as our armies awaited reinforcements from Granada, something strange occurred. We had been sure to keep our armies away from the prying eyes of the French, and yet, the Savoyards and Gascons seemed to respond to our every move, posturing perfectly as to prevent a surprise flanking maneuver. It seems that our ranks are comprimised, or perhaps, something more sinister...

Regardless, the only option by this point was a dangerous frontal assault from Burgos. The town of Bayonne was guarded by 15 thousand Gascon soldiers and a small French contingent. Against the 60 thousand strong combined Iberian armies, they stood no chance. However, most of the Navarran and Granadan forces were held in reserve, allowing only 30 thousand troops to take on the 16 thousand Franco-Gascon forces.

Gonzalo de Avellaneda, the brilliant general in charge of the armies besieging Bayonne, knew that with his limited intelligence, he could not afford to commit his full forces immediately. He was proven right nearly immediately. Our forces had barely even set up camp around the fortification's walls than a Savoyard-Gascon relief force arrived. 20 thousand of the reserve troops were sent to the front, and the French forces were once again put to rout decisively.

The route to Bordeaux was wide open. Sadly, the situation in Italy was beyond repair, and France gained control of the papacy.

We will have to accept this new reality, and despite our coalition's failure to defend the Pope in Rome, we have acquitted ourselves masterfully. Our statement has been made. And for his efforts, Gonzalo de Avellaneda was ennobled and made the Count of Vitoria, taking the name Barillas.

*The House de Genève has been a long and storied house in the region of Arles, like House de Savoie going back
events.fullText:
## A sad end to a noble Arpitan House

*The House de Genève has been a long and storied house in the region of Arles, like House de Savoie going back to the Burgundian Kingdom and having long played roles as both rivals and allies to our House over the countless years we have shared the alps.*

*Now though, their bloodline has all but died, with even the current Countess only having born daughters, the oldest of whom is married to some german upstart noble from Freiberg, as such with heavy heart, the Cabinet of Savoy has enacted a law absorbing Geneva to it's direct administration, in order to preserve the Savoiard domain.*

events.snippet:
## Jan Hus Grows in Influence

Having defended the propositions of John Wycliffe in 1399, becoming the dean of his philosophy department in 1401 as a
events.fullText:
## Jan Hus Grows in Influence

Having defended the propositions of John Wycliffe in 1399, becoming the dean of his philosophy department in 1401 as a rector of the University of Prague, and then appointed as preacher to the [Bethlehem Chapel](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1515810732955144243) in 1402, and he now works for the translations of renowned works of Wycliffe and other reformers into Czech. 

Even from his pulpit he denounces **moral failings**, **decadence**, and **corruption** on behalf of the Church which have **degraded** the authority of the Vicar of Christ—especially as there stands two of them. 

Now, the Church in Bohemia looks warily for the authority of the **Archbishop of Mainz**, and His Holiness **Síxtos V**, as the Kaiser is caught in his own conundrum: acknowledging, technically, neither papal claimant.

```diff
-"How lonely sits the city that was full of people! How like a widow has she become, she that was great among the nations."
```
**Lamentations 1:1**

Over a thousand years ago, the barbarians cast down the Empire, and Rome, jewel of the ancient world <a:excitedpig:899929193834479637> , was left broken beneath ash and ruin <a:q_fire:829161310595186698> . Yet time, patient and stubborn, saw her rise again from the dust <:prestige:587042877356376103> . Now, by the cruel hand of history, the same shadow returns. Once more do barbarians descend upon the Eternal City as heirs to the same hunger that shattered an empire long ago. The restoration of Rome, forged through faith of the holy Mother <:mary3:1126382749612249108> now stands before the fire once more <:blueflames:1139530132617445477>.

The Battle of Rome of 1403 saw intense fighting; thousands died defending the city and its people from ruin. It is impossible to say the true number of the dead. Some claim over 150,000 were slain in the battles and the events surrounding them. Heros among the dead <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> .

**Hell and fire, blood and sacrifice. 🩸 **

The Devil looked among the dead and smiled, cackling at the devastation that was his art, while beyond the heavens, Mary <:MotheroftheRosary:1336753726467342378> shed a tear at the destruction brought upon the seat of the Church of God and Heaven. The Kingdom of God was tested by the hearts of men, and Satan, in his cruel ways, destroyed the schism. It was by the force and hand of the French that it came tumbling down.

**The Schism is Over <:rose:865579087883272192> **

events.snippet:
# <:Flag_CYP_lusignan_armeniaa:1511103742529900716>

# A Middling End.
**The Later Reign of King Peter I: 1387 - 1404**

If King Peter I de Lusignan's
events.fullText:
# <:Flag_CYP_lusignan_armeniaa:1511103742529900716>

# A Middling End.
**The Later Reign of King Peter I: 1387 - 1404**

If King Peter I de Lusignan's early reign was something to behold, then his later reign did not live up to those years of excitement and rewarding opportunism. The decline of Lusignan fortunes began when Peter I's supposed "grand tour of Europe" ended prematurely due to the King catching a nearly fatal illness in Vienna <@190640828954181632> . On his presumed deathbed, he quickly and haphazardly tried to secure the succession by proclaiming his younger brother who was living in Austria, Louis, as the vice-King of Cyprus until Peter's daughter, Catherine, came of age. Fortunately for Peter, he would soon recover from his sickness, but the damage was already done. Unwilling to push his health to the brink again by risking another venture into the heart of Europe, he abandoned his tour and returned home. The result was that months of planning and preparation were wasted on a trip that went no further into Christendom than Vienna. It was an unmitigated disaster, and Peter I thus failed spectacularly in his goal of winning support for a new crusade.

After the King returned to Cyprus, it was as if the trip to the west never happened, and Peter I never again tried so directly to ask for aid against the Saracens. Now, through the next nearly two decades, he would instead merely get back to the tasks of removing Genoese influence over the island and continuing to integrate his miniscule gains in Cilicia. In these tasks he did admittedly do an unrivaled job at, and by 1404 it was safe to say that the Genoese no longer had any of the overwhelming control over the Cypriot economy as they once did back in the early 1370's. Furthermore, Korikos and Silifke were both tied strongly to the Lusignan Crowns over Peter I's later reign, but these successes could not in any way turn the tide in the eastern Mediterranean like what a successful grand tour of Europe could've accomplished.

So, in all real terms, Peter I's Kingdom in 1387 was just as adrift as it was upon his ascension nearly twenty years ago. The Saracens were still hegemons with no enemies who could dare face her, Cyprus still garnered no support for a crusade, and the Catholic world was in more chaos than ever before. This is not to say that King Peter I did not try anything else radical in his later reign to secure the future of his Kingdoms, but it is only that he missed what was a golden opportunity to gather together a still somewhat coherent Christendom and spurring them into action in the east. With that chance gone, it was then a natural consequence that the Kingdom of Cyprus was to fall yet again into an inadequate position politically.

At the very least it seemed that Peter I would have a stable succession, for after his near death in Austria and subsequent return to Cyprus, in 1389 he and his wife would have their third and last child, and for the first time it was a boy. The young Prince, named John, was a bright and able child, and Peter I saw much of himself in the young boy. The King spared no expense in his son's education, and he was arrogant in his presumption of John's future abilities as King. That however, ended suddenly when the young Prince John, just five years old, died after only a few days of illness in mid 1394. The King was utterly distraught at the loss of his only son, and it affected him so much that it led to his rapid decline in ability to lead and stay in good health. As his younger brother, Prince William, put it:

*"After the death of the Prince (John), the King regularly indulged in the excesses of drink every mid-day to the point of which he'd then be put in a lamentable stupor for the rest of the night, from which he did not recover until the following morning. Tired from the previous day of indulgence, none of us would aggravate his temper until soon the process would repeat itself again as the King fell deeper into mournful sin."*

---
This is perhaps an exaggeration by Prince William, but the King's declining ability was certainly real after the death of his son, highlighted when the King failed to take advantage of a second Cilician revolt in the Dulkadirid Beylik. <@874750350538727434> Nevertheless, the succession once more had to be secured. There were many potential paths that Peter I might've taken here, like choosing one of his younger brother's to succeed him, or marrying his daughter off to a distant relative to ensure the Lusignan rule over the three Kingdoms. Yet instead, in a spark of his old brilliance, Peter I offered the hand of his only child, the Princess Catherine, to the great rulers of Christendom. If one of these Kingdoms would marry a member of their close family members to her, it might secure for the Lusignan domain a powerful ally, one bound by marriage and eventually blood to defend, perhaps even enlarge, the Catholic Kingdoms in the east. It was a bold move, which upset many of the nobility, but if it worked it might finally win for Cyprus the ally it desperately needed. 

**Unfortunately for Peter I, the royal families of Europe were not exactly leaping at the proposed offer. Joint rulership of Cyprus, and their dynasty eventually supplanting the Lusignans was apparently not enough for many of them, and it was little wonder why. The Lusignan Kingdoms, for all their prestige and honor associated with the titles, were no true Kingdoms at all. Jerusalem was long gone, Cilicia barely hung on to the coast, and Cyprus was just a tiny island across the sea. The Roman Catholic east simply wasn't as important as it used to be in the minds of the great rulers of Europe. The days of Baldwin, Tancred, and Bohemond were over. Even the days of Richard the Lionheart and Saint Louis IX were long in the past. Fighting for the land of Christ now paled in comparison to fighting over Italy and France and Germany. To fighting fellow Christians, to fighting against the Church itself.**

---
Mercifully though, an offer eventually came, though not where Peter I nor the Frankish Knights and nobles expected it to come from. The powerful Crown of Castile <@422498641425596418> , consisting of the Kingdoms of Castile, Leon, and Portugal, sent an envoy to Cyprus to express their wishes of a match for Princess Catherine's hand. King Enrique II de Trastámara offered not just to marry Princess Catherine to a relative of his, but instead to his grandson and eventual heir, Prince Fadrique. This would mean the eventually union of the Crowns of the Trastámaras and the Lusignans, and it was an offer that, after of few months to muddle over, King Peter I accepted. 

Peter I could see the immense benefit that something like this could bring to the Lusignan domains. A union with the Crown of Castile would mean that the Iberians would be obligated to defend their eastern titles if they were under threat, and few could be better suited to do so. It is to his immense credit that he seized this opportunity when it struck, even if Peter I's ability to rule effectively was rapidly waning. But, it is also to King Peter I's major discredit that he could not foresee the great domestic disturbance this radical action was to cause. For Peter I was the only one capable of seeing the benefit of a union with the Crown of Castile, everyone else saw it for a grave disaster. The Frankish nobility and Knights were irate at this supposed "selling" of the Kingdoms, but in truth they were only *really* worried about losing their place of privilege and power on the island Kingdom. With Peter I's grip over his Kingdoms not nearly as tight as it was decades ago, he could no longer sooth the nobility with calm words and careful concessions, but instead to prevent a revolt across Cyprus the King had to grant to them yet more power in the Kingdom and the institutions within.

These major concessions have indeed once again stabilized the Kingdom and ensured the loyalty of the nobility, but that loyalty has come at the cost of all but guaranteeing that the Frankish Knights and nobles will have great power and control over Cyprus, even after the union with Castile is enacted.

-# The newest round of major concessions to the Frankish nobility of Cyprus.

---
Now, the Lusignan Kingdoms, even more rich and prosperous ever since the final renunciation of the Treaty of Famagusta, is bound to fall into the hands of a foreign King, for the good *and* ill that it brings. At the very least, the marriage between Princess Catherine and Prince Fadrique has been a successful one. By 1404 they've already had two children, one a boy, and Catherine is pregnant oncemore. With her father in a worsening state of health due to his over indulgent drinking, it is likely that she won't have to wait long for her turn to rule, and unless anything drastic happens, hers will be the last of the Lusignan rulers of the Kingdoms of Cyprus, Jerusalem, and Cilicia...

...and only the merciful God knows what fate will befall the Crusader Kingdoms then...

-# Portrait of Princess Catherine de Lusignan

**(End)**

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# a Small Gesture

When news arrived to Cyprus of the Emperor John V's death, there was some discussion within the royal court on whether to give thei
events.fullText:
# a Small Gesture

When news arrived to Cyprus of the Emperor John V's death, there was some discussion within the royal court on whether to give their respectful condolences or not. After all, John V was the same man who refused to aid Peter I's father in his time of need after a period of seemingly warm relations between the Lusignans and the Palaiologoi. That refusal cost King Guy II Cilicia. In more recent times, John V insulted the Lusignans yet more when he did not even try to send an offer to marry off one of his relatives to the young Princess Catherine. Of course, such an offer was very likely to be refused due to the clergy's almost certain disdain for such a match, but the fact nothing was even offered was an insult.

Yet on the other hand, John V had a, by almost all metrics, successful reign. The Empire of Constantinople was stronger both internally and externally than it had been since perhaps even the days of Manuel I Komnenos. Respect for the Empire's newfound power was prominent, and through it all John V was the figure at the top. A reign of 63 years is rarely seen, after all. And so, perhaps as an olive branch to further rapprochement, or perhaps only as a kind but ultimately meaningless gesture, the Crown Princess Catherine sent a messenger to Constantinople. He carried with him a letter from the Princess, to be given to the new Emperor Michael X:

---
*To his Imperial Majesty Michael X, Emperor of Constantinople and ruler of the Aegean. To also the other members of the late Emperor's faithful and honored family.

News has just arrived here of the loss of the great Emperor John. I wanted you to know that all of my father's subjects mourn his departure from this mortal world. Although we are comforted by the fact that the merciful God will value his presence greatly in the Kingdom of Heaven, due to his great piety, which we so often pleasantly received tales of throughout the years, I thought it best to share my own personal grief that I am certain you all feel in your hearts as well.

Our two countries had not always been on warm terms during his reign, but I hope that he knew in his heart that he could visit again to the Kingdom of Cyprus whenever he pleased, like he did during the reign of my late grandfather. I share with you all my sincere condolences and also the same invitation to visit my father's Kingdom. If any should ever accept it now or into the future, you will be welcomed with the proper merriment and honor that would be expected.

May you all receive the blessings of God in these difficult times.

From the Princess Catherine de Lusignan, heir to the Kingdom of Cyprus, the Kingdom of Jerusalem, and the Kingdom of Cilicia.*

-# The Princess Catherine de Lusignan

<@302128861209165824>

**(End)**

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**The Basilius Looks Beyond the Horizon**



The accession of Basileus Michail X marked the beginning of a new age for the Roman Empire that has spent
events.fullText:
**The Basilius Looks Beyond the Horizon**



The accession of Basileus Michail X marked the beginning of a new age for the Roman Empire that has spent more than 3 generations under the rule of Ioannis V. At fifty-one years of age, the new Emperor possessed no time for neither military ambition nor the temperament of many of his predecessors. He had served most of his life as a general in the Imperial Army, and had no fight left in him. The Empire's borders had been secured as much as they could be at the moment and the Queen of All Cities stood at peace. To Michail this peace was an opportunity.

For generations the Romans had lived surrounded by enemies and consumed by struggle. Yet the Empire endured, and in enduring it had grown prosperous. Markets bustled with merchants, who were no longer Italian only. Through the presence of Hansa, many other peoples had came to Constantinople. Granaries were full and overflowing, and the gold once again flowed through the City and the Empire alike. Such prosperity allowed men to seek more than mere survival.

Influenced by the teaching of his late [mentor](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1514801686638624840), the Basileus decided that the Empire should once more become the cradle of culture and knowledge, as it was once known across the civilized world. The Imperial University of Constantinople, already among the most respected institutions in Christendom, would be expanded and now include many halls for lectures about foreign cultures. 

Thus, during the year 1404, a series of imperial embassies departed the Queen of Cities. 

Some traveled northward to the Kingdom of Scotland <@1417260821674852363> , seeking scholars versed in law, theology, navigation, and the customs of the distant Atlantic world. Others journeyed to Ferrara <@315868897012219904>, whose peoples were known to be surrounded by most of the recent innovations of the European world. 

All would be welcome to teach what they consider worthy beneath the shadow of Hagia Sophia. From thez Crown of Aragon <@1007414347347271730> of Iberia to the learned men of Novgorod <@160455024994942976>  in harsh snowy lands of the rus, all would be invited to send their participants. Even those, who would see themselves as the successors of the Roman legacy would be welcome to share their knowledge <@215639063502258176>.

Not all Romans Welcomed this new policy. Some viewed these teachings with suspicion, they though that the Emperor was welcoming risk and chaos that would come to the Empire with their foreign influences. They prefered to believe that the Empire had survived because of it had remained steadfast in its traditions. Yet this could be no further from the truth from the Emperors view of things. And many others embraced the Emperor's vision. The alliance with Tunis had already demonstrated that cooperation with distant peoples need not weaken Rome.

And so, the Empire would seek knowledge and understanding, for while ***there is no war in Constantinople***, there remained much of the world left to discover.

# <:Flag_CYP_lusignan_armeniaa:1511103742529900716> 
**All Good Christians are welcome in the east.**

The Lusignan Kingdoms of the eastern Christian
events.fullText:
# <:Flag_CYP_lusignan_armeniaa:1511103742529900716> 
**All Good Christians are welcome in the east.**

The Lusignan Kingdoms of the eastern Christian world have always been an attractive target for those nobles seeking glory or piety. For even if the large Christendom-spanning crusades of the past were over, all good Christians were welcome to help fight against the Saracen menace. Some returned home after a while, some remained and were often given places of high honor for their service. 

Thus, if some Scotsmen braved to sail to the last Crusader Kingdom, they would find a very welcoming host indeed. Nobles, knights, craftsmen, petty soldiers, and all others would be lavishly rewarded by King Peter I and the Frankish nobility for their service. To the Scottish nobles and sons of nobles in particular the Frankish nobility would seek to attract to the Lusignan Kingdoms. The noble's power in the Kingdom had grown exponentially in the past decades, but there weren't many of them on the island after the Black Death swept through. Adding even more loyal noblemen to the Kingdom would naturally solidify their newfound influence and guarantee safety against royal interference. 

So the Frankish nobles would offer the Scottish nobles in particular great wealth, lands, and power within a Kingdom that they proclaimed was very traditionalist, pious, and *feudalistic* (gave great power to the nobility). Furthermore, many of the Frankish nobles would even promise to willingly transfer some of their own serfs to work the Scottish nobles potentially allotted land, if only the Scottish nobles would consider making their stay on Cyprus a permanent one. 

(I'm not exactly sure how much to spend on these Scots for hire, especially since the goal of the Frankish nobility is to persuade them to stay permanently. I'll just say I'm willing to spend 1k gold on this and uh...maybe I'll let you decide how much that wins me.)

-# The Frankish Nobility on Cyprus

**(End)**

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Y Gost - The Cost

*Has been in opportunity - now and forever.*

The north has survived but at the cost of the midlands and south. What scattered
events.fullText:
### Y Gost - The Cost

*Has been in opportunity - now and forever.*

The north has survived but at the cost of the midlands and south. What scattered prosperity existed in locations like [Cardigan](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515789633487900774) and Carmarthen was devastated by needful armies. Even fortified towns like Pembroke could not escape the always hungering mouth of war as they were forced to endure siege by both sides. If plans to move the administration south existed before they are gone now.

However, the Justiciar’s efforts to improve northern extraction first under Michel and now under his son Jean - the Baron Cardigan, have paid off. Half the Principality revolted and yet finances continued to operate supported by the mines of the north and pearl trade of Conwy. The House of Joinville, maintaining its poor reputation among the Welsh commonfolk, continues to prove its loyalty with admirable service to the Exchequer in London.

The event demanding continued attention has not been the fall of Pembroke or revolt of the countryside though. Instead, a series of **Penal Laws** has been passed in the wake of the two, nearly three, revolts. They have banned the right for Welshmen to arm themselves, removed their ability to purchase land in English towns, and barred them from high public office. Though most are of little concern for the common man ||who is apt to ignore it anyway||, it means the **effective end of officially recognized Welsh representation in their own country**.

-# *Thomas Jones’s ‘The Bard’*.
.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Y Budd-dal - The Benefit

*Is that more damage can hardly be done.*

Queen Eleanor’s sympathies toward her principality are already well known. Ho
events.fullText:
### Y Budd-dal - The Benefit

*Is that more damage can hardly be done.*

Queen Eleanor’s sympathies toward her principality are already well known. However they do not stop the tirade of curses that fall upon her lords and Marcher allies in their violent suppression of the region. Thus the goodwill that took literal legend to endear is being steadily consumed by the laws her government is imposing on the vast majority of Wale’s already hostile population. At the very least it's a complaint most are used to by now.

The continued plight of the commonfolk is contrasted by the abundant and ever rising fortunes of the nobility. Eleanor’s [blank cheque](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515797100712038601) to deal with the revolt as they wish has seen an immense growth of their control (over 50% estate power) over almost every aspect of the Principality. The most direct beneficiaries of this have of course been the Joinvilles of Cardigan, but even loyalist Welsh houses like the Tudurs have seen their influence [ironically rise](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515809279188144251) thanks to their actions ||or betrayal||.

The most immediately impactful incident to the common man has been the partial destruction of [St.Davids Cathedral](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Davids_Cathedral). It is a pilgrimage site for the entire faith and its namesake is the patron saint for all of Wales. Like many others it was a victim of local bandits and ‘warbands’ looking for quick profit - both sides naturally pointing at each other. The resident lords of Wales are unlikely to fund an expensive restoration of such a symbolic monument, though they are not so foolish as to shutter its operation totally.

-# *’View near Wynnstay’ by Richard Wilson*

events.snippet: Balthasar Türing, consul for the Hansa for the last 33 years, has died. His successor, chosen by the cities, is Bernard Siegmund von Walbeck, an admin
events.fullText:
Balthasar Türing, consul for the Hansa for the last 33 years, has died. His successor, chosen by the cities, is Bernard Siegmund von Walbeck, an administrator from Türing's cabinet and the son of the consul in 1337. He made a career within the Hansa, just like his father. He's much more competent than Türing, or so they say, but it is yet to be seen if he'll have the entrepreneur spirit of the last consul. The only project remaining to be implemented were the banking offices in kontore...

What joy, how unexpected! von Walbeck could not be more like his predecessor, and he's here to make the Hansa even richer (if at all possible) <:Price_expensive:1345840629258780703>  The policy of expansion is to continue

events.snippet: The loss of the hanseatic fleet by a storm during the battle against Visby has been taken with disappointment, to say the least. Nevertheless, the cof
events.fullText:
The loss of the hanseatic fleet by a storm during the battle against Visby has been taken with disappointment, to say the least. Nevertheless, the coffers of the Hanseatic League are filled to the brim, and new galleys will be soon built to sink the pirates’ ships and leave them at the Baltic sea. 

However, Walbeck is a man of action, and simply waiting for new galleys is not something he’d like, as it takes his focus form elsewhere. Perhaps a deal can be struck with the pirates to stop pestering.

# The Unification of Bavaria (1388)
On a rainy day in __1388 __ AD, Duke Johann I von Wittelsbach— the last living male of the Heinrichian line, would
events.fullText:
# The Unification of Bavaria (1388)
On a rainy day in __1388 __ AD, Duke Johann I von Wittelsbach— the last living male of the Heinrichian line, would pass from old age in his estate at Landshut. He would sire three daughters, leaving no heir to Lower Bavaria. Thus marked the extinction of the Heinrichian line— and the inheritance of Lower Bavaria by the Ludwigian line.

Subsequently the duchy was inherited by **Duke Ludwig V** von Wittelsbach, whom gave it to his eldest son **Ernst von Wittelsbach** to ensure the administration of the lands whilst Ludwig would be busy administering Upper Bavaria. Such marked the unification of the duchy of Bavaria, finally together *despite it's partition in __1253__— just over __150__ years ago*.

Alas, the triumph of the Ludwigian line would be a fleeting one indeed. Only three years later, Duke Ludwig V would pass and the lands to be partitioned once again...

events.author: The Kryft
events.snippet:
# The Inheritance of Bavaria (__1391__)
In June __1391 __AD, Duke Ludwig V 'the Roman' of Bavaria would pass from an injury related to a horse-riding
events.fullText:
# The Inheritance of Bavaria (__1391__)
In June __1391 __AD, Duke Ludwig V 'the Roman' of Bavaria would pass from an injury related to a horse-riding incident in Munich, at the Munich Residenz. His lands would inherited by his oldest son and heir presumptive, **Ersnt von Wittelsbach**. 

However, there would be the question of inheritance for his two other sons— Elector Karl Ludwig of Brandenburg ( <@255439449448644608> ) and Count-consort Josef Klementia of Rochechouart ( <@460814348487753730> <@564196329132261376> ). Although both held titles of their own and had not coveted any lands of Bavaria, such would change after their father's passing and the inheritance of Lower Bavaria.

An agreement of joint-ruling would be established by the older brothers in __1391__, Ernst and Karl Ludwig, each taking on equal responsibilities and legitimacy as co-dukes. The youngest, Josef Klementia would be excluded from co-rule or even inheritance of any kind, considering him to be more French than Bavarian.

The agreement would remain mostly stable until recently, where Karl Ludwig has begun to despise his greedy, overbearing older brother whilst Ernst considered Karl a nuisance and an incompetent fool who chooses to stay in Munich instead of ruling his lands north in Brandenburg. Such has earned the brothers quite the nicknames, being **Ernst 'the False'** and **Karl Ludwig 'the Dubious'**. The youngest has also earned a nickname from his brothers who seek to de-legitimize his claims, now called **Josef Klementia 'the Frenchman'**, owing to him leaving for Rochechouart when he was only 17.

Tensions continue to spike between the Wittelsbach brothers, each seeing themselves as the legitimate successor to their father's lands...

events.author: The Kryft
events.snippet:
# Duke Ernst is Dead!
> Allegedly...

In August of __1403__, **Duke Ernst** would rally the soldiers of Bavaria to ride south and aid Rome against Lyo
events.fullText:
# Duke Ernst is Dead!
> Allegedly...

In August of __1403__, **Duke Ernst** would rally the soldiers of Bavaria to ride south and aid Rome against Lyon in their struggle. He would leave his brother and co-duke, Duke Karl Ludwig to rule in Munich in his stead while he lead his armies personally.

They would begin marching south by October, taking on the herculean task of passing through the Austrian Alps during the harsh winter months. Many couldn't reach Italy before December, leaving nearly 16'000 men stranded in Tyrol whilst only Ernst and his vanguard of 4'000 would manage to pass before the winter snow fully rolled in.

By December, Ernst would arrive in Ferrara, lifting the siege of **Medicina** and routing the French in **Modena**. However, Rome would fall in January, Ernst never having reached the rest of the Italian forces in the Papal States.

The grim news of Rome travelled swiftly, reaching Munich before Ernst could even begin returning home. Along with the news however, spread a dark rumour regarding Ernst and his fate. Hearsay speak of **Duke Ernst having been slain on the fields of Modena,** and his vanguard scattered and broken!

Whilst confirmation is impossible due to the general chaos in Italy, the estates have already begun murmurs of the duchy's succession. Naturally, many support Ernst's son, **Philipp von Wittelsbach**. But some favour his brother, Duke **Karl Ludwig von Wittelsbach**, an older and already-established heir who currently reigns in Munich...
```ansi
[2;31mRumours say Duke Ernst is dead!

Some support his son, Philipp as successor!

Others seek stability in his younger brother, elector Karl Ludwig of Brandenburg![0m
```

events.snippet:
# The Kingdom of France, home to a powerful King and a powerful Pope

## King Henri II “The Sword of Lyon” de Valois
The Son of the Dragon, a title Ki
events.fullText:
# The Kingdom of France, home to a powerful King and a powerful Pope

## King Henri II “The Sword of Lyon” de Valois
The Son of the Dragon, a title King Henri II de Valois of France does not appreciate for he is not his father. Unlike his father, he respects French Law, does not hoard and embezzle wealth, nor kill or execute on whims. He is a devout man of God.

Thus he has attempted to do much to separate his identity from him. Mostly done by his adamant support for Pope Gregory XII, being a patron of holy works across France with plans for new monasteries and possibly a few Cathedrals if such organization could be found, sponsoring of religious art projects within France, and even education for the higher classes.

Beyond that, he does hold quite a variety of diplomatic interests that will battle one another for his attention as he cannot attend all of them.

### A patron of holy works, arts, and knowledge
With seemingly unending wealth within the court of Paris, King Henri II de Valois has made it his purpose to develop his Kingdom by supporting its faithful. He will aid in Pope Gregory XII development of Lyon into a great Holy City to lead Catholicism, ambitiously build atleast one hundred monasteries across France for his people’s religious enrichment, begin attempting at financing the best artists available to him to make works glorifying God, and invest a shiny franc into the education of the elite with new universities in the City of Troyes, Tours, and Toulouse.

### Interests within Iberia
King Henri II de Valois of France is sickened by the blatant expansionism of the ruling Trastamara line in Castile, and is appalled by his father's acceptance of two Kingdoms being subjugated. This is an error that needed to be rectified, and of course would require his guiding hand if Navarra and Portugal are to find independence. However, if there are already collaborators against the Trastamara presently ready to do so themselves, King Henri II would grant the collaborators a number of boons instead of leading such a movement himself.
-# <@1106651545296195655> <@298192682554228738> <@1007414347347271730> <@422498641425596418> 
-# Edit: Having been updated that Portugal is  already free, there is only interest to make Navarra Independent.

### Interests within the Eastern stretch of Catholicism
In the farthest stretches of Catholicism lies the peaceful and diplomatic conversion of Ruthenians towards Catholicism. A plan by the fallen Roma Papacy, and a plan without guidance. Seeing the goodness of such work, Pope Gregory XII has taken it upon himself to continue the project.

Thus, newly appointed Bishop Adam Robert is sent far across Europa to the humble Orthodox town of Hansyhorod to administer the frontier Diocese. He will overlook the construction and management of the Catholic Church there, and make it his priority to spread the Catholic faith to where his reach is currently limited. Hopefully in the future he will be permitted to spread the faith further with the inclusion of Papal funding to fuel such endeavors, and possibly utilize the Catholic resource nearby in Poland to strengthen his efforts.
-# <@1432820281000333392> <@252560004069064705>

### Interests within the Holy Roman Empire
His father the Dragon hated the Holy Roman Empire for its aggression regarding Flanders, and even made a personal vow to have his revenge. A revenge that would never come, and a hatred that would diplomatically isolate the Kingdom of France as the fire from the Dragon’s eyes died out.

However, King Henri II holds no personally ill feelings towards the Empire or most of its princes. One of the few exceptions being his dislike for the growing influence of the Habsburg House not too far from his border, but such concerns are quite small at present. Personally believing them to fall to internal skirmishes just as the quarrelsome Wittelsbachs. Besides that, he holds some of his own diplomatic interests to build positive relations with some of the princes if he can find the more agreeable and respectable of the bunch. 

Fortunately he has found success with the d’Alsace house in both Trier and Lorraine, but the entirety of this positive relationship is a by-product of being the Sword of Lyon and fellow ally of Pope Gregory XII. Although, his promise to Duke Hugues VI de Bourgogne could pose future strain on relations with the Holy Roman Empire or possibly even with the ecclesiastical leadership of Trier. The promise being that he, King Henri II de Valois of France, would recognize and protect Duke Hugues VI de Bourgogne pursuit of uniting the crowns of the Burgundian Union if he chose such. 
-# <@1123821809704374313>

### Interests in Italian peninsula 
While there is only one interest, it is a difficult one. That being the protection of the Orsini led Latium, and maintenance of its strength. Its territorial role is highly strategic, highly precarious, and deafeningly important. More important for Pope Gregory XII authority in being Bishop of Rome, but still important for King Henri II to hold onto for its political usefulness within Italy.

Of course there is also [the treaty he agreed to](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1515861778188210216), and he is a man of honor that sticks to his word.

### Interests on the Isles
King Henri II houses Prince Owain Glyndwr Mathrafal of Wales in Paris. A useful political tool to establish new allies upon the Isles, and possibly develop the reach the court of Paris has.

|| Thus King Henri II de Valois of France, on behalf of Prince Owain Glyndwwr Mathrafal of Wales, offers the arranged marriage of Prince Ithel Mathrafal of Wales, son of Prince Owain, to one of the daughters of Crown Prince Charles Stewart of Scotland <@1417260821674852363>  for an alliance between their two houses to press Prince Owain’s claim for Wales. 

King Henri II of France is willing to offer up his remaining mercenaries from his campaign into Roma, since their contracts are written to last years more, in the effort of backing Prince Owain’s claim to Wales. While it will be tricky to contest the much greater English fleet at sea, King Henri II includes the honorable promise of his fleets in support of pressing this claim despite grim naval outlooks. ||

## The Victorious Pope Gregory XII
In a long planned scheme the Lyon Pope, Pope Gregory XII, who lacked the clerical support his counterpart held had successfully used might to assert his holy authority as the sole papal figure. With victory over his captured counterpart, he would hold his schismatic rival at trial in Lyon where he was found guilty of [lust via private Roman baths](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1449171134317723791/1516227331226603550) ~~without paying indulgences for such sin~~ and for defying true Papal authority that stands in Lyon. His punishment is excommunication, and he is to be under close guard in an isolated and small monastery on the outskirts of Lyon to worship God for forgiveness of his sins without any opportunity for a place in ecclesiastical office.

Thus, only one true Pope remained to wear the Tiara and bear its responsibilities.

### The Excommunications of 1404
-# <@422498641425596418> <@234699731715096576> <@315868897012219904> <@763652399784919080> 
King Alfonso XII de Trastamara of Castile, King Giorgio del Carretto of Naples, Signore Alfonso da Este of Ferrara, and Duke Karl Ludwig von Wittelsbach of Upper Bavaria shall henceforth be excommunicated due to their martial response in defying true Papal authority.

If forgiveness is sought by these sinners, then they shall be forgiven. Although, they will only be forgiven if they make an indulgence payment to Pope Gregory XII, and kiss the Papal ring as a sign of respect and loyalty to him.
-# (Indulgence payment is your nation’s wealth stat, but for Castile that is times 3 their wealth stat. The greater sum expected from Castile being due to King Henri II de Valois ' private and biased request to Pope Gregory XII due to his developing rivalry with the Castilian Kingdom.)

However, Pope Gregory XII is benevolent and forgives Elector Friedrich August von Wettin and Prince-Bishop Anjou Laszlo since they made peace on their own without being forced to make peace in Roma. To him their separate surrender showed willingness to drop hostilities and communicate with him instead, but whether this was a shared opinion or not is unclear.

### The Trier promises & more
-# <@137101075411894272> 
As [promised in private talk many years ago](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1514198115316666448/1514198119988990064), Prince-Bishop Tinti d’Alsace shall take control of the Beasancon Diocese South of Trier. Granting him the right to subjugate the town and place a proper bishop there to rule. Alongside this, a Cardinal seat will be granted in the towns of Trier, Metz, and Beasancon.

A clear effort to empower the bordering Prince-Elector for their loyalty before Lyon victory against Roma, and open a doorway for favorable connections within the Holy Roman Empire with the aim to bring the ecclesiastical infrastructure and leaders within the HRE to recognize him. Obviously granting more to the ecclesially inclined if they too desired to give their support to him.

|| Pope Gregory XII sends his response to [Prince-Bishop of Trier’s message](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1516210369067221052)
“What you ask of me may be too great to pursue given the ecclesiastical council in Wurzburg, but their sign of heresy clearly present. I will send my inquisitors before making such a declaration of excommunication for now, they will investigate the matter and give any needed evidence for me to justly deliver an excommunication. 

However, I do hold the opportunity to hinder Habsburg growth in the Prince-Bishopric of Trier. I could support King Henri II de Valois younger brother, Jehan de Valois, as a potential candidate to your clergy to be elected as the next Prince-Bishop. He just may rule long enough for a favorable future candidate of either d’Alsace blood to be elected as heir, or possibly a candidate from de Dampierre who seem to be the growing allies of the d’Alsace as far as marriages have been made."||

### Concern towards the growth of heretical thinking
The rise of Lollard and Hussite faiths is a stain on the Catholic Church far worse than the now resolved schism between Rome and Lyon. So too is the rise of heretical  thought by Archduke Siegmund von Habsburg of Austria. While neither of the three realms have fallen to heresy, that may not be the case forever. A few inquisitors of the Lyon Papacy, whether desired or not, arrive to all three realms to investigate the degree of heretical growth and details about each as to give Pope Gregory XII information to come to a decision on how to handle the differing matters. 
-# <@190640828954181632> <@261290667106369536> <@1106651545296195655>

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
## One Realm's Trash is another's Treasure

Exile has long been among the most common punishments throughout Christendom. For many crimes, banishment
events.fullText:
## One Realm's Trash is another's Treasure

Exile has long been among the most common punishments throughout Christendom. For many crimes, banishment is preferred over the gallows, sparing the condemned while relieving towns and villages of thieves, vagabonds, debtors, and other troublesome souls. Imprisonment is costly, and few rulers are willing to feed men who have broken the peace. Better instead to cast them beyond the borders and forbid their return.

Yet even such men have their uses, provided they can be controlled. These *free souls*, are naturally drawn to places like Gotland, where few care for their past transgressions against the laws of men, or even of *God*, so long as they can prove their worth. Their talents are seldom in demand among respectable folk, but aboard privateering vessels, within mercenary companies, or among the more discreet guilds of smugglers and cutthroats, they are welcomed with open arms.

It would be a shame not to make use of such a steady supply of willing hands. On this, both the Brotherhood and the judges of Gotland find rare agreement. Yet neither wishes these *uninitiated murderous sheep* to wander unchecked through the harbors and streets of Visby. A place must therefore be found where such men may be gathered and put to useful labor. What better place than the recently seized island of Öland? Sparsely populated and neglected under its former lords, it requires little investment to transform into an open port, where all are *welcome*.

To truly benefit from this we require the word of Öland to spread far and wide amongst the realms of Europe, and a perfect opportunity presents itself... With the fall of Rome and Victory of the True Pope, we may expect *punishment* to befall the men on the *wrong* side of the Schism, we shall offer the service of ridding Realms of troublesome men...

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**Blood in the Snow**

March 12 1395. A date that Mikhail will never forget. The date when he found Grand Prince Ivan II "Breaker of Chains" Rurikovic
events.fullText:
**Blood in the Snow**

March 12 1395. A date that Mikhail will never forget. The date when he found Grand Prince Ivan II "Breaker of Chains" Rurikovich was found dead of poison in his own chambers. All of Russia shook at the death of the liberator from the Golden Horde, the unifier and one of the most inspiring man in Russian history.

The cause was officially ruled as a suicide, Ivan II, a skilled physician, knew well of poisons and how to treat them. The night before he was found he had no visitors, no servants entered his quarters within the Kremlin. He looked almost peaceful as he slept, not showing any signs of being dead.

Mikhail can't accept that. This great man, his father ||or so it seemed||, committing suicide? An affront against God and a sin? He was an example he aspired to, his personal hero. He would not do something like that.

Ever since, while being busy dealing with military matters regarding the tartar raids and rebellions in the south, Mikhail has been conducting a secret investigation, which has unearthed findings he could not believe. On the deathbed of his mother, 3 years ago, he heard the confession of his mother, that almost a year before his birth, his mother Antipara had cheated on Ivan II with another nobleman, though Mikhail would never know his name, for his ailing mother didn't remember.

He was devastated with this, knowing that he was not the son of his hero, and not the full blooded brother of the rest of his family. He ashamed his wife and children too for the actions of his mother.

His investigation would eventually reveal that to his surprise, his uncle Vasiliy, in Sweden, was the one who ordered the assassination. Vasiliy had died before he discovered this, and as such he kept the information for himself, safe in his diary.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**Sins of the Mother**



His investigation discovered, he redirected those feelings of outrage at his unworthy blood. His mother was dead, but her dy
events.fullText:
**Sins of the Mother**



His investigation discovered, he redirected those feelings of outrage at his unworthy blood. His mother was dead, but her dynasty would still answer for her sins. One more thing before he would go.

He travelled to Trebizond, where he was received by Basileus Andrónikos IV Komnenos, where the Gabras family had fled after Theodoro was conquered by the Horde. He informs Andrónikos about the situation, and the duel is allowed, not to the death however.

The duel is quick affair, Akákios accepts, as he is honorbound too, but he is not a fighter. In truth, Mikhail doesn't hate him, he is blameless, he blames more himself for the sinful action of his mother. He defeats his cousin in the duel, before making an announcement.

He proclaims his rule over Russia false, apologises to his wife Irina Komnenos, proclaiming his children to be only of the Komnenos dynasty, and passes the crown of the Rus to his brother Pyotr. This proclamation shocks his family, as he would later disappear, Pyotr would allow haven for his nephews and sister-in-law, but she decides to stay with her father Andrónikos in Trebizond.

Many things happened in quick order, but Pyotr's rule will need to be reasserted. Nevertheless, Russian nobles see this as a betrayal by the greeks, and salute Mikhail as despite not having russian blood has russian honor, and many of them see a need to reduce relations further with the greeks.

<@302128861209165824> <@160455024994942976> <@192490969092980737>

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**Dusk and Dawn**

Duke Ladislau is Dead! Wallachia weeps for its beloved Duke, and with Wallachia, before Ladislau’s coffin, kneels Balc in tears, mo
events.fullText:
**Dusk and Dawn**

Duke Ladislau is Dead! Wallachia weeps for its beloved Duke, and with Wallachia, before Ladislau’s coffin, kneels Balc in tears, mourning the loss of his father. Balc knelt as the heir to Wallachia and Moldavia, and after the final tear that he shed for his father, he rose as Duke of both realms. No matter how long he mourns the death of Ladislau, it will not bring him back nor ease the pain. Now the best he can do is rise and be the Duke that his people need him to be, that Wallachia needs him to be, the Duke that his father would be proud of.
A few days have passed and the funeral is over. Many notable nobles and important figures were present, both those who supported and those who opposed Ladislau during his reign. Balc knew that they were there not only to mourn the passing of Duke Ladislau, but also to see the new Duke. To see what kind of ruler he would be, to see where he stood in the affairs of the realm.
...

The funeral concluded, and Balc threw a feast to celebrate his new position as Duke of Wallachia and Moldavia. The feast was so large and lavish that all of Targoviste seemed to take part in it. The feast lasted from dawn to dusk and into the night. People enjoyed the festivities to their heart’s content, and all gave their condolences and congratulations to the new Duke. Close to midnight, after the main festivities, Balc rose and began to give a speech:

“Welcome, everyone. I hope you have enjoyed the festivities thus far. I wish to say a few words about the reign of my beloved father. We all know that the reign of Duke Ladislau was a prosperous one for our dear duchy. The realm has grown to the Black Sea, and new development has taken place along the Danube.” *(Balc nods with his cup towards the mayor of Zimnicea and the Catholic priest of the town, ignoring the Orthodox one.)* “And we have found a new friend from an old enemy. Ladislau won great victories on the battlefield against the Tatars and Bulgars, and let us not forget that he also expanded Basarab influence through diplomacy with the personal union with Moldavia.” (Balc greets the representatives from the sister duchy.) “Yet there is one matter in which he did not succeed. My father did not uphold his part of the treaty with our dear Hungarian friends, so I wish to remedy this and expand upon it further. Please enjoy the rest of the night and...” *(Balc raises his cup.)* “...to me, to the new Duke!”
...

After the speech, many were surprised by the proclamation, and many were outraged. Among them were some of the nobles who had opposed Ladislau, but also some of his former supporters. Particularly outraged was the Orthodox priest of Zimnicea. Not all were angered by Balc’s proclamation. Some of the Catholics were pleased, especially those who had opposed Ladislau for his leniency towards the Orthodox. The rest were indifferent, as it would not affect them at all.
The feast ended and dawn began to break. Duke Balc stood in his chamber, looking through the window at the rising sun as he prepared himself for the day ahead. For the first time, he would rule the realms as their Duke.

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**The Steppes in Disarray**

Balc stands in the council room looking at the map of Wallachia and the surrounding regions. Inspecting it, Balc looks to
events.fullText:
**The Steppes in Disarray**

Balc stands in the council room looking at the map of Wallachia and the surrounding regions. Inspecting it, Balc looks towards the southern border and sees security in the region, with Bulgaria defeated by Ladislau and then made to kneel before Hungary. They should prove no threat anymore, and our shores can rest easy while the trade of the Danube can flow uninterrupted to and from the Black Sea...

The Black Sea lingers in Balc's mind for a second as his gaze turns towards the east, towards the Steppes.

The silence is suddenly interrupted as a merchant from the recently conquered Theodore steps into the council room. Close behind him rushes a general, having failed to stop the merchant.

The merchant starts to explain in a greedy voice.

"Oh, Duke Balc of Wallachia, I come here with a proposal of opportunity for your realm, one that you cannot refuse."

"Speak then," proclaims Balc.

The merchant continues.

"After the fall of the Golden Horde, the lands there are in disarray, and that provides a great opportunity to seize land and especially seize the ports on the Black Sea, which will give you control over all of the trade in the Black Sea, oh great Duke."

After hearing these words, Balc turns his gaze towards the merchant with interest, but then the general interrupts the merchant's mumbling about riches and trade and points something important out.

"My Duke, please take a closer look further east and you will see that Muscovy has consolidated some of the breakaway chiefs of the Golden Horde and established outposts there. Therefore, it seems that they lay claim to these lands, and if we intervene in this region, they won't be happy with our actions..."
...

The merchant immediately interrupts, and Balc's gaze darts between him and the general.

"Muscovy won't pose a threat to you, oh great Duke. They are far away from the Black Sea and its important ports, so I say you should seize the opportunity while there is still a chance."

As the general opens his mouth, a scout from the east arrives in a hurry, almost storming into the room. All gazes turn towards him, to the point that he almost forgets to greet the Duke and his guests.

"My Duke, honored guests..." the scout bows hastily, struggling to catch his breath. "Forgive me, but there is a situation in the east. Kyiv, out of nowhere and without any claims to the lands, has stormed in and subjugated the Tatars of the Steppes, with the new extent of their lands stretching towards the Black Sea."

Two different expressions appear: the general smug and the merchant horrified.

The general speaks first.

"It is settled then. With Kyiv now in the Steppes and having expanded to the shores of the Black Sea, the ports have probably fallen into their hands as well, and even if we decided to contest them, we..."

Then the merchant exclaims:

"How far! How far has Kyiv expanded!"

He hears that Kyiv has only reached the shores of the Black Sea and that the peninsula of Crimea is still under Horde chiefs and Genoese traders. He exhales in relief and proposes:

"We don't care for the northern shores of the Black Sea on the mainland. The ports of Crimea are where the real gold lies..."

The general wants to interrupt, but then Balc frowns and shouts:

"Enough!"

With the dagger that was resting on the table, Balc suddenly stabs it into the map... through Crimea.
Then, in a commanding tone, he says:

"Send scribes to me. Then send an envoy to the Pope in Lyon. Now!"

Balc then steps out of the room.

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet: Håkan Sjöblad is remembered as the architect behind the sinking of the Hanseatic fleet, though such a title may be an exaggeration. No grand strategy
events.fullText:
Håkan Sjöblad is remembered as the architect behind the sinking of the Hanseatic fleet, though such a title may be an exaggeration. No grand strategy decided the outcome of the Battle of Blekinge Kust. Victory belonged as much to audacity as to planning. Long before the battle, Sjöblad had earned a reputation as one of the Brotherhoods fiercest and most gregarious Kapteins. In nearly every great venture it was Kaptein Klaas Störtebeker who commanded the fleets, yet at the last moment Sjöblad was entrusted with the campaign.

Known for his trusting nature, he had often been deceived, but none who betrayed him escaped execution in the freezing sea. In battle, however, he was the very image of fearless aggression. Always the first to charge, he fought alongside his men, inspiring them by example while relentlessly pursuing the complete and utter destruction of the enemy. It was precisely this temperament that made him the ideal commander against the cautious, defensive tactics of the Hanseatic merchants.

The stories tell that Sjöblad took command of one of the Brotherhoods three largest and newest ships and drove it directly into the enemy line. Grappling hooks flew, boarding ladders were raised, and what had begun as a naval battle soon became a sprawling melee. By the battles end, nearly ten ships lay bound together by ropes and shattered masts, transforming the open sea into a single floating battlefield.

The Brotherhood emerged victorious, but the triumph came at a price. Sjöblads flagship disappeared beneath the waves, dragged down alongside the sinking Hanseatic vessels it had helped destroy, of his original crew of 100 only 17 remain. Stories spread about Håkan Sjöblad, sailors drink in his honor across Gotland, lending him newfound influence amongst the ranks of his peers, as a result of his achievement he has become a central figure in the politics of the Brotherhood.
