events.snippet:
**The Power of the Purse**


Tense negotiations saw the Kingdom of Aragon bend to Carthaginian demands on the matter of Sardinian silver.  For the mom
events.fullText:
**The Power of the Purse**


Tense negotiations saw the Kingdom of Aragon bend to Carthaginian demands on the matter of Sardinian silver.  For the moment the silver remained under the control of Carthaginian merchants, however the influence of the successor to the ruined Papal States was sure to grow.  Unless more drastic measures were undertaken to secure the resources on Sardinia, the problem would only fester.

What’s more, the Burghers knew precisely the power and influence they held.  During the Carthaginian Civil War it was their influence that kept every major urban center squarely under the crown’s control, and it was their coin which fed the realm’s coffers.  They had been party to the construction of a massive fleet, the mightiest in the world, and now they clamored for its use.

This was not a call that the Sultan could restrain indefinitely…nor was he inclined to.

||Based on rolls, Sultan Bahir Hafsid is swayed by the Burghers to favor military intervention in Sardinia.||

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**Sicilian Clergy Celebrate Rome’s Defeat**


The defeat of the Roman Apostate by the French Catholics was received as cause for celebration in Sicily
events.fullText:
**Sicilian Clergy Celebrate Rome’s Defeat**


The defeat of the Roman Apostate by the French Catholics was received as cause for celebration in Sicily.  For too long had the decrees of mainland despots sought to steer the fates of those on the island, but no longer!  With the Pope returned to France, perhaps finally Sicily could be free of the Papist machinations which had plagued it for over a century.  At least, that is what was hoped.

## By Decree of the Archbishop, the expulsion of the Habsburgs

A herald makes his way to the Cathedral square within Trier. Taking a stand at the pod
events.fullText:
## By Decree of the Archbishop, the expulsion of the Habsburgs

A herald makes his way to the Cathedral square within Trier. Taking a stand at the podium, flanked by Noble guards of French origin he makes the following proclamation.

*By decree of the Archbishop, Prince-Elector of the Holy Roman Empire, Monseigneur Tinti d'Alsce

Prince-Bishop Leopold von Habsburg in death is condemned a heretic as he made no confession nor paid no indulgences for his four children born to his mistress. 

In light of this heresy his son Franz Frederich von Habsburg is exiled from the Bishopric of Trier and all its diocese. Nor shall Franz Frederich be permitted to hold ecclesiastical office in Trier nor its Diocese.

Nor shall Leopolds nephew Matthias Habsburg be permitted to hold ecclesiastical office in Trier nor shall he hold one within Triers Diocese. Due to the offenses of Mathias blood relation he is also exiled from Trier.

Archbishop Tinti reminds the populace of the land that all Sins may be forgiven via confession and by the purchase of indulgences*

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# A Harvest of Ruin - The Red Succession #
## The Dawn of the Hungarian Crisis ##

> "And I looked, and behold, a white horse! And its rider had a bow
events.fullText:
# A Harvest of Ruin - The Red Succession #
## The Dawn of the Hungarian Crisis ##

> "And I looked, and behold, a white horse! And its rider had a bow, and a crown was given to him, and he came out conquering, and to conquer."
> Revelation 6:2 

Since the ascension of King Charles I of Hungary in 1308, the Capetian house of Anjou has ruled the Hungarian realm uninterrupted for nearly a hundred years, with the royal title being peacefully passed down through the Angevin kings of Hungary. However, the actions and events during the reign of Charles II de Anjou, third Angevin monarch to sit upon the Hungarian, have sown the seeds of a bloody harvest to come. When the fateful arrow pierced the heart of Crown Prince Robert de Anjou and he breathed his last, the seeds of what was to come had been sown. Those seeds had been watered and nurtured by the tears of the grieving king, who, in his attempts to have his children inherit Hungary, uprooted the Salic laws of succession and proclaimed his second daughter, Princess Johanna, heir to Hungary. Now as many sprouts have appeared as there are claimants to the lands of Hungary. With each day, the old king grows older, and more so the harvest grows. One can only surmise may be the beneficiaries of this conflict, or who may triumph over when the dust has settled. One fact is certain. When the old King is embraced by the Almighty, a bloody harvest will be reaped, and a Red Succession shall engulf the land.

> “And there went out another horse that was red: and power was given unto him that sat thereon to take peace from the earth, and that they should kill one another: and there was given unto him a great sword." 
> Revelation 6:4 

<@1432820281000333392> <@190640828954181632> <@261290667106369536> <@252560004069064705> <@1123821809704374313> <@460814348487753730>

-# This is the post marking the beginning of the Hungarian Succession Crisis that started with the death of King Charles II's only son, Prince Robert, the uprooting of Salic Law in Hungary, and the proclamation of his second daughter Princess Johanna as heir. A list of claimants and factions will be listed…

-# (Note: The first daughter is being excluded for now as she is missing in Southern France and is presumed dead as well [she is living in secret - more RP on that to come ;).] However, the uncertainty makes the succession even more questionable.)

## The Johannians ##
This faction centers around Princess Johanna, second daughter and designated heiress to the Hungarians throne. Proclaimed as heir by her father regardless of Salic laws of succession, she is regardless the most legitimate candidate for being the Princess of Hungary and eldest child of the King. Married to the brother of the Duke of Normandy and scion of the royal Capetian house of Valois, Princess Johanna may have powerful supporters for the throne. She has currently sired three children, including a son Jean de Valois.

<@460814348487753730>

## The Burgundians ##
This faction centers around Princess Catherine, third and youngest daughter of the King. At the time of the proclamation, she was second in line for the throne. Now, with Johanna having sired children, she has fallen in the succession, and with it has lost much power and influence at court. Married to the heir of the Duchy of Burgundy and scion of the royal Capetian house of Burgundy, Princess Catherine also may have powerful backers for the throne. She has currently sired two children, including a son Hugues de Burgundy.

<@1123821809704374313>

## The Robertines ##
This faction centers around King Robert I de Anjou, younger brother of the King and King of Poland. At the time of the proclamation, he was the presumptive heir to the throne, before his brother effectively disinherited him by overruling Salic Law in Hungary for his daughter. Robert is however in a similar predicament to his brother, having also sired no male heirs, with his only daughter having sired children of the House of Savoy. As King of Poland and many other eastern territories, Robert I is a powerful force and could push his claim to the Hungarian throne should he wish.

<@252560004069064705>

## The Ruthenians ##
This faction centers around Prince Robert de Anjou, a distant cousin of King Charles II, his wife Grand Princess Eufemia Piast, ruler of the Principality of Halych-Volynia, and their son Louis de Anjou. In regards to the de Anjou, the Ruthenian branch is the last surviving branch of the house with a continued future. No other branch except the Ruthenian branch has been able to a produce male scion of the house. Prince Louis stands to inherit the lands of Halych and Volynia, a powerful realm itself.

<@1432820281000333392>

-# (If interested in pushing a claim and participating, please 👍 your @ message.)

events.author: Dods
events.snippet:
# The Three Courts

With the rise of Arch-Bishop Leopold von Habsburg, the dismal situation of the administration in the Prince-Bishopric is showing.
events.fullText:
# The Three Courts

With the rise of Arch-Bishop Leopold von Habsburg, the dismal situation of the administration in the Prince-Bishopric is showing. With the main court subject to constant episodes of mediation between the City Council and the empowered low Clergy, the failings of Andreas laying the foundation for divided lands, with little to no progress made of consolidating the importance of the court of the Prince-Bishop.

To make matters worse the counts of Westfalen are less privy to the scrutiny the nobility in the heart of the Prince-Bishopric are held to, wriggling a divide that has seen audacious moves such as the house of Soest revolting in the wake of the war of the Golden Bull League. This has driven a division between the western and eastern houses, sparking calls for the eastern counts to be governed stricter or for the nobility of the west be given more liberties. To the dismay of Leopold there's very little governance that could be installed with the constant dependency of free travel through Berg's domain. Two options present themselves, either secure land in southern Berg someway or concede new privileges to the local nobility. Bar these top options, we risk seeing the nobility uprising against us and see themselves ruling.

On top of this situation, the city council pushes their demands further for more autonomy, with the merchant class reigning stronger than the Prince-Bishop within the walls of Koln, with such growth of power many legislative decisions are beholden to the city council and the influence of the Hansa, this matter though we have little ability to pressure the city to relinquish such rights with the vast amounts of wealth flowing along the Rhine. The worst case scenario would be the city appealing for further independence with the emperor and neighbouring states, for now all we can do is placate the city with investments and good will.

With such looming disasters, Leopold makes preparations for the court of the Prince-Bishopic to move to Bonn, the modest town serves far better as a hub for the Arch-Bishops rulership without need of arranging access to the city, lending the influence of a holy man far more weight that in mercantile walls. These preparations while disruptive pushes the scales further in favour to the Arch-Bishop.

As the Brandenburg Crisis grows day by day, the new courts bicker over taking the Bergische Land, but
events.fullText:
**Immanuel Von Limbourg's Rock in a Hard Place**
As the Brandenburg Crisis grows day by day, the new courts bicker over taking the Bergische Land, but it has been rejected at every Landtag because it is core territory for the Duchy of Berg. If this is to escalate, all forces will be redrawn to combat the warmongering of the noble elite of Cologne. Immanuel will not take the aggression lightly and will also call on Holland, the Netherlands, to either join Berg or stay neutral. Immanuel will not, however,, attack first; it will only defend itself against Cologne and its aggression.

With the new Hansyhorod founded in Halych, droves of german traders are establishing themselves there. Among them are some heretics, who perhaps wrongly believe they will find religious protection in the town. And though they may find more tolerance, there are some heretic thoughts both orthodox and catholics condemn equally. There are also talks among Jewish people near Halych to establish themselves in Hansyhorod. A town dually governed by a King and a trade league, with some religious tolerance, is certainly better than the situation currently experienced by many jews. 

However, they may find a surprise, for the Wendish people are not the best neighbours of Jewish people… <@1432820281000333392>

events.fullText:
### Broken Faith - Camweddau

“They said we were fine, eh.” *The older man scoffs, leaning on his cane.*

“And now what? we haven’t done anything…” *the man’s son waveringly asked as he looked at the Norman Castle in the distance.*

“They’re taking it all!” *as he shook his head in what was probably best described as disappointment mixed with regret.*

> In accordance with her Majesty, Queen Eleanor of the House Plantagenet, lawful dictates - via her Marcher lords, the following feudal dependencies of the Principality of Wales:
> - Pembroke, Narberth, De Llawhaden, Haverford, and Walwyn’s Castle,
> 
> Are to be **cleared** of any person or persons incapable of speaking the French or English tongues forwarded by her majesty’s government. The environs of the holy St.Davids Cathedral shall remain open, but remain under guard until such decision as can be made by her representatives to secure its continued safety.
> 
> Peoples living in these areas incapable of fulfilling such lawful requirements must appeal to their local lord or to the Justiciar’s household in Carnarvon for special exception. Men at arms and sworn knights of the Principality of Wales shall enforce these measures if not followed through voluntarily by offending individuals.

The old man could not understand the pronouncement, for naturally it was posted in English and French at the village. However his typically calm manner was visibly disrupted when its contents were read out to him at the Cantref’s Uchelwr. Turning to his son he said the simple damning line:

“Gwell gennym pe bawn i wedi dy adael i ymuno â Glyndŵr.”

*In unrelated news, a number of prime and proven farming and grazing lands have opened up in Pembrokeshire for any willing to make the journey to Wales. Justiciar Jean Cardigan’s efforts have focused on Dutch <@420614608399761419>, Flemish <@99704182809853952>, Norman <@460814348487753730>, and of course English settlement <@1106651545296195655> to the already heavily colonized area. ‘Little England beyond Wales’ will prosper despite its location, Godwilling.*

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## *Quies ante Ignem*

The lands of Saint Václav stand a pile of dry kindling, awaiting a spark to be set alight. The roads which carried the realm to
events.fullText:
## *Quies ante Ignem*

The lands of Saint Václav stand a pile of dry kindling, awaiting a spark to be set alight. The roads which carried the realm to this point are old, their origins stretching centuries back in time, yet their travelers have changed many faces. 

Praha, first undertaken beneath development projects under Karel I/IV after the long absences and neglect of his father, and further contributed to by the reigns of Otto *der Fröhliche* and Kaiser Johann, stands as an opulent city, abundant in wealth and luxury. It is the imperial capital, the seat of power for the Roman Empire, only recently challenged by the nascent bounty of Wien. The splendor of Praha is a burden which it strains against, the majesty of a capital for an empire it will soon no longer rule, one built upon a past it tried, in some vein, to suffocate. To some, it stands as a capital squandered by occupiers, a colonization project in-process. 

If one walks the streets of Praha they will hear many languages, but among them they will hear certainly three. German dominates the counting houses and university gardens, Latin springs from the halls of learning and the St. Vitus Cathedral, energetic in debate of the wider schism abroad and at home, while Czech is heard in the streets, in the markets, in the Old Town, and certainly, most infamously, in the [Bethlehem Chapel](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1515810732955144243). German patricians sit in their manors, their wealth braided into the silver of Kutná Hora and the looms of Flanders, while the Czech commonfolk and nobles parse out words of resentment and jealousy with an anger that had not yet fully found its voice. It will, in time.

At the University of Praha, the faculty dominantly arise from the German nations, while some 20% of the students are Czech. Of the founding Four Nations, Bohemian, Bavarian, Polish, and Saxon, the Germans dominated therein as well. It is where **Jeroným Pražský** copied out the works of **John Wycliffe**, *Dialogus* and *Trialogus*. It is where **Jan Hus** translated *Trialogus* into Czech, and where in 1403 the University endeavored to clamp down on Wycliffe’s teachings after Hus defended them, and failed. It is an institution where once more the differences between German and Czech became paramount, with the collusion of German faculty and German clergy against the increasingly popular stances of Wycliffe pitted them mostly against Czech natives. The sides, naturally, were not so iron-bound: but the perception, nonetheless, entrenched amidst a wider disaffection over the German dominion over the University and its Czech students, who like in other walks of life, saw Germans seize the opportunities they felt were owed to them. And while that struggle fed into the omnipresent debates over the ideas of Wycliffe, Jeroným, and other reformers as well as to the state of the Church, those very debates were, like the Bible’s teachings, being orated just minutes away in Czech vernacular to the laity by the dean of the University’s philosophy faculty, **Jan Hus**.

The urban and academic landscape of Praha, like many of the other notable Bohemian towns, was ethnically charged in a manner worsened by the wider disarray of Christendom. It was not simply Two Popes and the divided Church which was observed, but the over-representation of the German clergy within the Church in Bohemia, whose ecclesiastical persons held a significant share of benefices, lorded over the monastic houses, and held their living from local revenues while themselves being either largely absent from their seats, or poorly versed in Czech and so remained foreign, alien to the communities they served. Similarly, the German patricians had advantaged access to the royal court, and a noticeable political ascendancy over the Kaiser, who had built up his reign through the same royal dynamic of colonization: inviting Germans in to forge a new power holding class, to weaken the grip of the local gentry, who in turn endeavored to cling to the rights of the elective monarchy. They dominated town councils, occupied seats at the head of many guilds, and leveraged their influence over the mining towns to exert considerable sway over the Kaiser, all-the-while they were a known and recognized tool firstly against the local classes on behalf of a monarchy that was, even now, still foreign. It is no wonder then, that underneath the University of Praha, the kingdom’s flagship institution for education, that the Four Nation structure and its involved German dominance in votes, which cascaded into their disproportionate influence on curriculum, material, and stance, quickly was giving rise to harsher experience of the Czech student body in being structurally subjugated within their own home, same as they witnessed with the other German settlement efforts.

The more that reform is clamped down upon by the Church in Bohemia, subject to its master, the German Archbishop of Mainz, and to his “agents” the absentee German clergy within, the more it seems that clerical reform in Bohemia grows synonymous with the assertion of the Czech identity against foreign institutions who deign to subjugate them. This process had already begun crystallizing when the Archbishop of Mainz called for the Bishop of Prague to be replaced from his seat for refusing orders to turn against the Kaiser, sent on the whim of a German league against him. The mythology of the Bohemian nation was weaponized by the most skilled of these vernacular orators, including Hus himself, who preached of varying severities the foreignness and invasion of the Germans, emerging at the critical hour to take the question of reform into greater politician dimension than it had been before.

Many have walked these paths before, and the road which Bohemia graced with its gait now, knew such figures before men like Hus. Conrad of Waldhäuser, Tomáš Štítný, Milíc z Kroměříže, Matéj z Janova, and more laid the path before him and his compatriots. Movements for church reform which began in conciliarist tradition, endeavoring for the institution to fix itself by way of the cardinals and bishops, gave ground to perspectives holding those same cardinals and bishops as corrupt themselves and evidence of the wider dereliction in duty which the Church buckled beneath. As the Western Schism developed, the corruption of the Church only grew, becoming more public as it grew inadequate in its armament to treat arising heresies which it gave cradle to. And as the age of relic accumulation drew to its epoch, and indulgences became ever more universal as a basis of income for the already loftily-rich Church, the spectacles of such fiscal decadence and wealth siphoning coincided with the ethnic pains of a nation under colonization, seen nowhere more directly than the affluent German entrenchment over the University of Praha, where the aspiring and educated Czech found little opportunity to serve within, where their views were repressed by the decision of semi-absentee and foreign superiors.

Nothing, which so presently crushed down upon Bohemia, was taken by the sword. An occupation backed by no invasion, engineered by math and royal assent for centuries. Everything was given, invited, chartered, blessed, and given permanence to by kings, dead and alive, who perhaps knew the folly of their choices, and perhaps not. Nothing was taken by the sword, but it shall be reclaimed by it. 

<@763652399784919080> <@288410456526422016> <@255439449448644608> <@423689694258397195> <@252560004069064705> <@306458253674545153> <@460814348487753730> <@112960565344804864> <@137101075411894272>

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
**HANSETAG** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 
Walbeck’s first presiding Hansetag has been called. It is gloomier; unlike his successor, he has failur
events.fullText:
**HANSETAG** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 
Walbeck’s first presiding Hansetag has been called. It is gloomier; unlike his successor, he has failures to show: the sinking of the hanseatic fleet, which has to be rebuilt and made larger, at least to double its previous number, reaching 40 or so ships. However, not all are bad news, for thanks to several good investments a South Germanic town in Austria has joined the League, as well as several towns in central Germany.

The profitability of the League is no longer a concern, given that the money flowing to the coffers of Hamburg equals to the money flowing to the Holy Roman Emperor, which is an achievement in itself. The most profitable markets are hard to expand on, and investments have become stale for a while. The only venue Walbeck sees is in exerting political power now (though not in the Baltic, given the recent defeat… <@326414282851876875>), expanding Hanseatic membership southwards and westwards through bribes and wielding the *Dispensatio*.

A more pressing concern, voiced by many cities, is the current situation of the Church. Some Burgermeister already spouse ideas incompatible with usual catholic doctrine. Though the Hansetag ought to be for trade affairs, heresies mean some refuse to trade with others on that alone. These are organized, structured breaks from the Pope, not your run of the mill religious differences.

This is in some ways not new, for communities in kontors sometimes partially convert, or mix their rites with local ones. But the siege of Rome and the temporal dominance of France over the Church have brought this to light in the Diet.

Buda appears upon the horizon as banners bearing the bison of Moldavia and the raven of Wallachia beneath the colors of
events.fullText:
**Bison, Raven and a Crown**

Buda appears upon the horizon as banners bearing the bison of Moldavia and the raven of Wallachia beneath the colors of House Basarab approach the great city upon the banks of the Danube. Even with Buda now visible, Balc's entourage would still require a full day of travel before reaching its walls and, beyond them, the King and the Crown to whom Balc was to pledge his loyalty as Duke of Moldavia.

The entourage moved at dawn with the first rays of the sun, travelling along the Danube through wilderness and villages, through forests and fields. Marching upstream along the road that followed the river's course, it was a peaceful journey. All around them, life continued as it always had. It cared little for what happened in the wider world, what wars were fought, who died, and who married. Animals roamed freely, golden fields of grain stretched across the plains, and peasants labored beneath the sun. Yet as life continued, so too did the march towards Buda, its walls drawing nearer with every step of Balc's merry band.

Through the city gates, Balc set his first step into the city of Buda. It was marvelous. Cobbled streets and stone houses lined the view in every direction, and people packed the streets, with merchants and artisans calling from their stalls in languages unbeknownst to Balc. The entourage itself was not a large one, so it passed through the crowd without much difficulty, though people still gave way to Balc and his company at the sight of noble banners, even if they were unknown to them. Balc wandered around the city, admiring it until dusk fell. On the morrow, he would stand before the King.
...

With the rising of the sun came the beginning of a new day and a new dawn, and with it Balc set out for his audience with the King. Arriving at the court, the doors opened and Balc saw the King waiting. When the doors finally opened fully, an announcement was heard throughout the halls of the court that Balc of House Basarab had come to Buda to pledge his allegiance to the King as the new Duke of Moldavia and vassal of the Crown. Balc knelt on one knee and spoke:

“Greetings, Charles II, King of Hungary and Croatia. I have come to pledge my allegiance and loyalty to you and the Crown here as Duke of Moldavia. I am sorry for being late with my pledge, Your Grace. Many different matters weighed upon me back home, matters which I had to attend to before I could leave Targoviste in peace. Also, on matters of Moldavia which I wish to speak of, both realms of Wallachia and Moldavia drift closer with each passing day, and I trust that the growing closeness of the two realms will not be seen as contrary to the interests of the Hungarian Crown.”

Duke Balc stood up and looked towards the King, still holding a respectful posture towards Charles, but now the dynamic in Balc’s speech shifted as one ruler spoke to another:

“Before I leave Buda, I wish to speak on matters concerning Wallachia's position in Balkan politics, as it seems that there was an agreement with the Roman Emperor to divide the peninsula, and I would like to know where Wallachia stands within this agreement. I would also like to bring news from Wallachia that the last unfulfilled part of the treaty that our fathers signed is being fulfilled, and Wallachia is finally being converted de facto rather than merely de jure Catholic.”
...

Balc shifted slightly into what looked like a slight bow and, in a softer and more requesting tone, continued:

“Lastly, I would like to request your support for Wallachian endeavours in the east, in the steppes. We wish to bring peace and enlightenment to those chaotic lands. So that these lands may not pose a threat in the future, but rather become bright opportunities and a buffer from the hordes roaming deeper in the steppes for our realms. I know this is a bold endeavour and request, and if you cannot provide support for it, I would only request at least a guarantee for Wallachia to freely enact its eastern ambitions... especially from outside interference.”
With Balc finally finished speaking, he stood before the King in silence, waiting for his majesty’s response.

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**Golden Liberty**


The struggle between the Polish Crown and the self-proclaimed [Lublin Confederation](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312
events.fullText:
**Golden Liberty**


The struggle between the Polish Crown and the self-proclaimed [Lublin Confederation](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1515735207590756415) would reach its climax in 1387 when King Robert called a *sejm* in the city of Poznan. The *sejm* included not only his own loyalists but also members of the Lublin Confederation, led by the Ostrzeszówski family. 

The death of the royal chancellor, Stephen of Hungary, weakened Robert hugely - his uncle had been the architect of the Angevin regime and his death robbed the young king of counsel and influence. For King Robert securing the Angevin line was essential - but that his young daughter Johanna was both not of age and a women weakened his position. 

Thus it was at Poznan when the Lublin Confederation the King came to terms. Johanna's succession as King in Poland would be secured but the szlachta would receive great privileges including but not limited to 

-*Wolna elekcja*, the right of nobles to determine the succession 
-*Liberum veto*, the right of all nobles to veto legislation by shouting *Sisto activitatem!* in session
-*Konfederacja*, the right to form a common organisation to achieve a political aim 

Together these three privileges compromise *Aurea Libertas* or the "Golden Liberty" of the Polish Royal Crown. With this, the szlachta formally recognised Johanna as heir and cheered at the liberty of Poland. 

The royal house however could not hide the fact - this was a defeat for royal power and a victory for the szlachta in no uncertain terms. After the consolidation under the Piests, Angevin rule had only achieved the ebbing of royal authority

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**Baptism of Lithuania**


The conquest of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania on Papal instruction upended decades of Polish-Lithuanian reconciliation. In t
events.fullText:
**Baptism of Lithuania**


The conquest of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania on Papal instruction upended decades of Polish-Lithuanian reconciliation. In the early years of the conquest the local Lithuanian nobility was marginalised with Polish magistrates running affairs. Zealous priests were upended from Poland proper to oversee conversion which was often violent. 

These methods were unpopular and invited dissent from the Lithuanian population leading to conversion to grind to a halt. Over time however these methods were abandoned in favour of the integration of Lithuania into the Polish Crown. Members of the Lithuanian nobility, most notably the House of Radvila (Radziwiłł) that would be granted vast tracts of Lithuanian land particularly around Vilnius. 

As the Polish Inquisition focused on the pagan populace in Lithuania proper a *modus operandi* was established between the Catholic Church and Orthodox Church in Byelorussia. For the numerous orthodox duchies under their control the Catholic church has left them to their own devices, with the stated aim to push towards the reunification and reconciliation of the churches to heal Christendom. 

Thus in Lithuania the sword has been replaced with sermons in the baptism, a slow process that is bearing fruit. As the Lithuanian nobility however undergo Polonisation there is a feeling that the sweeping liberties the nobility hold in Poland proper should be reflected in Lithuania as well - and that the Grand Duchy is itself a legal personality apart from Poland.

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**Urbanisation of Poland**


Vast agricultural lands are the mainstay of the Polish Crown and remain so. But in the past half-century there has the de
events.fullText:
**Urbanisation of Poland**


Vast agricultural lands are the mainstay of the Polish Crown and remain so. But in the past half-century there has the development of a new trend - the rapid growth of the royal capital of Krakow and its surrounding environs. 

The wealth of Krakow has a main source - the vast salt mines that surround it. The *Żupy krakowskie*, created in 1290, operates the Wieliczka Salt Mine in Wieliczka and Bochnia Salt Mine in Bochnia that provide a huge share of revenue for the crown. Tonnes of salt are shipped into Germania and Hungary every year becoming the backbone of the Polish economy. 

This has led to a great degree of urbanisation in Krakow. Spinners guilds, tailors, scriptoriums, weapon workshops and more have popped up as royal charters are given to merchants to set up show. At 1400 Krakow stood at over seventy-thousand people, one of the largest cities east of Germania. 

The growth of Krakow has been aided by migration of various groups. Germans are the most numerous but Christianised Lithuanians coming the grand duchy, Ruthenians from  Byelorussia and Jews fleeing [pogroms from Germania](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1515807434055090316) also make up a large bulk. Germans and Jews are most prized as bringing ideas from Francia and Italia to the kingdom alongside new sources of credit and bourgeois lifestyles whilst Lithuanians and Ruthenians are sent to work in the salt mines.

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**The Warsaw Plot **

The results of the Poznan *sejm* was a personal blow to King Robert. The Anjou succession was secured but at great political cos
events.fullText:
**The Warsaw Plot **

The results of the Poznan *sejm* was a personal blow to King Robert. The Anjou succession was secured but at great political cost. Since then he has been tied by the *sejm*, unable to raise armies without their consent and forced to give them numerous concessions. 

Thus, the king went about expanding what remaining power he had. The first duty was to continue the Angevin line for his daughter. As part of a tilt towards the rising power of France his daugther Johanna married the Duke of Aosta John II, a member of the House of Savoy and a subject to the King of France. This was to the dismay of the Polish Catholic Church, who had secured a seat in the College of Cardinals to the Archbishop of Krakow Piotr Wysz Radolinski who had affirmed his support for the Roman Curia. 

The king has developed a reputation for paranoia and cruelty. In 1399 a supposed plot was uncovered to enthrone King Robert's cousin, Janislaw Piast, in his place by members of the szlachta. When a plotter revealed the conspiracy after a night of debauchery royal spies informed the king, who had the plotters rounded up and executed after a public trial. Janislaw Piast in particular was given the most severe and humiliating execution, that by impalement. 

Since the so-called "Warsaw Plot" the king is suspicious and cautious, but liable to be wrathful if he is feel he is wronged...

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Crown Princess & The Fate of Rome.

Catherine de Lusignan wasn't destined to rule when she was born in October 1376. The second child of King Pe
events.fullText:
# The Crown Princess & The Fate of Rome.

Catherine de Lusignan wasn't destined to rule when she was born in October 1376. The second child of King Peter I and Queen Isabelle de Valois, she was the first to survive to adulthood, but her father never paid much attention to her. He was still sure that God would grand him a son to succeed him. Naturally then, Peter I didn't bother giving Catherine a proper education on how to rule a Kingdom, and it even seemed at first that he would be proven right in this course of action. For, in 1389 the Queen gave birth to his much anticipated son. The young Prince John soon took over all the spotlight in the royal court, receiving the fine education expected of royal heirs, and Catherine was destined to share the fate of the majority of Princesses before her, simply to be married off for political purposes. Yet, God seemed to have another fate in mind for her, as when John was just five years old, he caught a rather fatal illness that would take his life over a period of just a week.

Catherine's father was devastated, but life moves on, and so does the reality of the royal succession. Within a year, King Peter I proclaimed Princess Catherine as his heir-apparent, and then began to seek out potential suitors for her hand in marriage. Eventually such a match was found, with the young Catherine being wed in the Cathedral of Saint Sophia, Nicosie in 1397 to Prince Fadrique de Trastámara of Castile <@422498641425596418> . Throughout the whole ordeal from the death of Prince John in 1394 to her marriage in 1397, Princess Catherine had fulfilled her obligations dutifully and attempted to learn the complexities of ruling a feudal administration. Her lack of a proper education hindered her in this task, but all in the royal court could respect her for trying. If nothing else, she was taught as a young girl the proper courtesy's expected of a royal Princess.

This meant her social skills and ability to charm individuals was exceptional, even if she was lacking in other areas needed for one to be a good ruler. Fortunately for the Princess Catherine, her marriage meant that she need not rule Cyprus alone. For not only would the nobility aid her as long as she made no attempts to hinder their power, but her husband, the likely future King of Castile, Leon, and Portugal, would also be able to aid her Kingdoms if they ever came into danger. 

She was thankful for these years that Fadrique spent in Cyprus. For inevitably the Prince's father would die and then Fadrique would be forced to return to Iberia. Catherine would naturally follow him, but that would mean going to a land she had never seen before, a potentially unwelcoming place for a foreign Princess. Whilst her husband was in Cyprus however, she was able to grow used to his presence in an environment familiar to her. Over the 7 years Fadrique had spent thusfar in Cyprus from 1397-1404, their marriage was able to be solidified and an understanding between the Crown royals reached. Catherine even went through the effort of learning Spanish for her eventual trip to Iberia. There was hardly ever love between arranged matches like these, but both Catherine and Fadrique fulfilled their duties to each other faithfully, and from their marriage in 1397 to now in 1404 they have had two children together, with the Crown Princess currently heavy with child oncemore. By all accounts it has been a successful marriage between the two future monarchs, a match which is destined to unite six Kingdoms under one of their children.

It is doubtful that Fadrique has much love for Cyprus, however. For it is a land ruled by arrogant Frankish knights and nobles who have the pride of men who believe they are working in the interests of God in this most holy land. Nevertheless, the Crown Prince has been able to benefit himself and his father's Kingdoms here.

Just a year earlier, he was able to leverage King Peter I to gift his father, King Alfonso XII, much wealth from the royal treasury to aid in the efforts against the Kingdom of France, which was attacking into Italy against the Roman Papacy. Unfortunately for the Lusignan Kingdoms old reliable patron in Rome, the city fell to the French King's large armies. With the Roman Church in crisis and unable to influence such events in Europe, Peter I was left unsure of how to proceed. As always he hoped that Christendom might shake itself out of its stupor and take up the cross oncemore to crusade against the Saracens, but now all was chaos and uncertainty.

The Kingdom of France, once the most fervent of crusader realms, filled with a pious and brave people, led by the great Saint King Louis IX, now marched all its forces against Rome. It seemed to King Peter I that since King Phillip VI's cancellation of his promised crusade in 1336, the Crown of France had forgotten more and more about the sacred lands and the French spirit of holy war. Perhaps that is why he all but gifts his Kingdoms to the Castillian Crown instead. With the Lyon Papacy now supreme, King Peter I and the exiled Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem both would write their own kindly letters to the Pope <@460814348487753730> which they had not recognized for the great majority of the Western Schism. The contents of each letter was almost the same, both offering their submission to his authority, and trying in their own ways to win the Lyon Pope's sympathies for being "led astray" from the true Church by the false Roman Pope. And, like in every other letter to the west, the letters both eventually requested Christendom's aid against the Saracen menace in the east. In truth it wasn't all that different from any letter either the Lusignan Monarch or the Patriarch of Jerusalem had sent to the Roman Pope before the French marched into Italy to finally end the schism.

Of course, King Peter I held out no hopes that this latest letter would garner any attention, much less an entire crusade. All hope he had of that died out when he abandoned his planned tour of Europe many years before. Still, the Catholic east could not spare being on bad relations with the Pope, even if it wasn't the Pope that Peter I had initially supported...

-# The Wedding of Princess Catherine de Lusignan and Prince Fadrique de Trastámara

**(End)**

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Lost Daughter #

To the Hungarian court in Buda and the rest of Europe, the whereabouts of the first daughter of King Charles II and royal princ
events.fullText:
# The Lost Daughter #

To the Hungarian court in Buda and the rest of Europe, the whereabouts of the first daughter of King Charles II and royal princess of Hungary, Helvig de Anjou, are an enigma and to many she is considered to be dead. Since her last known correspondence in the Armagnac court, no further communications have been heard from the Princess, and none in either courts of Hungary or Armagnac have known of her whereabouts other than she had departed from Tarbes for Buda. For all intensive purposes, she has become lost in southern France. To all, though, she is another tragic and unfortunate loss for the Capetian house of Anjou in Hungary, a house that is seemingly cursed by God for past sins.

However, unknown all is that the Princess is alive and well, living a simple life in the County of Comminges with her beloved husband, a member of the minor de Mizemen family, and her two daughters. Although she would be first in line to the Hungarian throne, she has no interest in pursuing the claim and wishes only to live out her life with her family with her beloved that had helped to facilitate her  escape from her oppressive and intriguing family. Now she lives a peaceful existence. For now at least.

events.author: dani178i
events.snippet:
# Council of Wurzburg.

**Rome has fallen.**

This is public knowledge to all and it is well known, that with the fall of the gates to Rome and the de
events.fullText:
# Council of Wurzburg.

**Rome has fallen.**

This is public knowledge to all and it is well known, that with the fall of the gates to Rome and the death of many thousands of innocents that the great western schism at last drags to an end. 

But what is the end state? A pope wrapped in chains? The apostolic palace being left to rot and decay before at last being thrust into the hands of corrupt nobles? A single victorious pope in Lyon, hated by half the christian world?

And all the while the catholic world decays, heresies sprout up like weeds in the garden to the disgrace of all. 

This is not a proper conclusion. In a world beset by moral rot and weakening of faith, the church needs to be loved and accepted, not feared and loathed. Though CHRIST our LORD teaches us to forgive so, the average man often falls to sin and holds grudges. Peace must be struck and christendom must be united in the face of new threats. 

**SO LET THE WORD RING OUT!**

ALL YE BISHOPS AND MEN OF FAITH, COME TO WURZBURG TO CONGREGATE IN THE NEWEST ECUMENICAL COUNCIL, RAISED BY ARCHBISHOP AND PRIMAS GERMINAE ANJOU LÁSZLÓ, ON THE AUTHORITY VESTED UPON HIM BY POPE SÍXTUS V IN ABSENTIA.

events.author: Cayde
events.snippet:
# The Wiener Riots of 1404

*In the lingering light of a smoldering fire, an old man begins to tell a tale to the young Leopold von Habsburg.*

Some p
events.fullText:
# The Wiener Riots of 1404

*In the lingering light of a smoldering fire, an old man begins to tell a tale to the young Leopold von Habsburg.*

Some people think a university is a place where people study and pray. They have never been to Wien. The university in Wien is not like that. It was started in 1345 to teach people about the church and its laws..... Now it is a place where people talk about forbidden things. They talk about the ideas of Wycliffe and the Waldenses. These are people who do not agree with the church. The Archduke Siegmund von Habsburg has a collection of books and relics that the church does not like. He keeps them in a room under the university. The church could stop all of this. They do not. They are afraid of the Archduke. The students in Wien are not alone. They have formed groups, like families. They take care of each other. Each group has its house, its own saint and its own special cup for drinking. The Brotherhood of Saint Leopold is one of these groups. They follow the ways of the church and they are friends with the sons of important men from Austria. The Rhenish men are another group. They are from a place called Köln, I hear it has the best brews on the Rhine. More importantly they have new ideas about the church. They like to drink and talk about these ideas. Sometimes the two groups fight. They fight over women or money or bad beer, I too would fight over bad beer. There is no worse fate for a man than bad beer.

*The old man took a large swig from his tankard, before beckoning the server girl to fill him up another drink*

You'll pay for this one alright? Good. Back to the story. One day they fought over God. It started with beer. The Rhenish men did not like the beer they were given so they sent it back. The man who made the beer was angry. Said that the Rhenish men did not deserve good beer because they did not respect the church. One of the men got angry and hit the beer maker with a tankard. The beer makers friends came to help him. Soon everyone was fighting. The bells of the church started ringing. The bells of the university rang back. The whole city of Wien was in chaos. This fighting went on for three days. It was the people of the town against the students.. There was something more going on. People in Wien had been talking about how the university was a place where people did not respect the church. They said that the students were heretics. Then news came from Italy that the French king had taken the pope prisoner. This made everything worse. People did not know who to believe. So they fought. On the day the people of the town went to the university to burn the library and the Archdukes relics.. The Brotherhood of Saint Leopold stopped them. They did not want the library to be burned even though they did not agree with the ideas in the books. They wanted to protect the university and the Archdukes things. So they fought to keep the people of the town from burning everything.

When it was over many people were dead. Sixty students and thirty people from the town were killed. The university smelled like smoke for a month. Three brothers from the Rhenish house were hanged from a sign above their tavern. The Archduke Siegmund heard about what happened. He did not get angry. He just thought about it for a while. Then he went to the city. Told everyone what he thought. He said that the university was right and that the students should be protected. He told the church to leave the students alone.. He told the people of the town to forget about what happened. The Archduke said something too I think. He said that if you are afraid of something it can get worse. He did not want to make the students angry so he left them alone. The students buried their friends and drank to remember them. A new sign was put up above the tavern where the fighting started.. The city of Wien was quiet again but it was not really quiet. People were still angry. They did not trust each oth-

*And with the last remark said, the old man's head abruptly fell down to the table, with a thud, his tankard still in hand, emptied*

events.author: Hoang
events.snippet:
# The growth of the Assembly

Northern Italy is dotted with cities that serve as the engines of wealth and growth.  In Milan itself, the majority of t
events.fullText:
# The growth of the Assembly

Northern Italy is dotted with cities that serve as the engines of wealth and growth.  In Milan itself, the majority of taxes no longer come from the countryside alone, but from the cities: from the burgher class, the guildsmen, the merchants, and the workers who keep the cities alive.

The city of Parma, recognizing Milan’s growing authority, has agreed to join Milan and accept annexation under Visconti rule.

Barnada Visconti, wishing to appear both strong and just, has decided to expand the** Council of Justice** across all territories under Milanese control. Parma is one of the first cities brought into this new arrangement, serving as an example to other towns and communes that submit to Milan’s authority. This council will give the cities a formal place within Milan’s government, allowing local disputes, privileges, and petitions to be heard.

This will strengthen the loyalty of the cities at the cost of a more powerful Assembly that could check the authority of the Viscontis.

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## Tolling the Moselle

Long has it been discussed amongst the inner d'Alscae family about how to ~~coerce ~~bring the merchants of Trier to the marke
events.fullText:
## Tolling the Moselle

Long has it been discussed amongst the inner d'Alscae family about how to ~~coerce ~~bring the merchants of Trier to the markets of Metz. A market that would see the Trier merchants take their wares and their trade upstream via the Moselle, rather than downstream following the Rhine to the ancient market of Cologne. 
This aim is not born of a rivalry, Mainz does not own Cologne afterall. Nor is it entirely born of economic concerns, Trier and the lands of its people are not exactly rich. 
Instead as everything with rulers of ~~the late medieval age~~ this age, it was born of Pride and Honour. Metz lies as a jewel in the d'Alscae Ducal crown, Cologne does not.

But telling Merchants where to bring their wares, and where to do their trade is certainly easier said than done. 

The Metz Market was founded naught but 50 years ago, when the market was founded it was intended to cover the entire Moselle river. This was not to be the case, the Merchants of Trier clung to their old route to Cologne where they could deal with the Hansa and get important wares like silk, furs and beeswax that are not in abundance in Trier.

Tombo d'Alscae, the great steward of Lorraine had long attempted to bring the merchants of Trier to Metz.

His initial attempts to ~~coerce ~~ convince the Merchants of Trier to do their business in Metz by expanding the [Metz market facilities bore some success](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1514754938750898216). By 1386 the lands upto the Town of Trier were in the Metz market, but still Trier held out.

Between 1386 and the turn of the 15th Century, Tombo decreed that the merchants of Trier would have **Preferred Market Access** within the Metz market and while that convinced many hold outs, it did not convince them all.

But the final and unintended action that forced the last holdouts of Trier away from Cologne, where the Nobles that in recent times built **River Toll Castles** along the stretch of the Moselle from Trier to Coblenz. This development made it far more expensive for the Merchants to move their good down stream and to Cologne, as each and every step of the way they would be tolled by their very own nobility. And yet when these Merchants looked upstream from Trier they saw naught a **River Toll Castle** along the stretch of the Moselle from Trier to Metz.

And so at the turn of the 15th Century the Merchants of Trier, [based entirely along the continuousstretch of the Moselle which Leopold von Habsburg estbalished](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1512710992981659729), now do all their business in Metz

*Figure 1: Market view
Figure 2: River Toll Castles
Figure 3: The Preferred Market Access granted by Tombo d'Alscae*

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## The Regime of Tinti d'Alscae, how the Nobles tolled the Moselle

Tinti d'Alscae, like his predecessor has reigned as Prince-Bishop for more than a
events.fullText:
## The Regime of Tinti d'Alscae, how the Nobles tolled the Moselle

Tinti d'Alscae, like his predecessor has reigned as Prince-Bishop for more than a generation. But his reign has never been the most secure. He is after all the son of Duke from Lorraine. Tinti is a Noble Frenchman in some poor and forgotten German land. Surrounded by German Counts, and Dukes who hate the French.

The Nobles of Trier on the other hand have always been proud of their French connections. While it has now been 80 years since they aided the [French at Arques](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1508301666984919131), those connections still exist. 

Tinti has done his best to foster that French connection, his court is held in French and while the dialect spoken**Francique**, is that of his homeland in Lorraine it is nether less still French. And so the Nobles of Trier have learnt Francique and became closer to the Nobles of Lorraine and their wealth. 

So Tinti while Pious, **Favoured the Elites** in his rule. As the elites of Trier could be counted on to at least speak his language. And with this favour of course came **Privilege**. 

The Noble privileges of **Control of the Borderlands**, **Fortification Rights** and **Land Rights** all combined give the Elites significant control over the land. And significant rights to build Castles, whether to conduct little Noble feuds **or to toll the once profitable route from Trier to Cologne.**

*figure 1: The court Language of Trier
figure 2: The Privelege of the Noblesse*

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**A Regal Request**


In the wake of her grandfather Ioannis's passing, Queen Eirini Palaiologos has written to her father Mikhail with a very particu
events.fullText:
**A Regal Request**


In the wake of her grandfather Ioannis's passing, Queen Eirini Palaiologos has written to her father Mikhail with a very particular request.  She asks that the ancient Roman claim to the island of Sardinia be granted to her and her line, and thus, to the Kingdom of Carthage.

While the partition of claims was not uncommon, the granting of a claim to a granddaughter of a ruler is somewhat unusual.  It is no coincidence that this comes at the same time that the merchants in Carthage have pressured the Sultan quite hard to seize the island and its valuable mineral resources, many of which are critical to Carthaginian industry.

events.author: Murlock
events.snippet:
**The Challanges to the Roman Restoration <:romerome2:899964960506003477> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DV800dJ53E4

For the first time in cen
events.fullText:
**The Challanges to the Roman Restoration <:romerome2:899964960506003477> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DV800dJ53E4

For the first time in centuries, Rome is once more an independent and free kingdom, sitting in the middle of the Italian games. To the south lies the Neapolitan city, the sister city of Mezzogiorno. Further North, the scheming Italian families vie for influence, power, glory, and gold. And here sits Roma, once the heart of the known world, now restored under House Orsinii. <:rose:865579087883272192> 

It will take time for the new adminstration to flourish under the ideals of a new individualistic Rome, one the focuses inwards on the growth of the city. But as this new future looks bright to the Roman Families that has inhereited it, there are threats around to Rome.

It will take time for the new administration to flourish under the ideals of a new individualistic Rome, one that focuses inward on the growth, stability, and prosperity of the city itself. To the Roman families who have inherited this renewed future, the path ahead appears bright. Yet beyond Rome’s walls, dangers gather, and not all look upon the restoration with admiration.

<@297185126947880960>
The Carthaginians look upon the Roman market with envy. What was once peaceful trade across the Mediterranean may soon descend into piracy, hostile waters, and bitter rivalry. Such unrest is something merchants of every realm dread.

<@234699731715096576> 
The rise of this new Roman realm threatens the prestige of Naples, a city already struggling against decline in the shadow of the Roman Restoration. As Rome grows stronger, old powers may grow desperate. The threat of yet another Italian war looms, as rival states may seek to restore the influence of the Papal States and halt Romes return to glory.

<@315868897012219904> <@545657694489542666> 
To the North, the Italian Kingdoms have been bound under the chains of the Empire yet again, but Rome has, for now, remained outside of this Imperial Influence. The Imperial Borders are against the Orsini, an opportunity for Imperial expansion that must be resisted.

The Orsinii house is not as it was, but its name and history is what brought it to today. The Battle of Rome is over, perhaps now the Battle for Italy shall soon begin...

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Act of Separation

*"Holdynge here, to-day, that the Chirche in Engelond is beholden to Rome, nat to Lyons, and that Rome is withoute functioun at
events.fullText:
## Act of Separation

*"Holdynge here, to-day, that the Chirche in Engelond is beholden to Rome, nat to Lyons, and that Rome is withoute functioun at the force of armes of the Frensche Kyng, therfore the Romayn Chirche is decapited from the continent, makynge the Chirche in Engelond de facto separé, til suche tyme as the Chirche in Rome may be restored."*

With confirmation from the assembled nobles of Parliament, Eleanor has made reality of the simple fact that while spiritual rule from Rome may be tolerable, spiritual rule from France is not. 

Initially, the Archbishop of Canterbury was considered the de-facto leader of this decapitated Anglo-Roman branch, but fears of a Flemish or French invasion lead instead the Archbishop of York to be the spiritual head. 

<@1417260821674852363> Scottish representatives are invited to join communion with the Archbishop of York, if they so choose. 

<@1218063544940232784> 
Ireland is legally detached from the continental French church as well, but the hearts of the people are still up for grabs. 

<@1094722105855189062> 
Wales, being under the Archbishopric of Canturbury, is joined with the Anglo-Roman endeavor. 

<@460814348487753730> <@150411715757080576>

**(The Church in England will  drift over time, unless Rome is restored soon.)**

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**The Battle of Berlin, Lusatian League**



**The preamble**

Two roman Councilors, Konstantinos Palailogos, an architect and the younger brother of
events.fullText:
**The Battle of Berlin, Lusatian League**



**The preamble**

Two roman Councilors, Konstantinos Palailogos, an architect and the younger brother of the current Emperor Michail X Palaiologos as well as the general of their forces - Nikiphoros Apokaukos had arrived to the camp of Lusatian League as supporters of their interests, under the direct order from their general. Byzantine interests were not directly involved in the Civil War for Brandenburg, but they wished to assist the Sorbs who were for more than 5 centuries under the thumb of what future historian would call «Germans». 

This intervention came as a force of 600 cataphracts, a column of wagons with food and other goods for the value of 500 ducats, of which, the Byzantines would only use 100, which they would swiftly recuperate after the war ended. Most of the conringent arrived to the League without many loses and for the initial stages of the war - helped their brothers in arms to rid the surrounding lands of the Rober Knights that brought chaos.

After a successful campaign, and losing only around a 100 cataphracts, there came the time for the battle that would swiftly determine the end of the war.

**The ambush**

Once the Byzantine advisors recieved the news of the Army backed by Wittlesbachs that was marching to take Berlin, and after the joined council with the Lusatian leadership, a decion was made to attempt and intercept the attacking force and take the city for the Sorbs.

And indeed, this intention came to be. Though unable to prevent the main force to enter the city, the leading force of the Cataphracts has spotted the incoming reinforcements to the Wittlesbach force. Making a swift decision on the fly, the spear of cataphracts has burst into the marching lines of infantry, killing more than 600 men in a single charge. Though losing a significant count of Greek Cavalryman, and unable to prevent the rest of the reinforcements from entering the city, perhaps it was this charge that allowed the things to develop as they did in the end.

**The Assault on the Walls**

The Byzantine general knew the defensive strategy well, and knew that the men on the walls had advantage over the besieging forces. They also knew that there had to still be fighting in the city itself. So they attempted a swift siege in 3 separete segments of the walls. 

2 of these advances succeded, allowing the forces of the League access to the city. Though many men were lost in this battle, and around 1000 people would not return from these walls. Later in their report to the Emperor, Nikiphoros reported that a prolonged assault could allow the defenders more leasure in their organisation, and such a sacrifice was required to secure the acces to the walls. Perhaps he was wrond, and it was a waste of human life, but it did pay off in the end.

**The fighting for the inner city**

On this event, the Byzantine generals had little to say. They had successfully constructed a line of barricades and defences in the southmost part of the City, which had either by chance or strategy - prevented any attacks on the Lusatian forces within the City.

An ambush would be attempted by the Polish General who’s name shall remain absent, as to not put shame on his descendants. This polish fool had suffered incredible loses and then attempted a counter-attack lead not by strategy but by pure rage and strife for vengeance. This attack was not sanctioned by the Byzantine general and can only be blamed on the man himself. 

As Konstantinos stated later on, he had never witnessed such unnecessary display of waste of human lifes, he would express his pity and condolences towards the polish people who had to perish under the command of this man. 

None the less, after such failures, a diplomacy attempt was done to try and appease the Milanese. It had unfortunately failed with not much to tell about it. This refusal however, had doomed the last defenders of the City to perish or be captured in its walls.

**The Triumph of the Slavs**

The 2 final battles in the city would be fought in quick succession. The Wittlesbachs had attempted to wipe out the defenders of the City. They succeeded in this, but right after the battle, they would be caught in a flanking attack lead by the previously named Polish General. Though some allege that it was the Byzantine general who had lead the successful ambush, and that they simply gave this glory to the Pole as to reinstate some of the honor that he lost in his failures.

Whatever case may be, the rest of the hostile forces had retreated from the City, which was claimed by Lusatia. Both the general and the Brother of the Emperor had returned to the Empire, and had started to prepare a future collaboration effort with the new Sorbian power in Germany. Only the future will tell what will come as a result of this incredible event.

(The Byzantine/Lusatian POV of the Civil war in Brandenburg)

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Carthaginian Sultana & Christian Queen, Eirini Palaiologos**


Queen Eirini has become a figure for Catholics and Orthodox Christians to rally a
events.fullText:
**The Carthaginian Sultana & Christian Queen, Eirini Palaiologos**


Queen Eirini has become a figure for Catholics and Orthodox Christians to rally around within the Carthaginian Sultanate, and she has become a strong advocate and mouthpiece for the non-Muslims within the realm.

During her first years as Queen she spent considerable time in Sicily touring the countryside and getting to know the leaders in the various communities of Palermo, Syracuse, and Messina.  Her extensive time spent among Christians led to many at court referring to her mockingly as the *Queen of the Dhimmi*, a title which she embraced and made her own in spite of this.  Her lack of conversion, and thus presentation unveiled at court save on religious holidays, has led to significantly increased visibility for the christian population within the Carthaginian court, while the moderating effects of her marriage to the Sultan, such as their monogamous relationship as opposed to the adoption of the traditional harem, have had a similar effect among the Christians.  The Queen and the Sultan’s relationship serves as proof that tolerance, understanding, and mutual respect can overcome even the most divisive of differences.

There are hardliners on both sides that do not approve of their union, however in the aftermath of the civil war they have been forced into silence.  With the acquisition of the hereditary Roman claim to the island of Sardinia, she has firmly entrenched the support of the burghers, and even the nobility is forced to acknowledge her value and aptitude.  Her beauty and grace have enthralled the court and her cunning and statecraft have proven her value beyond doubt.  Truly a Sultan could wish for no greater a wife.

<@302128861209165824>

events.snippet:
**Declaration of Demands - Sardinia**


A formal letter of demands have been issued to the Kingdom of Aragon:

*By rights of blood, the Carthaginian r
events.fullText:
**Declaration of Demands - Sardinia**


A formal letter of demands have been issued to the Kingdom of Aragon:

*By rights of blood, the Carthaginian realm asserts the claim of Eirini Palaiologos, Queen of Sicily & Sultana of Carthage, asserts her most ancient & just claim as heiress of Rome to the island of Sardinia. As rightful Queen of Sardinia, the surrender of the island of Sardinia is hereby demanded.  Should these demands be refused, then we shall have no recourse other than to invade and seize the island by force.*

||This is a war dec 4 months from session start, declaring after May 3rd, 1404.||

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Pennal Letter
<@460814348487753730>

*A simple document by some accounts, and to some others the source of salvation. The disposed and self-pr
events.fullText:
### The Pennal Letter
<@460814348487753730>

*A simple document by some accounts, and to some others the source of salvation. The disposed and self-proclaimed Prince of Wales, before his departure from his homeland, penned its words in holy oath. Sworn before God and his personal theologian Gruffudd Yonge, the aging man committed his country to the cause of France - if she should liberate her from savage Saxon oppression and return its church to communion.*

> Your Most Serene Highness, you have deemed it worthy on the humble recommendation sent,
> to learn how my nation, for many years now elapsed, has been oppressed by the fury
> of the barbarous Saxons; whence because they had the government over us, and
> indeed, on account of the fact itself, it seemed reasonable with them to trample upon
> us. But now, Most Serene Highness, you have in many ways, from your innate goodness,
> informed me and my subjects very clearly and graciously concerning the recognition
> of the true Vicar of Christ. I, in truth, rejoice with a full heart on account of that
> information of your excellency, and because, inasmuch from this information, I
> understood that the Lord Gregory XII, the supreme pontifex intends to work for the
> promotion of an union in the Church of God with all his possible strength. Confident
> indeed in his right, and intending to agree with you as indeed as far as it is possible

> for me, I recognize him as the true Vicar of Christ, on my own behalf, and on behalf
> of my subjects by these letters patent, foreseeing them by the bearer of their
> communications in your majesty's presence. And because, most excellent highness, the
> metropolitan church of St. David was, as it appears, violently compelled by the
> barbarous fury of those reigning in this country, to obey the church of Canterbury, ad
> de facto still remains in the subject of this subjection. Many other disabilities are
> known to have been suffered by the Church of Wales through these barbarians, which
> for the greater part are set forth full in the letter patent accompanying. I pray and
> sincerely beseech your majesty to have these letters sent to my lord, the supreme
> pontifex, that as you deemed worthy to raise us out of darkness into light, similarly
> you will wish to extirpate and remove violence and oppression from the church and
> from my subjects, as you are well able to. And may the Son of the Glorious Virgin
> long preserve your majesty in the promised prosperity.

Owain Glyndwr, Prince of Wales
Dated at Pennal the last day of March (1395)
Yours avowedly
Owen, Prince of Wales.

(Actual text of the irl [Pennal Letter](https://owain-glyndwr.wales/pdf_files/PennalLetter-FullText.pdf), [Wiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pennal_Letter))

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Man Left Behind

Not all of Owain’s Welshmen left with him to France. Some felt that their mission in Wales overwrote even the necessity to su
events.fullText:
### The Man Left Behind

Not all of Owain’s Welshmen left with him to France. Some felt that their mission in Wales overwrote even the necessity to survive in this life. Those of this zealous extract were few in number, and mostly clerics, and have been headed by [Gruffudd Yonge](https://cy.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gruffudd_Yonge) ever since Prince Owain’s departure some nine years ago.

*Wales now stands **divided** along ancient lines in the wake of civil war.* 

Families like the Tudurs support the restoration of grand pilgrimage sites like [St.David’s](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1516269656158240868) under the crown’s patronage while turning quietly looking away from episodes like the [southern clearances](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1517583287743283421). They champion the cause of rebuilding and order led by ecclesiastically minded individuals like Arthur Tudur all while living in Norman designed stone keeps. They stand loudest in London and will remain so likely at the cost of continued antagonism at home.

In contrast, figures like Gruffudd Yonge frequent the hovels that once hosted his friend Owain’s impromptu parliaments. He is an Oxford educated Bishop, and by no means impoverished, but his words hold sway where English swords cannot reach. Variously labeled an agitator, a rabble rouser, or perhaps still a rebel, he is yet untouchable. If the Barons Cardigan and Principality of Wales were to pursue all figures of this sort it would cause another revolt, one that they reason is best left to die in the corners of the country’s many huts.

events.snippet:
# The road south

As final preparations are made, the Arch-Bishop packs up the last few books, missives and treaties from the offices of Koln, dust fl
events.fullText:
# The road south

As final preparations are made, the Arch-Bishop packs up the last few books, missives and treaties from the offices of Koln, dust floats through the air as faded treaties left undisturbed are once again rattled to life. Whilst the young Prince-Bishop has little attachment to the old offices, some admiration of a historic place is sombre to see it abandoned.

As those still loyal to Leopold await their final departure to Bonn, everything loaded. From the sprawling city of Koln to the fairly modest town of Bonn. Though, with such modesty comes less oversight, the ability to act with less constraints of city and hanseatic influence.

With this said, this makes the first moves of the new court open to endless posibilities and endless concequences.

events.fullText:

At last, the traitorous Count has been removed and a brand new Republic now governs the former lands of Negroponte. The Venetian holdings in the Aegean have grown substantially has a result of this, as stability returns to the sea once more.

The war has reached its conclusion, with Carthaginian forces seizing the island of Sardinia with little contest.  With the conf
events.fullText:
**Sardinia Secured**


The war has reached its conclusion, with Carthaginian forces seizing the island of Sardinia with little contest.  With the conflict at an end, Carthaginian mercantile interests have been secured, and Queen Eirini's claims enforced.

events.author: Moro
events.snippet:
# Establishment of the Signoriza di Venezia!
Significant political reforms have been undertaken in Venice, as the Republic redefined it's internal adm
events.fullText:
# Establishment of the Signoriza di Venezia!
Significant political reforms have been undertaken in Venice, as the Republic redefined it's internal administration to have the Consiglio dei Quaranta completely removed as a relevant entity, in exchange for the Signoria, the new supreme governmental body of Venice. This sets a defining stone in the history of the Republic, as much of the bickering between the council and the Doge has died down, becoming a thing of the past as the leadership of the Republic takes a much more centralized role.

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**Lower Lusatia | Land in doubt**
<@288410456526422016> <@252560004069064705> <@302128861209165824> <@261290667106369536> 
After their victory the leauge would find itself in that most curious position of creating a new realm in the absence of any others. the towns electing  the Town of Lubben as the capital, its Burgomaster Burgomaster Konstatin z Kalawa in effect becoming the lord of the new land, though the towns assembly remains in doubt of whether he shall become a permanent lord or simply the leader of the parliament. The realm split between **Byzantine, Polish and German** interests and influence. 

A just man the new Archburghermesiter would work to balance these interests in the short term, securing an alliance with the Hanseatic Leauge, Poland while establishing an embassy with their distant imperial friends from the war. Yet soon enough the luster of victory will wear off and the Sorbian people will need to decide which path to tread.

events.fullText:

In order to secure stability with the Soest dynasty in Westfalen lands, Leopold takes Constance to take as his wife. Such a bind puts less risk of revolts from the east, weakening the bargaining power of their court.

Theologians spilling out from Wycliffe's alma mater of Oxford have begun to infiltrate the rural hills of Wales just as Gruffu
events.fullText:
### The English Strain

Theologians spilling out from Wycliffe's alma mater of Oxford have begun to infiltrate the rural hills of Wales just as Gruffudd Yonge has. They preach of Lyon's corruption and the fall of Rome, of the French King's influence on the church, and of York's attempts to save England. These sermons have found some reception among the peasantry as it is clear that there is failings...though few are equipped to understand *exactly what* that is.

The self-declared Prince of Wales has returned at the head of a French mercenary army! The locals around St Davids
events.fullText:
### Prince Owain Glyndwr Returns!

The self-declared Prince of Wales has returned at the head of a French mercenary army! The locals around St Davids Cathedral and Fishguard have rallied to his cause as he attempts to put the most fortified castle in Wales - English Pembroke, to siege. Immeadate support is requested from the English Marchers <@1106651545296195655> as the local forces of the Principality of Wales are not capable of lifting the siege with current numbers.
