events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## English Army Decimated!

A rapid charge by the Justiciar of Wales - Baron Jean Cardigan found rapid success in relieving the poorly defended siege
events.fullText:
## English Army Decimated!

A rapid charge by the Justiciar of Wales - Baron Jean Cardigan found rapid success in relieving the poorly defended siege of Pembroke. The 9,000 strong army of marchers, Englishmen, and Welsh mercenaries reveled in this victory...**only to then turn in horror**.

Prince Owain had known the terrain of Pembrokeshire and had connections amongst the locals from his previous uprising. He and the rest of the French force was waiting over some nearby hills and fell upon the English host with fury just as their organization wavered.

*The defeat was near total and many English allies were taken captive <@460814348487753730>. The siege of Pembroke was quickly resumed and the Prince of Wales looked ever closer to his goal. If he could take this bastion then it might be the spark for a general rising over the rest of the country...*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Desperate Redemption

There was little doubt that a full English army was necessary on Welsh soil now. The French mercenaries and their allies had
events.fullText:
### Desperate Redemption

There was little doubt that a full English army was necessary on Welsh soil now. The French mercenaries and their allies had utterly destroyed the first force and stood ready to open a fortified base in the south of the country. Thus under the Baron of Windsor - Sir Richard Plantagenet, a direct cousin of the Queen herself, commanded a force from Essex to expulse the force from the British isles.

*The second force **broke** the long French siege with a careful approach and straightforward pitched battle. 'Prince' Owain fled to St Davids's with only 500 men remaining as the French fleet fought a desprate action at sea. Wales was thus 'saved', though the cost borne by the south was again terrible.*

*The French fleet slipped past English attempts to blockade them only to fight an inconclusive action off the coast of Cornwall later. Owain and his remaining loyalists once again slipped back into unassailable France..*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Fortifying the South

Pembroke Castle was clearly not enough.

The fortifications in Pembrokeshire fell to French assault one by one until it was
events.fullText:
### Fortifying the South

Pembroke Castle was clearly not enough.

The fortifications in Pembrokeshire fell to French assault one by one until it was only the singular great English settlement was left. Even modern bastions stood little chance if not defended by a network of surrounding keeps. Southern Wales, once thought the secure first conquests of the Normans, is clearly no longer held securely enough by centuries old motte and baileys.

*A **building spree** has consumed southern Wales as stockades are turned into impregnable castles. These efforts under Queen Eleanor and her newly appointed Justiciar have little regard for the locals or any other concerns than a potential return of Owain. God be good, they will withstand what will be done to them.*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Shadowy Cost

The burden of absorbing Swansea into the Principality had yet to be discovered. Only now, after negotiating with the crown over
events.fullText:
### The Shadowy Cost

The burden of absorbing Swansea into the Principality had yet to be discovered. Only now, after negotiating with the crown over 'donations' ||of 500 ducats|| to the Lancasters and ensuring the House of Mowbray was well compensated has it truly been unearthed. Wales, poor in many objects associated with commerce and now pirated heavily, has been taxed heavily only for its Justicar to receive a underpopulated and poor strip of land between Cardiff and Pembroke.

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## ELEANOR HAS DIED

A French-Catholic sympathizer coup manifested an army and marched on London! They were ultimately defeated, but a Welsh maid in c
events.fullText:
## ELEANOR HAS DIED

A French-Catholic sympathizer coup manifested an army and marched on London! They were ultimately defeated, but a Welsh maid in court poisoned the Queen with miasma, after which the Queen perished!

God save the Kingdom of England, a great Queen has died. 

**The villainous Roger Infield is to be imprisoned in London Tower**, and the regent Albert Piast will have a **dangerous road ahead for the succession crisis.

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
**Pope Innocentius V Marinus has decided to crown Giovanni Maria Visconti Holy Roman Emperor within Rome.**

Upon hearing of the Holy Roman Emperor's
events.fullText:
**Pope Innocentius V Marinus has decided to crown Giovanni Maria Visconti Holy Roman Emperor within Rome.**

Upon hearing of the Holy Roman Emperor's desire to not be crowned in Lyon, a compromised has been found to reassert Pope Innocentius V as Bishop of Rome, despite deciding to not rule from there, by crowning Giovanni Maria Visconti Holy Roman Emperor. A sign that Pope Innocentius V still holds ecclesial power over Rome, and is in fact Bishop of Rome despite residing in Lyon. This is to reaffirm his legitmacy, and to ensure awareness of Orsini custodianship of Latium and Rome.

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Spree Válka**
<@252560004069064705> 

Though the central towns of Lusatia had been secured the surrounding townships remained in political limbo
events.fullText:
**The Spree Válka**
<@252560004069064705> 

Though the central towns of Lusatia had been secured the surrounding townships remained in political limbo, some robber baron held, others semi independent polities who nevertheless competed with the united town leauge. 

in 1413 at a meeting of the towns, over the objections of the Archburgermesiter they would decide to form a militia to enforce control and incorporation into lower Lusatia of the surronding townships. However such a campaign, one designed by committee would be flawed, suffering from lack of direction or history of military experience would lead to a floundering campaign, only saved by the intervention of a polish regiment thanks to the negotiations of the very leader opposed to the campaign.

Now controlling the lands of Lower Lusatia directly the leauge is now faced with yet another curious test, how the incorpration of Brandenburg people and culture will go, many calling for the same laws excluding them from merchant guilds to be placed upon them, yet others now the blow it would take to the local skilled labour force to do so. For now handeled carefully by the expert diplomacy of the leader of the leauge Konstantin to invite their representives to meetings of the leauge but demand Sorbian to be spoken, a helpful middleground. All the while others worry for the state of the realms defence and a need to create a proper millitary force that can defend itself.


events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
***The 1409 Decree of Kutná Hora***

Faced with years of increasing strife over the Western Schism and its “conclusion” with the Council of Würzburg,
events.fullText:
***The 1409 Decree of Kutná Hora***

Faced with years of increasing strife over the Western Schism and its “conclusion” with the Council of Würzburg, the ethnic and religious conflicts dominating the University of Praha have finally come to a head. Bothered by the reports reaching him now that he has returned from Nürnberg and rests in the Castle of Praha, Jan fell under the influence of the pro-Czech elements within the university, and issued his decree on adjusting their vote.

The Polish, Saxon, and Bavarian nations at the University, dominated by German faculty, **resisted** the Council of Würzburg’s conclusion while the Czech faction endorsed it, if at least tentatively. Therefore, the 3:1 vote to reject its terms have, years later, resulted in the decree which gave the Czech nation 3 votes—reducing the Germans to one.

With this, the German faculty and students have left the University in flight, greatly depressing the majesty, prestige, and population count of the University. And, notoriously, elevated by the energies of the Czech students, **Dr. Jan Hus** was chosen as the new rector of the rump University of Praha.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### the Lord's Country

No, not __The Lord__, *the Lords*.

The death of 'Good' Queen Eleanor has thrown England, and by extension Wales, into serious
events.fullText:
### the Lord's Country

No, not __The Lord__, *the Lords*.

The death of 'Good' Queen Eleanor has thrown England, and by extension Wales, into serious chaos. There is no strong center of power in the rural country in comparison to London centrally managing England. Justiciar Gilbert of West Riding openly competes with his first cousin Princess Morfydd Mathrafal for influence in the north despite both being first cousins via their Welsh lines.

In the south, influential families like the Hastings, Audleys, Mowbrays, and of course the Cardigans compete for regional influence in the countryside. In contrast, newly urbanizing towns like Pembroke and Cardiff push back against the Parochial instincts of their nearby Earls and Barons. The ever independent Marcher Lords - the Fitzallens, Burghersh, Bohuns, and of course the Mortimers use the opportunity to further entrench their power after nearly losing it all in Owain's rebellions.

*Unity in Wales was short lived under a queen who showed them respect, and an invasion that offered them threat. What little still exists is as precarious as the country it claims to govern.*

The memorandum outlining the promise to keep the borders secure as the old Margravedom had done, to counter and fight banditry in the north <@326414282851876875> , to ensure a strong Lower Lusatia to avoid the past issues of Brandenburg, make trade flow and back Imperial interests in the Diet. 

Though some issues would cause tension the overall consensus would be a good one many newer nobles looking to Konstantin as a possible permanent prince due to his foreign successes and ease within the Holy Roman Imperial system.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Governance Debate

Contestation in the north, Lordships in the south, and Fedual domains in the middle. Given these conditions it is near impo
events.fullText:
### The Governance Debate

Contestation in the north, Lordships in the south, and Fedual domains in the middle. Given these conditions it is near impossible to govern as London directs and difficult to even discuss basic reforms not performed at the end of a sword. By sheer weight of tradition the fortified castle of Carnarvron has remained the dejure seat of the Principality of Wale's governance. This is despite the rapidly growing economic weight of southern formally confirmed towns like Pembroke and Cardiff.

- Many have suggested **Pembroke** as a new capital of Wales
- A number of northern lords continue to fight for **Carnarvon** as the government seat, despite the region's harshness
- Some southern lords desire newly elevated **Cardiff** as a capital given its flat and productive terrain
- A minority have attempted to push the silver mines of **Aberystwyth** as a very profitable middle ground

*Advice is asked from the King-Regent Albert Piast on what to do about the matter <@1106651545296195655>.*

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
***De Ecclesia***

**Dr. Jan Hus**, Rector of the University of Praha, has written his piece *De Ecclesia*, which gathers the epitome of Wycliffe’s wo
events.fullText:
***De Ecclesia***

**Dr. Jan Hus**, Rector of the University of Praha, has written his piece *De Ecclesia*, which gathers the epitome of Wycliffe’s work and constructs his own perspectives and establishments in comparison, and built upon them. 

In the wake of the **Decree of Kutná Hora**, the situation in Praha has, to some degree, grown both more and less tense. His adversaries within the University left for the hospitality of Germany, leaving his supporters with the educational field in the Bohemian capital. 

His, and Wycliffe’s teachings, are preached in the Czech vernacular in the Bethlehem Chapel, and by others across Bohemia. In his most pervasive arguments, he lists four views:

***On Indulgences…***
“The absolution of Jesus Christ ought to precede that of the priest; or, in other words, the priest who absolves and condemns ought to be certain that the case in question is one which Jesus Christ Himself has already absolved or condemned.”

***On the Authority of the Pope…***
“If the Pope uses his power according to God’s commands, he cannot be resisted without resisting God Himself; but if he abuses his power by enjoining what is contrary to the Divine Law, then it is duty to resist him as should be done to the pale horse of the Apocalypse, to the dragon, to the beast, and to the Leviathan.”

***On the Disbandment of the Clergy’s Secular Power…***
“As the secular body, to whom the temporal sword alone is suitable, cannot undertake to handle the spiritual one, in like manner the ecclesiastics ought to be content with the spiritual sword, and not make use of the temporal.”

***On the Corruption of the Church…***
“They are profuse towards the saints in glory, who want nothing; they array bones of the latter with silk and gold and silver, and lodge them magnificently; but they refuse clothing and hospitality to the poor members of Jesus Christ who are amongst us, at whose expense they feed to repletion, and drink till they are intoxicated.”

<@460814348487753730> <@306458253674545153> <@112960565344804864> <@137101075411894272>

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## A Marriage to... Save England?

The **Nevilles** have  long been detractors of the Eleanor reign, and this naturally extends to her slow daughter.
events.fullText:
## A Marriage to... Save England?

The **Nevilles** have  long been detractors of the Eleanor reign, and this naturally extends to her slow daughter. Regent Albert Piast did the best he could do, and arranged a marriage to the second son of Richard Neville and the young Judith Piast. 

This is by no means popular, as the Nevilles are, among Nobility, of somewhat low bearing.  

However, their coalition in the North is quite powerful, and might serve as a base of support in these turbulent times. 

After all, would nobles really march against their new King and Queen?

"You would abandon the King's own fortifications here!" *The room rang quiet with the accustion.*

"No. I would move the seat of g
events.fullText:
### Southern Shift

"You would abandon the King's own fortifications here!" *The room rang quiet with the accustion.*

"No. I would move the seat of governance to a city that has **twice** defended itself from enemies external and internal!" *defended Sir Gilbert - Justiciar of Wales.*

"And fallen just as many times." *quickly shot back Sir Jean de Cardigan.*

events.fullText:
In the light of minor german nobles from the County of Brünningen being set to inherit the important Valentinois and with no other members of House Poitiers able to take charge of the lands, the Duke of Savoy, Felepo has seen himself forced to step in and put the lands under the immediacy of Vienne, sending out ducal constables to begin an establishment of order. events.snippet:
## The Great Gascon Joust


As a celebration of Carles d'Armagnac's prosperous rule, the Duke of Gascony invites all of his neighboring countries and
events.fullText:
## The Great Gascon Joust


As a celebration of Carles d'Armagnac's prosperous rule, the Duke of Gascony invites all of his neighboring countries and nobles to a grand quarter century joust. Hosted in the port city of Bayonne, Carles intends the joust to be one of influence and general diplomacy.

The Duke constructs the fairgrounds to a grandeous scale, decorating the grounds with his family's crest. He will host lavish feasts and hunting parties to each attendee's heart's content. Come one, come all, to the great quarter century joust!

Duke Carles pens the following invitation to each guest:
> Dear Noble Guest,
> 
> Come join me in my lands for the greatest jousting tournament the Pyreenes have seen in decades! At the main stage, jousts will occur for honor and riches. Along with jousting, we will host archery contests, sword fighting duels, and a prize for the biggest game hunted during one of our many hunting parties!
> 
> Our feasts will be numerous and engorging, filled with quail, deer, cow, and rabbit. Luxuries from the deep will also be available due to our prosperous fishing companies.
> 
> Come one, come all. Celebrate the prosperities our of kingdoms and lands this century!

<@460814348487753730> <@564196329132261376> <@1123821809704374313> <@1106651545296195655> <@298192682554228738> <@422498641425596418> <@1007414347347271730> <@1094722105855189062>

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### End of the Northern Mathrafals

The dynasty has been a prominent part of Welsh politics since the time of the Norman invasion. After the extinctio
events.fullText:
### End of the Northern Mathrafals

The dynasty has been a prominent part of Welsh politics since the time of the Norman invasion. After the extinction of the formal Princly Houses they assumed the remnants of legitimacy and both cooperated with, and violently resisted English overlordship.

The Princess of Powys - Morfydd Mathrafal, is the last of her line. She is of the Northern Mathrafal line, those who stayed loyal to the English crown during Owain's rebellion. Her descends are of the distant Northern Italian Ceva line, themselves a vassal of the Savoyards.

*The last of the loyalist Mathrafal line is **ending**. The last remnants of independent Wales now lives in France with the line of Owain, and now his son Ithel <@460814348487753730>.*

*Baron Jean de Cardigan, former ||disgraced|| Justiciar of Wales and son of Michael de Cardigan, attends the joust.*

After repeated claims and insults from the English swine leftover from decades old rule of Aquitaine, the duchy of
events.fullText:
### Land Disputes with Aquitaine


After repeated claims and insults from the English swine leftover from decades old rule of Aquitaine, the duchy of Gascony makes declarations of claims and insults against the English swine. As part of the fabled joust, Carles challenges Duke Alain d'Albert, in the noble tournament,  to the country of the following counties:

- Auch
- Labrit
- Mimizan
- Mont-de-Marsan
- Belin

Carles also challenges the Duke for the control of the Bordeaux market. The outcome of each set of jousts per demand will be decided on the noble fairgrounds.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Southern Compact

Cardiff, once a rural backwater along the southern coast of Wales, is the second location in Wales to receive official **tow
events.fullText:
### The Southern Compact

Cardiff, once a rural backwater along the southern coast of Wales, is the second location in Wales to receive official **town rights**. The nearby capital of Pembroke is already the premier manufacturing hub for cloth and jewelry products in the country, and now Cardiff is the hub for metal and grain based goods.

This walled town is a visible addition to the continued English settlement and pacification of the south. For centuries this has been done exclusivly with forts and adventerous men-at-arms. Now it is done with economics and preferential migration from the rest of England. A policy only decided in the wake of these last decades of rebellion <@1106651545296195655>.

### The Conway River Dearth

The River Conway is the source of a consistent flow of high quality pearls to London. This was much of the early foundati
events.fullText:
### The Conway River Dearth

The River Conway is the source of a consistent flow of high quality pearls to London. This was much of the early foundation of the Principalities wealth and has continued to be a revenue stream in the decades following. As this resource became more and more well-known it became increasingly exploited as well. This began as an encouraged effort, and has now become a curse.

**Overharvesting** of the local freshwater Mussels has caused a sharp decline in their availability. Local Earls are for the first time stepping in to ensure that only their own collectors ||or those that pay them|| can harvest the Conway River. Hopefully with time this can restore the region's Mussels and their profit.

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## The founding of the University of Trier 1421-1426

Long have the Clergy of the Archdiocese of Trier gone to the University of Paris for their cleri
events.fullText:
## The founding of the University of Trier 1421-1426

Long have the Clergy of the Archdiocese of Trier gone to the University of Paris for their clerical education. 

But the needs of the Clergy of Trier have grown over time and the limited spaces that the relatively poor Archbishopric could acquire from the University of Paris was no where near enough.

Thus a new University was needed and the scholarly Prince-Bishop Geoffrey de Dampierre was more than amenable to the idea. But Geoffrey would not be the University of Triers founder, nor its Architect.

The man appointed as the first University Chancellor, Dean of Theology of Trier and to be known as its founder was the theologian **Jobst Gothan**. Jobst born a  commoner, || or in French a Villain|| is known by some for his disputation of the works of [Jan Hus based off the verses Matthew 28:18-20](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518356688846065825). And for [Jobst's Lecture tour in Lustria in 1423](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518366705674354809). But most of all Jobst is a cable and a **Well Advised** man. He aims to firmly establish the University of Trier as a beacon of traditional Catholic Orthodoxy in this time of new thinkers like Wycliffe and especially Jan Hus.

Jobst works to create a curriculum based on the Authority and Supremacy of the Pope. 
All Religious lectures shall be held in Latin, and the very few lectures presented on world means will be held in French.
Lecturers and Sub-Dean **shall be empowered to censor all Students work** and are encouraged by Jobst to do so. Punishment for being censored being immediate expulsion.
Jobst also works hard to limit the library of the University of Trier, keeping newer works out and focusing on the works of the Elders.
Lecturers from Lyon are regularly invited to teach and lecture on the virtues of Papal Authority.

In doing this Jobst decrees that the motto of the University of Trier shall be.

> *Docentes eos servare omnia quæcumque mandavi vobis*
> 
> *Teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you*

The students of Trier and its soon to be established satellite College at Cochem shall learn only that which Jobst has commanded.

*Figure 1: Prince-Bishop Geoffroy de Dampierre
Figure 2: Jobst Gothan, Chancellor of the University of Trier
Figure 3: Censorship Laws of the Univeisty of Trier*

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## An Invitation to lecture, to Jan Hus

The Prince-Bishop of Trier, Geoffrey de Dampierre is a Scholar, and a Sinner. He wishes to push the boundarie
events.fullText:
## An Invitation to lecture, to Jan Hus

The Prince-Bishop of Trier, Geoffrey de Dampierre is a Scholar, and a Sinner. He wishes to push the boundaries of knowledge forward, not just stay within the established norms. Geoffrey wants to question everything, even if some might find the answers offensive. 

Prince-Bishop Geoffrey feels stifled under University Chancellors Jobst Gothan strict censorship. [Where under the censorship over the first four years of the University of Triers](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518414342994001932) existence even the slightest deviation from the established norm is punished with explosion. 

Geoffrey looks to the freedom, discussions and advances of the University of Praha as something to be emulated. And Geoffrey looks at Dr Jan Hus as the beacon that lights the University of Praha as the shining jewel of innovation in Bohemia, if not the entire world.

Thus Geoffrey extends a personal invitation, with a **promise of protection** to Jan Hus to present a lecture in Czech, Latin or whatever Jan chooses to. The Lecture to be held at the recently founded University of Trier and personally sponsored by the Archbishop.

If Jan accepts Geoffrey hopes beyond hope that Jan Hus's lecture will change the University of Trier and the future College of Cochem for the better.

<@261290667106369536>

|| [Jobst Gothan](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518414342994001932) however does knows about the proposed lecture. **And if Jan Hus accepts**, Jobst intends to invite agents of the Pope, and wider Church in attendance. <@460814348487753730> <@306458253674545153> <@112960565344804864> ||

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
** HANSETAG** 

The Hansetag of 1426 has concluded: the towns and cities have acclaimed Albrecht as their spokesperson. Due to his deals expanding the
events.fullText:
** HANSETAG** 

The Hansetag of 1426 has concluded: the towns and cities have acclaimed Albrecht as their spokesperson. Due to his deals expanding the League in the Baltic, as well as his rapprochement to Lusatia, the towns are willing to collaborate closely and have become more prone to leaving deals to Hamburg and Lübeck. This reliance comes from various places.

-    First, the religious heresy that keeps sweeping everywhere. The southern kontors, convinced by the success of the Crusade, are zealots of Lyon, while most of the germans still doubt its intentions, and English burghers are preaching lollard doctrine.
-    Second, the increase in trade has become an issue; before, the Hanseatic traders were the only ones privileged enough to trade through several markets. Nowadays, everything is much more interconnected and less opportunities are available. This has been one of the reasons to promote Danish exploration.
-    Finally, the Emperor is a buffoon that is tempting the cities’ patience. He tricked the League into supporting his taxation, then was incapable of defending the North (solved, as is customary, by the League) and has sold out not one but two free imperial cities.

Seeking strong security, the cities are relying on Hamburg to lead the way. Should Albrecth manage to increase trade income and pull more cities into the League’s orbit, then he will secure the Consul’s authority for decades to come. Of course, the heretical schism might undo any unity among the League…

With the rejection from the Orsini, the Consul has decided to seek more concessions from the minor republics of Italy, as well as the Signoria of Ferrara. Expansion in Constantinople should restart, as it paused more than 20 years ago due to the greediness of Walbeck. At the same time, the chaos in England can prove ideal to achieve more concessions; London is one of the most profitable markets the Hansa is in.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## Amser a ddengys - Time will tell

The Aberffraw’s had half a century of [legitimacy and prestige](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1
events.fullText:
## Amser a ddengys - Time will tell

The Aberffraw’s had half a century of [legitimacy and prestige](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515793647189364866) on their side. The Mathrafal’s carry either the love of the people or the support of the Justiciar depending on the branch, even if their blood is [not as princely](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515552542455627827). And the Tudurs have…**nothing**. They carry neither princely blood nor the support of the people. They began their rise as [middling servants](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515552676207919206) and they have retained this role under every administration that has come to rule Wales since the arrival of the Normans. The Tudurs have simply **survived** - an impossible task that has frustrated almost every one of their rival houses, English and Welsh alike.
.

*Their perseverance has been rewarded.*

[Lady Margaret Beaufort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lady_Margaret_Beaufort) is the sole heiress to a vast English inheritance that is the end of her line. She is the daughter of [John the 1st Duke of Somerset](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Beaufort,_1st_Duke_of_Somerset), who was in turn the son of [John the 1st Earl of Somerset](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Beaufort,_1st_Earl_of_Somerset), who was in turn the son of [John the Duke of Lancaster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_of_Gaunt) - more popularly referred to as John of Gaunt. John of Gaunt, the founder of the House of Lancaster, was the third surviving son of King Edward III Plantagenet and he accrued a vast fortune in military service to his father. Unfortunately, long years of fighting in France also pushed him to not marry with legitimate issues. Thus when the matter of the legitimization of his four children with one his longtime mistresses, [Katherine Swynford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Katherine_Swynford), was raised before parliament they quickly approved the issue; fearing both the current lack of male Plantagenet heirs ||and the King’s wrath||.

-# *King Edward III Plantagenet crossing the Somme; he is the progenitor of the claims of the House of Beaufort*

-# (Slightly uncomfortable topic - WARNING)

Lady Margaret Beaufort is young, **very young**. She was raised by English lords who desired to marry her to their sons as quickly as possible to secure the vast lands her name came with. Unfortunately for them though, none of them were women. For when it came time for the young heiress to select whose advice she would take she chose the influential [Princess Morfydd Mathrafal’s](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1518367861171818507) recommendation. Naturally, the Welsh Princess of Powys suggested her first cousin Sir Iorwerth Tudur, also called Edward, as a suitable match. Though he was five years her senior he was still young and allegedly far kinder than the other lords who simply wished to use her.

The Princess arranged an initial meeting between the 13 year old English lady and 18 Welsh Marcher. It went well…perhaps too well. By what Lady Beaufort would later describe as a ‘divine guidance’ she would find herself seeking to marry the Welshman without reservations. The subject only became more complicated as the young lady began showing signs of pregnancy some two months later. Their son was born seven months later at Pembroke Castle to a family that kept the affair as controlled as they could. When Margaret turned 16 they had her and the now 21 year old Edward officially married posthaste and their three year old son **Henry Tudur** declared a lawful product of it by the Archbishop of York.

-# (An only slightly modified tale of the actual thing)

-# *Margaret Beaufort holding Henry Tudur*

House Tudur now found itself, after a couple years of an uncomfortable reality, the owners of a grand English inheritance and position in Her Majesty's government. Princess Morfydd has largely contented herself with the reality that her domain will likely be absorbed into the Principality after her passing and the end of her northern Mathrafal dynasty. An unfortunate, but foreseeable fate already inflicted on many other decayed Marcher lines.

However, the union of Edward Tudur and Margaret Beaufort presents a hope that, like many other Welshmen in past centuries, their extended family may find a more prosperous home in fair England. Though the liberties lost are bitter, the security and stability offered by an estate in its flatlands is the pride of many English Marcher families already. To hold such a vast allotment in the family name is security not just for now, as in Wales, but for generations onward. Thus a privilege once reserved only for Mortimers and Lancasters was now offered to a marginal Welsh house which was once the mere servants of long since dead Princes.

*House Tudur celebrates the birth of a boy and the inheritance of a once royal estate in England proper. Edward already prepares to depart Pembroke with his now of age wife Margaret Beaufort and inspect their long anticipated inheritance. What was previously a Welsh Marcher family of middling quality is now a firmly landed English family…and one with a distant claim to the throne at that…*

events.author: tintock
events.snippet:
## A Mission to understand the Lollards.

The scholarly || and bordering on heretical || Prince-Bishop Geoffrey de Dampierre [has not only developed a
events.fullText:
## A Mission to understand the Lollards.

The scholarly || and bordering on heretical || Prince-Bishop Geoffrey de Dampierre [has not only developed an interest in the contemporary Jan Hus](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518418039291379772) but also an interest in the works of John Wycliffe.

As such he sends the merchant **Lothar Maler** on a mission to England to acquire works and writings for Geoffrey's private collection.

**Lothar**, by trade is a prominent [merchant in the Metz market](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518181271426040028) and is also industrious scholar himself || but also a malevolent man||. [Has like his master grown dissatisfied with the censorship in the University of Trier](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518414342994001932). A University that in its founding was funded by Lothar's patronage. Lothar seeks to broaden his horizons by acquiring new knowledge and by partaking in new experiences. 

Lothar arrives in England in **November of 1426** unaware of the Game of Thrones that awaits him. 

*Figure 1: Lothar Maler
Figure 2: Lothar's part in funding the University of Trier*

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet: The fall of Rügen and Stralsund was taken quite badly by the Empire. The Hanseatic League thought of this a clear violation of imperial authority, but
events.fullText:
The fall of Rügen and Stralsund was taken quite badly by the Empire. The Hanseatic League thought of this a clear violation of imperial authority, but the Empire has always been absent in the North (despite the attempt by Milan to stop it…). Asked to defend the North, Albrecth refused to become any kind of imperial warden, certainly not without calling a Hansetag. ||Instead, he started talks with the pirates in Visby.|| <@326414282851876875> 
 
The Lusatian League was called to defend the North instead. What a twist of events! Regardless, the Hansa was not offended, considering that leaving the defence to leagues of cities is after all a good sign. Nevertheless, Albrecth saw opportunity here, for his talks with Visby could still bear fruit without the need of a war. Funds could be better used.

And so, the Hanseatic League and a representative of Gotland struck a deal to avoid imperial war, making of Stralsund a town under ‘Wolgast’… only, under real authority form the Hanseatic League. May deals like this be more common!

Albrecht gives public credit on the recovery of the Wolgast lands to the Lusatians, who have masterfully commanded the issue and done a great deed for the Empire.

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**The Cathedral is on Fire!**

The night in Sânladislau lights up as bells ring in distress and Saint’s Cathedral is engulfed in flames. The cathedral
events.fullText:
**The Cathedral is on Fire!**

The night in Sânladislau lights up as bells ring in distress and Saint’s Cathedral is engulfed in flames. The cathedral begins to crumble as the fire slowly weakens its wooden supports, turning them to ash. With the beams charred and no longer able to bear the weight of the bell tower, it collapses into the main square, destroying several market stalls, the fountain, and the statue of Ladislau. The fire spreads from the cathedral to the surrounding buildings along the square. These burning structures collapse. Some occupants escape in time, but others are not so fortunate and perish within the burning and collapsing houses. Eventually the fire is extinguished, but the cathedral and the main square now stand in ruins.

Morning comes, and people gather in the square to go about their usual routines, only to be greeted by the devastation left behind by the night's fire. Questions begin to arise: “What caused the fire?” “Who did it?” People stand distressed and scared as rumours spread throughout the city about what happened and who might be responsible. The mayor announces the city's position regarding the disaster. The city will not investigate the cause of the fire, as tensions are already dangerously high. Instead, the city wishes to avoid pointing fingers and focus on rebuilding the square so that normal life may resume. The fate of the cathedral itself will be decided by the city council. Both religious factions are displeased by the city's lack of initiative and immediately begin constructing their own theories regarding the fire. A council is called to discuss the future of the cathedral, and the leaders of both religious communities are invited to attend.
...

The council begins with all parties present in the mayor’s manor, as the town hall had suffered damage during the fire. Accusations fly across the room from the very beginning, with both sides convinced that the other is responsible. The Catholics claim that the Orthodox put the cathedral to the torch, arguing that it had become the symbol of Sânladislau embracing its true Catholic nature and that the Orthodox opposition could not accept this reality. The Orthodox answer with accusations of their own, claiming that the Catholics are outsiders and conquerors who greedily seized control of the city, and that God himself had shown His judgment by allowing the cathedral to burn. The arguments continue for hours, and no agreement or compromise is reached. The Catholics demand the full restoration of the cathedral and the punishment of the Orthodox faction for alleged arson. The Orthodox demand that the cathedral be removed entirely, that Catholics be sanctioned for their aggressive policies toward the Orthodox faithful, and that the old rites be restored throughout the city.
The other participants in the council can barely speak a word amid the shouting. Eventually the mayor loses patience and silences both sides. He declares that if neither party is willing to compromise, then the city will restore only those parts of the cathedral that can serve both communities, but it will not complete the reconstruction. The council concludes on a bitter note, with neither side receiving what they wanted.

An uneasy compromise settles over Sânladislau. Tensions are higher than ever, and everyone walks on edge, feeling as though the smallest thing could shatter the delicate balance upon which the city now rests. The flames may have been extinguished, but it seems these were only the first sparks of a much greater fire.
What will it mean for Sânladislau? What will it mean for Wallachia?

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**Orthodox Rise!**

Flames spread across Wallachia as tensions over religious matters are no longer merely on the verge of boiling over, but are explo
events.fullText:
**Orthodox Rise!**

Flames spread across Wallachia as tensions over religious matters are no longer merely on the verge of boiling over, but are exploding across the entire realm. These tensions had been growing for decades. Some signs had already appeared during Ladislau’s reign, though they subsided for a time before re-emerging stronger than ever.
Now uncontrollable chaos spreads throughout Wallachia, engulfing every corner of the realm as the country begins to break apart. Massacres begin as both sides kill one another mercilessly in their struggle for dominance over towns and villages. As fires light the skies of Wallachia, a dark chapter in Vlach history begins—one filled with fear, hatred, and blood.

The Orthodox strike first during the night, setting Catholic settlements across Dobruja and Vlach Bulgaria ablaze. The Catholics are driven from these lands and forced across the Danube. Only Tulcea remains neutral within Dobruja, becoming a haven for displaced Catholics fleeing the violence. The towns of Wallachia proper are far more reluctant to choose sides in the growing rebellion. Only the towns of Caracal and Drăgășani openly side with the Orthodox cause. There, the few remaining Catholics are executed, and the towns become Orthodox strongholds in western Wallachia. 
Amid the chaos, two prominent figures emerge as leaders of the Orthodox movement. The first is the Count of Bessarabia. The second is Octavian de Cherven, a noble who had previously been exiled and fled to Trebizond. Now returned to Wallachia, he establishes himself in Varna and takes up the mantle of one of the rebellion’s leading voices. 
The Orthodox faction refuses all negotiations with the Catholics. Their goal is nothing less than the eradication of what they see as a foreign rot within Wallachia and the restoration of the old order under a new Orthodox duke.
...

Even though the Catholics were aware of growing discontent, they were taken by surprise by the speed, organization, and brutality of the Orthodox uprising, as well as by how quickly the rebels established themselves across the Danube. In response to the surprise attack, Catholic forces gather in the heart of the country around Târgoviște and the fortress town of Câmpulung Muscel, as well as throughout the corridor between Târgoviște and Sânladislau. However, Sânladislau itself refuses to take a side in the conflict, still shaken by recent events. Additional Catholic strongholds remain along the Hungarian frontier, a legacy of the Treaty of Brasso and decades of Catholic influence in the region. 
The leader of the Catholic faction is Duke Balc himself, as the Orthodox have made it clear that they no longer recognize the rule of House Basarab. Around him gather loyal nobles, clergy, and towns that fear the destruction of the new order that has emerged in Wallachia. 
Many within the Catholic faction demand harsh repercussions for the Orthodox. While they publicly rally behind Duke Balc, some advocate for demands far more extreme than those of the Duke himself. They call for the Orthodox to bend the knee to the new order in Wallachia, abandon their old rites, and convert fully to Catholicism. They believe that only the destruction of what remains of Orthodox Wallachia can secure lasting peace within the realm. 
...

All sides begin calling upon powers beyond Wallachia's borders in hopes of gaining an advantage over their rivals. The Orthodox Count of Bessarabia sends envoys to the courts of Muscovy, distrusting the Byzantines for allowing Wallachia to fall into Catholic hands in the first place. Octavian de Cherven takes a different approach, appealing directly to the Roman Empire in the hope that the Emperor might aid in freeing Wallachia from what he calls Catholic domination.
The Catholic faction likewise looks beyond the realm. Duke Balc and his supporters hope that Hungary will honor the old and first treaty between Hungary and Wallachia, under which Hungary agreed to aid Wallachia in the event of a rebellion.
*There are other sides that wish for this madness to end and would provide an opportunity for external powers to push their interests in Wallachia.*

Wallachia now stands torn into pieces over the question of religion and allegiance. The resolution of this Wallachian religious crisis will determine the path the realm takes far into the future.

||<@423689694258397195> <@302128861209165824> <@307587752860581888> ||

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**The First City of Wallachia**

Suddenly, the bells of the cathedral ring, and a crowd gathers in the main square of Sânladislau. There stands the ma
events.fullText:
**The First City of Wallachia**

Suddenly, the bells of the cathedral ring, and a crowd gathers in the main square of Sânladislau. There stands the mayor at the centre of it all, preparing to announce something, something important. He finally starts to speak when the bells subside, and he announces that the Duke has granted the humble town of Sânladislau city rights. From now on, Sânladislau is a city, the first city of Wallachia.
People are overjoyed by the news of Sânladislau becoming a city and that their town was chosen as the first city of Wallachia. For those who did not hear the mayor's announcement, city criers spoke of Sânladislau’s city rights for days after the first proclamation.
The news spread beyond Sânladislau into neighbouring towns and villages. Some rejoiced, as it showed the growth of their home, Wallachia, and provided possible opportunities. Some were jealous that one of the younger towns had been chosen for this privilege rather than the towns that already stood when Wallachia gained its independence from the Hungarian Crown. This sentiment was most prevalent in Bucharest, but most people were indifferent to Sânladislau gaining city rights, as it did not affect their lives.
Many say it was already written in the stars that Sânladislau would become the first city of Wallachia. Seeing how quickly the town had grown and how greatly the previous Duke adored the town he had founded, many believed that Sânladislau’s city rights were a question of when, not if.

With Sânladislau’s newly found rights, life in the city changed drastically as new people flocked to it, and thus the city grew, with new development pushing into the surrounding countryside while also bringing new opportunities.

Many peasants lost their homes and livelihoods, and in response they established tighter communities around the city wherever land was still available. Some moved into the city and became helpers and apprentices to its artisans, while others went even further and became artisans themselves. 
With the growing city, the demand for goods rose as well, and the artisans were not prepared to accommodate such rapid growth, so everything they produced disappeared quickly. Merchants also stepped in, bringing more and more goods to provide what the artisans could not. Artisans and merchants grew richer from the chaos of the growing city, and their influence rose as they became key to the city’s survival.
With the influx of new people, new and divisive ideas, especially on religious matters, arrived in an already divided city. Some members of the clergy became worried that tensions might boil over and began sounding the alarm that people needed to forgive, forget, and find a way to coexist. Others did not care and pushed their agendas even further while undermining their rivals.
As the city sprawled into the countryside, nobles became distressed as they began losing their grip and influence over the city and its surroundings, desperately trying to hold on to whatever they could.
Chaos spread throughout the city, and life became harder for ordinary townsfolk and peasants as artisans and merchants increased prices. Nobles, fearful of losing their grip on the affairs of Sânladislau, began pressuring and threatening peasants to remain in the countryside and work the fields. 
With their newfound wealth and influence, the burghers began undermining noble influence even further, buying land and property from beneath their noses. Yet with rising tensions and repeated warnings from the clergy, many burghers grew worried and began expanding their influence beyond the city itself. With them, some of the city's wealth slowly started to seep out of Sânladislau.

The nobles fell into disarray, for even as they struggled to hold on to their lands and peasants, everything continued to drift towards the city proper, while the burghers steadily undermined them. Many nobles broke and lost everything in Sânladislau. In the end, many left, and those who remained were largely those who had already stood at the top of the hierarchy and possessed substantial influence within the city.

Chaos roamed through the city as people tried to find where to go and what to do in this growing urban centre, but slowly a new order began to take shape in Sânladislau, restoring peace.
With the newly granted city rights, a new order emerged in Sânladislau, announcing a new chapter in the history of both the region and its people. Within the chaos that swept through the new city, life itself changed, and there were both winners and losers. Yet despite its rough beginnings as a city, Sânladislau endured and even earned its place on the map alongside Targoviste. The recent events surrounding Sânladislau had happened so quickly, plunging the city into chaos, that it felt like a fever dream to many of its inhabitants. But now the future rests in the hands of the people, and only time will tell whether Sânladislau will grow and prosper... or burn.

events.author: Guy
events.snippet:
# I KNOW YOUR DESPAIR, SO CARELESS

# BUT HOW COULD YOU JUST DESPISE THIS?

# EVERY SECOND A THRILL, AND CLOSIN' IN FOR THE KILL,

*A weary peace had
events.fullText:
# I KNOW YOUR DESPAIR, SO CARELESS

# BUT HOW COULD YOU JUST DESPISE THIS?

# EVERY SECOND A THRILL, AND CLOSIN' IN FOR THE KILL,

*A weary peace had settled in Anatolia. The wars of the past, though not distant, was yet a memory. Should no outside factors involved themselves, perhaps that same peace could've lasted for far longer. Yet... stirrings from Italy had reached the Karamanid court, and the Beys interest was piqued. The Roman Papacy, once one of the most powrful forces within Christendom, was shattered. Not to the Cathaginians, nor the Byzantines, but rather one of their own. Rather by pwn of their own, the French crown.*


*This news reverberated through the Turkic Court, for in the first time in almost 100 years, the Knights of Rhodes were exposed. No treaty nor deal bounded the Karamanids, and the only Catholic which may help was far too weak to have any significant impact against the Beys mighty armies. The Byzantines were sat idly by, distracted by efforts withim Serbia and Hungary. Thus, with solid stamping of the horses tail in the Knightly court signifying war, Turkic armies marched west oncemore.*

*It was a quck vanquish, the Knightly armies were exhausted from the Papal war in Europe, so when faced with the might of a unified Turkic force, they were broken. Only one battle of any significance was ever fought, and it was a decisive victory for the Turkic armies. Thus swiftly they were forced to come to the table, completely shattred without a chance of aid. It the beys grace, he chose not to sieze the Isle of Rhodes and eject them from Anatolia, but rather they were totally wiprd off the face of the Mainland, with all their territories lying within being integrated into the Karamanid whole.*

*Yet something stirred down south, the Mamluke sultanate had grown weak and weary, and finally the whole rotten structure was collapsing in on itself.*

-# NOW WATCH AS OUR FIRE RISES.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**The Untamable Steppe**



A sea of grass all the way to horizon, no elevation, trees or signs of civilization. That has been the reality for most of
events.fullText:
**The Untamable Steppe**



A sea of grass all the way to horizon, no elevation, trees or signs of civilization. That has been the reality for most of is known as the Western Steppe to the nomads and tribes that live there, to the Russians, the Pontic Steppe, has always been a sort of barrier between the civilized lands and nomadic raiders that often attacked and pillaged, or sometimes traders coming from the East bringing silk, tea and porcelain.

The lack of major rivers like the ones that dominate Russia and gave transport and water access, permitting the rise of civilization, as well as lack of vegetation for defense and materials to build like wood and stone has resulted in few attempts by Russians to settle these untamed lands.

The furthest south being the Principality of Ryazan, and furthest east of Nizhny Novgorod, towns settled along rivers before the endless grass sea.

This has slowly started to change, with the campaigns to pacify the steppes organized by the Cossacks and the Grand Principality of Muscovy the latter in paper now holds control over large parts of these steppes, in reality, the matter is different.

These lands are still sparsely populated, filled with nomadic pastoralists who have armed patrol by the Cossacks to defend against tartar raiders, who even after having been "conquered", cannot be defeated easily.

Yet with the abundant wealth acquired from the trade deals made by the Veche, now incorporated with outposts all the way to the Black Sea, attempts are being done to remedy this.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**Riches of the Mountains**



Russia is notoriously a poor region, its land isn't as arable as much of the rest of Europe, dominated by rivers which
events.fullText:
**Riches of the Mountains**



Russia is notoriously a poor region, its land isn't as arable as much of the rest of Europe, dominated by rivers which create swamps, endless plains and forests without many sources of ores of any sort, at least, its eastern parts, as around Ruthenia proper and Kyiv it being incredibly fertile and rich in ores.

This has always led to the eastern principalities, led by Vladimir, now Muscovy, to be poor backwaters compared to the richer west, having to import most of its metals and rich clothes from it. And even with the growth of Muscovy, so it would have seemed it would continue.

However, knowledge by the Permians who reached farther east than any Russian had ever been, into the Ural Mountains, discovered numerous sources of ores, gold, copper, iron, lead and coal, in numbers not seen since the Silesian mines were started by the Germans.

Of course, the sparsely populated mountains, many within and Arctic climate, are not very attractable to many miners, specially with -20° Celcius temperatures in winter.

Nevertheless, Muscovy and Russia as a whole must acquire these metals, at any cost. Veche merchants shall be given charter to bring the ones that are already mined by Permian miners, while the crown has started sending more prisoners and vagabonds under control of Nametsnik Rurikovichs, where they have started constructing an outpost within the location known as Arti, known as St. Ivansburg, within the border with the Hordes to the south. Mining operations are still slow to go, but gold has started makings its way to Moskva, and it must continue.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**Rus, Ruthenia, Russia**



The word Rus is known to originate when the Varagians conquered and settled the separate tribes that called this region h
events.fullText:
**Rus, Ruthenia, Russia**



The word Rus is known to originate when the Varagians conquered and settled the separate tribes that called this region home, founding the city of Kyiv, and the state of the Kievan Rus, with the region under them being known as that from them on.

Ruthenia is a latinized version of Rus, brought with the Christianization of Hungary and Poland, as the Kievan Rus lands had gained recognition with the rest of Latin Europe, and the conversion of the Rus people by Constantinople cemented them as part of the Christian world. Contact with much of Europe would be lost with the mongol invasion and the establishment of the Tartar Yoke.

Russia is a more recent word, codified by state scribes Ivan Cherny and Mikhail Medovartsev under the word Росиа within the Russian script, signifying "of Rus", or Rusia.

With the consolidation of the Russian Principalities under Muscovy, utilizing the title from the former Golden Horde, Grand Prince of the Rus, with Novgorod formally falling under control of Muscovy, albeit through agreements, there can be little doubt that Muscovy now rules over all the Eastern Principalities.

Recently the Principality has started refering itself as the Grand Prince of Russia, but what does that mean? When Kyiv stands strong within the former Ruthenian capital, Smolenks still stands as a rival due to Polish interference and Halych now controls very rich parts of former Kievan Rus.

The title of ruler of the Kievan Rus was once considered to be of similar status to the Basileus or Kaiser of the Western and Eastern Roman Empires.

Grand Prince Ivan III Rurikovich consider this matters, as Halych seems to fall to Latin Christianity influences, Constantinople consorts and marries with sea muslim tartars, and Kyiv , while powerful, has fallen from its former glory. What does it mean to be Russian, and does Muscovy have claim to it?

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
### The Papal Gifts of 1426
Of all Popes in Papal history, Pope Michaelus the Dubious was among the most malevolent and incompetent. Embezzling fierce
events.fullText:
### The Papal Gifts of 1426
Of all Popes in Papal history, Pope Michaelus the Dubious was among the most malevolent and incompetent. Embezzling fierce sums of the tithe to finance Catholic strength in the Mediterranean against the encroaching Islamic influence as recently seen in Sardinia. Even sponsoring the *Holy Knights of Visby* to privateer the expansionist heathens in retaliation, but the incompetent Pope Michaelus would fail to realize the “Holy Knights of Visby” also used the opportunity to raid good Catholics. Even several crusades were planned to target Carthaginian aggression in the Mediterranean, but none of them were ever realized due to diplomatic resources running far too dry for the allies of the Papacy and France.

Despite that, it had not stopped him from sending the egregious gifts in 1426 on January 13th. Gifts aimed towards weak Catholic monarchs with hopes to empower their resistance against the hand of Carthage and its several powerful allies. Although, this was done with the hope they would be used in a future crusade against Carthage regarding Sicily and Sardinia. Among them were of course the “Holy Visby Knights” he endorsed, Queen Caterina of Naples, Regent Florence Sanudo for Crown Prince Marti de Barcelona of Aragon, Duke Alessandro Orsini of Latium, and even King Dinis de Borgonha. 

Being a man with few social skills and a babbling buffoon at his worst, he only sent a small letter along with the gift in an attempt to relay his desired use for the wealth.

“Forwards with rich, glorify God! Piss on the Devil, deny him you.”
-# <@326414282851876875> <@298192682554228738> <@1007414347347271730> <@234699731715096576>  <@150411715757080576> will receive a Papal stimulus check aimed at aiding your realms+monarchs at resisting further heathenous encroachment.

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet: <@255439449448644608> After the closure of the hanseatic offices in Brandenburg, all that was known in Hamburg was that a civil war loomed in the marg
events.fullText:
<@255439449448644608> After the closure of the hanseatic offices in Brandenburg, all that was known in Hamburg was that a civil war loomed in the margraviate. Occupied with a war in the Baltic and expansion elsewhere, Walbeck decided to not involve the Hansa. After all, they abandoned Brandenburg for good reason. Now, when everything has settled, a clearer picture has formed, and a surprise born from the ashes: a League amidst the chaotic breakdown of order! 

Walbeck has to capitalize on this as quickly as possible, for few leagues remain in the Empire, fewer even to oppose the princes of the Empire. He sends word to the towns from the Kontor of Magdeburg, for unlike the one in Praha it’s still functional.

||“To the esteemed members of the Lusatian League,

The cities and towns of the Hanseatic League celebrate your triumph against the destruction brought about by the barons in Brandenburg. Though some members asked for intervention to aid, it is regrettable we abandoned the margraviate a decade ago due to the wars between Emperor and princes. Thankfully, your prowess and unity have proven more than enough to prevail.

We are interested in a partnership with the League, to secure both our status in the Empire. In the Hanse, we were conceded a *Dispensatio* from the prohibition of leagues, though at this point the Emperor does not yield much authority regardless. We ask to enter a defensive pact once our wars in the Baltic are over, to defend against any prince that would attempt to trample against both of our hard-earned privileges; or against any unrest from Bohemia that may spill to you.

Of course, it is my desire that our partnership would be closer, but a Hansetag is to be convened before that. The Hansa will also provide the funds for expansion and infrastructure to establish proper channels of communication and trade between both if the League is interested (*funding a town, you have none*).”||
