events.snippet:
### Long crownless and drowned away in a foreign court, the Prince of Wales seeks a new ally to press his claim!
*Prince Ithel Mathrafal of Wales send
events.fullText:
### Long crownless and drowned away in a foreign court, the Prince of Wales seeks a new ally to press his claim!
*Prince Ithel Mathrafal of Wales sends forth a letter to King Valdemar 4. Estridsen of Denmark  <@210779670663135232> *

“To King Valdemar 4. Estridsen of Denmark, Sweden, and Norway,
I am a Prince of Wales by my father Owain Glyndwr Mathrafal, and my claim has gone unpressed by the French court long enough. I cannot sit idle my whole life, thus I come forward to you seeking aid to reclaim my rightful crown.  

If you are willing to accept me into your court and willing to press my claim for the  Kingdom of Wales, then I bow my head to you my liege. I’d even be happy to marry the fair Lady Catherine Estridatter, and forge my loyalty to you by connecting my kin closer to your splendorous house.

Respectfully, Prince Ithel Mathrafal of Wales”
-# Accept and you will gain a claim upon Wales to place Prince Ithel Mathrafal as ruler who will be your vassal, and the opportunity to marry your daughter to him to bring loyalty closer to your crown.
-# Peeps who may be interested to know about this are  <@1094722105855189062> <@1106651545296195655> <@1417260821674852363> <@1218063544940232784>

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Truth About the Orsini**


In the wake of a new wave of lies from another Orsini crony, perpetuating the fantasy that Carthaginian pirates exist
events.fullText:
**The Truth About the Orsini**


In the wake of a new wave of lies from another Orsini crony, perpetuating the fantasy that Carthaginian pirates exist, a set of horrifying details has come to light centered around the Orsini family, their bathhouses, and a small island off the coast of Pisa.

They traffic in slaves, but not just any slaves. ***Very** young* slaves.

After rioters protesting papal piracy stormed the Archbishop of Palermo's mansion, a trove of documents were recovered, including *confessional accounts* bearing the Archbishop's seal. The accounts detail how the Orsini family employed Genoese merchants, trading for Slavic slaves to fill their galley barracks, to bring *special cargo*, claiming it as the children of their slave cargo.  Based on cargo manifests and bills of lading seized after these documents were uncovered, these slaves were then transported to Rome–into the very bathhouses run by the Orsini, as well as to a small island off the coast of Pisa reserved for the Orsini patriarchs and their wealthy, powerful allies.

Those few high-ranking Catholic clergy remaining in Sicily following the riots have expressed outrage, claiming on the one hand that the documents are fake or that there is nothing in them, while simultaneously claiming that the documents are real and privileged, and that the breaking of a confessional seal is the true crime! Nevertheless, this explains why the Genoan slave trade was tolerated for so long despite Catholic wails about morality. It was all to feed the perverse appetites of the rich and powerful.

So disgusted by this was the Sultan, he has spared no expense distributing these documents to every port of call in the Mediterranean and beyond, making hundreds of copies, translated into more than a dozen languages, and having copies nailed to the doors of churches across the Mediterranean basin, such that no man can claim ignorance.

||Propaganda Post||

<@150411715757080576> <@460814348487753730> <@1007414347347271730> <@302128861209165824> <@422498641425596418> <@315868897012219904> <@190640828954181632> <@564196329132261376> <@493830084893999104> <@698400527834415145> <@307587752860581888> <@792904515795550218> <@137101075411894272> <@180085023389515776> <@261290667106369536> <@327561459976372224> <@423689694258397195> <@759277551117402194> <@192490969092980737>

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Dede of Hoom Tonge

*"In knowleching that the lond of Engelond is an Englissh contree, y-peopled with lordes and comoun folk that speken Englissh,
events.fullText:
## Dede of Hoom Tonge

*"In knowleching that the lond of Engelond is an Englissh contree, y-peopled with lordes and comoun folk that speken Englissh, from hennesforth shal the speche of the King or Quene and hir court be y-maked Englissh, for to removen any pretense of the rulers of Engelond being Frenssh in herte or in mynde."*

Albert Piast and William Neville both broached the idea before Parliament, that to secure the potential loyalty of the English nobility at large, a nod towards their English identity may go a long way. In addition, an implied vow against royal ambitions in France was included to help soothe concerns over Neville militarism. 

**This edict extends into Wales, where the speaking of both French and Welsh is forbidden in higher court, in favor of a native English.**

<@1094722105855189062>

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Diwedd y gân yw'r geiniog
-# (Roughly: Show me the money)

Preparations for war consume all and a [cold wind rises](https://discord.com/channels/5
events.fullText:
### Diwedd y gân yw'r geiniog
-# (Roughly: Show me the money)

Preparations for war consume all and a [cold wind rises](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1519818952912539750) across the British Isles.

It is frankly not a situation that the Justiciar of Wales, Sir Gilbert of West Riding, wishes to be in. His family was a [newly elevated](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1518333983719559349) one that has achieved its position by spite of Owain just as much as its own competence during the wars. Honors that the Barons Cardigan or even up and coming Tudurs felt they deserved were instead handed to him and his kin. Now these notables felt personal enmity toward his administration at a time when the crown in London was faltering and open war about to tear away any support he might have received from them.

***Money** was needed above all.*

Sir Gilbert was self-aware enough to know that his **abrasive** personality had won him few friends in Wales. He could offer concessions, positions, alliances and all the rest to secure loyalty from some. However he would ultimately need secure funds in order to make his continued rule a reality throughout the Principality. In order to do that he would need to make use of…creative methods of fundraising.

**Pirates**, or more generously privateers, have begun operating out of freeports along the Welsh coast. They often sail into the Irish sea, raid some continental traders meant for Ireland <@1218063544940232784> or western Scotland <@1417260821674852363>, then return to a friendly port - the isle of Anglesey most often <@1106651545296195655>. They bear little formal allegiance but for the mark of their language; English for its captains and men at arms and Welsh for many of the crewmen and longbowmen on each ship's deck.

*Justiciar Gilbert has referred to these as unscrupulous ‘men of fortune’ and ignored their presence so near his seat of power. He has, completely coincidently, began to come into a far larger than normal amount of money in recent months.*

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**Hatera, at the foot of Mount Olympus**

The wind carries ash-scented mist through the broken stone terraces where once stood small rural shrines, no
events.fullText:
**Hatera, at the foot of Mount Olympus**

The wind carries ash-scented mist through the broken stone terraces where once stood small rural shrines, now half-swallowed by ivy and silence. Yet today, that silence is no longer empty.

Gemistos Plethon walks among the gathered crowd as a teacher of forgotten order. His robes are simple, his voice even simpler, but every word he speaks feels carved rather than spoken, as though it had waited centuries to be heard again.

He speaks of reason, harmony with ones ancestors, and the divine structure that once shaped Hellas before doctrine and division fractured it. His writings, copied and recopied in hidden scriptoria, circulate quietly among students and clerics who no longer feel satisfied with the certainty of the present age.

Around him stand listeners: monks who hesitate before crossing themselves, scholars who pretend this is only philosophy, and villagers who do not yet have a name for what they are hearing, only the sense that something ancient is waking beneath Olympus itself.

Plethon raises a worn manuscript, its edges softened by travel and concealment. He reads not with fervor, but with calm conviction, as if reminding the Hellens of something that they had simply chosen to forget.

events.author: Dods
events.snippet:
# The castle of Wied
Standing tall on the hills of Wied on the edge of it's expansive forests, a castle acts as the barrier between East and West for
events.fullText:
# The castle of Wied
Standing tall on the hills of Wied on the edge of it's expansive forests, a castle acts as the barrier between East and West for the Prince-Bishopric of Koln. The Rhine dividing not only the land but influence.

Since the conquering of Wied and sale of Altenkirchen, an unincorporated culture has begun to play a more important part in the Prince-Bishop's court, local counts and the such seen as a disconnected part of the realms administration. 

The Moselle Franconian people of the realm reside in a rather essential position, with the corridor to the Westfalen lands being only accessible through the Counts of Westerwald, accords must be made with local power in order to further goals Eastwards.

Leopold sends Simon De Duren to the castle of Wied in order to strike an accord with local lords, riding with banners of the Bishopric and Habsburg dynasty and a lavish share of foods and beer, whilst a few days of dining and entertainment keep conversation going, an uneasy tension remains prominent, leading to the nobility of Wied blunty ask what Simon is here for.

The informalities coming to an abrupt end, seeing the ruse being up, Simon speaks up
"The court of the Arch-Bishop requires safe travels through the lands of Westerwald, the corridor to Westfalen. It is an expectation of the Arch-Bishop but we're no fools to how things work really, what would you like in return for handwaving any hassles that may burden us traveling through your lands?"

The counts discuss among themselves, with the occasional rattle of cups on the table from enthusiastic displays, as one of them pipes up, "Say if we're allowed to enjoy our time in these fine lands and you make sure we our pantries remain well stocked, we'll avoid giving any grief for you, seems only fair after all you ransacked our lands. The hospitality seems a reasonable compensation.

**Elaborate court life privilege will be added for the Nobility
Moselle Franconian will become tolerated**

<@792904515795550218> <@253679178552049665>

events.author: Bas:
events.snippet:
***The Veche’s Merchants set out to Eastern Europe!***

The success of the Veche’s merchants throughout Russia has brought them much success. Trade, b
events.fullText:
***The Veche’s Merchants set out to Eastern Europe!***

The success of the Veche’s merchants throughout Russia has brought them much success. Trade, both import and export into different markets has begun to make great strides in increasing prosperity and wealth within Russia. Allowing the production of its burgers to be sold elsewhere, something that assists rather heavily given that the lower classes in Russia does not yet have the wealth or money to be able to afford many of the more expensive goods such as fine cloth or the like. 

Though these successes have brought the required capital to attempt to push their influence past just the borders of Russia, of course this shall be a much more difficult task. Given that within Russia there was essentially no competition, elsewhere this shall be a more difficult challenge. Though one that they seem interested in tackling to help secure their influence. Requiring more wealth and profit to ensure they have enough influence to remain in their current strong position within Russia, they held a position of privilege, one they were rather interested in protecting.

events.author: Bas:
events.snippet:
***Lviv***
 

As one of the most precious crown jewels in all of Eastern Europe. It would be foolish to not travel south east towards one of the most
events.fullText:
***Lviv***
 

As one of the most precious crown jewels in all of Eastern Europe. It would be foolish to not travel south east towards one of the most prosperous realms and cities in all of Europe. Merchants from the Veche were already rather familiar with the city, given that much of Novgorod’s trade and goods found their final home within the halls of the city's great markets. 

They offer several goods used in local industries, being a major source of the fur trimmings used in the clothing made in the city due to its powerful dye industry. But also possibilities for more Iron to fuel its arms industries if the demand exceeds production within the region. And as the gold mines in the Ural mountains begin to build up, an alternate source of precious metals as well. 

It appears that they seek licenses and the allowance to create a local Veche office to assist their merchants with trading in the city and surrounding region to help increase the flow of trade throughout the region. Hoping for the reputation as well as the fact that the current head of the Veche is a native Ruthenian speaker to further assist their credibility. 
<@1432820281000333392>

events.author: Bas:
events.snippet:
***Praha***
While the gold mines of the Urals do not hold a candle to the massive production of precious metals found and forged within the realms of
events.fullText:
***Praha***
While the gold mines of the Urals do not hold a candle to the massive production of precious metals found and forged within the realms of Bohemia. The Merchants from the Veche still have plenty of other goods to peddle. Having no affiliation to any religious or local Empire conflicts they find themselves a neutral party trying to find an open place within the market.

They come bearing quite a few goods not found elsewhere in Europe. At least not exported in large enough amounts to feed the realms needs. Amber bought and traded from the Baltic. But most of all large amounts of salt from the city of Rusa together with fur and beeswax.

They seek to be given allowance to create a small trading presence within Bohemia to help export their goods as well as import goods that Russia lacks such as Jewelry
<@261290667106369536>

events.author: Bas:
events.snippet:
**Constantinople**
Even with the religious divide that has begun to spread as a great chasm between Russia and the Romans. That does not mean that tra
events.fullText:
**Constantinople**
Even with the religious divide that has begun to spread as a great chasm between Russia and the Romans. That does not mean that trade must stop at those borders, even if there are some complaints from the local clergy before they set off. Representatives of the Veche set off to the city of the words desire to peddle their wares.

Luckily enough, the language barrier was less of a problem due to the number of Greek speakers among their number, something that would likely help ease some of the barriers when it comes to their efforts to improve trade relations within the Black Sea. It may be difficult with the friction and tension between it and Moscow. The Merchants of Novgorod hope to be able to push past that difference as their own faction, even if it possibly clashes with the higher clergy in Moscow…
<@302128861209165824>

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
***An Imperial Declaration of a Holy Guarantee***

By the Grace of Almighty God, We, Thomas I Palaiologos, Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans, Basileu
events.fullText:
***An Imperial Declaration of a Holy Guarantee***

By the Grace of Almighty God, We, Thomas I Palaiologos, Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans, Basileus of the Eastern Roman Empire, proclaim before Heaven and Earth:

From this day forth, so long as the Imperial Crown rests upon the House of Palaiologos, the Kingdoms of Cyprus, Cilicia, and Jerusalem shall stand beneath the protection of the Roman Empire.

Let every ruler of Europe, Africa, and Asia hear these words and weigh them carefully.

Whosoever shall raise sword against the lawful crowns of Cyprus, Cilicia, or Jerusalem shall not contend with those kingdoms alone. He shall call forth the wrath of the Roman Emperor himself, and behind that wrath shall march the full military might of the Eastern Roman Empire.

This pledge is made openly before God, before princes, before kings and common man alike. It shall be proclaimed so that every sovereign may know where the Roman Empire stands.

May God judge Our House should We fail in this sacred oath.

Given beneath Our Golden Seal in Constantinople,

**Thomas I Palaiologos
Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans**

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Collapse of the Mamluk Sultanate**


At the turn of the 15th century, the Mamluk Sultanate was poised to become the next Caliphate.  Fabulously
events.fullText:
**The Collapse of the Mamluk Sultanate**


At the turn of the 15th century, the Mamluk Sultanate was poised to become the next Caliphate.  Fabulously wealthy, immensely powerful, with vast holdings.  All of this, however, hid a festering rot at the heart of the Sultanate, one which manifested itself, and within the century's first decade the Sultanate had collapsed.

The first signs were an explosion of internal strife, with the discontent of long-dissatisfied nobility boiling over into outright rebellion.  The Sultan's levies refused their call to arms, siding instead with the rebels.  Even those professional armies that stood were demoralized and hard-pressed to contain the conflict, a conflict which saw their ultimate defeat.

As if that was not enough, the crusader remnants on Cyprus seized the opportunity to attack the ‘Sick Man in Egypt’, inflicting greater harm still and seizing away the ancient city of Antioch, the Holy City of Al-Quds–Jerusalem to the Christians–and its surrounding lands.

This was a cause for great concern in Carthage.  A Christian invasion of the Holy Land was of little concern as history had proven that they could be driven out if the need arose.  What's more, the city was under Castilian rule, and the Castilians had proven to be some of the more reasonable Catholics that Carthage had treated with.  The greater concern was the instability of the Nile delta, for great multitudes of people resided there and massive wealth moved from its banks out into the Mediterranean.

For now, though, the Sultan would watch for further signs of collapse in the region and, if necessary, intervene to secure Carthaginian commercial interests, as well as stability in the region.

<@874750350538727434>

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Battle of Pest #
Following the battle of Szeged, both armies regrouped and marched to the city of Pest. When the Ruthenian and Hungarian forces
events.fullText:
# The Battle of Pest #
Following the battle of Szeged, both armies regrouped and marched to the city of Pest. When the Ruthenian and Hungarian forces again clashed on the outskirts of the large town, the result would see another glorious Hungarian victory and forcing to Ruthenian armies to retreat from the field. At a estimated cost of about 1900 casualties, the Hungarian army was able to inflict 4300 casualties on the ruthenian forces. The result of this battle began to feed into Prince Jean de Valois growing belief that one more decisive battle against the pretenders forces would see the conflict finally ended. However, little did the Prince know that King Louis of Ruthenia had statically relocated his army near to the town of Szolnok, marshy ground well suited for defense. Ground where he planned to make his final stand. As Prince Jean overconfidently marched his army towards the marshes of Szolnok, the ultimate battle to decide the conflict was about to begin.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# The Battle of Szolnok #
In the early hours of the first day of battle, the Hungarian army positioned itself into battle lines and prepared to attack
events.fullText:
# The Battle of Szolnok #
In the early hours of the first day of battle, the Hungarian army positioned itself into battle lines and prepared to attack. When the Hungarian forces assaulted the prepared and fortified lines of the Ruthenian forces, all attacks were rebuffed with the loss of 3600 casualties. A bloody toll given that the enemy losses lay around 1700 to 1800 losses. This stubborn tenacity surprised Prince Jean, but he resolved to see the battle won regardless. There would be no retreat from this battle.
On the second days, minor skirmishes between Hungarian and Ruthenians took place across the battlefield as both forces planned for their next attack. These skirmishes largely favored the Hungarians, though losses on both sides barely exceeded 500. However, this fed into Prince Jean's delusional that the Ruthenians, although strong, were close to breaking. Meanwhile in the camp of the Ruthenians, King Louis had been keeping a tight leash on his forces. He had a good position and intended to hold it.
The the next three days of battle subsequently followed a repetitive pattern of Hungarian charges against the Ruthenian lines, all of which failures that cost the Hungarians dearly. Prince Jean, despite the dire situation and much to the consternation of his men, still delusional believed that the Ruthenians were close to breaking and believed that they would break in the center. He would charge the center and break the Ruthenian scum.
On the sixth day, Prince Jean assembled his very reluctant men and attacked the Hungarian center with the remainder of his forces. The bloody fighting that followed quickly became devoid of strategy and devolved into a mass brawl, with Prince Jean in the thick of it. Then came the first blow, then the second, then the next. Blood split from the Prince of Hungary and he collapsed. With his death, the  rest of his forces surrendered or defected to the Ruthenian.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet: As a result of the battle, the Hungarian casualties reached roughly 12600 compared to 8500 Ruthenians. 21000 casualties. A bloody harvest indeed in th
events.fullText:
As a result of the battle, the Hungarian casualties reached roughly 12600 compared to 8500 Ruthenians. 21000 casualties. A bloody harvest indeed in the marshes of Szolnok. In the aftermath of the battle, the bloodied Ruthenian forces march forth to Buda and demanded entry. With the death of the Prince, the people of Buda felt disheartened and opened the gates to the great city. King Louis, still wearing his bloody armor led forth a force into the castle, seized and then deposed Queen Johanna before sitting upon the throne. 

In the coming weeks, King Louis would be coronated as King of Hungary and Croatia in Buda before passing multiple decrees restoring Salic Laws of succession and handing out multiple privileges to the nobility. To reward the two noble houses that supported him, King Louis I & II would proclaim Lord Istvan Bathory as Nádor, or Palatine, of Hungary and elevated Lord Tador Sátoraljaújhelyi to the Duchy of Moldavia.

Thus, the Hungarian realm had entered a new chapter in its history. A new chapter which's introduction was written with the blood of those lost at Szolnok. A chapter that ended the first great and glorious age of the Anjou monarchy. 

## With that, the Red Succession has conclude in a Ruthenian Victory. Thank you for your participation! ##

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**Return of Old Rites**

As the Catholic frontlines collapsed Orthodox forces marched towards Targoviste, encircling it and pressuring Balc Basarab to
events.fullText:
**Return of Old Rites**

As the Catholic frontlines collapsed Orthodox forces marched towards Targoviste, encircling it and pressuring Balc Basarab to surrender. Balc did so, as there was no longer any point in resisting, as he was outnumbered two to one. The rebels demanded Balc’s abdication, but let him and his family leave for Moldavia. Things will be shaken up in Wallachia with the rebels’ victory and the Basarabs deposed from their role of Dukes of Wallachia.

The rebellion devastated all of Wallachia as land burned and people died and buildings crumbled. Dobruja was hit hard by the massacres of Catholics that took place there as the flames of the rebellion sparked. Another place that was devastated by the roaring fire of the war was Sânladislau as the siege devastated the city and Orthodox victory in the battle over the city and its castle had destroyed what little remained of Saint’s Cathedral. The storm clouds that had cast a shadow over the lands of Wallachia were finally clearing, and the brighter days may return.

With Balc’s abdication, a candidate for the new duke was proposed and accepted. A six-year-old boy: Balc de Bukov, a grandson to the previous duke through his mother, became the new Duke of Wallachia. Although the new duke is too young to fulfill his new duties, one of the rebellion's leaders Octavian de Cherven would act as his regent and mentor until the young duke comes of age. 
...

With the arrival of the new duke, the Old Rites returned to the Wallachian court. Some of the Catholics were killed in the massacres and some were forcibly converted during the rebellion, but many are still living in Wallachia, especially in the lands of West Muntenia. In the region, the question of Sânladislau arises and more specifically the question of the city’s name. The duke regent and the Orthodox clergy wish to revert the name of the city to its original, but some folk in the city wish to keep the current name in order to preserve the history and legacy of the founder. The duke regent would not listen as the city wasn't on the Orthodox side during the rebellion, so it had little say in the matter. The city’s name will be restored to Zimnicea and the duke regent’s explanation for it is the restoration and preservation of the history of the whole region. Some of the devout Catholics in the city were unhappy with the change and their discontent was drowned out by the praise of the Orthodox townsfolk and indifference of everyone else. The Old Rites returned, but the new Wallachia will march on.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## *The Defenestration of Praha*

[The burning of Jan Hus](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1519884313242374154) wa
events.fullText:
## *The Defenestration of Praha*

[The burning of Jan Hus](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1519884313242374154) was a seismic event, whose shockwaves in Trier were felt as far as Bohemia at the pace at which the information could travel. The letters he had skipped out to compatriots of his at the University of Praha, facilitated by friendly nobles who had accompanied him, had certainly kept the wider community informed of his conditions during his imprisonment and lead-up to his execution. The atmosphere of the realm was already frayed and tense at the time of his arrest, resulting in the gathering of hundreds of Bohemian gentry, high and low, with their signatures affixing petitions decrying the imprisonment of Jan Hus and disputing the charges against him. This was a show of solidarity, and unity across confessional lines, typically a display reserved only for opposing the abuse of royal powers, such as the **League of Lords** which had briefly imprisoned **Karel II**, and reconstituted a breadth of the kingdom’s government from their own figures. It was a unity that was tested with the execution of Hus, and the further elaboration of Papal condemnation of his teachings through the office of [Elpido Abate](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520270026085503106).

The nobles’ front splintered at the news of Hus’ death, with the fear of a growing Papal militancy in response to his heresies becoming ever truer. Months after the martyrdom of Jan Hus, the ***Sdružení Husitů*** was formed: the Association / League of Hussites. In turn, weeks after, the ***Sdružení katolíků*** was formed in response. The Catholic League was smaller in number, but claimed a greater portion of the high lords and magnates of the realm. These organizations were formed in the context of such prior movements in the East, like the aforementioned League of Lords and the consistent *konfederacja* of Poland. The recently elected King **Jindřich II**, a Renaissance-patronizing and Italian-educated Luxemburg, could only tolerate the two leagues, for he depended greatly upon the nobility to affirm his reign. His unstable rule amidst the backdrop of a sharp incline in sectarian tensions highlighted how fractured Bohemia, the wealthiest realm in Germany and much of Europe, had truly become.

The Archbishop of Mainz tightened the interdict against Praha in October, 1425, for its hosting of Jan z Jesenice, a former legal student of the University of Praha who had issued many legal defenses of Jan Hus and disputing his charges, and his execution. This sparked the events of November, the “**All Souls’ Day**” coup, where Hussite Praguers expelled Catholic clergy from churches and parishes across the capital, replacing them with their own, Hussite-sympathetic priests. These events tightened a noose on Catholic rights in Praha, forbidding them to have service anywhere except Vyšehrad, or outside the city’s walls. By December, Jindřich II had finally been compelled to act, and demanded the restoration of the expelled clergy and for the reallotment of parishes across the city, leaving just eight to the Hussites as a concession. The Archbishop of Mainz announced a ban on rendering communion in both kinds, but it was ignored by most in Praha. The interdict on the capital was however lifted, but only after Jindřich II compelled Jan z Jesenice to depart the city in 1426.

Even as the interdict was released, the immense pressure placed upon Jindřich II by the magnates of the realm and his dynastic ties across Christendom, whom he still depended upon in case he was abducted like his father before him, resulted in him further restricting Hussite liturgies. They were only allowed in select churches, and public sermons and songs, which had greatly contributed to the rise of Hussitism in Bohemia, were also restricted to just three churches in Praha: St. Benedict’s in the Old Town, Our Lady of the Snows, and St. Ambrose’s in the New Town. Naturally this move sparked significant dissent across the capital, and resultantly when Jindřich visited the Church of St. Apollinaris, he was surrounded by a crowd led by Mikuláš z Husi, who demanded of him the return to the conditions of the city prior to the year’s decrees. Mikuláš was expelled from Praha for this, and would eventually find his way to [Moldavia](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1518640359486394398/1519461500551434240), with a gathering of soldiers. 
<@493830084893999104>

Following the expulsion of Mikuláš, Jindřich went further and replaced the New Town Rada, turning it from a Hussite to a Catholic majority. This new council immediately began a string of prosecutions against the Hussites, imprisoning those who had petitioned the king with Mikuláš, expelling students, and banning Hussite processions throughout the city. The latter move was aimed almost entirely at the growing popularity of **Jan Želivsky**, who had been expelled from the Church of St. Stephen by Jindřich’s decrees and had since taken up preaching at the Church of Our Lady of the Snows. These moves of repression characteristically only radicalized the Hussites of Praha and across Bohemia, where the bans in the capital led to pilgrimages to the countryside where Hussite leaders convened and schemed for an armed uprising in the New Town, to return it to the status quo.

Many who appeared at Želivský’s sermon at the Church of Our Lady of the Snows were armed, as planned for. The crowd went to the Church of St. Stephen after his first sermon, a church which had been Hussite before Jindřich’s restrictions on the liturgies, and drove out the Catholics attending their Sunday Mass. Looting the rectory, the firebrand Želviský delivered another sermon, and the armed mob set down Žitná street towards the New Town Hall. As the crowd gathered before the hall, mayor Jan Podvinský appeared before them from his balcony, where he was addressed with their demands to release those whom he had imprisoned for supporting two-kind communion. Podvinský refused, and in the courtyard even, the crowd saw some of the prisoners being carried out by armed guards, as if hostages. The tension between the council and the crowd grew until, at last, one of the prisoners was executed on the spot without warning. The crowd surged forward then, armed, and stormed the New Town Hall. Seizing the councilors within, and others who had accompanied them, they defenestrated them to the street below, where the rest of the mob beat them to death. Fourteen people died, including four Catholic councilors. 

The Chamberlain, **Jindřich Novak**, himself a member of the Hussites in the royal court, arrived with a detachment of mounted soldiers, though he did so deliberately late, and left the scene without engaging the crowd. Homeowners and tenants across the New Town were summoned to elect four new magistrates for the council, who could serve provisionally until new ones were appointed. Thus the next move for the Hussites was to secure the recognition of the King, and negotiate the pardoning of their crimes in return for securing the status quo they now created. The issue which arose, and which they failed to anticipate, was the death of the youthful Jindřich II crossing the River Spréva during his royal tour of Upper Lusatia. The news of the king’s death unleashed another torrent of unrest in Praha, with monasteries assaulted and Catholics expelled from the city. Quickly the Queen **Maria von Baden** tried to gather the royal council for a provisional government, aided greatly by the High Burgrave **Tichomír z Příbor**. To first try and ease the crisis the duo seemed willing to issue pardons and negotiate with the Praguer rebels, but soon decided instead to send royal troops to occupy the royal castle in Praha and the Lesser Town, to intimidate the Hussites. This backfired for the royalists, who were then attacked by the New Town Hussites and the Old Town Hussites simultaneously. After a few days of urban skirmishing, a truce was signed, which handed over Vyšehrad back to the provisional government, which was of course tasked firstly with securing the election of a new king.

In the backdrop of the religious strife across Bohemia, which had now broken out into sporadic bouts of fighting across the cities and countryside, the lack of a clear successor to Jindřich II severely disrupted efforts to bring the Hussites and Catholics together. There could be no movement to negotiate the recognition of a dual-faith system without a legitimate and sufficiently weak monarch, and with the Diet called and swamped with dissent and fights, no such move could be made uniformly. Maria von Baden fell out of favor with her own Catholic party, as a Swabian Queen who had learned very little of the Czech culture, and for attempting to secure the election of her four year old son, Jan, to the throne. With the country having found itself in the chaos it did, the Catholic League and its associated lords broke off from the Diet and convened their own at the capital of the Rožmberka Demesne, **Český Krumlov**. Fearing the rapid advance of the militant Hussites, and how the Praguers could rely on the rural communities to field great hosts, the Catholics felt necessary a course of reaction which could only further destabilize the realm. But, if their plot worked, it would secure their victory over the Hussites.

The Catholic League, an a illegitimate Diet, elected the aunt of Jindřich II, sister to Karel II, **Hana z Lucembursko**, the Archduchess of Austria—and thus, ***jure uxoris***, **Siegmund von Habsburg**. 
<@190640828954181632>

In return, the rest of the Bohemian Diet, now under Calixtine Hussite dominance, moved to depose the provisional government of Tichomír z Příbor, and elected **Jindřich Novak**, a devoted Hussite, as High Burgrave. The Queen-Dowager Maria von Baden was thus rendered politically obsolete, an ornament for legitimacy, while erecting a Hussite provisional government to see through the armed struggles awaiting the Catholic League’s actions.

**OOC Note: These events are carrying on through the first 2-3 months of the session. By the time the Hussite Wars situation pops for you in game, consider these events having fully passed.**

<@150411715757080576> <@460814348487753730> <@423689694258397195> <@1423762053050662983> <@215639063502258176> <@763652399784919080> <@1432820281000333392> <@545657694489542666> <@210779670663135232> <@112960565344804864> <@792904515795550218> <@451075783088275497> <@253679178552049665> <@883471267506843668> <@288410456526422016> <@255439449448644608>

Following the troubling
events.fullText:
**Jindřich Novak declared the Anti Christ <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> **
*(doing this after the months have past)*

Following the troubling news emerging from Bohemia, it seems that divisions within the Church over recent decades have led to outrageous reformist uprisings, with cries such as, "Death to the Pope!" 

This uprising must be put down, and those responsible put to the sword. There can be no reasoning with those who abandon Holy Mother Church, for as Scripture teaches:

```diff
-"If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned."
- John 15:6```

Therefore, there is a 1000 Gold Award for anyone who captures and bring Jindřich Novak to justice!

Furthermore, anyone who joins the league will be rewarded and funded to the full extent that is needed to destroy this menance that has brought fire and war to the peaceful kingdom of Bohemia!

It will be the job of the new Nádor, or Palatine, of Hungary Lord Istvan Bathory as the noble house of Bathory.

The piracy perpetrated by the Visby Pirates has gone on for too long already, and it will be put to an end.  Now.  The Visby will be forcibly expelled from the Mediterranean and peace shall be restored to the seas, delivered at the end of a blade.

With debates over Bohemia and the North Konstantin has had enough of political infighting yet can not act as an autocrat to declare or unite foreign policy of the towns. Opting for a subtle touch in the Parliament of 1429 Luckau would offer a bill of expansion of rural exports into the North, though genuine at an outward glance the implications are most curious for to do trade in the area would mean securing the security of said merchants who would demand their own towns involvement, creating a knock on effect.

Taking only some months later the towns of lower Lusatia would almost universally declare the need for a campaign into the warlord realm of former Brandenburg.. just as planned.

Carnarvon has long been the gatekeeper of Wales's northern wealth, but Anglesey has long been its source. The rise of patrolling sh
events.fullText:
### The Holy Isle

Carnarvon has long been the gatekeeper of Wales's northern wealth, but Anglesey has long been its source. The rise of patrolling ships ~~to combat the Justiciars piracy~~ has proven the need to fortify such regions no matter the cost.

Thus the long neglected [Beaumaris Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beaumaris_Castle) is thus chosen as the spot for an extensive rebuilding effort by Justiciar Gilbert Wentworth. While Carnarvon protects the eastern entrance of the Menai Strait, this new keep on the holy island of Anglesey shall protect the west.

events.snippet:
**The Chalice Coup**
<@261290667106369536><@763652399784919080><@252560004069064705> 

With imperial maneuvering the pro imperial faction backed by Ko
events.fullText:
**The Chalice Coup**
<@261290667106369536><@763652399784919080><@252560004069064705> 

With imperial maneuvering the pro imperial faction backed by Konstantin would join the second Hussite crusade, angling to secure hard imperial support for the army and even economic concessions. years of planning would crumble in five days. the sudden Hussite counter attack would smash the Sorbian forces, the towns on the border with Hussite populations would rise in revolt, joining Bohemia and the rule of Luckau would collapse, the defenses of the town, built by the Byzantines would fall to cannon and elite veteran troops in a matter of hours. 

Konstantin would be defenestrated from St. Nicholas church, his family chased from the town by troops and mobs of men. in its place a council of pro hussites, priests, soldiers and nobles would dominate the league, effectively becoming a vassal of the government in Prauge. Though a roaring success in the area, the Hussite armies would suffer the heavy costs of leaving their southern flank completely open, a sad irony as now Lusatia is not only on the losing side once but twice....

events.fullText:
### Wales Divided, Still Stands
Faction Views previous: https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1518755524210851931/1520487454501634190

The factions of Wales have come to no firm conclusion...**however** the loss of the Nevilles by Scottish betrayal have changed opinions.

Continued Papal support has convinced many lords that the more extreme views of the Nevilles regarding England's faith are misguided. Meanwhile, the moderate stance of Edward Plantagenet attracts ever more support amongst the Marcher lords, the Mortimers, and the Tudurs. Even if the Justiciar remains impassive, or even pro-Neville, the rest of the country moves out a sizeable contingent into England proper.

*A Marcher army readies to march support the Percys at York. An Irish allied contingent is also hired from allied Dublin and the FitzGeralds <@1218063544940232784>.*

### Coronation of Leopold II in Aachen

After the victories in Bohemia, Wurttemberg and also having his debt paid off, Leopold II can finally be crown
events.fullText:
### Coronation of Leopold II in Aachen

After the victories in Bohemia, Wurttemberg and also having his debt paid off, Leopold II can finally be crowned in Aachen.

A dignified ceremony, owing to the ~~greedy~~ well-mannered nature of Leopold.

And now, he looks south, to be crowned as emperor in his Italienzug!

*The Free City of Frankfurt is condemned!*

*The Pope is lauded!*

events.snippet:
# The Battle of Szeged #

After meeting each other outside the town of Szeged, the Ruthenian and Johannian Valois armies clashed in a bloody brawl tha
events.fullText:
# The Battle of Szeged #

After meeting each other outside the town of Szeged, the Ruthenian and Johannian Valois armies clashed in a bloody brawl that resulted in roughly 2400 Ruthenian casualties compared to the 1200 losses of the Hungarian combined army. In the aftermath of the battle, Prince Jean de Valois felt a great sense of confidence in his army and the ability to win the conflict. However, the Ruthenian army had conducted an orderly retreat despite the loss and prepared to make moves towards the large town of Pest. The Hungarian armies also moved to nearby Buda and planned to cross at Pest to head east, laying the groundwork for the Battle of Pest.

Just as swift as the Hussite council had taken power and supported Bohemian forces Hungarian troops would with great cost crush
events.fullText:
**The Bavarian Hold**

Just as swift as the Hussite council had taken power and supported Bohemian forces Hungarian troops would with great cost crush Hussite troops in central Bohemia, the council would be overturned. Troops under the Wittelsbachs entering Saint Nichlos Church and declaring the League an illegal institution abetting the Hussite, who would be summarily executed outside and put into a mass grave. 

With having no ability to defend themselves, Poland elsewhere and seen as helping the Hussite burning away all good diplomatic will elsewhere.  Killing two birds with one stone the region would be declared a fiefdom of Bavaria, using their claim as lords of Brandenburg to legally integrate them as under the title. the newly risen duke of Sorbia would be Klementia August Von Wittelsbach, a pretender to Bavaria who can be conveniently sent to this land to pacify the angry populace who not so long ago declared they would have no Wittelsbach rulers, a cunning move by the emperor yet a risky one for they have little patience or want to put resources into purging the Hussites as the Hungarians have, even as they claim control of the land they create a hotbed of radicals, all united in their hatred of one thing, Bavarians.

**(Sorbia Becomes Ducal vassal of Bavaria, the League is dissolved and Autocratic rule is enforced, Hussites from Bohemia may flee to the region as Bavaria is lax on such things)**

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Cardiff Double
<@1218063544940232784> 

The English army arrived to force the submission of Wales, as expected. Edward Tudur refused to give battl
events.fullText:
### Cardiff Double
<@1218063544940232784> 

The English army arrived to force the submission of Wales, as expected. Edward Tudur refused to give battle for some months as the English army set up an extensive siege around the castle and town. He wrote weekly to his cousin, the Princess of Powys, for her to send her 8,000 men to join them post haste. She continued to press for a harassment of the English marches but eventually linked up with the Welsh-Irish army at Brecknock.

The Welsh-Irish army suddenly attacked the complacent English army to **massive victory** before the main enemy host camped in the hills above could reinforce the battle. By the time they returned to re-invest the siege the Welshmen had hunkered down in their position and made it a a prize only achievable with much blood. This second battle, much to the surprise of everyone, was also a crushing victory for the Welsh.

*The Welsh army has emerged victorious...and shattered. As renewed armies of northmen, Scots, and even Welsh come from endless England it is unclear if Edward Plantangant <@1106651545296195655> can press his hard won advantage in England proper.*

<@205264068053827584>

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**The Balkan League**

Under the threat of the Angevin aggression towards the realms of Serbia and Wallachia, the Balkan League was formed to protect
events.fullText:
**The Balkan League**

Under the threat of the Angevin aggression towards the realms of Serbia and Wallachia, the Balkan League was formed to protect each other from Hungarian and Halych-Volhynian ambitions in the region. With the Anjous threatening the League, Kyiv stepped up and joined as a guarantor of the independence of the League's member realms. Kievan guarantees alone were not enough to deter Angevin aggression, the League sought outside help, and their calls were answered by the Queen of Poland. They would stand with the League’s side in case of Angevin attack.

events.fullText:
**Time At the Roman Baths 🛁 <:Beer:1514296080865558588> **
<@763652399784919080> 

After the coronation of Emperor Leopold in Rome, he was invited to the baths, where he was joined by the Pope. There were maidens, beer, wine, and every earthly desire he could wish for. It was a time to indulge in the pleasures of this world, and he would enjoy it!

Let this be a lesson! Divination is an affront to the Holy Mother Church, an invitation to the Devil, and a path that leads only to death and damnation. Let there be no doubt: the Church is the foundation of your salvation. Forsake her, and you forsake Gods grace. Turn from her teachings, and the pit of Hell awaits those who embrace darkness over the light of Christ.

Perhaps it was a lack of attention. Perhaps it was too much. It seems impossible to know the whims of heaven in these matters.

events.fullText:
### The Cost of War

Perhaps it was a lack of attention. Perhaps it was too much. It seems impossible to know the whims of heaven in these matters.

Henry Tudur, heir to the rising Welsh family and a strong English claim, has died of a sudden sickness during the war. The family now looks to its last male heir of **Arthur Tudur**; though Edward and Margaret still hope for more children in their young marriage.

**The Boethios Reforms <:prestige:587042877356376103> **

Following the invention of the printing press in <@1007414347347271730> , there must be standardised versions of the bible that will be printed. Bibles that are not confirmed by the church, WILL NOT be allowed to be printed in Workshops. 

This must be enforced across **ALL REALMS** @here

The bible that has been produced for printing is the https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1513260384835080306 Jacobus Bible Standardised in 1376. Boethios Halkias, a Greek who defected from the Byzantium Empire <@302128861209165824> has made some minor changes to the bible to better follow its Greek Origins, including affirming text such as:

```diff
-Old Translation (Hebrews 13:17)
-"Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls..."

+Reformed Jacobus Bible (1441)
+"Obey those whom the Holy Church hath set over you, and submit unto their godly instruction; for they keep watch over your souls as stewards appointed before God."

-Old Translation (James 2:17)
-"Faith by itself, if it does not have works, is dead."

+Reformed Jacobus Bible (1441)
+"Faith, if it be not adorned with works of mercy and holy devotion, is dead; and blessed are they who, with humble charity, aid the works of Christs Church."```

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Formation of the Duchy of Sorbia**
<@763652399784919080> <@261290667106369536> 

Though it would take a two year campaign of military occupation and
events.fullText:
**Formation of the Duchy of Sorbia**
<@763652399784919080> <@261290667106369536> 

Though it would take a two year campaign of military occupation and political dealings with town leaders the Upper Lusatia League which had become de-facto independent of Bohemia due to the Hungarian occupation forces only extending into Bohemia proper combined with no immediate imperial forces occupying the area would be dissolved. Citing previous claims and historic marchdom of Lusatia Klementia August Von Wittelsbach would in a ceremony at Sts. Peter and Paul Church at Görlitz declare himself Duke of all Sorbs, Görlitz to become the new capital of the realm and to be within the wider Bavarian demesne. 

Though he would withdraw the rule of the merchant towns they would be offered to remain to sit on his council as a Kaufmannsrat alongside the German and allied Sorbian nobles. the Urban privileges would remain as well, one part a concession and one part simply lacking the ability to enforce centralized power. Though there would remain much ill feeling the losses suffered in the Hussite wars coupled with a shift into German markets making more merchants eager to keep the empire happy would accept Klementia as Duke, yet such a thing hangs dangerously over the precipice. Resentment high and Upper Lusatia nobility now dominating lower lusatia whose nobility mauled by the war is split betwen German Bavarian plants and merchants whose powers have been reduced, shifting power southwards and leaving only bitterness in its wake.

**(Spremberg/Upper Lusatia unites with Lower Lusatia to form the Duchy of Sorbia. Unrest remains high, only kept in balance by a lack of unity among the Sorbs)**

The Urals, the land of riches that is to be claimed by Muscovy, a personal mission by Grand Prince Ivan III, who seeks to christianize the region and utilize its wealth for the mineral poor region of Russia.

## Eyes on the Horizon

London stands garrisoned by the Portuguese. The northern marches occupied by the Scottish. The Nevilles shelter somewhere in t
events.fullText:
## Eyes on the Horizon

London stands garrisoned by the Portuguese. The northern marches occupied by the Scottish. The Nevilles shelter somewhere in the Cotswolds. 

**England teeters on the precipice.**

Into this chaos prepares the only as of yet uncommitted army. They make ready to either march or defend from the refortified [Cardiff Castle](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520887122763190436) depending on the signal given. At its head is Theodore Tudur, brother of Edward Tudur, and captain Rory of Navan, an Anglo-Irish mercenary captain provided by Lord Deputy Thomas FitzGerald <@1218063544940232784>. Both men have been assured by their respective lords that the events of the next few weeks will be decisive. Neither yet knows in what way...or more importantly, how much blood it will cost.

events.snippet:
### Eglwys agos, ond rhelyw bell - Near the Church, ||But far from God||

“Bendigwch fi, O Dad, oherwydd rwyf wedi pechu.” *quietly began Edward Tudur
events.fullText:
### Eglwys agos, ond rhelyw bell - Near the Church, ||But far from God||

“Bendigwch fi, O Dad, oherwydd rwyf wedi pechu.” *quietly began Edward Tudur before continuing louder in French,* “Bless me father, for I have sinned. I raise arms against my fellow Christians and knowingly ignore my [ally’s actions](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1518755524210851931/1520944949208682657). Can these deeds be proven correct in order to save the faith of all men?”

He directed his question to the [Papal representative](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520870673398894602) in front of him <@150411715757080576>. The act seemed one part convicted **zealotry** and another part **calm** political theatre. Whichever interpretation was correct it seemed genuine at least.

“The schismatic Nevilles make shelter near our border and the Lollardy heretics ignored under the Plantagenets still infest England. I, God’s faithful servant, ask for your blessing to make right in the Regnum Angliæ.” *Edward looks into the distance as if to see the army they both know camps less than a day away.*

“Do I have your permit to make war in the name of peace? Will our Holy Father support the church in England?” *he asks with a pregnant pause, only seemingly praying for the answer.*

-# *The interior of St.David’s Cathedral, Seat of the Welsh Church and home of its Patron Saint*

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
**HANSETAG** 



In the last Hansetag https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518548987278266378, much faith was placed o
events.fullText:
**HANSETAG** 



In the last Hansetag https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518548987278266378, much faith was placed on Albrecht to achieve results for the cities. It would seem he delivered, for in the Hansetag of 1443 his merits were numerous: expansion on Rome (both Romes, in fact), the lands of Milan, what could be a victory of Edward V, defence of Frankfurt and an astounding increase of 40% in trade income. Hamburg led the way, and the League looks to be back on track.

The London market hasn't collapsed, but it has become stagnant due to the civil war. A lot of demand for weapons, yet no growth at all, no new opportunities. Meanwhile, Bordeaux has become untransitable due to the French embargo, making equally impossible to trade in Castille. And Brugges, one of the most profitable markets, cannot be expanded due to that same embargo. Truly, the French are the most evil people, restricting Hanseatic trade...

The trade data also reveals that this has alienated the traditional kontors, given that most of the income now comes from Italy and beyond instead of the traditional markets (bar Lübeck, the jewel of the Hansa), but the reforms brought forward intend to silence their complaints. The betrayal of the London Kontor showed that their influence has overreached, and the german communities established out there must answer to the League, or else. They depend on the members, not the other way around.

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet: Geoffroy de Dampierre passed away at the age of sixteen, only one day after his marriage to Blanca of Burgundy, having succumbed to a sudden fever. As
events.fullText:
Geoffroy de Dampierre passed away at the age of sixteen, only one day after his marriage to Blanca of Burgundy, having succumbed to a sudden fever. As Geoffroy died so young, he was never able to father an heir who could continue the dynastic line. Therefore, Geoffroy Dampierre, Edouards younger brother now stands first in the line of succession to the County of Namur. Out of love for my late brother, we shall see him married to a suitable noble at court, so that he may continue our family line.

Furthermore, it is no secret that Lorraine has maintained close ties with the Archbishopric of Trier for a decades, and that the head of Trier has often come from the ruling family of Lorraine itself.
Therefore, House Dampierre puts forward Robert de Dampierre, also Edouards younger brother as the preferred successor to the current Archbishop Geoffrey de Dampierre. In addition, because of the long-standing friendship and the many common interests shared between Trier and Lorraine, we offer Archbishop Geoffroy de Dampierre the opportunity to place the Archbishopric under the protection of the Duchy of Lorraine, entering into a formal relationship of fealty as becoming a vassal. Such an agreement would not only provide greater protection against foreign conquest, but would also allow the Duchy to use its well-filled treasury to invest in the holy buildings of Trier. Furthermore, we pledge to the people of Trier that we shall continue to increase their prosperity while guaranteeing their security and peace.

events.author: Stalker
events.snippet:
## A day in Pisa
It was a warm, sunny day in Pisa. Giovanni had just returned from a  trade voyage to Cyprus aboard his new ship, the O Finâ, named af
events.fullText:
## A day in Pisa
It was a warm, sunny day in Pisa. Giovanni had just returned from a  trade voyage to Cyprus aboard his new ship, the O Finâ, named after his hometown. She was a fine vessel, built by skilled Levantine shipwrights. Though expensive, she had already proven her worth, trade with the established Kingdom of Jerusalem was booming.

Now back in Italy, Giovanni intended to sell the goods he had brought from the East and purchase new wares for his next voyage.

Coin pouch in hand, he strode confidently into the marketplace. Approaching the first stall, he dropped his purse onto the counter with the slight arrogance that only an Italian merchant could muster and demanded to see the merchant's wares.

The merchant simply shrugged.

"My stall is empty."

Like a hawk, Giovanni seized his coin pouch and marched to the next stall.

Empty.
He frowned and continued to a third. Surely this one would have something to sell.

With the same smug confidence, he demanded to see the merchant's wares. Before he could even finish his sentence, however, his eyes fell upon another completely bare counter.

Now thoroughly agitated, Giovanni demanded an explanation.

"It's the Germans," the merchant replied.

"The Germans?"

Giovanni laughed.

Certainly, German traders occasionally ventured into the Mediterranean, but never in numbers large enough to empty the markets of Pisa.

Yet the merchant's expression never changed.

"Look around."

For the first time, Giovanni truly observed the marketplace.

Every stall stood empty,
 the entire market had been swept clean.

The Germans most notably the merchants of the Hanseatic League had expanded their commercial influence enormously. Across the city, massive warehouses had been built. Italian merchants found themselves unable to compete, struggling to buy and to sell their wares.

Frustrated, Giovanni returned to his ship, where his own cargo remained unsold. With nothing left to do, he made his way to a nearby tavern.
At least, he thought, he could drown this bitter revelation in a good Italian wine.

But as Giovanni took a sip,
Something was wrong.

The taste was unfamiliar, he turned the bottle around and read the label.

**Deutsches Weingut Rheinpfalz.**

Soon, complaints from Genoese merchants spread like wildfire. Merchants from Pisa voiced their complaints, followed by those from Rome, Marseille, Milan, Constantinople, and across Greece. Throughout the Mediterranean, German merchants were beginning to outcompete their Italian rivals or had already done so.

The reports eventually reached the Consiglio in Genoa.

Now the Republic itself would have to decide how to respond.

events.fullText:
## Ry’n ni yma o hyd - We are still here
<@150411715757080576> <@1106651545296195655> 

Many considered the heart of Wales permanently damaged after the decline of St.Davids [over 40 years ago now](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1516269656158240868). The cathedral not only functioned as the center of the Welsh church and a major pilgrimage site, it was the literal representation an unbroken heritage of **peace** and **resilience**. St.David’s own emblem of a white dove came to be recognized as a symbol of these very things and the site where he laid down his lesson - Tyddewi or the House of David, showed its example.

Its stones first became a Monastery, then a source of inspiration to King Alfred the Great, a place of holy contemplation to William the Conqueror - whose family built its current state, and finally a place to which “two pilgrimages is equal to one to Rome” in the words of Pope Callixtus II. Few other places have survived in such rare form, despite Viking raids and banditry, and few have known such volatile fortunes. For in Wales this is simply life, and the Cathedral is simply Wales. *Still here*.

St.David’s has functioned as an institution of **Catholic** education, illumination, and monastic order for many centuries. The example of [St.David himself](https://www.visitwales.com/info/history-heritage-and-traditions/st-david-five-facts) is one of hard work and reaping what you sow. He practiced strict Asceticism: he would not plough fields with anything but his own hands, he consumed naught but leeks and water, and he eschewed all possessions. His ultimate advice was that which anyone who saw him already knew, simply to - “Gwnewch y pethau bychain mewn bywyd” (Do the little things in life).

*The lessons of St.David are **far more resistant** to the Lollard message. Though England grows ever closer to embracing the creed’s defiance of top down church organization, some say rightly, its grievances find far less fertile soil in the hills of nearby Wales. For some this has become a badge of honor, as when all else holy fails, **we are still here**.*

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## THE MARCHERS' WAR 

*Those damn dogs.[ Barking](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1519818952912539750) all throug
events.fullText:
## THE MARCHERS' WAR 

*Those damn dogs.[ Barking](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1519818952912539750) all through England, they are!

Have we not given them enough? From the marches they come, taking what they please, and burying nought  but the bones. 

Can a shepherd hope to care, to divine the mission of the feral and ravenous as they strip the land? Does a farmer care with what purpose they howl at each other through the night? 

Three men, all dear family, could not be bothered to consult a judge for their matters of inheritance, as any responsible sons would. 

No, when they are Normans, what use have they for English law? English sons are the only way they know how to settle their disputes. 

A question of inheritance, decided not by law, but by which cousin could send the most souls to St. Peter and the Lord.

All through England, they howl hour by hour. In the North, it is the boys who must fear Cŵn Annwn.* 

***They ran out of Men years ago.***

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Tomb of Eirini Palaiologos**


In the year 1428, Eirini Palaiologos, the proclaimed Queen of the Dhimmi, Queen of Carthage, Sicily, & Sardinia d
events.fullText:
**The Tomb of Eirini Palaiologos**


In the year 1428, Eirini Palaiologos, the proclaimed Queen of the Dhimmi, Queen of Carthage, Sicily, & Sardinia departed this mortal coil, following in the wake of her husband some years prior.

Eirini had proven indispensable to the rule of her husband, Bahir Hafsid.  Her arrival in the country had caused much turmoil–a changing of laws, an unending of norms, even a civil war to preserve their marriage.  Through it all the Queen endured with poise and grace and proved her quality.

Her lineage provided the legal claim to justify the merchant-backed invasion of Sardinia, her faith allowed her to connect with the Christian subjects of the realm, and her temperance cooled the Sultan's malevolent nature and ensured that, when possible, cooler heads prevailed over hot blood.  The gentle hand with which she helped to guide the ship of state helped lead Carthage to its highest point, certainly in the last century or more.

For a full month the blue and white of Carthaginian banners within the city were replaced with black and gold, in mourning of her passing.  Entombed alongside her husband, she became the first woman to be laid to rest within the crypts of the new **Great Palace** in Carthage, which was still under construction, yet these portions of the Palace were completed with haste to allow it.

Although a public ceremony was conducted according to the Sunni burial rites, the private ceremony which took place with her remains was conducted according to Orthodox rites, as was her wish.  Her family in Constantinople was notified, and of course invited, to this private ceremony.

She left an indelible mark on the tapestry of the reborn Carthaginian state, from the halls of power to the halls of worship, yet it was within her own family that one of her most impactful contributions had yet to be revealed.

<@302128861209165824>

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Faith of the Children**


According to the agreement reached by their parents, the children of Bahir and Eirini were raised interfaith–that is,
events.fullText:
**The Faith of the Children**


According to the agreement reached by their parents, the children of Bahir and Eirini were raised interfaith–that is, they received a formal upbringing and education in both Sunni Islam and Orthodox Christianity.  This was oftentimes a source of friction between the clergy, nobility, and the royal family, yet the marriage of Hafsid and Palaiologos that had brought prosperity to both families was of such value that not even the most ardent cleric could overturn this agreement.

There were six children born to the couple.  Their three sons Hanno, Hamid, and Mikhail and three daughters Didi, Eirini, and Maria.

Hanno, being the firstborn male heir, was the *de facto* heir to the Sultan Bahir, and it was impressed upon him the most that, although the choice of faith was his alone to make, the support he would gain from being a Muslim was of significant value, and without it his rule would be no-doubt contested.  The other children, upon whom lesser pressure was levied, were more free to choose their own faiths.

In an attempt to evoke some of the more legendary figures from Carthaginian antiquity, many of the children bore their names.  These were Hanno, Hamid, and Dido–the couple's first daughter.  The remaining three were given more Orthodox names, being Eirini, named for her mother, Maria, and Mikhail–the couple's youngest son, named for Eirini's father, the Roman Emperor in Constantinople.

There were silent hopes that these naming conventions and the reinforcement of the importance of religion to the body politic of the Sultanate would guide the children down certain paths in their belief, however none could predict what would occur.

All three daughters–Dido, Eirini, and Maria–were devout Muslims, while two of the princes also favored Islam, though perhaps less ardently and zealously than the clergy had hoped, given their need to command soldiers, sailors, and levies of multiple faiths.  Of the three princes, only one chose to favor the Orthodox faith–the crown prince who would later become Sultan, Hanno.

Public knowledge of Hanno's faith, however, would not be revealed until years into his rule, and shortly after his mother's passing, for very strategic reasons.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**The Prince of Perm**

By decree of the Grand Prince of Muscovy the young Örti Mozhga has officially been named Prince of Great Perm and the Urals, a
events.fullText:
**The Prince of Perm**

By decree of the Grand Prince of Muscovy the young Örti Mozhga has officially been named Prince of Great Perm and the Urals, and given near-free reign over the lands east of the Rus'. This he receives in return for his and his ancestors fealty to Muscovy and in particular his **conversion to the true faith of Christ.**

Already during the last decades Muscovites have been steadily streaming into the country to work in the Ural mines or elsewise clear lands for farms in the wooded expanses of Great Perm. With Örti now truly in every sense of the word subjecting himself and his country to Moscow the flow of immigrants from the west continues to increase near exponentially. Court in Perm is held increasingly in Russian.

As more and more Muscovites enter from the west Örti continues to expand his influence eastwards; the Urals are truly rich and Muscovy provides not only manpower but funding for ventures deeper into Siberia than ever before. Upon the Ob, the princes of the Mansi and Khanty submit one by one to the suzerainty of the west; further east still the Selkups grow anxious; and to the north the Nenets wonder if their age old friends in Komia will honour old bonds.

**The east will never be the same.**

*Komi pay tribute in the blessed capital of Perm*
**||<@307587752860581888> <@302128861209165824> <@160455024994942976> ||**
