events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# Growth and Decay.
**The Humbling of Syria. 1427 - 1431**

With the Papal Conclave electing Elpidio Abate, who took the Pontifical name of Leo X <@15
events.fullText:
# Growth and Decay.
**The Humbling of Syria. 1427 - 1431**

With the Papal Conclave electing Elpidio Abate, who took the Pontifical name of Leo X <@150411715757080576> , as the newest head of the Christian Church, funds were soon sweeping into the recently restored Kingdom of Jerusalem. The elites of the Kingdom, including the Queen, nobility, and clergy, were all naturally very accepting of this welcome surprise. Soon, they would use such funds to expand the Kingdom to the very heights not seen since before Saladin waged his brilliant campaign to throw the Crusaders out of the Levant. Syria was still reeling from the chaos surrounding the last decades of Mamluk rule and the subsequent Tenth Crusade. With the Kingdom of Jerusalem having the clear momentum, it was an opportunity the nobles of Jerusalem would not waste. In 1430, the armies marched beyond its eastern frontiers, and set out to restore the whole of the Kingdom. Within a year it was done, the armies of Syria and Filastin were both routed, and the cities and forts along the border captured. Of course, the commander of the army, the nobleman Peter de Nicosie, did not dare embark on something so fantastical as marching on Damascus or Aleppo, for such an action would take much more men, supplies, and funds to accomplish, but by the beginning of 1431 all of the Oultrejourdain was conquered, and the cities along the Mediterranean coast were either besieged or had already fallen. With victory assured, the Noble Council in 1431 would vote to accept the peace terms offered to them by Syria, which would cede to the Kingdom of Jerusalem all the territories along the coast and its old frontier of Oultrejourdain. 

And just like that, the only borders of the Kingdom of Jerusalem were restored to their previous zenith.

Sure, the Duchy of Antioch didn't own quite as much as it did when it was a Principality, and neither was all of the old County of Tripoli retaken. Yet the Kingdom of Jerusalem itself was fully restored, and the entire Levantine Coast was owned by Crusader states oncemore. This allowed Acre to truly prosper oncemore as the premier Market Center in the Levant, and it would rapidly surpass Damascus in wealth and the flow of prized eastern goods for trade to the west.

The administration of these newly conquered lands would not be easy, however. It was many more thousands of Muslims and eastern Christians, most of whom were not eager to get along with their restored Catholic overlords. In response, both old and new tactics were to come into play. Like in the First Kingdom of Jerusalem, the territory of Oultrejourdain was to become very autonomous, for though it stretched from the Dead Sea to the Red Sea, it was a land of dry deserts and local Arabic tribes, the both of these would ensure it to be a wild land not suited for centralized control. Yet, in despite of the old order, the lands of the old county of Tripoli would not quite have the same luxury. Tripoli itself would remain under the control of the Kingdom of Jerusalem, whilst the northern half of the defunct County would be absorbed into the Duchy of Antioch and the Chivalric Order of the Knights Hospitaller. All in the east seemed to be going very well, yet the death of the Crown Prince in 1329, and then the Queen herself in 1330 would lead to the largest revolt yet seen on the once vital island on Cyprus...

-# Map of the Kingdom of Jerusalem

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
**The Revolt of Cyprus. 1432 - 1433**

The success of the Tenth Crusade brought Christian Joy to millions, yet there were those who soon found that th
events.fullText:
**The Revolt of Cyprus. 1432 - 1433**

The success of the Tenth Crusade brought Christian Joy to millions, yet there were those who soon found that they'd have rather it not succeeded at all. Hundreds of nobles left the island of Cyprus to either restore their old titles on the mainland or to seek new opportunities. To those who stayed behind it seemed like a golden opportunity to expand their own influence on the rapidly ignored island, but this was not to be the case at all, because those who reclaimed their old titles or acquired new ones in the Holy Land still acted with full entitlement to the properties that they had all but discarded in Cyprus. And to make matters worse, time and time again the Queen, and then the Noble Council after she had died, would routinely side against the Cypriot nobility in favor of the mainlanders. Many of these landowners would, in fact, begin to invest much less of their time, energy, and most importantly coin on Cyprus in favor of their new lands in the Levant. This led to entire plantations becoming poorly managed and equipped, lowering the wealth and prosperity of the island significantly. So even whilst the island was becoming increasingly ignored and forgotten by those on the mainland, it was denied time and again the autonomy it thus needed to thrive without the Kingdom's governance being based there. Resentment amongst the Frankish nobility and Greek serfs were both on the rise against the Noble Council, and the death of the Queen would give them an opportunity to strike back.

Queen Catherine I de Lusignan was buried in the Holy Sepulchre alongside the other monarchs of the Kingdom in 1330, yet the death of her eldest son in a hunting accident a year before her own demise had now left the Kingdom in a precarious situation.

The Crown rightfully passed then to her grandson Fadrique I(of Jerusalem) /II (of Castile) de Trastámara, yet he was only 11 years old at that point. This interregnum period thus allowed the scorned nobility on Cyprus to agitate their serfs into revolt. The young King, they claimed, could not defend the island from the Tunisians or the Genoese, and thus they must force him to recognize the legitimacy of a new Kingdom of Cyprus! The message worked and thousands of Greek serfs, led by their Frankish masters, seized the strategic forts and cities on Cyprus. Within weeks the whole of the island was under the control of the rebels. It was the first revolt of such a nature that the Kings of Cyprus had ever faced before, and the rebels seemed poised to enforce their demands. Unfortunately for the rebels, the tensions amongst themselves would soon spell the doom of their movement.

Firstly, the Frankish nobility on Cyprus could not figure out how to properly divide the newly acquired plantations on the island, with each noble trying to gain as much for himself as was possible, at the expense of everyone else. This naturally led to heated arguments and even some defections to the Kingdom of Jerusalem. Skirmishes were even fought between rebels, theoretically on the same side of the conflict against Jerusalem but nevertheless hostile to each other. Even more disastrously however, was the failure to find amongst them a worthy King who could unite these feuding noblemen into a coherent cause. In a meeting of the twelve most powerful nobles participating in the revolt, it was said that ten all claimed the Crown for themselves, some pointing to how much wealth they had, others to how much land they owned, and others to the dynastic connections they had in their blood. By the time of the counterattack by loyalist forces, these issues were still completely unresolved, and it meant that the revolt had already all but defeated themselves, before the first battle was even fought.

And what a battle it was. Fernando de Trastámara <@422498641425596418> , uncle of the young King and himself only 23 years old, landed in Famagusta at the head of a three thousand strong army. The city quickly fell, for its defenders fled at the sight of the flagship of the Holy Navy named the "*Styx*", a heavy ship boasting 40 cannon. Fernando, buoyed by his success marched straight on to the capital of the island, Nicosie. There he was to meet what army the revolt could muster against him. Desertions amongst the revolt was already extreme by this point, and where once they could've outnumber Fernando's force by 2-1, now they only had 4 thousand to match his 3 thousand. Still, with the situation only bound to get worse they sallied out of Nicosie to meet this overconfident Spanish Prince in battle. For many hours it was fought, with hundreds of casualties on both sides. The young Castilian general proved himself quite the inspirational leader, and throughout the battle it was as if he alone kept his army together. Eventually, only after the loyalists had broken both the rebels left and right flanks did they finally begin to rout in earnest. Losses were high on both sides, but the loyalists refusal to accept Greek prisoners meant that the rebels had probably lost more men in the battle. In any case, with the opposing army swept aside Fernando marched in triumph into Nicosie, where he allowed many of the Frankish nobles to surrender themselves to him.

Reprisals were swift. Plantations were seized and others burnt. The ringleaders were executed and their families exiled. Many of the other nobles who had participated in the revolt were either imprisoned or made to pay extraordinary fines. Worst of all was the Kingdom's newfound mistrust of the Greek serfs on the island. The Greeks would find themselves discriminated against at a level they had not previously experienced under the Lusignan Kings.

At first this discrimination would probably not be given much notice by outsiders, for the pleas from Cyprus were either suppressed or too easily justified by the recent revolt that had caused the deaths of thousands. Yet by 1444 these accusations of discrimination and increased violence against the Greeks on Cyprus would only grow louder and louder, perhaps even drawing the condemnation of Byzantines <@302128861209165824> and the Pope in Rome <@150411715757080576> . Calls for increased autonomy on the island also grow, for although the notion of another violent revolt is far from the minds of anyone left on Cyprus, the benefits of autonomy are still enticing enough to frequently petition the Noble Council for. Yet so far the Noble Council of the Holy Kingdom of Jerusalem has ignored all notions of *any* Cypriot autonomy in favor of instead ruling over the island with the same iron fist as it was before the Tenth Crusade. Despite their efforts, the ability to exercise control over the island is miniscule, and this vain attempt to maintain direct control is actually to the detriment to both Jerusalem and Cyprus.

Yet it will take action by the Noble Council to enact these needed reforms, and it is unlikely that they would want to do so anytime soon...

(To represent this discrimination in the face of the major revolt, Greek Culture has downgraded from being Tolerated to Discriminated in the Kingdom of Jerusalem in-game)

-# (Left to Right) The Revolt on Cyprus, Prince Fernando de Trastámara.

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
**The Noble Council of the Holy Kingdom of Jerusalem. 1434 - 1441**

When King Fadrique I <@422498641425596418>  was finally crowned as King of Jerusa
events.fullText:
**The Noble Council of the Holy Kingdom of Jerusalem. 1434 - 1441**

When King Fadrique I <@422498641425596418>  was finally crowned as King of Jerusalem in 1434, he would stay for a few years to both learn the basics of governance and to give his assent to the system which was to rule these eastern Christian Kingdoms in his stead when he inevitably traveled to Iberia to rule over his more wealthy and homely throne. That system was the Noble Council. In truth, the young and inexperienced King had no part in its creation, for it had effectively existed since Queen Catherine I was given the throne and needed an experienced group of nobles to practically rule for her, for she had little idea on how to rule by herself. The Noble Council was made up 10 noblemen of proud lineages, all of whom were influential and agreed to a basic oath to honor the rulings of the Council, even if the vote, which was decided by a simple majority, did not go in their favor. It throughout the reign of Catherine I and now Fadrique I managed almost all of the affairs of the Crusader States including foreign policy and domestic affairs. Its power stretched across the Kingdoms and in every sense it was the real authority in the Kingdom, instead of the royal officials. With the death of the Queen, their power is now even more unchecked, and it is up to them alone whether they will enact changes in the Kingdom. For instance, royal decrees from the King that are supposed to apply to all the Crowns he owns are not immediately implemented in Jerusalem. Instead, such a decree has to be voted on, and many times they would be outright rejected...

One of the matters most fervently pursued was the integration of the local Christian populace into the Kingdom. These "Syriacs" had been an important foundation in the First Kingdom of Jerusalem.

Now, with the King of France refusing to send settlers to the Holy Land and Crusader momentum waning since the massive rebellion on Cyprus, these local Christians would be of more significance than ever before. Syriacs were Christians of many different sects, there were many Catholics, Orthodox, Miaphysites, and even some small number of Nestorians. To integrate these peoples, more protections to these schismatic, and even outright heretical minorities would have to be granted. This was done so over a series of votes within the Noble Council, which made efforts to elevate these local Christians above the local Muslims. How this effects the Kingdom long-term is yet to be seen...

-# (Left to Right) The growth of the prosperity of the Nobility, the Noble Council of the Holy Kingdom of Jerusalem, a Syriac manuscript depicting Christ seated on a donkey entering Jerusalem followed by an overjoyed crowd.

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
**The "War" against the Romans.**

The death of Queen Catherine I had another important effect on the Kingdom. It meant that the Treaty of Silifke was
events.fullText:
**The "War" against the Romans.**

The death of Queen Catherine I had another important effect on the Kingdom. It meant that the Treaty of Silifke was on a more shaky foundation, for it was the signature of the Queen who bound the Kingdom to the Karamanids' defense. In 1441, when the Romans <@302128861209165824>  struck out again for southwestern Anatolia, there were many in the noble council who thought that they were not obliged to honor the Treaty. In a 6 - 4 vote, however, honor won out over neutrality. The levies were called and the army in the months following the declaration of war traveled north into the central Anatolian plateau. Yet the Byzantines, following successful talks with the Noble Council and the Queen in the previous decades, guaranteed the Kingdom's safety publicly. There was no real desire amongst the nobility nor the army to do battle with the war-hardened Empire, and the inclusion of the Hafsids to the war on the side of the Byzantines struck much fear that there was to be an invasion of the weakened Cyprus, or perhaps the Holy Kingdom itself...

Thus in secret talks between the Noble Council, and the Emperor, they agreed that as long as the Crusaders did not aid the Karamanids directly in the major theatre of war, southwestern Anatolia, then the Kingdom would be guaranteed its safety from Byzantine and Hafsid attacks. Thus, the Sultan Bulend Karamanoğlu was soon frustrated by his ally's lack of cooperation in the southwestern theatre. Refusing the offer from the Crusaders to guard northern and central Anatolia from a possible Byzantine-Hafsid invasion, he petitioned them to instead march east into the Armenian mountains, to fight off an invasion by the Georgian Queen Bedisa I. This was agreed to, but yet again more problems quickly arose.

Despite the fact that the rulers of Bitlis and Jolemark swore their own loose fealties to the Karamanids, the mountain passes to these lands, which were being occupied by the Georgians, were blocked by other rulers who did *not* owe any allegiances to the Karamanids. Despite months of negotiations, both the Sabrans and the Sutayids refused to let the Crusaders march further east to relieve Bitlis and Jolemark. Not particularly wanting to fight in those inhospitable mountain passes anyway, the Crusaders marched back to Cilicia and sent a messenger to the Karamanid Sultan explaining that they could not fulfill the duties required of them anymore by the Treaty of Silifke.

By then, the war in the west was all but finished, with the Karamanids finally suffering a major defeat at the hands of the Empire, and southwestern Anatolia now lost to the Byzantines. With the Byzantine's triumphant and treaty of Silifke practically dead, Cilicia prepares itself for renewed Turkish raids along its borders, and the Noble Council begins to search oncemore for a local ally in the region...

-# The Byzantines victorious over the Turks.

**(End)**

( <@460814348487753730>  forgot to tag you at the beginning of this message)

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**Hanno the Orthodox; Hanno the Riddah**


Shortly after the passing of his mother and the completion of her funerary rites, the court of Sultan Hanno
events.fullText:
**Hanno the Orthodox; Hanno the Riddah**


Shortly after the passing of his mother and the completion of her funerary rites, the court of Sultan Hanno I Hafsid began to take notice of a change in his attitude, particularly when it came to observance of matters of the Islamic faith.  His attitude, to put it generously, was minimalist, engaging most typically with various Sufis within the faith.  Those who listened or participated in these conversations observed that the Sultan was intensely focused on the topic of one's personal relationship with God.  In particular, he tended to favor those who preached against the strict confines of traditional jurisprudence.  None had guessed the true reason for this.

One year after his mother's passing, Sultan Hanno I Hafsid openly declared that he had found God not through Islam, but through the faith of his mother–that of the Christian Orthodox Church.  He likewise declared that the laws of succession which stipulated that only a Sunni Muslim could be appointed heir would be changed and, to honor the tradition of his late mother and father in allowing all of their children to choose which faith they favored, the religion of the heir would no longer be limited, and the Crown Prince could elect the religion that his heart and his faith dictated.

This had many effects, the most immediate of which was the riotous response from the traditional hardline clergy and the entrenched Sunni nobility who saw this as a betrayal, with many storming from the halls in protest and others strongly encouraging the Sultan to recant his statement before word spread.  The only supporters remaining to the Sultan among the Clergy were those Sufi Muslims who preached the pursuit of sanctity irrespective of laws or customs, and they were in the minority.  Among the nobility those seeking advantage or advancement likewise spoke against ‘the Sultan's Folly’, but those for whom faith held less importance saw opportunities for advancement by being more accepting.  Among the commoners the issue was split, owing to whether other policies had improved their lives or not.  

Conversely, support amongst the Dhimmi surged among every strata of their society, and support among the Burghers–for whom coin was the supreme authority–remained steadfast, for they generally recognized the opportunity to further erode the power of the troublesome nobility.  Among the fleet and the army, support for the Sultan remained strong, for he had led directly during the campaign in Anatolia, and he had earned the trust of the fighting men.  By these graces were his ruling coalition maintained, and the risk of immediate revolt severely diminished.

News of this spread swiftly, both to the Sultan's Moorish allies in Morocco and to his extended family in Constantinople.  It was clearly stated that this was a personal faith of the Sultan alone, and not reflective of the faith of the state or the court, which remained that of Sunni Islam.

In truth, the Sultan had made this choice long before, though kept his faith private, as he knew well that under the country's laws he would not be permitted as heir.  In this way he had not only ensured his own advancement, but made certain that not only his own children, but the princes yet unborn would be free to choose as their hearts demanded.  Likewise, he spared his mother from suffering the blame, threats, and harsh words that would have inundated her.  The tumult and rage would be his to bear and his alone, and that is precisely how he wanted it.  He could weather this storm, so long as it advanced the cause and growth of the Carthaginian *asabiyyah*.

<@327561459976372224> <@302128861209165824>

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**The resolution of the Treaty of Hierapolis**

Almost a century had passed since the [Treaty](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1508990
events.fullText:
**The resolution of the Treaty of Hierapolis**

Almost a century had passed since the [Treaty](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1508990612345720852/1510097286200430802) was signed between the Noble Knights Hospitaller and the Great Roman Empire of The East. As the major points of that agreement, the Empire would assist the crusading host in reclaiming the Holy Land, in return for which - the Western Anatolian lands would be fully returned under the direct administration of the Empire. And thus, though more indirectly than initially predicted, the old accord was done. The Romans kept their promises and at the same time - restored much of the Glory of their Anatolian holdings. 

The story started by Andronikos IV, was thus finished by Thomas, The First of His Name, the liberator of Anatolia and the savior of Greek people. But that would not be the end of the restoration of the Empire on its way back to its Glory. Phoenix has risen from the ash and will never forget those who saw opportunity in its crisis. 

A letter of appreciation would be sent to Carthaginian <@297185126947880960>  court, as without them, such a deceive victory could not have been accomplished. It was the first time in the last century since the creation of the alliance between two ancient enemies, that their troops shared a battlefield. In the marches during the war, and in its aftermath, many Greeks and Carthaginians shared food and drink, laughing and sharing stories of their homelands and families. Nothing makes two peoples closer like a good war.

Though the happiness was not widespread outside of military camps, by the end of the war, new rumors started circulating in the Empire. A conflict was brewing in the troubled lands of Italia. And perhaps, with a new victory in the east, opportunities could now be seized in the west.

<@150411715757080576> <@698400527834415145> (the pact concerns the Papacy, so you are also tagged)

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**Νέος Αυτοκρατορικός Εμπορικός Κώδικας**



The years had weighed heavily upon **Thomas I Palaiologos**. In his late 60's he wished to make a final i
events.fullText:
**Νέος Αυτοκρατορικός Εμπορικός Κώδικας**



The years had weighed heavily upon **Thomas I Palaiologos**. In his late 60's he wished to make a final impact on his now more and more prospering Empire.

The cities of western Anatolia now once more answered to Constantinople. Greek lands long thought lost had returned beneath the Imperial Eagle. Trade once again filled the harbours of the Aegean, and the roads of the Empire carried merchants like they havent since long ago.

And so, in what many at court had begun calling the labour of his lifetime, the Emperor withdrew from most of his affairs, devoting his attention to a more important task. Around him gathered a carefully selected circle of jurists, tax officials, experienced merchants, and representatives of the great trading ports of the Empire. Their debates stretched late into the nights.

The Emperor himself presided over nearly every discussion. Which was considerably hard with years, as he became more frail.

None expected the work to be of much consequence, as the legal code of the Empire remained relatively stable and still since the time of the Great Emperors like Justinian I. What came as a result, surprised nearly everyone.

The new Imperial Code sought to establish a single legal foundation for commerce throughout the Empire. It defined the obligations of merchants and tax collectors alike, regulated the operation of foreign commercial houses and colonial offices, standardized customs duties between provinces, clarified maritime trade, and sought to replace local contradictions with a single body of Roman commercial law.

No less importantly, it placed limits upon Imperial officials themselves. Taxation was to enrich the Empire, not those entrusted with collecting it. Foreign merchants were welcomed, as never before, but they would always have to look behind their shoulders if they would plan to take advantage of corrupt officials. Likewise, Roman merchants would receive protections to establish certain hierarchy of order in the markets of Imperial Cities.

Old and pale, The Emperor in person, had declared before the assembled Senate, clergy, and representatives of the merchant guilds:

"I am not eternal, but our Empire will be! And it can only prosper in this eternity when a merchant in Prousa, a shipmaster in Smyrna, a banker in Constantinople, and a foreign trader arriving from distant lands all know that the Law of Rome is one and the same."

By the Golden Seal of the Emperor, the collection was proclaimed binding throughout every province of the Empire.

Some at court whispered that Thomas had become too occupied with merchants, others that he thought more like a scholar than a conqueror. Yet those who know the stories of the greatest Emperors of the Roman Empire, recognized his work as it was.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Prif ddynion - Chief Men

*English or Welsh, it makes no difference. Boy or young man, the choice is still the same. **Tink, Tink, Tink** strike t
events.fullText:
### Prif ddynion - Chief Men

*English or Welsh, it makes no difference. Boy or young man, the choice is still the same. **Tink, Tink, Tink** strike the picks and out pours the fruits of these labours.*

Never to the people of course. For the Iron mines of the Vale of Glamorgan flow to Cardiff and from there make the tools and trinkets of England. The copper of holy Snowdonia descends to the ever imposing battlements of Carnarvon; as it had under the earliest Celts and continues to now. The lead of Cardigan goes to fuel its ever ambitious familial seat. All the while the true source of power - gold, silver, and [pearls](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1518374667830952116) are dredged up from near mystical Roman tunnels for the benefit of a few landed lords with high title.

Mining is a **harsh** and **dark** life that many daily toil in the hills of Wales at. Despite its grime, the skilled profession is one well compensated and sought after by Englishmen, Irish, foreigners, and the many Welshmen looking for a better life than herding or tenant farming. Its lords ever seek bodies to fill the high mortality tunnels and the people, strangely willingly, jump to fill them. For perhaps a brief chance at a better life is better than a long one without one.

*Wales has a strong tradition of **mining communities**. It has grown ever more prominent in these past decades of turmoil as the Marcher lords of the Principality seek to autonomously enrich themselves at nearly any cost. The prosperity of such exploits is jealously guarded by these entrenched families even if they sell to nearby [southern towns](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1518371143139069982). None of it is glorious work, but it is dependable and profitable. In Wales that is enough for both lord and subject.*

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Calls for Settlers to the Holy Land grows Louder!

Beyond the first wave of settlers that stayed in Jerusalem after the successful Tenth Crusade
events.fullText:
# The Calls for Settlers to the Holy Land grows Louder!

Beyond the first wave of settlers that stayed in Jerusalem after the successful Tenth Crusade, immigration from Christendom proved exceedingly scarce during the past thirty years of the Kingdom's restoration. Time and again the King of France refused to allow settlers from his Kingdom to take up the most holiest of acts. Cut off from the manpower of the previously most pious of all the Kingdoms in Europe, Jerusalem has been practically forced into accepting more of the local Christians in the region, despite the fact that many do not follow the will of the Roman Church. 

In a desperate effort to counteract this change, letters would be written not to the traditional destination of the rulers of the French Crown, **but instead to other parts of Christendom**. In such letters the rulers of Europe, whether they be in Germany, Italy, Iberia, or elsewhere are all implored to help the Holy Kingdom by encouraging some of their own subjects to move to the Christian east, where they may help strengthen and defend Jerusalem. There would seem to be no monetary reward for doing so, but instead the letters, penned by the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem, would claim that the rewards for such aid would be seen when they had died and sat in the judgement of God.

Yet, as the world moves increasingly into an age of Renaissance and new ideas, it is not known if these traditional arguments for Holy aid will win the hearts of the current generation of European rulers. The quest for more settlers will either succeed or fail depending entirely on the holiness of these Kings and Dukes and Counts many hundreds of leagues away from the Holy Kingdom, for there were no other incentives to do what was asked other than the promised gratitude of Christ...

-# King Baldwin I of Jerusalem

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Bloody Friday
-# A Return To Peace Isn't Free

When Edward Plantagenet approached London, the defending Neville retainers found themselves deserted
events.fullText:
## Bloody Friday
-# A Return To Peace Isn't Free

When Edward Plantagenet approached London, the defending Neville retainers found themselves deserted within hours by the true Englishmen enlisted to defend by their side. 

**Could any Saxon man be faulted for refusing to fight against their true king?**

"I am not here for blood," said Edward, lying, 
**"I only seek justice."**

As the gates parted for the true king, his column turned swiftly towards Westminster Hall. Families left their homes at the sound of the majestic Portuguese horses clopping across the pavement stones, audibly gasping upon seeing **the Plantagenet banner proudly held high across every corner of the column.**

There was no mistake to be made. **The King had returned.** 

Westminster Hall was in chaos when he arrived. Lord-Protector William Neville had ordered a week before a parliament, to raise funds in hope of competing with the seemingly **endless pockets of the Plantagenet camp**, with mixed success. 

Lords and nobles of all stripes rushed to leave before Edward arrived, but Edward had ordered the doors barred before his arrival. He entered the building, and stood before the treasonous parliament. 

"Bring before me Bartholomew of Grey."

The assembled nobles did, in fear of the Portuguese knights standing beside Edward. 

Edward made a small motion, and Sir Grey was cut down.

"Very good. I hope the rest of you will spend your next few weeks to help **explain to the Commons why you** ***aren't*** **guilty of treason against the true king.** I now tire, and I must bid you all a good rest."

Edward set about preparing his new bedchambers in Westminster Hall. 

**(The House of Commons will be putting under trial members of the Parliament under the Nevilles.)**

events.author: Matchasachi
events.snippet:
## The ever prideful Provencals
*The Provence is a region that has long been defined by an independent and civic spirit, since the days of Boson and e
events.fullText:
## The ever prideful Provencals
*The Provence is a region that has long been defined by an independent and civic spirit, since the days of Boson and even before in the ancient days of Rome and Massilia. It's rulers were once Emperors and Kings, of the House Bosonid, later becoming essential to the Kingdom of Burgundy.*

*Ever since the Kingdom of Arles became absorbed into the Holy Roman Empire, it like it's sisters of Dauphine and Savoyen had enjoyed great amounts of independence even if formally sworn to the Holy Roman Emperors. Over time, due to inheritance it became briefly divided, with the Marquisate of Provence and the County of Forcalquier falling to another House, though Forcalquier would later return to the County of Provence. The Marquisate itself became fractured into the Principality of Orange and Avignon, while it's northern parts fell to the Valentinois.*

*It would become embroiled in the troubles of the Albigensian Crusades, for by then the Marquisate had passed to the mighty Counts of Toulouse and as a consequence a certain soon to be prestigious House of Anjou came to be it's Counts. This House, with Provence as it's base would then spread to various thrones of Europe, to Naples, to Hungary, yet in that process Provence soon became a smaller fief for the House of Anjou, tied to the Throne of Naples. As the House died out, Provence remained loyal to the new Kings, even if some nobles desired greater honour for their County than being a mere fief to the Kings in Naples.*

*Even as the Throne of Naples once again passed away, this time to the House de Barcelona, which was a return to history for until 1246 the County had been ruled by the Catalan Dynasty of Barcelona. Still, such would not remain for long, when following the disputed succession of Naples, the King of France decided to intercede and the banners of the Realm descended first upon Provence, which was left abandoned.*

events.author: Matchasachi
events.snippet:
## The Union d'Tarascone
*This is then the reality the County finds itself in, having been occupied and turned into a battlefield, far moreso than Nap
events.fullText:
## The Union d'Tarascone
*This is then the reality the County finds itself in, having been occupied and turned into a battlefield, far moreso than Naples or Ferrare and especially not so Aragon, the supposed Count of Provence never appearing to defend the lands. By decree of the French King it would fall to the House de Savoie, in a way reuniting large parts of what was once Arles under a single House.*

*Still, while Provence was certainly ravaged by war, the forces of the Savoyen, Viennois and Valentinois had suffered from the Italian Campaigns as well and were greatly depleted, with little lust for more war. As such, it was the cities of Provence, that would come together to form the Union of Tarascon, in order to represent their interests to the the new Count. And Duke Phillipe de Savoie deigned to listen to them, for he in his advanced age had little wish to leave a legacy of trouble to his heirs.*

*After much deliberation and negotiation, which also involved the nobility of Provence, the Settlement of Tarascon was forged. Reviving civil spirits that had long been buried by previous Counts, the Cities would once again have the rights to their own Consuls, whom would represent their interests, a right quickly extended to other parts of his domain, forming a foundation for civil liberty that had already been burgeoning in the Duchy. In order to appease the nobility, a Ducal Senéchal would be appointed in Aix, while Occitan would be elevated to a language equal in standing to Francien. Any legal documents, would henceforth be both written in the Arpitan French and the Provencal Occitan Dialects.* <@460814348487753730> 

*These new liberties conferred upon the coastal cities and towns of Provence would also mean that the presence of foreign merchants, be they from the Hansa <@288410456526422016> , the Italian Banks or Genoa <@302527114283057153> would not find themselves harshly regulated.*

-# The new Coat of Arms which Duke Phillipe introduced, in order to equally represent his domains. Provence would be represented by the Occitan Cross in red upon a golden field

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**The Russian River**



The Volga, the massive river that comes from the Lake Volgo, running almost completely through the Eastern European Plain, an
events.fullText:
**The Russian River**



The Volga, the massive river that comes from the Lake Volgo, running almost completely through the Eastern European Plain, and crossing a large part of the most important cities and towns of Russia, with its tributary the Moskva river connecting the Russian capital to the greater river, it coarses through Tver, Yaroslav, Nizhny Novgorod, Lyskovo, Kazan, Sarai, all the way to the south and ending in the Caspian Sea through Astrakhan.

While some small parts are still not under Russian control, the majority of the river and its tributaries serves as a connection between the western parts of Russia to the eastern, distances so big that would be part of numerous countries within the west. Without this river, it is more than likely that Russian eastern expansion would be halted without massive road infrastructures.

By size the biggest river in all of Europe, comparable to the Nile, with the conquest of Astrakhan, Russia now directly controls the start and end of the river, and can truly call the Volga river the Russian River.

In commemoration of this feat, Grand Prince Ivan III has proclaimed the renaming of the town of Kazan, which had seen extensive effort to assimilate its population to Russian, to Volgagorod, representing the eastern most city within the Volga river in the north, before the coming of the Urals and Ivanogorod.

events.author: Rafaam
events.snippet:
**The unexpected reply**

Even though the call was not directly addressed to the current Emperor of the Roman Empire of the East, Roman court has ears
events.fullText:
**The unexpected reply**

Even though the call was not directly addressed to the current Emperor of the Roman Empire of the East, Roman court has ears everywhere. And not a second later when the news reached Constantinople, a letter of proposition was drafted by the Emperor. In that letter Emperor would speak of Catholics that were only recently liberated from under the Turkish rule in Western Anatolia, and many thousands of Catholics that were living on the island of Rhodes before the Knights retook their rightful place near the Holy Land.

As was promised to the representatives of the Western Christianity, the Orthodox patriarchy and the Imperial Court cannot enforce any changes of livelihood to those Catholics living in the borders of the Empire. Because of this, the Empire will not be sending any settlers to the Holy Land. But what is significantly more important, the Emperor is instead inviting missions from the Kingdom of Jerusalem, to come to the Empire and persuade any of its willing catholic citizens to come to the Holy Land. Any willing citizens of the Empire will not be barred from living, no matter their number, as long as they share the faith of Catholicism. 

The Emperor would like to believe that this gesture will be perceived as a branch of renewed friendship between the two faiths.

-# There are 140,000 catholics currently living in the Empire, most of them in Western Anatolia, which only recently came under the control of the Empire. So i dont think it would be hard to persuade most of those people to re-settle. You can either decide on the number yourself, or roll for it, i would be willing to do the counter-rolls if you want to make them. Either way, i give you carte blanche

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## The Second March on England

The Justiciar was [incapacitated](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1518755524210851931/1520944960269062
events.fullText:
## The Second March on England

The Justiciar was [incapacitated](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1518755524210851931/1520944960269062254) and the armies of South Wales were free to move without threat to their estates. On all their sides stood the sea, once ‘[pirated](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520473547976216586)’ by Welshman but now [free from disturbance](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520895538491424920), and beyond that the domains of Lord Deputy Thomas FitzGerald  - a proven ally of the Welsh. Edward determined that he would not let this march end like [the first](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520882658316648641). This time, [Cardiff and its men](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520890111049334914) would not have to pay for the divided loyalties of the Principality of Wales.

*The armies of Wales - [nearly some 12,000 trained men at arms](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520925843830997052) - break camp at Cardiff Castle. They raise the Red Dragon banners of Tudur, the clans sigils of numerous Irish bands and lords, the houses of nearly every English Marcher Lord save the Wentworths, and most visible of all was the Cross of Saint George preceding all.*

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## In Ten-Days' Time

The townspeople of Farleys End lived a quiet life, for the most part. They tended to their cattle, sheared their sheep when the
events.fullText:
## In Ten-Days' Time

The townspeople of Farleys End lived a quiet life, for the most part. They tended to their cattle, sheared their sheep when the time came, and shipped the produce down the river Severn in small, flat-bottomed craft to Bristol in the fall, to trade for much-needed furs and firewood for the winter. Save for when the Alderman would pick a few of the strongest sons to leave for muster, none in this village had seen war. At least, not for generations. 

One fair December night, a young boy of scarcely more than 13 was taking the sheep out to forage through the snow, when he heard a chilling sound. 

*"Wel dyma ni'n dwad
Gy-feillion di-niwad
I ofyn am gennad
I ofyn am gennad
I ofyn am gennad i ganu"*

He had heard enough stories to fear throngs of marching Welshmen. 

He ran back to the village, rousing the elders, with cries of, **"Þā Wēalas cuman! Bēoþ wærcne!"**

The elders of the village remembered, from the stories of their great-grandfathers, what to do in a time like this. 

They chose the fastest rider amongst them to ride to Haresfield, which upon a hilltop nearby had a beacon. He brought with him a torch, and threw it upon the beacon flame, which lit, being well-maintained by the customs of the Haresfielders. 

From hill-to-hill, the flames were lit in turn, every Englishman doing as their duty demanded.

Within hours of the Welsh army crossing the Severn, it was known in Westminster Hall that *something* had happened in Wales, and at once, riders were dispatched to determine *what*. 

Edward knew, if all should fail, he had no more than **ten days' time**.

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
**The reforms of Albrecht**
The League has expanded a lot and, while the standing navy prospers, there has been a lack of proper army coordination. Th
events.fullText:
**The reforms of Albrecht**
The League has expanded a lot and, while the standing navy prospers, there has been a lack of proper army coordination. The kontors that hold so much influence made impossible to fund Edward Plantagenet until the last moment, and despite this, Albrecht made sure he has a good chance at victory. An army would have ensured the cities’ interests to be fought for.

This is not all, for in these years the Lusatian League was disbanded and substituted by a duchy by the Emperor, again free imperial cities (Frankfurt) were threatened, and the city of Riga annexed. It has become clear that no one stands up for the cities, except the cities themselves.

The Consul, ruling for 20 years now, has promised to undo this issue by establishing proper “centralizing” reforms in the Hanseatic League, which have always been hinted at by Consuls and never done due to lack of support. The reforms will protect the interests of Hanseatic members everywhere. He has not pointed fingers towards the kontors, but it’s in everyone’s minds that the League as a whole should stop favoring them and be placed under further authority of a powerful Hansetag. Albrecht is aware that he will have to concede power if he desires to do so, because no Burgermeister on their right mind would accept his reforms with a Consul set for life. Thus, the reforms entail:

-    Increasing the manpower contribution for a standing army dedicated to defend every city. The standing navy exists, so it’s only natural to extend the League’s protection to land too, especially if we are to assert Hanseatic power in the Empire.
-    The Consul will be able to assume further control on trade matters with the explicit consent of the Hansetag, and the affected city.
-    The League changes its laws to elect a Consul every 4 years, so the Hanseatic cities can pursue change of leadership and policy more frequently. The Consul is renamed as Syndic and elections are called for next year (… which Albrecht expects to win)
The result will be an empowered Hansetag, with the main cities acquiring more influence than before. When no city has clear say, Hamburg will take precedence if nearby. Delegations of each city will have to stay in Lübeck permanently.  

Of note, the town of Hansyhorod has the rights to supervise the affairs of the kontors in Constantinople <@1432820281000333392> <@302128861209165824>, and Melk has rights over the kontors of all Italy <@190640828954181632>  both mainly due to being the ‘closest’ member to those markets.
-# Primacy and influence of the main cities of the Hansa, more or less

events.author: Onnirio
events.fullText:
https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1522011016282771527
<@302128861209165824>
 
When Albrecht received news on the Commercial Code in Eastern Rome, they came with maps collected by hanseatic traders, much more detailed and precise than anything he could have had before, with barely an outline of Eastern Rome. Albrecht was surprised by the maps, and after reading a summary of the Code, he understood. The uniformization of trade regulations were good, for a proper standard is always helpful, yet only as long as the Hansa could become privileged or predominant enough in those lands.

He looked around on the maps. It was not easy. Already, the practice of a joint city was developed in Hansyhorod, looked for expansion in England, and now perhaps in Greece. Only someone as ambitious as Albrecht could have seen this through. But he needed good, hard-working men that the Hansetag could trust so far away, and for that he would petition the Emperor for a rough spot, where the germans had to endure to gain their coin. Leukás, an island in Epirus, poor and barren as they come, with its only renowned product being… sand, which is coarse, rough, and irritating, and it gets everywhere. 

However, the islands are situated in the right spot to move goods from the Adriatic to the Gulfs of Patras and Corinth, to Athens and then to Constantinople, much better than going around Laconia. Almost no way to place a ship in those coasts, but a hanseatic community makes possible of the impossible. With so little people, and an Albanian minority already, there would be less friction with the germans. Tight contact between them and Melk would be required. Indeed, after choosing the spot, Albrecht left any further negotiation to the Melk council, otherwise this would stall for years.

-# AI image
events.title: Eastern Europe and Asia Diplo

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet: Albrecht receives the letter from the city of Köln. Perhaps it is the right time. With the loss of so many cities, it would only be right for a new on
events.fullText:
Albrecht receives the letter from the city of Köln. Perhaps it is the right time. With the loss of so many cities, it would only be right for a new one to be granted status, to demonstrate that their role is at least respected and that the number of free cities can increase. But if the League fails in this, it could not be made clearer that all cities are under threat. Though in that case, a conqueror Albrecht will consider that perhaps some cities would need to be expedited urgent membership for protection…

He sends word to the Emperor in the Nuremberg Imperial Diet, and to Bernard who represents the League there. A copy of Köln’s cry for help, for a freedom that de facto they enjoy and do not want to see crushed by the Archbishop, and the Hanseatic League’s formal support to recognize it as a Free Imperial City.

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet: After the ‘deal’ has been struck with the genoese Consiglio, 3000 germans and italo-germans leave the Ligurian sea and the Gulf of Lion. Ships from Ma
events.fullText:
After the ‘deal’ has been struck with the genoese Consiglio, 3000 germans and italo-germans leave the Ligurian sea and the Gulf of Lion. Ships from Marseille <@564196329132261376> and Pisa depart, some to Piombino and Rome, most back to Melk, to see if the council has business for them. After all, they are heading back to their request, thus they expect some kind of compensation or, at the very least, direction. They are professional merchants and bankers with families to feed. Their expertise must be needed somewhere, and before establishing themselves in Genoa they came from the kontors in Urbino, Marche or Friuli, so they are used to Italy and will not go back to northern Germany. 

With the war against Aquileia raging on, they start lobbying the Hanseatic Council of Melk, and the city will join the other merchants in Austria to pressure the Archduke: Venice is a god-forsaken republic whose traders should be substituted by the astute bavarians. The venetians have become a true nuisance to the purposes of the Archduchy, their chokehold on the Adriatic must finally be put to rest and allow the true traders of Europe to do proper business! Only then can Austria achieve glory that would make God tremble in ectasis! <@190640828954181632>
events.title: Central Europe and North Africa Diplo

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Where there is help there is cost | Duke of Sorbia Presents himself in Milan **

<@190640828954181632> <@763652399784919080> 
In the midst of the di
events.fullText:
**Where there is help there is cost | Duke of Sorbia Presents himself in Milan **

<@190640828954181632> <@763652399784919080> 
In the midst of the diet of Nuremburg there is yet another battleground of Dynasties, that of Milan. Much the same as in Sorbia so to does the central branch of the Wittelsbach aim to extend its reach, creating a cadet branch into northern Italy. Such a move externally could be well seen as the simple propensity for the expansion of noble dynasties, yet it so much more, for the issue plaguing the Bavarian lords has always been the inability to control their own titles, spread out and apart. Such disorder though problematic for them is a boon to the cadet houses, keeping each in the balance, a microcosm of wider imperial politics. Seeing the trajectory one such Cadet, Klementia August von Wittelsbach is not so idle, he too going to Milan but not to support his nephew, but instead himself. Presenting himself as a candidate instead, so saying to his nephew that he lacks for a wife and so it is only natural.  

Arriving late to the assembly *(after leaving Nuremburg half way through the diet*) he makes a showcase of dramatic showboating, riding atop a horse painted red and wearing a thick auroch leather red cloak to match.

> *“Esteemed men of Milan i do come to present myself, not as some child nor lecherous old man nor as a greedy merchant for while gold does so flow in these lands you all are proud and quite rightfully so! I know well the might of Milan whose warriors fought so well in the Hussite wars alongside myself and others or even before not that hold any ill will for the matter of Brandenburg. You need not just a name and husband for your lady no no you need a leader! I Duke of Sorbia offer leadership, I direct scion of the emporoer offer imperial might! I commander of battles and warrior offer you all my sword! Where now French stalk these lands and would but choke you from the seas i would take such coasts and wash their beaches in the blood of the enemies of Milan! I offer myself well and true and in return i would but offer as well service, for i have lands who stand empty, whose savage lords have been driven off and could well be doing with new lords, and for one other thing” *
-# his gaze falls to Franseco Sforza 

> *“i will say with confidence that soon a new imperial army, bearing with it the might of the empire shall be rallied and we shall need a leader of it, i belive the men of Milan may well lead such a force like no other, what say you all?!” *

**(Offers lands in Sorbia ((will give more power to nobles in my land and give money to Milan)), and to republican military leaders command and control over the imperial army if it forms ((yes i know how much of a bad idea it is, but such is politics)) **

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Ambivalent Dukedom | Sorbia  **

> *“Much like the Roman legions of old i find myself in dark woods, surrounded by all manner of savages, unlike
events.fullText:
**The Ambivalent Dukedom | Sorbia  **

> *“Much like the Roman legions of old i find myself in dark woods, surrounded by all manner of savages, unlike the Romans at Teutoburg i shall not so blithely presume such people to be my friends for want of my gilded greatness alone, i shall not stray into those woods, i shall stay here and rule” *
- Klementia August von Wittelsbach first Duke of Sorbia 

For some years within the independent Sorbian realm of lower Lusatia there had been talks of uniting the lower and upper regions, entrenching a Slavic aristocracy in the Holy Roman empire, of all ironies this would be done, in a manner of speaking. There now stands in 1444 a Sorbian Duchy, yet it is not the Sorbs who rule but Germans, there stands an united dynastic government rather than a loose collection of merchant towns, yet not a Sorb or perhaps Byzantine lord as had been the aim but instead the very name that had been so cursed some forty years before, Wittelsbach. A duchy formed out of happenstance and political maneuvering, one that many a man could expect to collapse, for who there would support such a hated family and one that had gotten rid of so much liberty, yet it does not. So stands the eastern sword of the Wittelsbachs, the Duchy of Sorbia.

**The Two Body problem  **

The current Duchy of Sorbia is an artificial entity, formed from the unification of upper and lower Lusatia, from an outsiders perspective not so much an oddity, however either through genuine wit or more likely luck such a unification of lands does more ironically to disunite the Sorbs. The Leagues of Upper and Lower Lusatia had long diverged in governmental and economic practice, lower being a union of self-made merchants who claimed open titles, whose trade was focused as being an intermediary between the Hanse and Poland. Upper Lusatia, a personal crown land of Bohemia was instead a self-autonomous loose alliance of true nobles and civil officials, an economy made on merchant guilds and trade with Bohemia proper. Forcefully united in the wake of the Hungarian occupation both lands remaining higher classes are ill suited to one another, lacking connections or sense of unity. 

Though causing issues for the new Bavarian lord in the form of hampering his ability to mobilize resources it has been an overall boon, the towns isolated bear not enough of a threat alone to his rule, for as long as this disunity stands the risk of a coup is lessened, at least from the inside.

**Those who would despair  **
<@252560004069064705> 

Though the rulers of Luckau the Kazawa’s would be executed during the Chalice coup this did not stop prominent families of Gubin, Forst and Breshckow from fleeing into Poland. Though not the strongest friends with the realm if there was one reliable thing to tug at the Sejm of Poland it was the subjugation of Nobles, much more so Slavic ones to centralized power, giving a safe sanctuary to start over. Though their calls for an intervention into the region would remain a loud but fundamentally powerless, however for as long as the nobles remain Poland may yet claim a measure of legitimacy into stepping into the Holy Roman Empire. With a sufficiently ambitious king it could even see a Polish foothold in Germany, a buffer for if the empire ever proved... problematic.

<@288410456526422016> 
For the traders of Lower Lusatia their issue would be with the Hanse instead, for one hand the Duke of Sorbia, Klementia has looked to German merchant class to take back power from the local Sorb merchants, in the long term likely looking to undermine the urban rights established in the last decades, in this regard giving a wide range of powers and support to Hanseatic merchants. Yet for that this is ever counterbalanced by those more belligerent traders, especially those of Hamburg who would argue that such a loss of an merchant ally would but set the precedent for any merchant city or cities to become so amalgamated under the nearest pretense, the league protected not by rights but by arms alone, the annexation of free cities in the Rhine doing little to dissuade many of this notion. The funding and support of a Sorbian merchant alliance could turn back such a dangerous move by the Second Estate and even secure Lusatia into the Hanseatic league, though one would wonder just how the aristocracy of the empire would react to merchant involvement in dynastic affairs.

**The New Sorbia **

For the efforts of the Duke himself the young ambitious man has been at work, though seizing much land and offering it to trusted allies from Bavaria Klementia has unlike his late distant dynast of Brandenburg taken a personal interest in his won lands. Outwardly appearing fully invested in ruling and controlling this wayward realm, much to the pleasure of his uncle in Bavaria who would have feared for a dynastic opponent over the jewel of the family. Such an assumption is wrong, for Sorbia has one left tool upon which this scion may claim the seat of his line, the imperial barracks of Luckau.  

Though not fully built in time for the Hussite wars the realm of Sorbia still holds the title of base of the Imperial army, though a ceremonial title until given powers or wealth such a thing has the capabilities to give Klementia great powers. Fully intending to build up his realm as a Margravedom of old, for what he will claim the good of the Empire, and if he can happen to use such an army to march on Munich, well that's just a nice bonus.  

-# Crest of the New Duchy of Sorbia, the quartered arms of both Lower and Upper Lusatia crests, along with the central Wittelsbach crest

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
# King Charles V de Valois
“What is a man, but his ambitions in life.”

Midas touched and administratively skilled, he has ruled France well enough. H
events.fullText:
# King Charles V de Valois
“What is a man, but his ambitions in life.”

Midas touched and administratively skilled, he has ruled France well enough. However, his external ambitions may be far too grand for an old man with his skillset. Ambitions that have angered several neighboring realms, forced others to be within de Valois' web of power, and underlined the growth of belligerent diplomacy from the French crown.

## Ambitions in Italy
As Jacques Coeur best put it, “Italy must be controlled by us and our likeminded allies”.

The Kingdom of France has gone long without defeat, and stands as military hegemon among the most powerful. A might to be wielded in favor of the Valois, and a might to enrich the French crown. By diplomatically or martially pressing the claims of French nobility, and de Valois, there can be economic and political domination in Italy. This agenda is of high priority for it offers many boons. Jehan de Chatillon, a closely related family member of de Valois house, made a defining comment in court regarding the matter. Stating that “we must crush the competition, there is too much wealth and  power on the line.”

The Italian peninsula presents far too much power and wealth to ignore, and with the unchecked French power there will be bold pursuits within the Peninsula. An exertion to be made with the expensive royal army the crown maintains, and with the condoterri that are plentiful.
-# Outlining that the 100k+ French levy will not be used for offensive campaigns in the Italian Wars, and will be left solely for the defense of the French Kingdom proper. The French crown will only utilize prof and mercs in Italy.
-# <@315868897012219904> <@302527114283057153> <@234699731715096576> <@422498641425596418> <@763652399784919080> <@545657694489542666> <@302128861209165824> <@150411715757080576> 

### Neapolitan Kingdom forced into the French sphere
The first among all ambitions, and the one that began factionalism across Italy and outside of it, was pressing Countess Jeanne III de Saint-Omer claim for the Kingdom of Naples and Duke Felepo II de Savoie ambitions in Provence. 

A great and bloody war where more than 100,000 French died besieging Provence with abysmally terrible skill, and facing unfavorable battles in Ferrara. Despite the cost of life, it was still won with King Marti de Barcelona of Aragon abandoning his inherited realms and the overwhelming strength of French steel.

While the outcome would greatly empower the houses of de Saint-Omer and de Savoie, it also empowered de Valois sphere of influence. Bringing economic reach deeper in the Mediterranean for French trade, and establishing a new and powerful French ally in Italy.

### Valois candidate for Milan
-# <@545657694489542666> 
While a closely related Valois candidate can tragically not be found, there are several among the Valois house who could prove to be feasible candidates to marry Heiress Elisabetta Visconti. The best closest related candidate being Pierre de Valois. Surely a difficult candidate to press due to closeness to the French crown and age disparity, but one that comes bearing the wealth and prestige of de Valois name!

He is proposed by de Valois house as a candidate to be arranged married to the Duchess of Milan for when she comes of age. King Charles V de Valois offers the gift of 500 francs to the head of the Viscounti house, Giovanni Visconti, seeking his support for the marriage and to put in a good word with the nobility of Milan.
-# (These two sentences above are the official valois offer for now.)

Besides the official Valois offer the Milanese estates should note that he is 2nd cousin to the young Milanese Queen, but more importantly that he is a dullard. Giving them the opportunity to press for greater privileges at the expense of the crown, and opportunity for riches via brokering favors and special trade deals with Valois connections. However, for every give there is a take. Pierre de Valois is 3rd in line for the French throne at present, and poses the chance to place the duchy into the hands of the Valois in Paris. Consequently it would give de Valois house greater influence over Italy, and firmer interests within Milan.

## Fun Valois Facts!
Everyone loves the Valois and France, of course you want to know more!

### Hochmeister Francois de Valois of the Teutonic Order, the next King of France
King Charles V de Valois of France holds no heirs, which has forced him to determine who is the most legitimate candidate for inheritance for his Kingdom. The best and closest candidate is his 1st cousin Hochmeister Francois de Valois of the Teutonic Order. A man with experience in rule and experience in warfare, but with questionable real skill and being notably too careful at times. Even more interesting is his sinful nature, and active lack of practice in faith. While the details of his sinfulness are obscure, he is well known for paying indulgences often enough.

### No talent like family talent!
In a world where many stand as your enemy, family can be your best ally. King Charles V knows this well, and he has put in great efforts to tie de Valois house with other notable and prestigious houses within France. Even granting family members exclusive positions in court, and roles of great prestige and power. Actions done to cement Valois legitimacy to rule, and gather greater support from the powerful noble estates.

Of course there is Francois de Valois who was made Hochmeister of the Teutonic order decades ago, but another very prominent member of the de Valois house is Philippe de Valois. He is a naturally talented general that only lacks experience, but has had his iron tested recently against the Neapolitan Kingdom and its former allies. He has a long life and career ahead of him, and it is expected he will only come to hone his skill since France holds many new and old loyal enemies.

Other prominent men that are closely connected to de Valois house are Jehan de Chatillon and Philippe de Bourbon. While not independently exceptional in their work, they are greatly cooperative. Rotating in tasks to develop the urban areas of France, manage recruitment of new soldiers for France’s armies and castles, and management to aid in making lowly peasants into useful laborers across the growing Kingdom. Philippe being a **fiscal hawk** has organized several important budget cuts on the King’s treasury, found new finances in long existing areas once not exploited, brought greater control to regions of France that were once fairly autonomous, and even challenged France’s current economic structure.

Of all the boons, his challengement of the Kingdom’s economic norms is a growing concern. The deviation may bring even more riches to France, but is creating factionalism among the nobility and the merchants who oppose one another on economic privileges. Especially those within urban areas, since Philippe de Bourbon’s efforts are empowering the merchant class ever so little every year against the noble estates. This has created the potential opportunity for Hanseatic re-entrance into French markets, the potential opportunity for the more mercantile to broker deals, present likelihood of economic interests pushed wildly by feuding houses and guilds, disrupt the Kingdom’s stability by said feuds and challenging the norm, and possibly even developing out of hand into a major conflict if tradition is challenged far too hard by the merchants.
-# Will likely become the defining point of a future major internal conflict similar to the Griffion-Dragon war. Generally speaking as a reference, a French version of the hook-cod conflict.

### The Frencien culture, and its entrance into a renaissance 
The Kingdom of France is full of variations in what it means to be French, but the most dominant among them is Francien. It is the most populous, influential, and deeply engraved culture within France.

This is not unintentional, since a majority of artistic funding and educational infrastructure resides in predominantly Francien regions of France. Regions where Normans and Languedociens reside still see significant development too, but the bias of where to build the finer infrastructure is very clear. As a result it has become the centre in which de Valois finances artists to write literature, build extravagant palaces,  paint fine art works glorifying god, and even expensive and splendorously designed prayerbooks for the growing monasteries of France.

-# (Future financial burden the French Crown will gladly endure)

Despite all these efforts, more can always be done. Thus King Charles V is interested in indulging further into the arts, and plans to spend egregious sums of wealth making his court and palace splendorous displays of French, more specifically Francien, culture.

Being a court of splendor, it will hopefully be tantalizing to talented artists who seek to show the greatest extent of their skills. To be added among the best works displayed within the court. At present a skilled Flemish artist by the name of **Barthelemy d’Eyck** who currently nears the end of his current work on **The Aix Annunciation** in Southern France has been invited to paint great pieces within Paris. With talent like his the royal court will flourish.

events.snippet:
# A Kingdom born of the Tenth Crusade.
**And the differences between the Kingdom born of the First Crusade.**

When Godfrey and his allies breached th
events.fullText:
# A Kingdom born of the Tenth Crusade.
**And the differences between the Kingdom born of the First Crusade.**

When Godfrey and his allies breached the walls of Jerusalem in 1099, they tore into the city in an orgy of death and destruction. Tens of thousands of Muslims and Jews in the city were slaughtered over the following days, and the First Kingdom of Jerusalem was born after the long and horrifically bloody road that was the First Crusade.

Yet...the Tenth Crusade, which saw the Kingdom restored oncemore, did not endure the same hardships that those first Crusaders did more than three centuries before. Instead, with the Mamluk authority over the Levant rapidly crumbling, they seized the opportunity gifted to them and invaded the region at several points from their close launching point of Cyprus. Faced by insignificant forces...it was easy to take crush all who opposed them. The First Crusade had no such advantage, and instead had to make their way overland through the hostile lands of the Turks, under constant attack and attrition. Their journey at several points could have failed spectacularly, but guided by the blessings of God they triumphed over all the insurmountable odds. 

But the Tenth Crusade swept aside all of their meagre competition, and whilst the heroes of the First Crusade stormed the Holy City in the sight of the Lord, the Tenth Crusade had not the need to even march south of Acre before the resistance crumbled and peace was hurriedly offered. Jerusalem was occupied peacefully, there was no slaughter or feverous proclamations of God's glory whilst bathed in the blood of the non-believers. In a way this lack of glory despite conquering nearly all that the First Crusade had accomplished three centuries before was a perfect representation on how the times had changed since Bohemond, Tancred, Godfrey, and Raymond became legends.

For the Third Kingdom of Jerusalem was not the First, and they well knew it. Twice before the Kingdom had fallen, despite all the prayers and brave Holy warriors fighting with all their might, and the confidence in the Kingdom's continued existence was no longer absolute. Many even in 1444 would remember when the old Crusader nobility was still exiled on the island of Cyprus, all of their titles titular, without money or land to their names. Thus where one Kingdom was born with vigor and fervor and glory, this one was reborn with weariness and pragmatism and humility. Of course there would always be the zealous, those that believed that the Kingdom, finally restored, was destined now to remain forever as long as the King had the blessing of God. Yet those men who were there when the Tenth Crusade concluded were cautious. They knew why the Crusade was such a success, and it was in large part due to the great weakness of the Mamluk Sultans in Egypt.

Those Crusaders may have cried tears of joy when the Kingdom was restored, but in most of their hearts they feared the day that Egypt regained its strength. For that was the death of the first two Kingdoms, and it could very well be their death as well. A certain weary attitude thus was built into this renewed Kingdom. Local Christians, despite their heresies, were given more power to increase the security of the Kingdom, great efforts were made to tolerate the local Muslim and Jewish populace, and the Kingdom of Jerusalem has quickly begun to abandon its offensive momentum in favor of a defensive policy. For instance, once the treaty with Syria was signed in the early 1430's no attempts have been made to push on further, perhaps to pressure the important cities of Damascus, Aleppo, or Edessa. Instead the Kingdom seems to focus more of its energies internally rather than externally.

Thus this Third Kingdom of Jerusalem seems to share a mindset much more aligned with the Second Kingdom instead of the First, despite the fact that the Second Kingdom of Jerusalem was barely a Kingdom at all, clinging onto the coastal cities after the Third Crusade had failed to take back Jerusalem from Saladin, whom had destroyed those in the First Kingdom who believed in glorious battle and zealous conquest. What was left after the Third Crusade was a broken realm, unable to do anything but defend its lands and hope for another Crusade to save it. There was little pride left to be had. After the Second, pitiable Kingdom fell in 1291, what was left was an even more broken Kingdom. For the next century it lived in exile on Cyprus, and it too could be nothing but a middling local power until it was saved by others.

The Tenth Crusade finally did save it, but there was little recovery from the past two centuries of failure in the minds of many. For there was no great siege of Jerusalem to win glory and honor back in the hearts of those who fought, nor was there any great battle where they triumphed despite the odds stacked against them. Instead they had won because of the weakness of their enemies, more so than the strength of their faith in the omnipotent God, and that reality does little to restore a Kingdom's proud spirit.

**The Kingdom of Jerusalem *had* been restored, yes, but it was not the same Kingdom of the 11th century. Instead, forged from the failures of two centuries of disasters and continued losses, it was a much more weary power... a Kingdom terrified of being destroyed oncemore and forced into yet another era of utter humiliations...**

-# The Fall of Acre, 1291

**(End)**

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**Halych Proclamation**

Following years of struggle and uncertainty, Kingdom of Halych-Volhynia has undergone many changes in the religious make-up.
events.fullText:
**Halych Proclamation**

Following years of struggle and uncertainty, Kingdom of Halych-Volhynia has undergone many changes in the religious make-up. Reign of Louis d'Anjou has brought confidence to the Catholic priests, as they have recieved a much free-er hand in the Kingdom following his coronation in Lviv. Yet, the decades following his victory in Hungary have become the true kick-start of their efforts - secure in a Catholic Kingdom and supported by nobility of the single most powerful realm in the region, King Louis has completely removed any and all stops for the conversion of Ruthenia. Opposed only locally, the Kingdom has undergone huge change, with thousands actively converting whether out of their own choice, or because the winds of time seem to have blown away the advantages of remaining Orthodox.

Yet, Catholicism isn't the only Christian confession that has come to benefit from the absence of the King - Prokopivtsi, followers of one of the students of Jan Hus, Prokip, have become a significant movement in the immediate years following Hus's rise in Bohemia. Coinciding with the events both in Hungary and far-away lands of the Czechs, Prokopivist movement has come to reinterpret the Orthodoxy in the more modern argumentative terms of Hussitism, keeping much of the rite and mysticism, while also adding heavy autonomy of both Church and man, of both body and mind. Festering in the cities at first, the movement has come to spread mostly in Halych, until at a certain point it has gained enough following and support among the students of the Halych Academy. Professed now by dozens of young nobles, burghers, dignitaries and scholars, this sect has spread much further than Catholicism, making a solid base in Orthodox regions of the country, such as Podolia and Volhynia.

Yet, the most crucial point has come to be the Halych Proclamation, solidifying the movement and marking it as a significant confession in the Kingdom, seeking to decentralise spiritual power and oppose both centralisation of the Catholic and Orthodox Churches

events.author: Klatos
events.snippet: following the decline of the Kievan rus,internal strives between local princes and a threatening mongol horde, the full nation shattered into  smaller
events.fullText:
following the decline of the Kievan rus,internal strives between local princes and a threatening mongol horde, the full nation shattered into  smaller princedoms to the dissatisfaction of the people and the nobility of kiev who held power for so long over the rus, with the last hammer in the nail that the mongols hit, what followed became soon an disaster for kievan control and each princedom sought his own power including the rising muscovites whom were for an while an concern to the princedom,yet after seeing them kick out the turks and mongols from our lands , kiev became neutral of them but the dreams of an second kiev centered rus state died with that feeling of neutrality and to the other princedoms who only wanted power yet struggled keeping themselves upright they became increasingly weak compared to the regional powers and a concern to the present Prince of kiev,


Anatole the second, with time and an disturbing realization, his conclusion of kiev is that they will stand but weaker compared to everyone else, with the hungarians on the border and their overreach into lands that were not for them to have their hands in, he started to modernize and upgrade his army and planned with the local nobles of their respective regions campaign into the lands of the crimeans, that succeeded and he had his share of the land from the mongols, with little effort to convert them to either culture of religion he focused on the next goal which was to unify all ruthanian people which succeeded again, now the age of difficulty seems to increasingly creep into his mind with the hungarians to be wary of,muscovites to be wary of and the polish to be wary of he started to plan an way, that was to unite all previous princedoms if he can without causing issues with either muscovy or the polish, thus started his dream of an united ruthenian state.
