events.author: Ceb
events.snippet: > “God be good Theodore, could you not have restrained the men?” *Almost demanded Edward Tudur of his brother.* “They were not of sound mind Edward.”
events.fullText:
> “God be good Theodore, could you not have restrained the men?” *Almost demanded Edward Tudur of his brother.* “They were not of sound mind Edward.” *the three year junior Theodore retorted, attempting to protect his own reputation.* “Many had seen service at Cardiff, they were determined to extract their justice for injuries suffered at that time.”
> 
> “And cousin Goronwy…” *attempted to continued Theodore,* “don’t tell me again about that man.” *snapped back Edward,* “The fool managed to lose most of our 800 cavalrymen against a pack of peasants with longbows.” *his anger directed more at someone not present.* “...is heavily injured.” *finally concluded Theodore.* “His reward then.” *coldly responded Edward.*

The Tudur army had **won** at Moreton Marsh. It was achieved not through the [deceptive negotiations](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1523098668943540385) of Theodore and his son or the [tactics](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1524101053296148661) of Edward. The victory was had by simply pinning the smaller force down, and then crushing them with superior numbers and equipment. In deed, if not result, the Longbow and defensive works of the Neville army showed their superiority over even armoured knights.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet: *The fields once green were now strewn **red**. A crime in better times, but perhaps the pursuit of justice - as the men claim - after decades of civi
events.fullText:
*The fields once green were now strewn **red**. A crime in better times, but perhaps the pursuit of justice - as the men claim - after decades of civil war. Nevertheless, the object of Edward Tudur’s design was before him. It was time to decide the fate of those he now had under his power.*



### Tynged - Fate
-# (or Destiny/Doom)

> - Queen Judith Piast and her husband Lord Protector William Neville are brought as prisoners to the Black Tower of **Cardiff Castle**. Many believe it is the most fortified town in all of Wales.
> - The unmarried Prince Richard Neville and Lady Nancy Neville are brought to the increasingly lavish fortress-town of **Pembroke**, center of the Tudurs and most governance in southern Wales. They are each to be treated according to how they behave.
> - Lady Rachel Neville, her husband Morgan (Morcant) Mortimer - the Earl of March’s nephew, and their children are brought to **Carmarthen Castle** as ‘guests’. She is Judith’s eldest and to be given all hospitality ||as a guarantee of good conduct against the Earl of March||.
> - Any surviving nobles like d'Arras are sent to nearby **Bristol** where they will be held until ransom is paid or otherwise.

All are treated according to their claimed station. All are also made aware of their situation and the necessary behaviour they must exhibit in order to preserve it. ||Guards surrounding the former two groups are given permission to kill their prisoners if they should attempt or come close to escape.||

-# *The main gate and Black Tower of Cardiff Castle; Carmarthen Castle and surrounding town*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## Returning to Foundations

The Tudur host arrives on the outskirts of London a week after the battle of Moreton Marsh. Throughout their slow march t
events.fullText:
## Returning to Foundations

The Tudur host arrives on the outskirts of London a week after the battle of Moreton Marsh. Throughout their slow march they were greeted by both respect, as one receives at the head of an army, and deep seated fear at rumors of their deeds against the Neville host. The Welsh-Irish and increasingly English army travelled via the settled and academic Oxford, the idle grazing lands of Henly, and the fertile fields of Harrow. Orders were given to prevent any depravity after the recent battle but it [hardly mattered](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1449171134317723791/1524495808349736970); a soldier paid in gold was loyal only to his own aggrandizement it would seem. No matter, for each day the world seemed to grow smaller and yet ever faster as the walls of London loomed larger every hour.

A Tudur banner, defaced as always with the Cross of Saint George, is shown in clear signal upon their arrival at the city. They remain in full view of the newly crowned King Edward Plantagenet, his steadfast Portuguese allies represented by Capitão Elpidio Pacheco <@298192682554228738>, and the King’s recently reduced court. At the host’s head is Edward Tudur himself. The de facto commander of the Principality of Wales makes no show of delay or confusion as he had his brother do at Bristol. In his hands is a crown, the very one torn off the head of Lord Protector William Neville after his defeat a week prior.
.

*From bent knees he offers up crown and following submission and loyalty of Wales, as well as his own personal loyalties sealed in blood besides:*
> “The crown, stolen from the head of King Edward, the King of England and France and Lord of Ireland. May it be restored to your line and may it return this land to the prosperity it once received.”
> 
> “For myself I ask only for your own everlasting health. May you take my daughter Elizabeth, now of the age of adulthood, as your lawfully wedded wife. From your union you will secure your lineage’s continuation, the same line that carved this herein country's foundation to start.”
> 
> “Long may you reign, King Edward V.”

-# (Sorry for the use of HotD and wrong decor. Shared themes are too good not to use here tho)

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
**Where the River and the Sea Meet**

With Wallachia prospering over the past few peaceful years after the rebellion, the trade along the Danube has d
events.fullText:
**Where the River and the Sea Meet**

With Wallachia prospering over the past few peaceful years after the rebellion, the trade along the Danube has drastically grown, as the flow of trade from the interior has returned. Throughout Wallachia’s dark times and its resurgence, the trade in the Black Sea has flourished. Now, as trade flows down the river and up the shores, the town of Tulcea stands where the river and the sea meet.

The favourable location of Tulcea sparked opportunity and many seized it. People flocked to the town, and it expanded rapidly, pushing away the marshes that had lain in the surrounding lands. New stone houses rose from the ground where wooden huts had stood before, a proper market square stood in place of the few stalls that had once surrounded muddy roads and a single well. Docks appeared along the river and the coast, where only fishing shacks had once stood, now accommodating the new merchants arriving at Tulcea. The town is unrecognisable to the old townsfolk, as the landscape of woods and marshes changed into houses and streets.  

With the opportunity appearing on the horizon in these peaceful times, will the young duke seize it or will it fall into someone else’s hands?

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**The Reichsarmee** 
<@763652399784919080> <@1216097200401154181> <@306458253674545153> <@288410456526422016> <@385875387608989714><@137101075411894272><@564196329132261376><@261290667106369536> <@792904515795550218> <@215639063502258176> <@545657694489542666> <@302527114283057153> <@315868897012219904> 

After the de-facto [independence of Lower Lusatia](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518313605060231321) in 1404 the Slavic merchant league would find itself in an awkward position, surrounded by hostile German princes and legally within a grey area the league would vote to attempt to secure themselves within the empire, to this end [bargaining](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518352850776752189)with the then newly elected Milanese Emperor in 1415. Successfully securing recognition and titles it would come at a curious cost, being charged with [repelling banditry in northern Germany](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518363555169308896), not just within their borders but without, organizing a resistance to the then rife reaving of the Visby pirate alliance, being viewed as a purely neutral unconnected political force the Sorbs would be a useful middlemen for such an coalition or worst case easy scapegoats. Such a duty was less then enviable within Lusatia and was often pushed back against by the local townships, slowing down any attempts at putting up a practical imperial force, then something unexpected happened, it worked.

In 1421 Visby influence would be removed from Wolgast and the North, officially credited to the threat of an imperial coalition led by Sorbia. In truth instead it being the [work of the Hanse](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1518371999024549889) who in a diplomatic move had removed Baltic pirates through bribes but gave all due credit to the Sorbs in an attempt to bolster ties. From this political play however would spawn a key touchstone in local Sorbian politics and wider within the empire, that the idea of a joint imperial force had legitimacy. Turning a once reviled payment for recognition into a prevailing [pillar of Sorbian foreign policy](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520207566343241759) , seeking a way for the Slavs to carve out a position for themselves in the empire.

Even when the [Lusatian leagues would be dissolved](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520887520630669595) in the wake of the disastrous end of the Hussite wars for the pro Hussite Slavs the newly installed Duke would be an [energetic supporter of the idea of an Imperial army](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520893796055777332) that he could lead, historical debate would remain on why this was the case, some believing it was an attempt to win the support of the martial byzantine influenced new noble class of lower Lusatia , to protect from a possible Hungarian backed invasion into his upper Lusatia titles or perhaps the most scandalous part of an audacious plan to unseat his distant cousin the emperor. Getting his moment in 1444 as part of the [Imperial diet of Nuremburg](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1520937458320146643/1520937608518045767) Klementia would use the then ongoing Hungarian crisis to sell the empire on his proposal. Somehow succeeding despite not attending the final vote himself the [Imperial Army bill would pass](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1520937458320146643/1524415755716661441). 

So it is that across the empire a grand gathering shall be set, men and arms, an army is to be made with one man keen on leading it, yet keenness alone shall not determine the future nor for those who would carry the banners of the great empire, of what character shall this endeavor be made? Steel or Blood?

```ansi
[1;2m[1;37mNew Narrative Mechanic: Imperial Army Influence[0m[0m

[1;2m[1;37mThe Holy Roman Imperial army remains in a nascent stage, one that has not been gradually built upon like other military structures but artistically created. The building of such a force could see any types of foreign officers and soldiers fight and shape such a new institution, each bringing varying forms of millitary strategy and culture, brining with it their own political implications as well.[0m[0m

[1;2m[1;37mEach nation across Europe and within the empire may send and attempt to influence such a force, in turn creating perhaps an allied sword or more nefariously a dagger that can be thrust into the emperors heart. 

Rolling dice over multiple sessions along with RP actions (such as wars or treaties) to set influence gain and modifiers based on investment of both leaders and resources [0m[0m
```

**[Scottish Adventurers](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gallowglass>)**  
<@1417260821674852363> 

Based in Neumark and a relic of the now decades gone civil war in Brandenburg the wider region has been a chaotic patchwork of bandit and robber knight fiefdoms, only the [land of Neumark ruled by Scottish lords](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520205087027494972) who seized fortresses remain stable. Linked in turn to the wider unofficial Scottish international network of mercenaries and wayward lordlings the use of Scots as officers and shock troops would avoid much in the way of political issues, though some may complain of communication problems....

Scottish Mercenaries offer experience and outsider status, savage and unruly these warriors would fight to the bitter end (or until their whisky runs out)

**[Byzantine Magisters](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tagma_(military)>)** 
<@302128861209165824> 

Within Sorbia where much of the support for this institution comes from there exists little martial tradition, instead it is the [Byzantines whose culture diffuses](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520206085904928792) the landscape in many respects, military manuals written in Greek and the common military language used in the region. Inviting from the resurgent empire nobles would match well the grandeur of this empire of Romans as well, though would no doubt ruffle the plumes of more than one great eagle.

[1;2m[1;35mByzantine Effect:[0m[0m

<@1432820281000333392> <@423689694258397195> 

In the brutal Hussite wars, there has been learned a new model of warfare, not one of gleaming knights or crushing weight of levies but of black powder. The combined arms of firearms and steel, [Hungarians the most learned](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1520890515397279915) of this than all. The use of Hungarian line officers bringing forth the sharpest edge in innovations though such an irony and issue that the army made mostly to combat them would be used in such a manner.

<@460814348487753730> 

None could deny of which kingdom stands the greatest of all military powers, the one who has bested black princes, red lions and mighty eagles, the Dragon’s realm, France. A pale mirror of the Empire where the kings gather ever more power, [whose royal army](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1513579436031737987) is not made of squabbling nobles but crown appointed constables and enforced command bearing to but a single true ruler, such centralism would be a boon of efficiency and power though frighten every Imperial prince from minor to Elector for fear of what shall happen to them if such a thing became the norm rather than the exception.

The empire has fought together more than once, yet as a swirling mass of wolves rather than as of a lion. In the wars against France such wolves would [drown in their own blood](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1510759035887030333). Such disorder cannot be had for a grand army, yet every prince would think themselves a king of their own demesne and no king bows to another's martial capabilities, yet such variance can well be a boon if only commanded well. The empire filled with both regional mercenary companies and various princely regiments could well be molded into a force, with enough effort, though it will leave few happy but in war few ever are.

For now, Klementia August von Wittelsbach sits for this title, ambitions agent, already attempting to [barter with such a name in Milan](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1522296768790790144) , as time would go on such an office would be fortified by the ever-increasing demands of such an army. events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### London is well worth a letter

The marriage progressed in royal but modest fashion. On one hand, a destitute King paying in marriage for the past
events.fullText:
### London is well worth a letter

The marriage progressed in royal but modest fashion. On one hand, a destitute King paying in marriage for the past and future service of a large army outside his walls; on the other, the alleged descendants of Marchudd ap Cynan and more likely [Ednyfed Fychan](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515552676207919206) fulfilling their ‘duty’. The former was of a line traceable directly back to the Norman conquerors who carved modern England out from the Anglo-Saxon times before it. The latter was of the Ministerial Aristocracy of northern Wales and claimed blood that came before even the Saxons. Both seemed sated in design - for nobility, no matter its purchase, was security in both the lands of the White and Red Dragons.

*Tudur genealogists would later label this an implicit “Cynefin” or “Understanding”. Though later actions solidified it, its peaceful beginning was at the marriage of Elizabeth Tudur and King Edward V.*
-# (Look up the word Cynefin if you’re curious about its deeper meaning)

The House of Tudur already possessed a partially anglicized origin when [Sir Arthur Tudur](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1515809279188144251) took his grandfather's name, Tudur, as his last in the English custom. Its Celtic meaning of “people-ruler” (perhaps) is ancient and synonymous with the powerful Germanic name Theodoric. The family’s survival has thus always rested partially upon this adaptability and loyalty to nobility over strict dogma. Now, it grants them power in their own right ||as defacto diarchs|| as opposed to exercising it simply in another’s name like so many of their ancestors.

The [union of Margaret Beaufort and Edward Tudur](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1518498742163079279) has produced three living children. These are descendants of both the Princes of Wales ||the Tudurs say|| and King Edward III via the Beauforts. In the age old tradition of many Welsh aristocrats before them they now fully anglicize their family name from **Tudur** to **Tudor**. Alongside their growing holdings in England proper this completes the transition that so many other Welshmen have already done in the preceding centuries. The House of Tudor is, or at least soon will be, an **English House** with ties to Wales as opposed to the inverse.

*May God, their new son-in-law the King of England, and ||most importantly|| the English aristocracy grant them favor after such leal dedication to crown and country.*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### Deadly Reminder

*It was hardly yet daybreak and already a knock rattled Prince Richard Neville’s door.*

His rooms, if you could call them that,
events.fullText:
### Deadly Reminder

*It was hardly yet daybreak and already a knock rattled Prince Richard Neville’s door.*

His rooms, if you could call them that, were hardly fit for a lord and never a Prince. Still, they were better than a cell like he knew existed here in Pembroke. He believed these small concessions to his condition to be a minor note of civilization on account of Pembroke’s English population, even as it was scattered amongst the barbarism of the Tudors and this country’s true nature. *The Prince got up and walked the short distance to the door.*

“My Prince.” *Began a lightly armoured man with only a superfluous bow and without introduction,* “Gather your things and prepare to depart. You will be leaving this place in an hour when the light properly arrives.” *any time for questions was silenced as the hurried man finished.* “Earl Edward has decided to defect from King Edward’s cause given his recent purges. You are to be released and gather another force to reclaim your family’s rights.”

“**Secretly**”, *the word came with clear emphasis,* “he has tasked me with releasing you and bringing you out a service gate. There are many who would see your line slain in Wales.”

Stilled silence concluded the episode. The moment faded quickly though as still well-informed Richard realized the antipathy that Edward’s actions in London must have caused. If not now, he would never reclaim his father’s, and his own, crown.

*Prince Richard Neville was quietly brought down to the eastern gate of Pembroke Castle and given a horse with supplies. He was told that only 20 minutes east northeast of them there was a camp with men ready to escort him to England and support him in his cause there.*

…**Five minutes later the city’s alarms were raised and hunting parties deployed…**

*Richard Neville was hunted down and **killed** by a swift arrowshot to the heart. He had behaved contrary to his oaths solemnly sworn as a noble captive and aimed to raise rebellion against His Majesty Edward V in England once more. The Neville sympathizing guard who aided his escape was found shortly thereafter and **hanged** without the right to speak and defend himself. Earl Edward Tudor, remarking on the situation some few days later in London, simply said he knew the young man would try something foolish like this sooner or later.*

> - Richard’s sister - Lady Nancy Neville - is made to swear vows to the Church in her ‘grief’. She is condemned to the secure but relatively luxurious Caldey Abbey for the rest of her days.
> - The elder Lady Rachel Neville and her children via Morgan Mortimer are made to foreswear any claim to the throne of England. ||Unofficially being placed under house arrest,|| Otherwise being allowed to return to Wigmore and their father’s household.
> - Most importantly, Judith Piast and her husband William Neville are made **permanent residents** of the Black Tower at Cardiff Castle. May the rest of their days be restful and filled with peace.

*The House of Neville, or at least those with royal blood, is extinguished or forsworn. May God grant favor to England and its people for the necessary and ‘unpredictable’ actions taken to secure this peace.*

events.snippet:
**THE TOLEDO CONEFRENCE OF 1444**

In light of recent events, namely French and Tunisian incursions into Iberia and the Western Mediterranean, King Fa
events.fullText:
**THE TOLEDO CONEFRENCE OF 1444**

In light of recent events, namely French and Tunisian incursions into Iberia and the Western Mediterranean, King Fadrique of Castile invited the Monarchs of Iberia to a conference of kings in the city of Toledo. Many matters were discussed over the course of a single day. And at the end was published a binding treaty containing the following:

- The Council of Iberia will he held once every 10 years, the seat rotating between Toledo, Barcelona, and Lisbon, with the next council to be held in Barcelona in 1455. This council may be convened early in a city of the caller's choosing should an emergency arise.

- The Monarchs of Iberia will create a defensive alliance against the Kingdom of France, and commit to a campaign to remove French influence from Navarre and Iberia as a whole, and to restore the rightful rulers of Navarre.

- King Fadrique renounces for himself and his heirs his claim to the Portuguese throne, and King Alfonso of Portugal officially cedes Galicia to the Castilian kings to rule in his name.

- The Monarchs of Iberia will crack down upon Saracen piracy in the Mediterranean and Atlantic oceans.

- The Kingdom of Castile recognizes Sardinia as Aragonese territory and commits to any future campaign to reclaim the island.

- The Kingdom of Castile agrees to open the straight of Gibraltar to Aragonese ships.

Are all parties involved <@1007414347347271730> <@298192682554228738> in agreement?

In the view of God, His Majesty Edward V, **has passed**.

He lived a long 61 years but failed to pro
events.fullText:
# The Act of Succession
<@1106651545296195655> 

In the view of God, His Majesty Edward V, **has passed**.

He lived a long 61 years but failed to produced legitimate heir. Though his reign was contested at times, he triumphed over those that would throw glorious Britannia into chaos. In his crowned years he took to ward the young **Arthur Tudor**, whose father Edward had proven to be the unfailing pillar and Lord Chancellor of the late government.

At the end of his days he declared the now young man to **be his heir**. Arthur's claim was distant, via Edward III and the Beauforts, but his **kind-hearted** nature and aptitude is undoubted. By witness of the reinstated Archbishop of Canterbury, the King, and his own father, Arthur Tudor was declared the **Prince of Wales** and heir of England and her domains.

events.fullText:
### Rebellion out the Gate

The announcement of the Tudors as the heir apparent was met with doubt from some, though tacit acceptance by many ||after bribes||. Notably, this sentiment was not shared amongst many of the [now marginalized](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1525940495199768647) Lollard community who rightly viewed the ascension of the more zealous Tudors as an end to their liberties tacitly granted by willful ignorance from previous monarchs.

*Rebels rose up around the country and were swiftly put down in many. Heavily Lollard Lincolnshire resisted to the bitter end and had to be put down to by both the new King's uncle Theodore and the reformed Royal army itself.*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Light of the Night

Arthur was not attached to luxuries of the body, he was often spartan in their regard in fact. However when news of his su
events.fullText:
### The Light of the Night

Arthur was not attached to luxuries of the body, he was often spartan in their regard in fact. However when news of his subjects rebellion and Uncle's march against them arrived in London he could not understand it. England was his before the eyes of God. Were those that were now his subjects unhappy with his short reign? Had he done something to displease them already?

More than all else, he desired to make good for their needs. He was King by God...and by the assent of his people. Thus was the custom of Wales and his father's people. Arthur would govern according to that as well as the customs of his Norman forebears as well.

*Comfort came in the form of Beatrix Hood. A woman of more senior age, but kind and understanding eyes. She spoke no lie, and spoke no harshness that ran contrary to his own generous nature. From their first meeting the ending was known to all but the two. Some months later she was showing signs of pregnancy, and with it, the eventual birth of **Henry FitzArthur** - the unacknowledged but widely known son of the young King.*

A common practice among kings. Few questioned it, and some even celebrated the seeming support both provided each other...so long as Arthur did not lavish titles upon his clearly illegitimate son of an already dubious line.

**## The Hussites Return to Communion with the Holy Mother Church <:mary3:1126382749612249108> **

A papal representative, **Phelix Dinglinger**, a na
events.fullText:
**## The Hussites Return to Communion with the Holy Mother Church <:mary3:1126382749612249108> **

A papal representative, **Phelix Dinglinger**, a native of the monastery in **Munich**, met with Jan of the Hussites. For many days, weeks, and eventually months, they debated the spiritual concerns of the Hussites and the authority of the Church.

They argued over the language through which the Word of God should be taught, the proper administration of Communion, the nature of the Body of Christ, and the question of His Blood 🩸 . Their discussions often continued deep into the night, with the wine <:resource_wine:1481705094922768505> running dry and fresh barrels repeatedly brought up from the monastery reserves.

At last, confusion gave way to understanding, and an agreement was reached:

Prague shall be elevated in status, and a new **Archbishopric of Prague shall be established.** The Archbishop of Prague shall answer **directly to the authority of Rome.**

All churches must use sanctioned Bibles. These need** not necessarily** be copies of the Jacobus Bible, but they **must be written in Latin** and formally approved by the Holy Mother Church.

Communion shall be offered in both forms, bread and wine, in every church. A full barrel of sacramental wine shall be commissioned for every parish (500 gold given for this). Any parish found without a full barrel of wine shall be investigated and charged with heresy. Furtermore, 15 gold a month will be sent to Prague in payment for Communion Wine.

Papal Wine is being delivered to Prague <@261290667106369536> . This wine was bought from <@315868897012219904> (when we unpause i will give you 500 gold)

Of Ednyfed Fycha, of Marchudd ap Cynan, and eventually Magnus Maximus himself. The Welsh of now and old have
events.fullText:
## Kymry a Romani - The Welsh and Romans

Of Ednyfed Fycha, of Marchudd ap Cynan, and eventually Magnus Maximus himself. The Welsh of now and old have grand claims about their lineage from the first people of the land and the Roman conquerors who then settled on Britannia in the ensuing centuries. Its truth? Largely an irrelevancy for those intent on their own plans.

Arthur Tudor never intended to marry outside the British isles. By design of his father, the former King Edward, and himself he had advocated for an English match ||to secure his own uncertain legitimacy|| as is proper. When an envoy of the young Greek Emperor Demetrios <@302128861209165824> approached he was thus **rejected**, just as Portuguese envoys <@298192682554228738> had been some months earlier.

However the question stuck with the **kind-hearted** and **benevolent** King. Would not a match to the distant and venerable east be to his advantage, no matter his necessity?

*Arthur Tudor and Theodora Palaiologos were married in the Autumn of 1455 to much celebration in London and Wales. The Crown in England is not yet secure, but come the birth of a hopeful son it will be revitalized as it has not been since the White Dragon first landed on Albion's virgin shores.*

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# Oh Holy God, let us prosper in life...
**The Kingdom of Jerusalem in the mid-15th Century.**

It was a time of peace in the Levant. With Syria still
events.fullText:
# Oh Holy God, let us prosper in life...
**The Kingdom of Jerusalem in the mid-15th Century.**

It was a time of peace in the Levant. With Syria still recovering, the Mamluks confined to Egypt, and the Kingdom of Jerusalem having grown complacent, there was no one single state to claim authority over the whole of the region. Provided with ample funds from the Pope <@150411715757080576> , the Kingdom of Jerusalem thus prospered. Urbanization continued, with many new guilds being established in the major cities, and Ascalon and Beirut both seeing great improvements to their urban infrastructure. New monasteries, libraries, and even a couple of universities were constructed all across the Holy Land. Laws were passed by the Noble Council to encourage the nobility and the wealthy to educate themselves, and literacy amongst the elite thus rose markedly during this period. Settlers from across Europe also arrived, including tens of thousands from southern Anatolia, several thousand Savoyards and Sorbs, and some even from the distant lands of Scotland or Ruthenia. This influx of Catholic faithful has bolstered not only the presence of the True Faith in the Kingdom, but it has also provided many more laborers, soldiers, and nobility to a Kingdom in need of all three. Jerusalem may still not be the wealthiest city in the world, or even the Mediterranean, but it undoubtedly is better off than it was when the Holy Kingdom was restored.

As the years passed, it was not only the settlers from Europe who prospered in the Kingdom, however. With the Noble Council's increasing acceptance of the local Christians, some rose to prominence in this era of tolerance, including a wealthy Syriac named Addai Bar Khamoro. He rose all the way into the administration of the Kingdom itself. A devout Catholic, he has led the efforts to convert those in Beirut to the beliefs of the Roman Church.

Though his fellow Syriacs may not have risen to the same great heights as Addai, he nonetheless serves as an example for other local Christians to follow, and follow they doubtlessly shall. Through the ascent of the Noble Council, these local Christian figures are sure to continue to rise amongst the elite of Jerusalemite society. To these peoples, whom so often have found themselves under the thumb of foreign oppressors, the Kingdom of Jerusalem offers a place to be more than simply oppressed. For now the Kingdom remains under the firm control of the Frankish elite, but perhaps in time the formerly oppressed will become the oppressors themselves...

In any case, as the Kingdom of Jerusalem increasingly looked to the mainland as its prized possession, it was time for the Noble Council of Jerusalem to bow to the inevitable...

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
**A Bow to the Inevitable.**

Ever since the revolt of Cyprus was crushed in 1433, the royal domain of the Kingdom of Jerusalem has tried to maintain
events.fullText:
**A Bow to the Inevitable.**

Ever since the revolt of Cyprus was crushed in 1433, the royal domain of the Kingdom of Jerusalem has tried to maintain a solid grip over the island. However, with the immense task of administrating the Holy Land, Cyprus was increasingly left to rot. Though the vital island served as the last refuge of the Crusader nobility for centuries...it was simply no longer practical to treat it as a royal domain of the Crown. In 1451, after a series of contested votes, the island was finally given degrees of autonomy, allowing the Frankish nobles living there much greater power to rule the island, though still in the name of the King. The island of Cyprus is no longer just a Kingdom, but also a Fiefdom, with all the privileges that come with that distinction. In the decade since, the Greek serfs upon the island have found that the plantations prosper oncemore, and they are afforded much more protections and acceptance by the empowered local Nobility in the newly created Fiefdom of Cyprus. The general despair after the revolt's failure has now been replaced by contentment oncemore. As the island has settled it has prospered, much alike to the Kingdom it swears to beyond the sea. In giving Cyprus its long sought autonomy the Noble Council has benefitted itself and the island far more than anything gained from futilely attempting to maintain direct control over it. Again it would seem that the feudal system brought over from the elites of Europe still works in the far east, even if it remains a slow system to implement, and it remains an overall inefficient means of governance in so distant a land...

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# ...and spare us death in war.
**The Turkish Incursions.**

Upon the death of the Treaty of Silifke, the Nobles of Jerusalem expected retaliation fro
events.fullText:
# ...and spare us death in war.
**The Turkish Incursions.**

Upon the death of the Treaty of Silifke, the Nobles of Jerusalem expected retaliation from the Karamanids, and it came swiftly. Over the previous decade whilst the Kingdom of Jerusalem prospered, the Kingdom of Cilicia came under constant raids from the ruthless Turkish nomads. Though not as devastatingly efficient as they once were when the Anatolian plateau was still ruled by the Seljuks, the Turks still knew very well how to raid and destroy. The border defenses of Cilicia were well guarded, aided by high mountains and few passages between them, but even they could not fully defend the Kingdom. There was no singular great battle, for this was not warfare of a traditional manner with roaming armies and Kings fighting for glory, but instead it was a war of skirmishes and burning farmhouses. In this increasing violence the Turks inflicted many deaths upon the Armenians. Though the Turks had no major successes, they were able to make minor breakthroughs into the Cilician heartlands where they wrought havoc upon the fertile farmlands until they were eventually ousted back across the Taurus Mountains. The situation threatens the peace that Jerusalem has maintained in the mid 15th-century, for the raids have gotten to a point to bring even the complacent Noble Council to a great fury. Finally, after a unanimous vote, the banners have slowly begun to raise. Knights from across the Holy Land are commanded to gather, including the powerful Knights Hospitaller. In addition, the Noble Council have now written a letter to the Emperor of Constantinople, the King of Jerusalem's brother-in-law after the Emperor's marriage to the King's sister Eleanor in early 1445:

<@302128861209165824> ||To his Imperial Majesty, the faithful Demetrios I Palaiologos, Emperor of Constantinople, ruler of the Aegean, master of the Romans, and Holy Protector of the Sacred Lands of Christ.

I admit my ignorance, your Imperial Majesty, but I am not aware if you have knowledge of the Turk raids into the faithful Kingdom of Cilicia. Every year with regularity they come to slaughter Christians in the name of their heathen devil, and by the Rightful God the situation is untenable! I write to your Imperial Majesty, as protector of the Holy Land of Christ to help us put an end to this menace! For, as you know, your glorious Empire and our Holy Kingdom have made arrangements in secret regarding these savages before, and now the Holy Warriors of Christ will not only pretend to make war with you, but we will fight alongside you! Such a force of ours and yours fighting together as loving brothers in the embrace of Christ could not be defeated by a hundred thousand Mongols nor a million Saracens, for the all-powerful God will always grant victory to the righteous.

We know of your war against the Venetians. It is no concern of ours, and upon the restoration of peace with the Latins we would beseech you, as a brother to our King and protector of Christians, to launch a great war of liberation where we together may free the occupied lands of your ancestors and ensure Christian peace for all time.

The Third Member of the Noble Council of the Holy Kingdom of Jerusalem, and Baron of Napleuse, William of Simmern.||

Thus it would seem the Kingdom of Jerusalem has had enough of peace, for conflict in the north seems inevitable. Yet, unlike some of the Crusader conflicts in the past, this potential war at least seems to be noble on the surface. To end Turkish raids against Cilicia, that is a cause worthy of God, no? Such an assertion would be tested by the likely conflict ahead...and whether the Kingdom of Jerusalem emerges victorious.

-# The Banners of the Nobles of Jerusalem raise near Antioch

**(End)**

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
**Hansetag** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 


The Hansetag of 1456 celebrated the expansion of the Hanseatic League, with the cities having become
events.fullText:
**Hansetag** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 


The Hansetag of 1456 celebrated the expansion of the Hanseatic League, with the cities having become predominant in all the Empire, England and... well, in Constantinople. Thanks to the Leukás deal, the Hansa has a proper foothold in the Mediterranean, where several thousands of germans trade and maintain contact with the Greek lands, and a fleet of 20 ships is being built. The Leukas delegation has become one of the richest ones in the Hansetag. 

Several cities turned their membership into proper oversight from the Hansetag, including Nuremberg & Augsburg. However, the cities with the most importance remain Hamburg, Köln, Erfurt, Riga, Hansyhorod, Melk and, now, Leukas. In England, the cities eye Portsmouth, where several germans live, as a possible member to deal with all the English issues, but a bigger german population would be required for that. 

The most important issue being discussed in this Hansetag is the Teutonic Order, no longer an Order. The ruler of the Order had the right to attend and participate in the Diet, but is that privilege granted to its new head, the King of France, as Duke of the Teutons? Several cities have protested, for a ruler that embargoes the cities cannot possibly have representation. As Duke, he is not Hochmeister. Yet, he still holds Danzig, Kongisberg, and… Riga. Indeed, some consider that forbidding a representative of the King from attending will mean the closure of kontors and offices in the Teutons’ lands. Before sending out an invitation, a vote will be held.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## Fathers and Son - The Two Edwards

> "Y dygvys Caswallawn arnunt, ac y llas y chwethir [or chwe dyn], ac y torres Cradawg y galon o alar, o welet y
events.fullText:
## Fathers and Son - The Two Edwards

> "Y dygvys Caswallawn arnunt, ac y llas y chwethir [or chwe dyn], ac y torres Cradawg y galon o alar, o welet y kledyf yn llad y wyr, ac nyd adwadei pwy a’e lladei. Caswallawn a danyassei llen hual arnaw, ac ny welei neb ef yn llad y wyr, namyn y kledyf. Ac nyd oed llon ef llad Cradawg, canys nei idaw oed, mab y geuenderw."
> 
> A King cannot wear a crown already on another’s head. A son cannot shine in the shadow of his father. These self-evident truths cannot be disobeyed, yet they are often forgotten. Woe be to the family that should trample upon them knowingly.
> -# (Not at all what the Welsh says btw)
.

Many counts have started to use their ‘towns’ as an excuse to ask to be protected by the League, especially those besides Saxony, but the Hansa already has Holstein-Plön under its wing, it is unlikely that many more princes will be accepted, given that princes are especially fickle. events.fullText:
With the civil war certainly over now, the Hanseatic merchants are flooding England at Albrecht’s word that Edward V, undoubtedly grateful as he is, will not complain at their appearance. The german and frisian communities will establish themselves along England on those towns and cities that will have them, to tap their true potential and make the recovery of a battered England much faster. 

There is also much interest in Wight, despite Edward’s silence on that regard. Many germans are looking to establish themselves in the island, in theory only to trade. The League is willing to pay for any buildings and roads that need to be made to accommodate this new influx 
-# The Hansa will pay 600 ducats to make Wight a town, if Edward allows it

events.fullText: It has become clear, with the re-election of Albrecht, who was up against... his younger brother, and against a von Lübeck, that the Hanseatic League is shifting to a more dynastic principle. This is not new, for cities usually select their meisters form a bunch of important and rich families. What is unusual is this process being replicated in the Hansetag, where the Syndic is being chosen from the most important and well-connected families (the Münsterlands, the Lübeck, the Walbeck...)

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
###  A Tithe of Blood

*Ones made in matrimony, hardly yet blood.*

No matter who sits the throne, one can hardly forget that it was Percy pikes and P
events.fullText:
###  A Tithe of Blood

*Ones made in matrimony, hardly yet blood.*

No matter who sits the throne, one can hardly forget that it was Percy pikes and Portuguese blades that [drove off](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520910408578502786) twice their own number at Northhampton. A state off affairs made possible only by the bankrupting of the Neville treasury during the [sieges of Cardiff](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1520887122763190436) Tudor loyalists reply. Nevertheless, Edward V's eventual return to London was, most admit, a shared effort of the two once doggard houses. Yet in the division of honors the greater part seems to have been taken by Earl Edward Tudor and the new dynasty. The venerable Norman House of Percy has thus been left to their threatened northern domains and little else - a sorry reward for a deed sorely paid for.

*King Arthur's youngest sister Katherine Tudor is [to be married](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1525985564774629467/1525989742926102752) to ||the over twice her age|| Earl William Percy of Northumbria. This, in conjunction with [long anticipated plans](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1525969000104722535) in the north, aims to rectify the growing divide within England's ruling body before it can affect its actions further afield. For God and necessary justice, Arthur hopes this to be true ||in anticipation of an upcoming Parliament long neglected||.*

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## The Dirk to Arthur's Sword

Sir Holland had tried his best to tutor Arthur in the ways of court politics. He believed, erroneously perhaps, that he
events.fullText:
## The Dirk to Arthur's Sword

Sir Holland had tried his best to tutor Arthur in the ways of court politics. He believed, erroneously perhaps, that he had failed. 

Sir Giles Holland had become, in the decade since he was brought into Edward V's circle, an administrative workhorse. He knew almost every law one should know in the pursuit of his duties, and, more importantly, **knew exactly who he needed to have dinner with to expedite the slow wheels of deliberation.**

In his view, Arthur was - while a good king - completely naiive. He had tried to instill in Arthur a healthy understanding of which corruptions a King should tolerate and which they should squash, and how sometimes a King must be ruthless - but Arthur would have none of it. 

Still, Holland admits to himself, the man is *idolized.* This child of Welshmen had somehow overcame his crude, barbaric homeland and become the most charismatic King England had seen, since Edward III. 

Perhaps that is his place? Arthur, the bold, chivalrous sword, surely needs someone who can accomplish things in less... appealing ways. 

**Does not every sword need their dirk?**

**Sir Giles Holland would accept the role of Lord Chancellor, and serve with dilligence.**

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## The Yeomanry Stand Proud

By tradition, two men from each borough are sent to the capital upon the calling of a parliament, in order to represent t
events.fullText:
## The Yeomanry Stand Proud

By tradition, two men from each borough are sent to the capital upon the calling of a parliament, in order to represent the interests of the common folk in their common towns. 

These representatives occupy the **Lower House"** (which is, in practice, merely a different room in Westminster they occupy), which is a body with remarkably extensive power for its distinctly vulgar nature. 

Following the last Parliament of Edward V, there was discussion among the Lower House that the current selection of representatives by landed stakeholders (such as clergy, nobility, and such) is woefully insufficient to represent the interests of the boroughs, and that **landowning yeomen are surely responsible and learned enough to be able to select representatives from amongst themselves.**

This theory largely extends from the absolute chaos of the War of the Northern Marches, and the[ apparent moral corruption of the Upper House by so-called "malopapists"](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1525941149423108136), supposedly proud nobles who are willing to do France's bidding for coin.  

**Sir Giles Holland is well aware of this coming motion for the next parliament, and makes sure King Arthur is aware of this threat to nobility.**

<@1094722105855189062>

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
## The Round Parliament

***Windsor** is to be the point of congregation for all of England’s notables.*

From its Motte-and-Bailey foundation it has
events.fullText:
## The Round Parliament

***Windsor** is to be the point of congregation for all of England’s notables.*

From its Motte-and-Bailey foundation it has expanded into one of the principal locations on the middle Thames. This prosperity comes from the joint function of the more ancient Anglo-Saxon ‘Old Windsor’ with the nearby Norman ‘New Windsor'. The former was already a settlement of royal dignity and more comfortable lodgings before 1066, the latter a fortified castle given town rights which quickly expanded to fill regional needs. Both benefit tremendously from the traffic of the royally operated Windsor Bridge between it and nearby Eton, and both have been bastions of Crown authority for generations - no matter the King in question.

King Arthur opens the Parliament with the foundation of the *Kings's College of Our Lady of Eton beside Windsor* - Otherwise known as [Eton College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eton_College). It is to be a poor boy’s school based upon the model of [Winchester College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Winchester_College) and seeded by members of their faculty. Its initial foundation is relatively grand due to endowments by Earl Edward Tudor and the royal family itself. However it still has some years to go before it can produce able pupils for the likes of Magisterial Oxford and Cambridge.

||King Arthur **declines** to tamper with or disadvantage any of his subjects in anticipation of Parliament. He is King and he is to hear their affairs, grievances, and suggestions no matter their origin. God has given him no other way to conduct himself.||

Lord Chancellor Holland is commanded to summon the House of Lords and the Commons of England to Windsor. The Dragon headed [Black Rod](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_Rod) ominously raps on the doors of the Commons three times in order to summon them to hear the opening ceremony. All stand before Arthur himself, as he addresses the House of Lords and assembled persons, by unusually saying a few words himself:

> “My Chancellor shall address this herein chamber and I shall preside, as is right.” *he began in a deliberate tone. His accent is a [nearly perfect](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1449171134317723791/1527024080187031823) reflection of the accents of mainland England.* “Our high court of Parliament has been neglected and forgotten for too long. For it is set down by law and tradition that it should gather to discuss the realm in its totality when necessary for its people. A responsibility forgotten by some of my predecessors.”
> 
> *Arthur maintains a slow look around the room. He is yet a boy of less than three decades to many venerable lords. He does not permit himself to show the concern he has even upon his raised dias.*
> 
> “The matters of the Crown are as such: The representation of all the extensions of this contiguous land in this body, the use of sanctified speech in our churches, the governmental use of our esteemed language and its relation to the crown in Paris, and the raising of necessary dues to end all discord in the northerly theatres. I earnestly desire the words of those gathered. May God grant our crown the wisdom to see justice done for all in these sacred responsibilities."

*With that, Arthur would have his Lord Chancellor release the watching Commons. No monarch had yet deemed it necessary to infringe upon the Knights of the Shire and assorted Burgesses in their local deliberations. He himself would hear the sensible debate of the Abbots, Bishops, and Peers of the Realm before him.*

(The House of Lords is permitted to open debate in front of Arthur.)

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
The True Heir of Wallachia

The rumours of old were proven right, the Duke Ladislau Basarab was a bastard and the main branch was illegitimate all the
events.fullText:
The True Heir of Wallachia

The rumours of old were proven right, the Duke Ladislau Basarab was a bastard and the main branch was illegitimate all these decades. As the news reached Hungary, where the junior branch of Draculesti resided, the current head of the house, Vlad, proclaimed his branch to be the true one and laid a claim on the lands that should be his by birthright. 
Vlad gathered all possible evidence to back up his claim in the eyes of the Wallachian court. With the documents in hand and a small army, Vlad marched to Targoviste to challenge the current Duke and seize his duchy from the false ruler. 

Vlad arrived at Targoviste and demanded that the Council be summoned or… he would force it. The town and the court were surprised by Vlad’s quick and unexpected arrival, so they agreed to Vlad’s demands, in fear of what his army may do if refused.
As the Council began Vlad started to recite the old rumours and the news that had reached his holdings. Vlad, not able to persuade the Council, presented the documents that he had gathered. The Council had decided to examine the legitimacy of these documents and found them to be authentic. They proved what Vlad recited and the Council had little left but to agree reluctantly, but they boldly requested that Vlad would not convert Wallachia back to Catholicism. Before Vlad could agree, the current Duke Balc de Bukov objected. He tried to argue that the news and the documents could be fabricated and untrue, but as the documents were verified to be legitimate, this argument crumbled. Balc tried to continue to argue desperately that the main branch was legitimate, as his own legitimacy was tied to his connection to it, but alas he failed to do so. Vlad took back the stage and agreed to the demands of the Council.

After the Council had concluded, Vlad claimed his position as the new Duke of Wallachia. As compensation for Balc's troubles, Vlad granted him a castle in the western regions of Wallachia.
events.title: Eastern Europe and Asia Diplo

events.author: Hermans
events.snippet:
Opportunity Seized

Tulcea is no longer a small village on the marshes of the Danube. With trade flowing through the land, with it came opportunity an
events.fullText:
Opportunity Seized

Tulcea is no longer a small village on the marshes of the Danube. With trade flowing through the land, with it came opportunity and merchants. People flocked to Tulcea, trying to find a better life in this opportunity. The town grew so much that the Duke noticed it.

The Duke, seeing the growth of Tulcea, decided to visit the town himself. The Duke was impressed by what he saw: bustling streets and a market square, busy docks with merchants from all across the Black Sea and the Danube. The splendor of all of this was greater than that of the capital. The Duke decided to make an announcement before leaving back to the capital. He announced that after he saw what had risen on the marshes of the Danube delta, he was astonished, and now he decided to grant Tulcea the honor of being the second city of Wallachia.

As Tulcea became a city, this placed it on the maps and opened for Wallachia the gates of trade into the Black Sea and Wallachia to all of the Black Sea.

events.author: King Jester The III
events.snippet:
**The Bergish Struggle**

The constant involvement of the Bergish realm in foreign wars is starting to strain Berg proper, as economic growth has show
events.fullText:
**The Bergish Struggle**

The constant involvement of the Bergish realm in foreign wars is starting to strain Berg proper, as economic growth has shown what Berg can be, and the expansion of the Ruhr has become an ambition of the courts of Berg and Ruhr. However, Berg's constant involvement in Austria's wars, the Hussite wars, and now the Hungarian war has led to discontent among the Lower Franconians. But all efforts have not been lost, as the nobles have started to scheme to sway the nobles to the west, given their shared culture, to become more easily interconnected with Koln and the other lands.

<@253679178552049665> (If territory is not gain for Ruhr discontent will reach to another level as people in Berg lose faith in Berg's capability)

<@112960565344804864> (Will start to have noble characters make diplo rolls to sway other Rippuran Franconian nobles in the west to be more favorable to berg)

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet:
**The Russian Proclamation**



The Grand Principality of Muscovy-Vladimir, though mostly often just called Muscovy, has held control, through either
events.fullText:
**The Russian Proclamation**



The Grand Principality of Muscovy-Vladimir, though mostly often just called Muscovy, has held control, through either diplomacy or military prowess, gotten control over all the eastern principalities of the Rus. Initially established as outposts in the east from the Kievan Rus, they slowly grew farther away from Kyiv, with Novgorod being the center of the eastern Rus principalities.

Yet it was Moskva, a small settlement built within the 12th century, that grew in power through intrigue, diplomacy, marriage and military might, to the greatest city of the eastern Rus. Becoming the Grand Prince, ordinarily a title given to the tribute collector for the Golden Horde, yet kept as a symbol of authority after Muscovy-Vladimir had defeated the Golden Horde and broken the Tatar Yoke.

Yet Muscovy did not stop there, liberating Ryazan from the hordes, diplomatically subjugating Novgorod, and integrating them into the greater realm, expanding eastward into the rich mountains of the Urals and establishing settlements within the Pontic Steppe, to civilize it and stop raids from hordes, Muscovy has grown immensely, in size, power and riches.

Yet the eastern focus of the Muscovite rulers have led to Kyiv and Halych-Volhynia to gain control over the Rus principalities west of Moskva, creating 3 powerful Rus states, neither of them being able to proclaim to being the successor of the Kievan Rus. A schism in culture also has started, with the word Rus being called Russia, the Catholic conversions of the ones near the Carpathians under control of a French dynasty, and the Schism from Constantinople, while Kyiv keeps their relation to them.

The fate of the Rus people is divided, and it seems it will continue to be so. Nevertheless, Muscovy has grown beyond a mere principality. As such, it has started utilizing the name Russia, to showcase its new standing, and the Tsar title, same as king.

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Dracula and Bathory Clash**
<@493830084893999104> 

The dreaded duke of Wallachia would despite being one of the few military leaders to deal a blow
events.fullText:
**Dracula and Bathory Clash**
<@493830084893999104> 

The dreaded duke of Wallachia would despite being one of the few military leaders to deal a blow to Imperial forces and act as a loyal ally to the Anjou union would find himself rebuffed after the war from his ancestral seat in Transylvania. The new Monarch favouring the support of House Bathory the current rulers of the region over the support for a foreign duke. 

Stephen V Báthory would from his seat in Ecsed refuse to negotiate on the issue the fortress being a strong and well developed one, the Hungarian king offering in turn only support in Bulgaria an offer that would only weaken relations. 

now as Hungary marshals itself to move southwards it leaves a deadly threat within its rear that could well undo them all, for want of a single castle.

events.author: Murlock
events.snippet:
**Pope Leo XI Dolas Announces Removal of Censorship Laws for Roman Printers on his Death Bed <:admin:587043136988250153> **

On his deathbed, he refle
events.fullText:
**Pope Leo XI Dolas Announces Removal of Censorship Laws for Roman Printers on his Death Bed <:admin:587043136988250153> **

On his deathbed, he reflected harshly upon his own policies. He had been too strict in demanding that all Scripture be spoken only in Latin and that only works approved by the Church could be read or distributed. Yet he knew that, in Poland of his birth, compromise had been blessed by God, allowing many of the damned to be guided towards salvation. Perhaps what had happened in Bohemia should be permitted throughout all Christendom.

And so, upon his deathbed, perhaps moved by wisdom or by fear of Gods judgement, he declared **that men should be allowed to speak through the written word.** Books, arguments, translations, and learned works were to be copied and spread, rather than hidden from those who sought knowledge.

**It is the right of all true scholars**, theologians <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> , and learned men to explore Gods creation, to question what they do not understand, and to seek truth through study and reason. Faith should not be weakened by knowledge, **for all true knowledge must ultimately lead back to God.**

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**Looking towards a different eagle**
<@302128861209165824> <@759277551117402194> <@493830084893999104> 

The lose of Bohemia would not spite the king
events.fullText:
**Looking towards a different eagle**
<@302128861209165824> <@759277551117402194> <@493830084893999104> 

The lose of Bohemia would not spite the king for long, he would die it is said of a heart attack upon hearing the terms signed, the Anjou union rocked and humbled by the empire, though not so well bled for the kingdoms had no so exhausted themselves in the conflict. 

The newest king Anjou Laszlo the fifth of Hungary, Walych, Croatia would in a dramatic move declare the Hungarian court to be moved to the city of Pest on the other side of the Danube, calling for a meeting of all the great noble houses and even attendaded allied tributary realms. Though never in so many words, these actions along with the speech made to his new subjects would make it plain that the Anjou monarch now seeks to look southwards, though those who know him mark him as an arbitary and fickle man so it is hard to gauge such an event as a true shift in diplomacy or the simple flightiness of a aged monarch. 

Nevertheless the realm recovers swiftly and nobles are well bribed and made ready for a campaign southwards.....

**(Planning to launch a campaign to strike at the Byzantines in Skopia)**

events.snippet:

In humble reverence for Pope
events.fullText:
**Canonization of the First Polish Pope <:prestige:587042877356376103> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f0qIRuGVX60

In humble reverence for Pope Leo, the newly elected Pope Nicolaus V, the second Polish pope, has ordered the creation of a magnificent mural in his honour. The artwork will commemorate Leos life, his reforms, and his devotion to scholarship, ensuring that his memory endures within the Church for generations to come.

**Pope Leo has also been canonised as a man of thought, reform, and unwavering faith. <:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> **

Saint Leopold has been canonised for his good nature, one who fought for a better world.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Black Tower's Guests

Ten years in the solitary confinement. No guests allowed, no social calls, all letters screened. Their son was slain by
events.fullText:
### The Black Tower's Guests

Ten years in the solitary confinement. No guests allowed, no social calls, all letters screened. Their son was slain by his own '[foolishness](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1525575228464562287)' and their youngest daughter condemned to the convent. Their only visible legacy left alive was the memories of those guards that hated them, and the occasional correspondence from their eldest and yet surviving Daughter in the nearby Marches. A somber existence thus permeated the entire castle to perfectly reflect Wales's often gray skies.

Suggestions to find permanent solution to the two longstanding guests of the Black Tower at Cardiff have been thoroughly quashed by King Arthur himself. He will not have the blood of former royals on his mind and against his soul...||even if it is already on his father's||. The couple has been left to their own devices, as much as can be permitted, while the world moves on from the Neville reign via its deficient Queen - or at least that's how the official court historians take it down.

*Many expected former Queen Judith Piast and her husband William Neville to expire some years ago. Instead, they have survived for over a decade in their imposing prison as 'guests'. King Arthur only hopes that there was some connection between the couple more than a lust for the crown when they married...*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Continuity Act

Many of the acts passed under the dictatorship of the Neville's were revoked in the days after their fall. Their decrees, no m
events.fullText:
### The Continuity Act

Many of the acts passed under the dictatorship of the Neville's were revoked in the days after their fall. Their decrees, no matter the intent, have largely been labeled as the war-mandated actions of a dynasty under pressure. Arthur himself has pulled back on some of the more reactionary policies of Edward V and his father though.

Uniquely, the Neville's **emphasis on English** as the language of Government has endured. The Tudors themselves were of a marginal background in the previous language of court - French, to begin with. Given the choice of either the more local English, or their night unlearnable Welsh, they have chosen the former as the court language of their new and recovering Kingdom.

*By [decree of Parliament](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1528140974272942360) and King, French has been displaced as the language of England's government in favor of the land's native (and heavily French influenced) English.*

### The March on Scotland

The affair cannot wait any longer. The familial spat between Tudor and Lancaster has persisted even into the early years of Arthur's reign and has thus cast doubt on the stability of England and her renewed Crown.

The faithful <@150411715757080576>, brought back in line with the Tudor's reforms, have been made a part of this effort. Glory and God be with Engalnd and her armies in this final reunification.

(Just war for Northumberland, no going for Scotland proper rn)

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Fall of Newcastle

The civil war is, for all intents and purposes, done.

Newcastle was taken in assault ordered by King Arthur himself. The r
events.fullText:
### The Fall of Newcastle

The civil war is, for all intents and purposes, done.

Newcastle was taken in assault ordered by King Arthur himself. The remaining bastions of the Lancasters are fallen or soon to be taken without strong resistance. The Tudor army now prepares to march into the Scottish Lowlands themselves in order to compel the end of the northern war.

Arthur intends to gift large tracts of the formerly rebellious north to the neglected Percys and their strong support for his ascension.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### England United

The war ends conclusively after the outskirts of Edinburgh itself are reached. The Lancastrian domains, in their entirety, are reu
events.fullText:
### England United

The war ends conclusively after the outskirts of Edinburgh itself are reached. The Lancastrian domains, in their entirety, are reuinited with the crown and the House of Percy who have long lived there. Public trials against the House of Lancaster are forgone in the view that their male line is itself extinct. All claimants left are women after the passing of the aged Earl Peter during the war.

*England once again stands united. Wales and Ireland are loyal. All that remains is the foreign dynasty in Scotland itself...*

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The University of Pembroke

By approval of the Curia <@150411715757080576>, support of the Lords, and endowment of the King himself the **Universi
events.fullText:
### The University of Pembroke

By approval of the Curia <@150411715757080576>, support of the Lords, and endowment of the King himself the **University of Pembroke** is established in both Oxford and rightly Pembroke. The body at the University of Oxford shall be the main branch of this college, but the physical buildings in southern Wales shall equally provide many of the pupils and educations necessary for the crown's servants in years to come.

*These bodies, like most higher education establishments, have a distinctly theological bend. Lawyers, Judges, and artists that overwhelmingly go to work for the Tudor government itself attend its well supported halls in both Wales and England.*

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Anjou Royal Army**

As the realm looks inwards the issue of ruling such vast lands is ever a pressing concern, the spread of heresy and orthodox
events.fullText:
**The Anjou Royal Army**

As the realm looks inwards the issue of ruling such vast lands is ever a pressing concern, the spread of heresy and orthodox unrest while the military losses against the Imperial army show the need for a more standing reliable force. Using the core of veteran Mercenary regiments and officers from the Hussite then Imperial war the Anjou crown has levied (*through great political effort*) a fund of which to salary and hire such an army. 

inspired by the renaissance works of the university of Pdnesio the crown has organised forces partially along old roman lines, seeing well the Byzantine empires own successes and the need for discpline not just pure firepower. The armies black plate and standards setting them apart from the feudal forces of the Pannonian plane, much to the disdain of the nobility, such a force only able to maintain itself provided it gets results, lest the nobility calls for their dissolution.

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### The Passing of an Era

*Queen Judith Piast, King-Consort William Neville, the last of the Mathrafals in France, and now even Edward Tudor himself.
events.fullText:
### The Passing of an Era

*Queen Judith Piast, King-Consort William Neville, the last of the Mathrafals in France, and now even Edward Tudor himself.*

The generation that saw endless war and strife in England has largely passed. Those that now bear the burden of the land's current state are only bound to their memory, no longer their direct voice. As this time comes to pass it is hoped that some semblance of peace and prosperity will once again return...even as fears loom about King Arthur's still unsecure succession...

events.fullText:
**The Church Buys the Wycliffe Bible for a staggering 7000 Gold <:eu4_gold:839128116436664370> **
<@1094722105855189062> <@1106651545296195655> 
The church has found the orginal copy of the Wycliffe Bible, and in a moment of clarity and scholarship, offers to not burn or destroy the bible, but to buy the bible and for it to be on display in the Papal Libary to be studied. The Bible shall be translated into Latin, and show the many things Wycliffe got wrong, as well as point out how Latin is the word of god. The Bible is an interesting part of Church History, and it should be displayed.

**The true catholic church of christ has nothing to hide.**

This also represents the largest art purchase in modern times. <:eu4_gold:839128116436664370> <:eu4_gold:839128116436664370> <:eu4_gold:839128116436664370>  (Perharps forshadowing)

events.author: Ceb
events.snippet:
### A Strengthened Name

The marriage of Henry FitzArthur and Jeanne de Valois is **accepted**. King Arthur himself has all but acknowledged his only
events.fullText:
### A Strengthened Name

The marriage of Henry FitzArthur and Jeanne de Valois is **accepted**. King Arthur himself has all but acknowledged his only son as his own in the eyes of court, but has stopped short of legitimizing him in the eyes of the law. As time progresses this position increasingly slips as he now gives him royal dignity in at least France via his new marriage to King Henry's eldest and capable daughter.

*Tensions continue to mount as Queen Theodora <@302128861209165824> has yet to produce a son and the lords look to the King's only bastard son - who increasingly spends time in Paris <@460814348487753730>.*

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Great Entrenchment**

The years of careful power plays by the Anjou union would see a select few of Hungarian houses consolidate power over thei
events.fullText:
**The Great Entrenchment**

The years of careful power plays by the Anjou union would see a select few of Hungarian houses consolidate power over their peers both in Halych and the local older houses, invited in and favored the French would appoint the variously separate to a Báró council, a military command officially but unofficially a means by which the Magnates can organize their affairs over the head of the King, though Anjou protection of their lands and Dynastic prestige keeps them for now all well in line. 

- **Hunyadi **to the south of Hungary, lords of the river ways and breadbasket for the growing cities of the Danube

- **Cillei ** to the west within Slovenia and imperially focused, lost much in power and influence in the wars within Bohemia their house remains the middle men to Hapsburgs and with it the gateway to the west

- **Garai** of Croatia and much the voice of the liberals and opponents to the hard power of the church, though many whisper his house has the support of Hussite and Bogmolists nothing has been proven

- **Báthory** of the east and lords of Transyvania, holding with an iron grip the great many mines and towns of the eastern lands, their material support fueling the Anjou Black army, holding very much the sword of Damocles above the heads of the crown

- **Rozgonyi** of the north who since the courtly move to Pest have only risen in power, the voice of the growing Urban centers and much the sword of the great lords of central Hungary

- **Frankopan ** of the Adriatic, their voices ever leaning towards Italy, though at each turn overuled for favour of the south they remain a persistent opponent to the current Anjou policy, leaning towards instead Naples and Venice.

As the century turns Hungary remains at the vanguard of the future, while held ever back to Medieval standards...

So soon does the eagle become hunted in its own home.

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet: The andalusians revolted, their moorish descent betraying their allegiance. How many of them are moriscos, and do not respect the authority of their l
events.fullText:
The andalusians revolted, their moorish descent betraying their allegiance. How many of them are moriscos, and do not respect the authority of their lawful Crown? A man as kind-hearted as Fadrique, who heard their pleas, has been betrayed. They clearly deserve no mercy.

Tomás de Torquemada, Inquisidor General of the Crown, has been sent appointed and sent to the andalusian lands. No more will they defy the Crown, because they will adapt, or else. <@327561459976372224>

The Austrians will be allowed to go back to their families. Perhaps a smaller retinue of german soldiers would suffice, to serve the King? Their service in the Navarre War has proven how reliable they are, and such act would only improve the relations with the Archduchy
events.title: Central Europe and North Africa Diplo

events.fullText: The marriage of Juana de Trastámara with Ferdinand von Habsburg, heir to the Archduchy, and María de Trastámara with Guglielmo Wittelsbach, heir to the Rhine, marks a stark shift towards the Empire. This has no doubt been promoted due to the strong interest in Italy, Aragon, and the help of many german princes against the French invasion in Navarra <@763652399784919080> <@302527114283057153>
